About:
It’s a song that helps us move on from our memorable first loves. It looks back on the firsts, the memories, laughter, and the very mundane things about love.
We were all so young when we first felt love. And once it ends and we grow up, we tend to hold onto the entirety of the memories of it, thinking and knowing we could’ve done better. After all that time, the only feeling we felt aside from reminisce, was regret.
We hold onto those memories because we fear the loneliness and emptiness away from those happy times of feeling free and living.
The song states that it’s okay to be awkward and make mistakes when in love, but it guides us to the conclusion that we all must let go and say goodbye to the memories that we hold onto dearly, even if it pains us to do so because sometimes, holding on hurts, especially if there’s no one to help you back up again. No one but yourself

Title: 약먹고 존나 우리는 음악을 잘한다 물론 감기약!
i love you!
About:
“i love you” is a soft acoustic ballad that explores the struggles and feelings from a complicated relationship. The song’s chorus features vocal harmonies by her brother, FINNEAS.
On May 13, 2018, she teased a 15-second snippet of the demo to her fans. This snippet was re-posted to her Instagram story on January 2, 2019, where she confirmed it to be the twelfth track on her debut album. However, it was replaced by “listen before i go,” becoming the thirteenth track instead.
When asked by Radio Energy for her favorites on the album, Eilish listed “i love you” as one of them, citing the track as, “still one of my favorite songs I’ve ever written in my life.”
In an interview with PopBuzz, Eilish confirmed that the final three song titles on the album were intended to form a sentence, beginning with “listen before i go,” then “i love you,” ending on “goodbye.”
The sentence was initially supposed to begin with a track titled “i dont know, i just wish i wasnt breathing,” but Eilish stated that the song was never completed, and therefore could not be included on the record.
“i love you” was the longest track of Eilish’s discography at 4 minutes and 52 seconds, but was overtaken by the title track of her sophomore album Happier Than Ever, which is 4 minutes and 58 seconds long, just 6 seconds longer.
Many have also pointed out similarities between the chorus of “i love you” and Leonard Cohen’s iconic song, “Hallelujah”.
The song’s Spotify artwork was done by @junkie.cowboy on Instagram.

Title: Lose Yourself!
About:
“Lose Yourself” is the theme song from Eminem’s semi-biographical 2002 movie 8 Mile. Eminem is narrating the life of the film’s protagonist, Jimmy, up until the third verse, where Jimmy and Eminem’s journey converge.
Engineer Steven King told Rolling Stone in 2003 that Eminem “laid down all three verses in one take”, which Eminem has since shed doubt on.
Eminem stated in his 2008 autobiography, The Way I Am, that he wrote “in-between shooting scenes [for 8 Mile] and taking care of [his] kids” (pg 108-109.) Scans of the scrawled lyrics on A4 writing pad pages were also featured in the autobiography (pg 217-218.)
In 2014, the demo version appeared on Shady Records' SHADY XV compilation album, which featured the same beat and tone but almost entirely different lyrics.
Commercially, “Lose Yourself” was hugely successful and reached #1 in 20 countries, including the US. It marked Eminem’s first US #1, and held the top position for 12 weeks, becoming the third-longest chart-topper from a movie soundtrack (behind “I Will Always Love You” and “End Of The Road”). As of 2022, it is 13x Multi-Platinum in the US.
The song was lauded critically, too. It won two Grammys (Best Male Rap Solo Performance & Best Rap Song) and became the first rap song to win the Oscar for Best Original Song. Eminem didn’t attend the ceremony as he didn’t think he’d win, meaning he didn’t perform it, which is atypical for winners of the category. This was remedied when he performed the song at the 2020 Oscars.
The song has also had a huge cultural impact. Apple used the song for two commercials in the mid-‘00s to promote iTunes and the iPod, with Eminem featuring in the latter video. Chrysler used the instrumental and Eminem for their 2011 “Born of Fire” Super Bowl commercial. In 2021, Eminem opened up a restaurant in Detroit called “Mom’s Spaghetti”, which is a lyric that’s been meme’d extensively (even being given its own fan-made song.)

This song cover was released on White Day, March 14, 2018, as a gift for ARMYs from Jungkook of BTS. Before its release, Jungkook posted a preview of the song to the group’s personal Twitter account on February 25th, 2018.

RM replied to Jungkook’s post saying that he asked Jungkook to cover the song and Jungkook sent it to him on KakaoTalk (a Korean messaging service). RM also said, “When you’re listening to the song, it’s not Jungkook, it’s heaven.” The statement is also a wordplay on Jungkook’s name (정국), which sounds very similar to the pronunciation of heaven (천국/cheon-guk).

Title: Stairway to Heaven!
About:
“Stairway to Heaven” is one of Led Zeppelin’s most famous recordings, with many considering it the greatest rock song of all time. It tells the story of a greedy woman who is overly optimistic about her unpromising future.
The song resonated with young listeners, opening up a door to a different realm of spirituality and transporting them into a more mystical view of life. The vagueness of the lyrics also allows listeners to interpret the song in their own way, adding to the magic and mystery of the track.

Title: I Hate YoungBoy!
About:
On “I Hate YoungBoy,” YoungBoy Never Broke Again goes on the offensive against Lil Durk and many of those affiliated with him, from Apple Music to Gucci Mane.
The track is a same-day response to Durk’s “AHHH HA,” which features disses at YoungBoy, his child’s mother, and his 4K Trey crew. The escalating beef between the two artists largely stems from the death of Durk’s label signee and close friend, King Von, on November 6th of 2020. Von was killed after an altercation with Quando Rondo (a signee to YoungBoy’s Never Broke Again label) and Quando’s friend Lul Timm led to shots being fired.
YoungBoy calls out Durk directly and asserts that he is not afraid of any threats or attempts against his life. He tells those who don’t support him fully to pick a side of the beef and live with the consequences of their decision or indecision.

Lyrical gems such as “Christmas time, wake up, wake up, it’s Christmas Day!” – groundbreaking lyrics for a Christmas song – cement Paul’s status as one of the best lyricists of our generation. As the keen-eyed amongst you will notice, the song references Team 10’s seminal hit “It’s Everyday Bro”, but in order to keep it festive, he has changed the title to “It’s Christmas Day Bro”. Title: 너에게 못했던 내 마지막 말은 (Unspoken Words)!
The NeverEnding Story!
About:
“The NeverEnding Story” is the end theme of the 1984 film The Neverending Story. The film is, in turn, an adaptation of the novel Die unendliche Geschichte (1979) by Michael Ende.
In keeping with the film title, the song does not have a definite beginning or ending per se: It fades into verse one, and fades out during the coda, as if the song were part of a loop. It was composed by disco pioneer Giorgio Moroder, with lyrics by Keith Forsey. Forsey was a disco drummer and songwriter who also penned songs for the films Ghostbusters (1984), Beverly Hills Cop (1984), and The Breakfast Club (1985).
Limahl and Moroder recorded French and English versions of the song at a studio in Munich. The English version features soprano vocals by American singer Beth Anderson, while the French version (translated by Pierre-André Dousset) features French pop singer Ann Calvert.

Title: Hello
About:
Almost five years after her groundbreaking 21, Adele examines the past and tries to right her wrongs on “Hello.” On the surface, the song appears to be about a break-up from long ago, but the song can also be interpreted as Adele’s attempt at reconciliation with her former self. In a tweet, Adele called 21 a “break-up record” and 25 a “make-up record.”
Adele revealed the broad inspiration for the song:
No, it’s not about anyone specifically. It’s about friends, ex-boyfriends, it’s about myself, it’s about my family. It’s also about my fans as well.
A day before the release, Zane Lowe interviewed Adele about her future goals, touring, the album, and the “Hello” music video. Although “Hello” was released on October 23, 2015, a portion of the song was first featured during a commercial break on the UK’s X Factor on October 18, 2015.
The accompanying music video broke the Vevo record for the most views in 24 hours with 27.7 million total. Taylor Swift previously held the record with her video for “Bad Blood”, which had 20.1 million views in a single day. “Hello” was directed by Xavier Dolan and was the first music video to be shot by IMAX cameras.
In the US, “Hello” debuted at number 1 on the Hot 100, selling a whopping 1,112,000 downloads in its first week, almost doubling Flo Rida’s previous sales record (636,000 with “Right Round”.) The track significantly broke various streaming records after being replayed 61 million times in its first week.
In an interview with i-D, Adele contextualizes “Hello”:
The song is about hurting someone’s feelings but it’s also about trying to stay in touch with myself, which sometimes can be a little bit hard to do,“ she explains. "It’s about a yearning for the other side of me. When I’m away, I really, really miss my life at home. The way that I feel when I’m not in England, is…” (she pauses) “desperation. I can’t breathe anywhere else.” Why? “I dunno. I’m so attached to my whole life here. I get worked up that I’m missing out on things. So ‘Hello’ is about wanting to be at home and wanting to reach out to everyone I’ve ever hurt – including myself – and apologize for it.

About: By Michael Preston and Dion McGregor. 1959

Title: 그 겨울이 잠든 거리에서 (The street where our winter is)!
JISOO - 꽃 (FLOWER) (English Translation)!
About: “FLOWER” is the lead single in JISOO’s debut single album “ME”. The song is about “a woman who feels cheated and taken for granted in the relationship”. With the use of floral metaphor, the song is more about how happy the woman is after that broken relationship and she’s ready to “blossom”. “FLOWER” set a record of having the highest views in the music video of any K-Pop music videos in 2023 so far (as by 31 March, 2023).

Title: Lord I Need You!
About:
On “Lord I Need You,” Kanye reflects on his strained relationship with estranged wife Kim Kardashian in light of the couple’s separation.
In the bittersweet song, Ye accepts the reality of their separation, recounting the good aspects that she has brought into his life, such as their children. Kanye even specifically highlights Kim’s presence at his Donda listening events, expressing his gratitude for her support post their separation.

Title: AleXa - 오랜만이야 (Never Let You Go) (English Translation)!
TWS - 첫 만남은 계획대로 되지 않아 (plot twist) (Romanized)!
About:
“첫 만남은 계획대로 되지 않아 (plot twist),” the title track from TWS’s debut mini-album, Sparkling Blue, is an electrifying hybrid-pop song that perfectly captures the excitement and uncertainty of a first meeting. With its powerful drums, exhilarating guitar riffs, and captivating synth sounds, the song begins with an intro that feels like stepping into a dream. The contrasting lyrics further enhance the song’s message, portraying the awakening from a dream-like state.
TWS, a rookie boy group, aims to bring forth naturalness and honesty in their music, and it’s immediately evident in their debut title song. Through “첫 만남은 계획대로 되지 않아 (plot twist),” they candidly express the excitement they feel at this very moment. The thrill and exhilaration of a first encounter resonate with everyone, and TWS channels this excitement into their debut song.
The accompanying music video for “첫 만남은 계획대로 되지 않아 (plot twist)” follows a school drama concept, capturing the vibrant moments of boyhood against the backdrop of a school setting. The video symbolizes the space of youth by showcasing scenes of friends, the dust settling on the white playground, sunlit classrooms, square desks, and green chalkboards. The storyline is a plot twist as the title suggests. They prepare themselves all night to make the first encounters perfect, but everything doesn’t go as they’ve planned.

Title: Perfect Night
About:
In collaboration with the video game, Overwatch 2, here’s LE SSERAFIM with their first ever English release, “Perfect Night.” This single marks the game’s first-ever in-game musical artist collaboration. Alongside the release of the single “Perfect Night” is an exclusive in-game event, featuring a limited LE SSERAFIM-inspired game mode and exclusive in-game items such as LE SSERAFIM-inspired legendary skins for Overwatch 2 heroes Brigitte, Tracer, Sombra, D.VA, and Kiriko.
On the chill dance-pop track, LE SSERAFIM sing about the fun and exciting energy when they’re with their friends late at night.

Title: Super Lady
About:
“Super Lady” is the lead single from the second full-length album, 2, by (G)I-DLE. This empowering song is dedicated to all the superwomen in the world, celebrating their beauty and strength.
With its bold lyrics, the song encourages everyone to face hardships and adversity head-on while emphasizing the importance of unity and collective progress.
Their unwavering confidence leads to a dynamic performance directed by Kirsten Dodgen, who has worked with global artists including Justin Bieber, Rihanna, Ciara, CL, and more.
To convey the song’s message, the music video referenced powerful women such as Taylor Swift and Cruella in MIYEON and YUQI’s scenes.
Top Left: YUQI's scene in "Super Lady" music video | Top Right: Emma Stone in Cruella | Bottom Left: MIYEON's scene in "Super Lady" music video | Bottom Right: Taylor Swift in "Look What You Made Me Do" music video

Title: The Way I Am!
About:
The second single from Eminem’s Marshall Mathers LP, also featured on his 2005 album Curtain Call: The Hits
In the tradition of most of Eminem’s follow-up singles, “The Way I Am” features a much darker and emotionally driven sound than the album’s lead single, “The Real Slim Shady”. However, “The Way I Am” was actually recorded before “The Real Slim Shady”, as a direct result of the record company putting pressure on Em to create a poppy first single for the album.
The song has also been remixed by Danny Lohner featuring Marilyn Manson, who has performed the song with Eminem live on stage.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rDgokTyrzMc
It’s worth noting somewhere that this is one of the only rap songs to rely heavily on anapestic tetrameter— four anapestic feet (da da DUM) per line. In fact, the entire track except the hook uses this meter.
Anapestic tetrameter is more familiar from comic poetry, as in Twas the night before Christmas, Yertle the Turtle, and other Doctor Seuss stories–meaning that this form is not suited for the anger Em expresses in this rap, but being controversial is what he does best.

Title: Maniac!
About:
As explained by Conan Gray via MTV News, this upbeat bedroom pop banger is about people who send out rumors. More specifically, it was written “after getting a midnight drunk sob text from [his] ex.”
They’d been running all around town telling everyone that I was some freak who wouldn’t leave them alone when in reality, they were the freak, […] sending strange late-night texts and showing up at my shows unannounced.
The song was first teased by Conan in a Tweet when he revealed that “Maniac” is his favorite song off his debut album. The title of this track was also teased through a cryptic Twitter thread, giving out one letter at a time with the chance to let fans guess.
Six days before Halloween, the spooky-themed music video was released alongside the track. It’s starring Jessica Barden, an actress famous for her role as Alyssa in the Netflix series The End of the F*`ing World. In the video, Conan and Jess are on the mission to fight off Jess' ex-boyfriends, maniacs, who have risen from the grave.

Title: Loveleen!
About:
Loveleen is a song about Rejjie growing as a problematic kid, even though, beloved by his parents. Apparently his father was kind of a thug (drug seller and shooter), and his mother was more of a moral and rational person.
In the hook he repeats some iconic elements of being a thug, these are also symbols and ambitions of the type of people he and his father may have frequented and belonged to on their best times.

Title: Three Lions!
About:
A song of hope in the face of defeat, “Three Lions” is a song by English band The Lightning Seeds in conjunction with comedians David Baddiel and Frank Skinner, and was produced to commemorate England holding the 1996 European Championships.
Its refrain, “Football’s coming home” has become a popular rallying cry for fans of England’s football team, and is in reference to the fact that England is recognized as the birthplace of modern football as it is known today.
Despite its release in 1996, the song’s popularity during international football events has allowed it to periodically pop back into the charts, most recently during Euro 2020, but most notably during the 2018 World Cup. England was unable to bring it home yet again and lost the semi-finals to Croatia.

Title: NewJeans - Super Shy (Romanized)!
About:
“Super Shy (Romanized)” is a song by K-Pop Band “New Jeans” off the EP “Super Shy (Romanized)”.
The song is about Danielle singing about how she really wants to go out with a guy, while also talking about how he might not be into her, and how she’s afraid of a rejection. Hence the “I wanna tell you but I’m super shy, super shy.”

Title: (G)I-DLE - 퀸카 (Queencard) (English Translation)!
About:
This new title track of (G)I-DLE “Queencard”, released on May 15, 2023, introduce the Y2K aspects and mixes them with modern tropes; having early 2000s song melodies but keeping it in the times.
In “Queencard”, (G)I-DLE talks about not changing for people, to able to be comfortable in your own skin, and always standing out from the rest in your own, wonderful, unique way.

Title: NewJeans - Hype Boy (Romanized)!
About:
“Hype Boy” is the second single recorded by South Korean girl group NewJeans from their debut extended play, New Jeans. The song combines moombahton and electro-pop sound with lyrics about the feeling of being infatuated by someone.
“Hype Boy” became one of the most popular singles of 2022, garnering commercial and critical success. The choreography for the song went viral on social media platforms like TikTok and Instagram. In December 2022, Rolling Stone Magazine placed “Hype Boy” on their list of the top 100 best songs of 2022, highlighting it as a “standout” from the group’s EP.

Title: NewJeans - Ditto (Romanized)!
About:
Different with OMG, this song has more calming and unique sounds that make this song itselfs feels special. This is the track 2 of the ‘OMG’ Album. This was also the pre-released single, before the release of OMG.
This song has a deep meaning, its about the relationship between fans and Idol. Though in the lyrics itself wasnt really appearing the true meaning, we can totally see them by the MV of Side A and Side B. The main character of the MV is also called Ban Heeso (which pronounce Bunnies, and Bunnies is the fandom name). One of the members, MINJI were also credited in this song. Marking this is the first ever her credited song.

Title: NewJeans - ETA (Romanized)!
About: The song “ETA” by NewJeans (뉴진스), is a song about NewJeans' attempt to get their friend Eva to finally break-up with her cheating boyfriend. The pre-chorus shows this heavily in which the translation states “The day you couldn’t come to my birthday party, The day Hyejin got in so much trouble, The day Jiwon broke up with his girlfriend, The day without you he was always, The day he showed up dressed to the nines. Heard him say". This verse is describing all the times the girls had seen Eva’s boyfriend out with another girl. Jiwon broke up with his girlfriend because she was caught with him, Hyejin got into so much trouble because she was caught with him. Eva is furious about all of this, before at the end of the MV you can see Eva’s now Ex-boyfriend’s shirt sticking out of her trunk.

Title: Spicy
About:
“Spicy” serves as the Bonus Track on the 1st King’s Disease album. It marks the first time Nas has collaborated with either Fivio Foreign or A$AP Ferg. Nas would later link up with fellow A$AP member, A$AP Rocky, on Wave Gods, for his 2021 Christmas Eve album, Magic.
On this record, the artists discuss their lifestyle and origins, over a fast-paced Hit-Boy beat

Title: Standing Next to You
About:
In “Standing Next to You,” Jung Kook (정국) adopts the grand “us-against-the-world' mentality prevalent in many 2000s pop songs such as Westlife’s “You Raise Me Up” and Beyonce’s “Halo.” These simple yet powerful emotions are laid on top of this dramatic, jazz-funk, disco-pop instrumental that compliments the romantic retro aesthetic of the song.
The song’s lyrics express the strong feelings the narrator feels for a loved one from simply standing side-by-side with each other. There are also small easter eggs in the lyrics that seem to reference Jung Kook’s past solo songs and some past BTS projects.
The music video is a performance-dominated piece, with powerful and texture dynamic-heavy choreography that resembles the suave routines of the King of Pop, Michael Jackson. This strenuous choreography is performed throughout abandoned and warm-colored, usually golden-colored, scenery and sets.
Ukrainian model Pasha Harulia acts as Jung Kook’s female love interest in the M/V, adding some steamy tension to the video.

Title: Standard!
About:
KitschKrieg, welche bisher größtenteils für Trettmann, Haiyti und Joey Bargeld produziert haben, veröffentlichten im Oktober 2018 ihren ersten eigenen Song „Standard“.
Für den Song featurten sie drei der angesagtesten Künstler des Jahres: Gringo, Ufo361 und Gzuz, sowie Trettmann, welcher die Hook übernahm. Neben ihm wird in der Hook ein Sample aus dem Song „Knöcheltief“ von Gzuz und Trettmann verwendet, welches das Wort „Standard“ von Gzuz enthält und mehrfach wiederholt wird.
Den ersten Part übernimmt Gringo. Im zweiten Part rappt Ufo, welcher im Musikvideo durch einen Altar und die Texteinblendung „R.I.P. Ufo361“seinen „Tod“ inszeniert.
Dies diente zur Promo seines großen Comebacks 2019 und Ankündigung seines Albums „Wave“.
Im dritten Part rappt dann Gzuz.
Wie, aufgrund der Besetzung, zu erwarten, stieg der Song auf Platz 1 der deutschen Singlecharts ein und erreichte nach kurzer Zeit den Goldstatus in Deutschland und der Schweiz, sowie über 50 Millionen Klicks auf YouTube.
Anfang 2019 zeichnete Hiphop.de den Song als „Song des Jahres 2018“ aus.

Song: 이브, 프시케 그리고 푸른 수염의 아내
Title: 이브, 프시케 그리고 푸른 수염의 아내 (Eve, Psyche & The Bluebeard’s wife)!
About:
“I wish for what is forbidden to me.” The key message of the tenth track in LE SSERAFIM’s first full-length album, “Eve, Psyche, and The Bluebeard’s Wife,” is that Jersey Club’s heavy use of “taboo” was solved by breaking it and moving on to a new world.
The song was hyped up by fans since the Track Sampler Video was released with a catchy rhythm. And more hyped when the Highlight Medley Video unveiled that the ‘I’m a mess’ part, which was originally written for The Hydra Trailer only, would finally be released in full song.
I’m a mess in distress but we’re still the best dressed
Fearless, say yes, we don’t dress to impress

Title: OMG
About:
“OMG” is a track released as a “Summer Double Feature” along with “Havana” on August 3, 2017, featuring rapper Quavo. She first performed the song on June 24, 2017, during B96 Summer Bash.
Camila’s most recent performances of the song feature a sample of Kendrick Lamar’s “DNA”:

Title: After LIKE
About:
Coming to break more records after the release of one of their top hits, “LOVE DIVE.” “After LIKE” is the title track to IVE’s third single album, of the same name.
Musically, the song consists of an electronic-disco pop track, and samples the iconic “I Will Survive” by Gloria Gaynor. The song’s lyrics express the feeling of admiring one so much that your feelings are more than just liking them.
The song blew up on many streaming platforms and websites. Including YouTube, where the song placed #4 on YouTube’s most trending music videos, celebrating the achievement of 40 million views in just two days. “After LIKE” also peaked at #4 on the worldwide iTunes album chart, and was the #1 album on multiple iTunes charts across the globe.

Title: Fast Forward
About:
“Fast Forward” highlights Juice’s new life with exotic cars, violence, and money after his fame and his success in the music industry.
After the 2018 summer hit “Lucid Dreams”, it would only make sense that Juice is now cashing out and living fast in life, which is also a lyric from the song “Fast” in his sophomore 2019 album Death Race For Love.
The song was leaked unexpectedly on May 16, 2019, therefore it is unknown if the track will make an official release.

Title: 음악의 신 (God of Music)!
About:
SEVENTEEN celebrates their festival of happiness in “God of Music,” the title track from their eleventh mini-album, SEVENTEENTH HEAVEN.
“God of Music” is a soul-funk-based song featuring a brass sound and cheerful synth. Impressive, funky, and rhythmic atmospheres remind the listeners of a festival and feel the energy of happiness in the group’s unique music styles.
The music video filmed in Hungary and the United Kingdom showcases a colorful world with music and instrumentals as the primary concepts to convey the song’s message that ‘no matter what language you speak, we were all happy together when the music played.’

Title: SEVENTEEN - 손오공 (Super) (Romanized)!
About: “Super” is a song by SEVENTEEN that encapsulates their strong willpower to further reach greater heights. The lyrics, written by Woozi, S.Coups, Vernon, BUMZU, and August Rigo, express how eager they are to go higher and solidify their stance as successful artists all while being in a safe and loving environment. The song is inspired by characteristics of the various forms of Sun Wukong (Sonogong) that exists around the world.

Title: BSS - 파이팅 해야지 (Fighting) ft. Lee Young Ji (English Translation)!
Dynamite
About:
“Dynamite” is an upbeat disco-pop song that sings of joy and confidence, bringing a new surge of ‘energy’ to reinvigorate the community during these difficult times. The song finds global superstars searching for happiness by doing again what they are best at—spreading joy to the world through music and performances.
It marks BTS‘ first song to be released completely in English as a lead artist. It is featured in the ad for Samsung’s Galaxy S20 FE series.

Title: Attention
About:
In “Attention,” Charlie expresses his encounters with an ex-girlfriend who’s trying to tempt him to come back to her by playing with his emotions, though he knows she is just looking for attention and isn’t interested in a permanent relationship. The song was released as the lead single from Puth’s second studio album, Voicenotes.
To promote this single, Puth opened an exhibit named “The Attention Room” on Melrose Avenue, Los Angeles which featured a LED infinity tunnel, further emphasizing the song’s theme of attention.

Title: 3 Musketeers!
About:
“3 Musketeers” is ppcocaine’s first official released single since her breakout hit, “DDLG,” and on this track features NextYoungin—ppcocaine’s close affiliate. Via TikTok, ppcocaine went on to talk about how her parents didn’t approve of her sexuality and other topics that impacted the song’s illicit meaning and lyrics.
ppcocaine first previewed “3 Musketeers” on June 26, 2020, through a now-deleted Instagram post, then released the song weeks later on July 7, 2020.

Title: H1-KEY - 건물 사이에 피어난 장미 (Rose Blossom) (English Translation)!
About: 건물 사이에 피어난 장미 (English Title: ‘Rose Blossom’), released in January 2023 from upcoming rookie girl-group H1-KEY is an upbeat ballad featuring on the group’s first mini album. Written by Young K (DAY6) and Hong Ji Sang (JYP producer, composer and songwriter), the song achieved H1-KEY their first #1 on Korean Music Charts.

Title: The Unforgiven!
About:
A metal ballad co-written by Ulrich, Hammett and Hetfield. This song is about the struggles he had with religion-related to his mom’s death. His relatives did not want to give James’s mom the treatment she needed because they thought God would help her through it. He starts to question the legitimacy of religion.
The horn sound intro was taken from the movie score of the appropriately titled The Unforgiven, and reversed, for legal reasons.
Hetfield’s singing was inspired by Chris Isaak’s in “Wicked Game.” He was used to screaming, not singing.

Title: Off The Record
About:
“Off the Record” is the second single from the album I’VE MINE by South Korean girl group IVE. The dreamy city-pop track acts as one of the three title tracks from the album.
A song which reminds of a scene from a musical movie about a group of girls wondering about love in the depths of the night when no one is asleep. It is balanced by luscious vocals and groovy and heavy bass guitar. It is an attractive song that features the colorful tones of the members on top of rhythmic beats.
The song features Ryan S. Jhun and Seo Jieum’s work, who has worked with IVE in their past tracks like ELEVEN, LOVE DIVE, and many more.

Title: Digital Dash!
About:
This song sets the tone for the record. Drake and Future are reaching the pinnacle of their careers, and they have no plans of slowing down.
A “digital dashboard” is the heads-up display found in luxury vehicles. Future refers to a slew of expensive rides throughout the song, including the BMW 6 & 7 Series, Rolls Royce Ghost, 1963 Chevy Impala, and Lamborghini.
The Los Angeles Times described the song, along with several of the tracks, as “grandiose productions that feel ripped from Future’s playbook,” while The Fader compared the song to Future’s “Never Gon Lose” from his 56 Nights mixtape.

Title: JISOO - 꽃 (FLOWER) (English Translation)!
About: “FLOWER” is the lead single in JISOO’s debut single album “ME”. The song is about “a woman who feels cheated and taken for granted in the relationship”. With the use of floral metaphor, the song is more about how happy the woman is after that broken relationship and she’s ready to “blossom”. “FLOWER” set a record of having the highest views in the music video of any K-Pop music videos in 2023 so far (as by 31 March, 2023).

Title: Teddy Bear
About:
In an interview with Groundsounds, Melanie said “Teddy Bear” is about a teddy bear that’s trying to kill her. Perhaps this homicidal bear might just be the one she viciously ripped apart in the music video for “Pity Party”.
Alternatively, considering the running allegory of Cry Baby’s life for adult situations, “Teddy Bear” may be about an abusive relationship, or one that took a turn for the worst.
Either way, Melanie has gotten a Teddy Bear tattoo.

Title: Sunday Candy!
About:
The first single off The Social Experiment’s project Surf. Chance appears all over the track powered by vibrant live instrumentation – rapping and singing throughout what seems to be an ode to Chance’s grandmother and her lifestyle and personality.
For many black Americans, grandmothers are the staple matriarchs of the family, and the foundation of identities.
Artwork by Orlando Hays
(note: the candies in the bowl are life savers – not peppermints)
The music video was released April 12th 2015 (check the behind the scenes video here, and an overhead view of the action here):

Title: All Night!
About:
“All Night” is a groovy love ballad. The song illustrates a wise love, one that has experienced deep pain and disappointment yet was able to come out the other side determined to keep going.
This track serves as the unofficial ‘close’ to the album’s emotional journey, before Beyonce’s first single from Lemonade, “Formation.”
The chorus samples the infectious brass line from OutKast’s “SpottieOttieDopaliscious.”
It’s not the first time Beyoncé has sampled the “SpottieOttieDopaliscious” horns either—those same trumpets announce Nicki Minaj’s first verse on the “Flawless Remix.” She also used the horns during “Flawless” performances in the “Mrs. Carter Show” tour before the remix was even out.

Title: 화이트 (White)
About:
“White” by The Boyz is a remake of the Fin.K.L song named White.
The song revolves around the theme of winter and the warmth of love. The lyrics describe a couple reflecting on their relationship and cherishing their love during the winter season. The song uses imagery of snowfall and white to symbolize purity, innocence, and the special moments shared between the couple.

Title: Bad News
About:
Along with “Say You Will”, “Bad News” is one of the more instrumental tracks on 808s & Heartbreak.
The first two minutes features Kanye’s vocals. The song then shifts into instrumental territory with an orchestrated version of the main melody, followed by a refrain of bare drumming and low octave harmonics.

Title: No More Parties in LA!
About:
This song is the first collaboration between Kanye West & Kendrick Lamar. The L.A. based artists swap stories on “No More Parties in LA,” playfully venting their frustrations with “rich people” problems and highlighting Hollywood’s greatest production: the fake celebrity lifestyle. Kanye’s verse has drawn parallels to his sophomore album, Late Registration.
“No More Parties In LA” has its origins in the My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy sessions, where Madlib left five beats with Kanye, including this one:
I just paint the scene of how I felt sitting there with Madlib working on these tracks and just hearing the textures.
After the pair joined forces for Kanye’s Yeezus Tour and Kendrick appeared in the writing credits on Kanye’s 2015 hit, “All Day,” rumors and speculation began to swirl about a possible collaboration. The song’s snippet was originally released as the B-side to “Real Friends.”

Title: NewJeans - Ditto (Romanized)!
About:
Different with OMG, this song has more calming and unique sounds that make this song itselfs feels special. This is the track 2 of the ‘OMG’ Album. This was also the pre-released single, before the release of OMG.
This song has a deep meaning, its about the relationship between fans and Idol. Though in the lyrics itself wasnt really appearing the true meaning, we can totally see them by the MV of Side A and Side B. The main character of the MV is also called Ban Heeso (which pronounce Bunnies, and Bunnies is the fandom name). One of the members, MINJI were also credited in this song. Marking this is the first ever her credited song.

Title: 3D!
About:
“3D” is an R&B pop song by South Korean singer Jung Kook. The track uses an impressive minimalist instrumental composition and percussive rhythms to create a nostalgic sound. The song is the second mainstream solo release from the singer after “Seven,” in July 2023.
On the song, Jung Kook expresses his strong feelings for someone he can’t reach with a witty touch. Since he can’t be there with his significant other, he chooses to watch in “3D.” The single features American rapper Jack Harlow, marking their first collaboration.
The song was released alongside its music video, which allowed Jung Kook to showcase the crisp choreography for the track.

Title: (G)I-DLE - Nxde (Romanized)!
About:
“NXDE” the title track for (G)I-DLE’s 5th mini album “I love” mixes a sampling Georges Bizet’s “Carmen — Habanera: L'amour Est Un Oiseau Rebelle” 1875 violin melody and a modern tone to provide a new melody.
For (G)I-DLE’s new title track the girls have mixed a number of celebrity influences, one being Marilyn Monroe, to attack the idea of female sexualization, female stereotypes, and the male gaze.

Title: Celebrity Skin!
About:
“Celebrity Skin” is the tenth single by Hole, released on September 1, 1998 by Geffen Records. It is the debut single from their third studio album of the same name and is Hole’s most commercially successful single, being the only one to reach #1 on the Modern Rock Tracks chart.
In October 2011, NME placed it at number 126 on its list “150 Best Tracks of the Past 15 Years”.
Billy Corgan reportedly wrote the song’s main guitar riff. Love stated that she hates it, but recognizes its catchiness and effectiveness.
I hated that riff. I hate it. I still hate it to this day. I love the rest of it, because I wrote the rest of it. Billy said, “Trust me, they will be doing this” (headbangs). And he was right!

Title: aespa - Next Level (Romanized)!
About:
“Next Level” is the second single released by the South Korean girl group, aespa. The single continues the group’s journey in their fight against the fictional villain dubbed as ‘Black Mamba.’
The storyline of the lyrics was explained via
which found aespa losing connections with their aes. In “Next Level” the girls travel to ‘KWANGYA’ or the ‘wilderness’ to stop Black Mamba by diving into the next level.
The track is a hip-hop-influenced song that interpolates elements of the soundtrack to the 2019, Fast & Furious Presents: Hobbs & Shaw.

Title: Eleven!
About:
“Eleven” is expected to be the second single from Khalid’s upcoming third studio album. He first teased the track on Twitter and Instagram on January 7, 2020, two days before its release.
The track is all about driving with a significant other, and is over a slow, acoustic-centered instrumental. Khalid croons to his significant other about their relationship, as well as asking about her feelings for him.

Title: Amar Sonar Bangla!
About: In this song, Rabrindanath is stating his love for his mother-tongue (also the language this is written in) Bengali, or the way Bengali speakers say it, Bangla. Note that Bengali is the anglicized way of saying it. This song is best known for being the national anthem of Bangladesh.

Title: Cupid (Twin Version)!
About:
On “Cupid (Twin Version),” FIFTY FIFTY find themselves in a love situation as they think Cupid and the whole thing about love is nothing but a dumb lie, and yet they still wish for love in their life. The song gives the listener the perfect Valentine’s feel with a taste of light dance-pop.
The song is the “twin version” of “Cupid” due to being sung primarily by members Aran and Sio and the change of lyrics—which is that this song is an English version. The single blew up throughout social media for its catchiness and simplistic genre in just a matter of one month.

Title: Memories
About:
“Memories” is an evolution of the more acoustic sound put forth in Maroon 5’s sixth studio album, Red Pill Blues, and specifically their #1 hit, “Girls Like You.” and was the first song released from their 7th studio album, JORDI. Lyrically, the song is a reflective look back at the past, while also paying homage to those in our lives who may no longer be with us.
Structurally, the song is a reimagining of “Pachelbel’s Canon”, a famous late 17th/early 18th-century canon created by German Baroque composer Johann Pachelbel. The progression of “Memories” borrows from the harmonic sequence of “Pachabel’s Canon”, with additional changes made by the song’s diverse team of songwriters who include Billboard-charting hip-hop recording artist Jon Bellion and members of the production and songwriting team The Monsters & Strangerz.
The song was a commercial success for Maroon 5, peaking at #2 on the Billboard Hot 100, becoming a landmark 10th Top 5 and 15th Top 10 hit for the band. The song also extended their prominence atop the Adult Top 40 and Adult Contemporary charts, becoming the band’s fourteenth number-one single on the Adult Top 40 chart and holding the number one position on the Adult Contemporary charts for twenty weeks. The song ultimately was ranked as the eighth biggest song of 2020 by Billboard.

Title: Fearless!
About:
“Fearless” is a message to the youth of today to live your life without fear and to never measure your self-worth based on money or the opinion of others.
The title was revealed on January 2, 2020, when Louis replied to a fan on Twitter who asked for the title of the ninth track on the album.

Title: 마지막처럼 (AS IF IT’S YOUR LAST)!
About:
BLACKPINK released “As If It’s Your Last” as a surprise gift for their fans intended to refresh them during the summer heat. A sort of prequel to the third edition of their ‘SQUARE’ series, this track was BLACKPINK’s first song to be released as a single title track rather than part of a double.
The song is centered around a heavy synth and bass line, with a variety of drum rhythms keeping the beat. In the classic YG Entertainment style, hip-hop and mood-building verses work together to build up to a catchy hook. Differing slightly from their previous releases, BLACKPINK shows a more free and upbeat side to themselves with this track.
The lyrics, of course, talk about love: a ride-or-die type of love, encouraging the listener to give their all as it if was the only love of their life. The choreography centers around the infamous finger heart so commonly shown by K-Idols, as if BLACKPINK offers their love to their audience.
BLACKPINK teased this song through a variety of means, including multiple teaser photos, an MV teaser, and a behind-the-scenes preview, although the hairstyle changes on each member prior to the news of a comeback built the hype enough to begin with. They also aired a live broadcast on Naver’s V-Live around 2 hours after dropping the track and music video.

Title: Baggy Jeans
About:
NCT U comeback with their new single, “Baggy Jeans”, which also serves as the second title track off the NCT’s fourth studio album Golden Age. The song is a hip-hop dance song with the members’ charismatic vocals, and the lyrics compare their confidence in their incomparable charms to baggy jeans.
For this song, the listeners see the return of the lineup of The 7th Sense – Taeyong, Mark, Jaehyun, Doyoung, and Ten.

Title: TAEYEON - INVU (Romanized)!
About:
Taeyeon is indeed a prominent figure in the K-pop industry, known for her powerful vocals and emotional performances. “My Voice” was her first solo album released in 2017, showcasing her talent as a solo artist. The song you mentioned, which is the title track from her third solo album, was released on February 14, 2022.
The song’s theme revolves around unrequited love, with the vocalist being deeply affected by the fact that the person she loves does not reciprocate her feelings. Despite knowing the risks of falling too hard, she still struggles to get over the addressee. Taeyeon’s lyrics express her commitment to love and how she puts her all into relationships, which makes it even more challenging for her to move on.
The song was produced by PhD, a duo of European musicians named Daniel Davidsen and Peter Wallevik. They collaborated with writers Jess Morgan, Rachel Furner, and Jinli to create this track.
Overall, Taeyeon’s solo career has allowed her to explore her individual musical style and showcase her vocal prowess beyond her work with Girls Generation. Her albums and songs have resonated with fans around the world, and she continues to establish herself as a successful solo artist.

Title: Breathing Underwater!
About:
Romantic sixth track to the band’s sophomore album Choose Your Weapon. It’s an ode to the concept of love, romantic or otherwise; and a tribute to the musical prowess of soul legend Stevie Wonder.
Nai has said a few times that Stevie Wonder’s works were a big part of her childhood and her understanding of music. She also said that «the riff for “Breathing Underwater” was inspired by kora, an African harp from north West Africa.»

Title: Untouchable!
About:
“Untouchable” details the racial injustice in America from a white person’s perspective on the first two verses and finally switching to the views to that of a black person in the final verse.
The song was released the day after the former South Carolina police officer Michael Slager was sentenced to 20 years in prison for the shooting death of Walter Scott in 2015, while he was fleeing from the scene in broad daylight and was unarmed.
Speaking to Michel Martin on NPR’s “All Things Considered,” Eminem explains that the frequent stories of U.S. police brutality prompted him to want to address the subject in his music. Moreover, he hopes that both “Untouchable” and Joyner Lucas' “I’m Not Racist” will inspire a broad audience to see a controversial issue in a new light, as well as continue this national conversation on racial inequality. He further states:
I’m not talking about, “All police are bad.” I’m saying that this is the perspective from the racist white cop. This is what got me infuriated, and two years ago got me so flustered I couldn’t even write about it because my thoughts would get too scattered.

Title: Permission to Dance
About:
“Permission to Dance” is a dance-pop song that was made to “make hearts beat to the rhythm of BTS’s positive energy.” The track marks the group’s third non-Korean main release of 2021, following “Film out” and “Butter.”
In the livestream the group hosted on V Live after achieving their fifth week atop the Billboard Hot 100 chart, the members revealed that they worked on “Permission to Dance” in February 2021, before their latest record-breaking single “Butter.”
For the song, the septet teamed up again with people they’ve trusted musically before—singer-songwriter Ed Sheeran with whom they collaborated on “Make It Right” off their 2019 album Map of the Soul: Persona, and Jenna Andrews who worked with the group on “Dynamite,” “Savage Love (Remix),” and “Butter.”
The name of the song was released by accident on June 15, 2021, by Universal Music Japan with the album’s pre-order announcement, and was quickly deleted. Again, the name of the song and the involvement of Ed Sheeran was spoiled by the artist himself during an Instagram Q&A, when he confirmed that he had teamed up with BTS once again for the group’s upcoming song. He officially confirmed it later during an interview with the radio show Most Requested Live on June 27, 2021.
“Permission to Dance’s” official teaser received over 16M views and 3M likes on YouTube in its first 24 hours of release.
It’s worth to mention that the song’s release date was set not without a reason, as it’s the eighth anniversary of the group’s fanbase, ARMY.

Title: The Weekend!
About:
SZA has said that this song is about three women, not just two, all of whom are being played by the same man. However, one decided that they did not care.
The song serves to empower women above the idea of a man as the center to all of their actions. In positioning all the women in this one man’s life as being aware of what they want from him, it diffuses his lies to them and makes him look like the foolish one.
The rather artistic and simple music video (released on December 21, 2017) is directed by Solange and features SZA in different settings moving to the song. The video also features words at the bottom of the screen that are not lyrics, but are still important to the song’s theme:
One opted out
An imbalance of power, shifted the whole tide
It waved
And waved
The cover art for the single was designed by SZA’s friend Sage Adams and is inspired by Henri Matisse’s “The Dance.”

Title: Fact Check (불가사의; 不可思議)
About:
Kicking back in 2023, here comes NCT 127’s new single “Fact Check”, the title single in their fifth Korean-language studio album of the same name. Written in the key of F Minor with a tempo 130 BPM, the group deliver an energetic, Afrobeats synth loops-based track that finds them in supremely confident mode, even in the face of being fact-checked against timeless art pieces.
For the accompanying music video, it was scheduled to release the same time of the album’s release, at 1PM (KST) on October 6, 2023. However, due to some technical reasons, the music video was delayed two hours and finally released at 3PM (KST). The dance choreography was done by Taeyong himself.
With eight members participate in dancing for the track, this marked the first time that NCT 127 will promote the album without Taeil, who had to being operated his femur after his terrible car accident in two months ago.

Title: Love Me Like This
About:
NMIXX slayed again with the title track from their first EP, “Love Me Like This.” Expresses way to be their lover: love them like that, and they’ll love you right back.
“Love Me Like This” is another MIXXPOP track with a different vibe from O.O or DICE. Mixed with street vibes, bouncy rap, and R&B-style vocal lines, and is an up-tempo R&B pop song with a lively percussion sound.
And they repeated the chorus with the catchy phrase “Love me like this, love me like that,” which can easily attract all listeners.
It captures the entire connection and process of change of people who have come to love themselves and each other due to “Wisdom, Love, and Courage,” which is also the primary concept of this album.

Title: everything i wanted!
About:
“everything i wanted” sees Billie recalling a nightmare she experienced where she committed suicide and saw all her friends and fans turn their backs on her. The lyrics showcase Billie’s strong relationship with her brother FINNEAS, as he’s the one she goes to for support when dealing with these types of problems.
The song is Billie Eilish’s first single released after her debut album, WHEN WE ALL FALL ASLEEP, WHERE DO WE GO?. The song was announced on November 11, 2019 via Twitter and Instagram stories. It had also been previously teased via Twitter on November 5th, 2019. On November 12, 2019, one day before its release, Eilish teased a 12-second snippet of the track via Instagram. A year before the track’s release, an eight second instrumental clip of “everything i wanted” could be heard in the background of one of Eilish’s Instagram stories.
Billie Eilish’s song ‘Everything I Wanted’ encapsulates the theme of the dichotomy between dream fulfillment and internal struggle, reflecting on the complexities of achieving one’s desires juxtaposed with feelings of isolation, the pressure of expectations, and the profound need for genuine understanding and self-acceptance.

Title: SEVENTEEN - 아주 Nice (Very Nice) (Romanized)!
About: Very Nice is a song by Seventeen with upbeat and funky style. It can be found as track #2 on their first studio album repackage Aju NICE.

Title: IU - 라일락 (LILAC) (English Translation)!
About:
With the spring season blossoming, “라일락 (LILAC)” is the title track of IU’s 2021 full album, LILAC. The blossoming track involves disco-pop vibes mixing with the element of electronic-pop; these feels give the song a groovy mood to the listeners.
Lyrically, the song is an anthem that represents IU’s birthday—May 16 1993—which takes place in spring. She waves goodbye to the years of twenty and waves hello to coming close to the age of thirty while pointing out to the fake fans to love her only till this spring.
The track also performed good on charts and music-streaming websites such as Genie, Melon, FLO, and Bugs. For charts, “LILAC” was No.1 on K-pop Hot 100 in South Korea and No.11 on US World Digital Songs in Billboard; No.151 on best songs and more.

Title: Lovesick Girls
About:
“Lovesick Girls” is about yearning for love, in spite of being fully aware that the pursuit could end in heartbreak.
On September 28, 2020, “Lovesick Girls” was officially confirmed as the leading track off BLACKPINK’s first full-length Korean record THE ALBUM. Many fans had concluded that a week prior to the announcement after they found the file name to the first album teaser was “LSG_teaser.jpg” on the girl group’s official website.
On September 27, 2020, BLACKPINK released THE ALBUM concept videos with a snippet of “Lovesick Girls”, which could be recognized by Shazam, a song identifier app.

Title: Stray Kids - 비행기 (Airplane) (English Translation)!
About:
“Airplane” (Hangul: 비행기) is a song recorded by the South Korean boy group Stray Kids. It is the fifth track from their first studio album “GO Live”.
Wrote by: MosPick, $un, Young Chance, Bang Chan, Changbin
Composed by: MosPick, $un, Young Chance, Bang Chan, Changbin
Arranged by : MosPick, $un

Title: (G)I-DLE - Allergy (English Translation)!
About: this song is about girls being insecure about themselves cause of the internet. being influenced by always being pretty and popular and having a high status and those are the expectations of people in the world. fake followers, crying to get clout, always buying new stuff, always having to exercise, and by the way you dress is all because of the internet and SOYEON here is like us and MIYEON MINNIE YUQI and SHUHUA are those girls SHUHUA telling us that we look like a “tomboy” and also SHUHUA and YUQI are trying to make us happy by buying new clothes but later in the MV they kick us out. MINNIE is the most popular girl and with the highest reputation and MIYEON is trying to get the spotlight by crying for clout. if you watch the mv you can see MINNIE exercising and MIYEON struggling with trying to exercising so in the end its just about not having confidence and not standing up for yourself when it comes to beauty standards

Title: Over the Top!
About:
On “Over the Top,” Smiley enlists OVO head-honcho Drake for their first officially released collaboration.
The upbeat track, produced by Drake’s frequent collaborator Tay Keith, arrives via Drake’s OVO Sound record label, which Smiley signed to in 2021.
The song was a commercial hit, reaching #57 on the Billboard Hot 100, marking Smiley’s first entry on the chart.

Title: 도깨비불 (Illusion)
About:
“도깨비불 (Illusion)” follows the newly-found adventure of aespa in their second empowering mini-album, Girls. The song was unexpectedly released on June 1st, 2022.
In the song the girls seductively tease off in the lyrics and plan on consuming their future challenger in seconds, along with a track of an electronic hip-pop sound.

Title: 롤린 (Rollin’)!
About:
The first sleeper hit of 2021 in South Korea, “롤린 (Rollin’)” is Brave Girls’ “first love in summer” dance song infused with a jazzy tropical house beat. The song brought the girl group into unprecedented spotlight in late February after VIDITOR’s video compilation of their K-Force Special Show performances with user commentary went viral on YouTube.
Brave Girls had been going through numerous member changes since 2011 and couldn’t make a mark on their career even until February 23, when they began talks of disbandment. However, the ROK Armed Forces’ unofficial fanbase introduced Brave Girls to the general public, further bringing them onstage. On March 13, Brave Girls came back on MBC’s Show! Music Core with “롤린 (Rollin’)” for the first time since its release in 2017. The song achieved perfect all-kill in the same week.
“롤린 (Rollin’)” is the lead single off Brave Girls’ eponymous fourth mini album. The 2017 original has a dark concept, whereas the new 2018 concept is much brighter with brass elements.

Title: In the End!
About:
“In the End” is mainly based on one person’s failure.
Its most possible meaning is that it’s about time wasted on trying to build a relationship with a girl who doesn’t want to be with you.
Mike Shinoda said this about the song:
I remember putting this together in our rehearsal studio on Hollywood and Vine, working overnight in a room with no windows. I had no idea what time of day it was; I just slept when I was tired, and worked on this song until it took shape. The first guy to hear it was Rob, who told me (I’m paraphrasing) that this was ‘exactly the kind of song he wanted us to write.
During a Q&A, Chester Bennington admitted that he wasn’t enthusiastic about this song:
I don’t really participate in picking singles. I learnt that after making Hybrid Theory. I was never a fan of “In the End” and I didn’t even want it to be on the record, honestly. How wrong could I have possibly been?

Song: That That (prod. & feat. SUGA of BTS)
Title: That’s That!
About:
This song is often regarded as one of DOOM’s most impressive tracks due to the rhyme density on display. While the lyrics are somewhat abstract, the primary theme on display is braggadocio: dismissal of weaker MCs and the supremacy of DOOM as a vocalist.
The instrumental contains a sample of Galt MacDermot’s “Princess Gika”, off Woman Is Sweeter soundtrack. The sample was suggested to DOOM by then general manager of Stones Throw Records, Egon, who is a great crate digger. The instrumental had previously been released as the song “Styrax Gum,” off DOOM’s collection of beats, Special Herbs.

Title: Just Kidding!
About:
“Just Kidding” contrasts its soothing instrumental and catchy baseline with its rather gut-wrenching lyrics depicting confessions about Awsten’s Knight’s struggles with mental heath in tandem with social media during his break amidst the peak of the coronavirus pandemic.
Prior to the songs release, Awsten published four snippets of the song to social media:
The first clip which was published on December 27, 2020, depicts Knight playing bass guitar along with the bridge of the song.
The second snippet was posted on April 22, 2021, teasing the opening shot for the song’s music video.
The third clip was posted on May 9, 2021, showing an instrumental portion of the track, with the caption teasing lyrics from the song.
The final teaser was posted on May 10, 2021, showing Knight listening to a section of the bridge.

Title: BLACKPINK - 불장난 (PLAYING WITH FIRE) (Romanized)!
About:
“불장난 (PLAYING WITH FIRE)” is a song by South Korean girl band BLΛƆKPIИK (블랙핑크).
It’s the lead song from their second single album titled “Square Two”, released on November 1, 2016 by YG Entertainment and distributed by KT Music.
On October 19, 2016, it was revealed that BLACKPINK would be the next YG artist to make a comeback. On October 24, teasers images of members Lisa and Jennie were revealed, along with the name of the title track, “Playing With Fire”, followed by teaser images of Rosé and Jisoo the next day and on October 31, the behind-the-scenes video.
‘Square Two’ has a double A-side, “Playing With Fire” (written by Teddy and composed by Teddy and R.Tee.) and “Stay”, and bonus track “Whistle (Acoustic Ver.)”.
The official Music Video for “Playing With Fire” were directed by Han Sa Min, whom previously directed “Gotta Be You” by 2NE1 and “Sober” by BIGBANG.
BLACKPINK also released a dance practice video for “Playing With Fire” on their official YouTube channel on November 4, 2016 (KST).

Title: Better Things
About:
This song explores Rocky’s relationships and issues that he has around women. Based on this interview, it’s no surprise he has girl problems.
It samples “High School Lover by Cayucas, “All Around And Away We Go” by Twin Sister, and “Carry On” by Bobby Caldwell.
In an interview with Sway, Flacko explains that this is one of the oldest songs he recorded and it was one of A$AP Yams' favorite tracks. By the time he recorded it he had problems with Rita Ora but he decided to keep it on the album because it was Yams' favorite track.

Title: Cool With You
About:
A song of companionship, a sort of “we don’t need anybody else if we have each other”
To me it sounds like the singer is day-dreaming, maybe he wakes up next to a lady he fancies and desires something more than a one-night-stand, wants to know if that feeling is mutual.

Title: Young Tak (영탁) - _ FORM (폼미쳤다) (English Translations)!
About:
“_ FORM” is part of Young Tak’s 2nd full album “Form”.
This nu-disco, EDM song showcases the artist’s confident and determined mindset. The lyrics highlight his unique personality traits and the pursuit of his dreams.
The song was written and produced in the key A Minor, with a tempo of 120 BPM.

Title: Bank Account!
About:
“Bank Account” sees 21 Savage rapping about his wealth amongst other things over a dark beat produced by Metro Boomin and 21 Savage himself.
The track samples Coleridge-Taylor Perkinson’s 1974 song “Flashbulbs,” a sample also used on Travis Scott’s “Oh My Dis Side.”
On the 10th of November the music video was released, starring comedian Mike Epps and directed by Matt Swinsky.

Title: 붉은 노을 (SUNSET GLOW)!
About:
“Sunset Glow” was released as the lead single from the quintet’s second studio album Remember, released on 5th of November, 2008 through YG Entertainment.
It is a remake of 1998 track “Sunset Glow” by South Korean ballad singer, Lee Moon Sae
During the 2008 KBS Music Festival, BIGBANG and Lee Moon Sae performed the song together.
It charted on the Melon Top 100 Chart for 31 weeks.

Title: OH MY GIRL - Dun Dun Dance (Romanized)!
Young Forever!
About:
Over a sample of Alphaville’s “Forever Young,” Jay-Z contemplates his place in the rap game and whether or not he’ll be around forever in our memories.
It also is a play on one of his many aliases, Young, and how he’ll be Young forever.

Title: In Bloom
About:
Cobain’s lyrics poke fun at the people outside of the underground music scene that began showing up at Nirvana shows after the release of the group’s debut album, Bleach.
Nirvana biographer Michael Azerrad wrote, “But remarkably, [the song’s lyrics] translated even better to the mass popularity the band enjoyed.” Regarding the song’s chorus, Azerrad commented, “The brilliant irony is that the tune is so catchy that millions of people actually do sing along to it.”

Song: 작은 것들을 위한 시 (Boy With Luv) (Feat. Halsey)
Title: BTS - 작은 것들을 위한 시 (Boy With Luv) ft. Halsey (Romanized)!
About:
“작은 것들을 위한 시 (Boy With Luv)” is the title track of BTS' 2019 album Map of the Soul: Persona featuring American artist Halsey, who has been a fan of the group since 2017. The pop anthem recalls how love is one of the strongest forces for someone to have.
Halsey has hinted that she is collaboration back in November of 2018 by saying the following:
I think we both have this conceptual thing that we really like to do so it can be cool to find some kind of like bridge, like that you know it’s really great for their mo and my ma that it kind of like signify the whole thing together.
Its English title is reminiscent of their 2014 hit “상남 (Boy in Luv)” similar to how the intro song for Map of the Soul: Persona, “Persona,” is reminiscent of their 2014 intro song “Intro: Skool Luv Affair.” Both are throwbacks to BTS’s Skool Luv Affair era, and both indicate growth from that time.
The translation of its Korean title, “A Poem for Small Things,” hints toward the emotional message of the album.

Title: Plain Jane (Remix)!
About:
After “East Coast REMIX,” “Work REMIX,” and “Shabba REMIX,” A$AP Ferg comes through with yet another remix of one of his popular songs. This time, rap superstar Nicki Minaj hops on “Plain Jane REMIX,” marking the first time she collaborates with the New York native.
The Trinidad MC flips several lines from the original version, including a new chorus:
Ride with Minaj, mmh Mashallah
Check in with me, then do your job
Queen is the name, Rafaello did the chain
This is Monse, patty Plain Jane
The song was released on the 15th of December 2017 across all digital platforms.

Title: ISTJ
About:
The song “ISTJ” by NCT DREAM seems to portray a message about appreciating and embracing the unique qualities and routines of an individual, specifically referencing the personality type known as ISTJ.
The lyrics highlight characteristics associated with an ISTJ, such as introversion, being analytical, judgmental, and adhering to strict principles. The song emphasizes that even though the person may appear perfect, they also have their flaws and vulnerabilities.

Title: Lonely!
About:
“Lonely” was Speaker Knockerz most popular single, with the music video racking up over 80 million views on YouTube. The song is a tribute to those committed to the grind – even willing to sacrifice friendships in order to get ahead.
Speaker Knockerz father said “he wrote ‘Lonely’ because he felt like in the music business he was lonely…Because he felt he was bigger than what he was at that time, and he felt nobody was accepting him for his music. Everything he did was dark after that. He wanted to do something representing how he felt about not getting enough credit for his music. He wasn’t getting credit. But the money was coming in.”

Song: 미리 메리 크리스마스 (Feat. 천둥 Of MBLAQ)
Title: 미리 메리 크리스마스 (Merry Christmas Ahead)!
About:
“Merry Christmas Ahead” is a delightful and heartwarming song sung by IU featuring Cheon Dung of MBLAQ. The song is part of IU’s 3rd EP album Real.
This track celebrates the joy and excitement of Christmas. IU’s velvety vocals, coupled with the cheerful melody, create a sense of warmth and excitement that perfectly embodies the spirit of Christmas.
The lyrics, although simple, speak volumes about the desire to spend a joyful time with loved ones and express heartfelt gratitude for the year’s blessings.

Title: Californication!
About:
This song is mainly about the dark side of Hollywood that lies underneath the glossy surface. The band might love the city of Los Angeles, but they saw firsthand the effects the Hollywood lifestyle has on its inhabitants and the rest of the world.
The song, particularly John Frusciante’s guitar work, sounds different from the rest of the album. That is because the band originally planned to scrap the song because they couldn’t find the music to match Kiedis' lyrics, but Anthony encouraged Frusciante to get creative because he knew they could be dealing with a great song. From his autobiography Scar Tissue:
“I showed "Californication” to John, and he loved the lyrics and started writing some music. But for some reason, even though there was a perfect song in there, we couldn’t find it. We tried ten different arrangements and ten different choruses, and nothing ever worked. All these other songs were pouring out of us. We’d been working for a few weeks when someone started playing an ultra-sparse riff that sounded like nothing we’d ever done before. As soon as I heard it, I knew it was our new song."
The music video features the band as characters in a fake video game, exploring California and often seeing the bad things going on behind the scenes.
This song was one of their biggest hits, peaking at #69 on the Billboard Hot 100 chart and #1 on the Modern and Mainstream Rock charts, and, according to Flea, “Californication is the best record the Chili Peppers have ever made.”
Supporting Kiedis' statements on his autobiography Scar Tissue, an “alternate version” of Californication leaked onto the internet, an early demo from the Californication sessions.

Title: Chasing That Feeling
About: “Chasing That Feeling” by TOMORROW X TOGETHER explores the concept of seeking and pursuing a specific emotional sensation or state of being. The lyrics depict the protagonist as someone who has fallen from heaven, possibly representing a loss of a blissful experience or happiness. Despite this, they continue to chase after that feeling, even if it means sacrificing themselves in the process.

Title: 화이트 (White)!
About:
“White” by The Boyz is a remake of the Fin.K.L song named White.
The song revolves around the theme of winter and the warmth of love. The lyrics describe a couple reflecting on their relationship and cherishing their love during the winter season. The song uses imagery of snowfall and white to symbolize purity, innocence, and the special moments shared between the couple.

Title: 매일 크리스마스 (Everyday Christmas)
About:
“Everyday Christmas” by DAVICHI is a korean R&B, soul song which is their 19th digital single. It’s a song that celebrates the joy and happiness of the winter season, comparing it to the spirit of Christmas.
The lyrics convey a sense of anticipation and excitement as the winter season arrives, symbolized by the first snowfall and the warm lights illuminating the streets.

Title: Dear Santa (English Version)!
About:
It is the English version of the title track ‘Dear Santa’.
The song “Dear Santa” is impressive in the composition of the dramatic song with a medley of cheerful and rhythmical pop and swing jazz, following the introduction of a soft, warm ballad and R&B melody, and the addition of a gaffel and chime makes you feel the Christmas atmosphere.
The song also contains a message from member Seohyun, who participated in the lyrics and wished Santa Claus a Christmas wish, and since it was specially made for Girls' Generation-TTS at SM Songwriting Camp, it further doubles the charm of Girls' Generation-TTS in consideration of each member’s vocal characteristics.

Title: Say Yes To Heaven!
About:
“Say Yes To Heaven” was arguably Lana Del Rey’s most popular unreleased song by 2023, gaining popularity since 2016.
The demand for an official release continued growing, gathering a large number of videos and views on social media apps such as TikTok and YouTube from leaks of the song. The demand was finally met in 2023, when the song had its long-awaited release.
It’s rumored that “Say Yes To Heaven” was first intended to be included on Ultraviolence, Del Rey’s third album, and recorded around 2013 along with other scrapped songs such as “Your Girl” and “Fine China.”
A snippet of the Ultraviolence version of “Say Yes To Heaven” first surfaced on the Internet in August 2016, just a few months before the complete song eventually leaked on December 24 through SoundCloud. Between 2017 and 2021, other versions of the song also appeared online, presumed to be outtakes from sessions for subsequent albums Honeymoon and Lust for Life.
In early 2023, reports of videos that featured the song being taken down due to copyright claims sparked discussion about an official release for “Say Yes To Heaven.” The speculation was later fueled weeks later when the song was found registered with a different length than that of leaks in PPL’s repertoire database.
On May 19, 2023, without any prior announcement, a fresh recording of “Say Yes To Heaven” was officially released to streaming services alongside a sped-up version of the song.

Title: Don’t Tell Nobody!
About:
In this 2014 song, Tink suspects that her lover is cheating. Even so, she can’t bring herself to leave him. Meanwhile, her lover is asking that she keep her suspicions to herself.
Tink told Gawker about the planning behind the song:
Before I went to L.A., I got on the phone with Da Internz, the producers, and I was just explaining to them that I both sing and rap. What I was looking for was a way to incorporate both, and at the same time keep it me. When I got to L.A., we had it in our minds that we had to make this certain sound. This certain sound is going to be different from anybody in the game right now, from anything I’ve ever done. I wanted to implement the rapping and the singing. So it was in our heads before even going into the studio and: bam, it came out so great.

Title: Instant Crush!
About: “Instant Crush,” the fourth single off of Random Access Memories, sees Daft Punk team up with Julian Casablancas from The Strokes in a mid tempo song with bass driven mellow verses and uplifting synth based choruses.

Title: Candy Cane Lane!
About:
“Candy Cane Lane” is the second song of Sia’s all-original seasonal LP Everyday is Christmas. Lior Molcho, director of the claymation for Sia’s holiday album commented:
The music was incredibly inspiring, as I listened to the songs, vintage Christmas movies like Rudolph The Red-Nosed Reindeer kept popping in my head.

Title: SOS!
About:
Rather than serving as a complete song, “SOS” is an introduction to the album’s themes as a whole. This is also the first time in SZA’s discography where we see a titular track. This draws an immediate parallel to the “CTRL era.”
SZA uses this track to reintroduce herself to the population; seeing as it had been five years since her critically acclaimed album CTRL, touching on how she has a new sense of self.
The song begins and ends with SZA “crying,” presumably as those in need of assistance cry for help – or “SOS”.

Title: Diamond Days
About: Diamond Days is the First official single for band Cruel Youth. The band members include Teddy Sinclair with the previous moniker Natalia Kills along side her husband.

Title: Back 2 Back
About:
On October 29th, 2015,Young Money Artist Stephanie Acevedo Snapchatted a video of Lil Wayne in the Studio recording the Remix to Drakes 2015 Diss Track Back To Back. Lil Wayne later confirmed that this track was off No Ceilings 2.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FMe2nN2uH9M
On November 1st, Footage of Lil Wayne Performing Back To Back was posted online.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ektvVHiTPOc

Title: Perfect Strangers!
About:
“Perfect Strangers” is a light and airy summer jam about not overthinking romance. It features production by Jonas Blue and vocals by fellow English musician JP Cooper. The tune harkens back to the tropical house sound of Blue’s breakthrough single—a 2015 cover of Tracy Chapman’s “Fast Car.”
Sadly, the song has nothing to do with the popular ABC sitcom that aired from 1986 to 1993.

Title: BOYNEXTDOOR - 뭣 같아 (But Sometimes) (English Translation)!
About:
The song “뭣 같아” is the title track of the 1st EP of BOYNEXTDOOR named Why... This song explores the emotional turmoil and conflicting feelings that arise after a past relationship.
The lyrics describe a series of frustrations and disappointments towards the person the singer is addressing, culminating in the singer’s realization of his own worth. It emphasizes the importance of self-growth and learning from past experiences.

Title: Bitch, Don’t Kill My Vibe!
About:
The content of this song doesn’t advance the narrative of the album. It is told from the perspective of present-day Kendrick Lamar, who isn’t happy with the state of rap music, and strives to be different by maintaining his vision. He also discusses his career so far and how it has changed his life, for better or worse.
Kendrick explained this song in an interview with Complex saying:
That’s really one big subliminal at everybody getting mixed in a situation where everyone wants to have creative control. That’s the vibe I wanted to kill. That’s why I threw that record out. If you listen to some of the words, it’s real intricate, but it makes sense.
This song samples Danish electronic group Boom Clap Bachelors’ 2008 tune “Tiden Flyver.”
Originally this song was supposed to feature Lady Gaga. After the song was officially released, Gaga posted the original version on Twitter. Kendrick explained to Complex why Gaga didn’t make the album saying:
“We had to meet the deadline for the pre-order date. That’s just the business side coming through and messing things up.”
Gaga was also supposed to work with Kendrick on the song “Partynauseous,” but that was never released either. The female vocals on the song are instead performed by Anna Wise.

Title: GUMMO!
About:
“GUMMO” was the track that introduced Tekashi 6ix9ine to a mainstream rap audience for the first time and “changed [his] life”.
The song’s title is meant to draw a comparison between the way the characters in the film of the same name spend their time wandering an Ohio wasteland, to the way 6ix9ine wanders the decrepit streets of Brooklyn.
The song’s instrumental was sent to 6ix9ine by Trippie Redd before their falling out over Tekashi’s 2015 charge of use of a minor in a sexual performance. Pi'erre had no idea that his beat was being used by Tekashi, and only found out once the song’s music video was already released on YouTube.
Pi'erre publicly objected to being associated with the song, citing 6ix9ine’s previously mentioned child sex charges, and has since asserted that he will never work with Trippie Redd or 6ix9ine ever again.
For a long time, “GUMMO” was Tekashi’s most viewed and streamed song. It reached over 300 million views on YouTube, and garnered over 100 million streams on SoundCloud, which lead to the track reaching the top spot on its streaming charts for all of December 2017 and January 2018.

Title: Sugar Rush Ride
About:
“Sugar Rush Ride,” the lead single off TXT’s fifth mini album, The Name Chapter: TEMPTATION, is a multi-genre filled song that evolves around the topic of swirling into devilish temptations, but wanting it anyway. Lyrically it also fuses elements of traditional Korean music.
The song made an instant hit and topped both international and domestic charts, including achieving No.1 on MelOn and No.1 on iTunes in 19 countries.

Title: seven!
About:
This song is about one of Taylor’s childhood friends who seemed to have had an unhappy life at home. Taylor reflects back on her innocence back then, how she thought that their problems could be easily solved.
This is reminiscent of the song “It’s Nice to Have A Friend”, from August 2019’s Lover album, which talks about a childhood friend that Taylor grows up with and eventually marries.
On August 20, 2020, Taylor included “seven” on the folklore: the escapism chapter EP, indicating that it fits thematically with folklore’s other songs about seeking relief from unpleasantness.

Title: Billie Jean!
About:
One of Michael Jackson’s signature songs, “Billie Jean” tells the tale of a young girl who claims to be the mother of Jackson’s alleged son. The song was the second single from his sixth solo album Thriller. It reached #1 in several countries including the US.
In an interview with Martin Bashir, Michael revealed his original inspiration behind “Billie Jean”, sharing, “I said to myself beforehand, ‘I wanna write a song with a great bass hook.’” He was driving down Ventura Boulevard when he came up with that hook.
In his autobiography, Moonwalk, Jackson wrote:
There never was a real Billie Jean. The girl in the song is a composite of people my brothers have been plagued with over the years. I could never understand how these girls could say they were carrying someone’s child when it wasn’t true.
One peculiar case where this happened to Jackson himself was in 1981 when a woman wrote him letters claiming he was the father of ONE of her twins. She initially appears on the first song of the album, “Wanna Be Startin' Somethin'”.
Producer Quincy Jones originally did not want to include “Billie Jean” on Thriller. He disliked the intro, the bass line and song’s title (feeling listeners would associate the track with tennis legend Billie Jean King). Jackson fought to keep the song on the album, and not change its title to Jones' suggestion of “Not My Lover”.
Despite Jackson’s claim in a 2002 interview that the song’s bass line came ‘from above’, Daryl Hall has said Jackson once told him he’d stolen the groove of “Billie Jean” from Hall & Oates' song “I Can’t Go For That (No Can Do)”.
The song was notably performed by MJ on Motown 25: Yesterday, Today, Forever in 1983, where he debuted the Moonwalk to rave reviews. Fred Astaire even called the day after to give him props, saying, “Man, you really put them on their asses last night.”

Title: Yeezy Sneakers!
About: Wayne remixes Kodak Black and XXXTentacions 2017 song “Roll in Peace”. The track was originally produced by CuBeatz & London On Da Track.

Title: We Don’t Die We Multiply Forever!
Get Up 10!
About:
On “Get Up 10,” Cardi B discusses her uprising in the rap game as well as her rags to riches story.
Despite Cardi’s success, she revealed in an Instagram caption that listening to “Get Up 10” still makes her sad, presumably because of the “rags” part of her story and the fact that this is still so many people’s reality.

Title: Lowlife!
About:
“Lowlife” is the second single released by Poppy, under her previous name That Poppy, from her debut EP Bubblebath. Explaining the track in an interview with Teen Vogue, she said:
