Drunk in Love!
About:
Beyoncé brings her husband Jay Z aboard for their first collaboration since 2013’s “Part II (On the Run)” continuation of their first collab “‘03 Bonnie & Clyde”.
“Drunk” seems to be a more sexualized continuation of their first track together, “Crazy in Love.” Over Detail’s blaring beat, the lyrics repeatedly touch on female and marital sexuality, and the effects of alcohol.
Bey described her entire self-titled album as “much freer than anything I’d done in the past,” but none seemed more free than “Drunk In Love” with its freestyled lyrics, studio fun, and improvised beach music video:
We were in Miami for Jay’s concert, and it was just the two of us, on the beach, amazing weather, and one outfit! It’s beautiful in its simplicity. If you want something to feel real and urgent, you can’t overthink it.
Kanye West hopped on the official remix featured on the deluxe version of the album, but the song’s influence stretched to several other remixes and covers from The Weeknd, Lil Wayne, Future, T.I., Ed Sheeran, and more.

Producers WOOZI, created this song dedicated to one of his fellow members SEUNGKWAN, hoping that he could find comfort from this piece during his break, as SEUNGKWAN shared his feelings about it before the music video was released during an interview.
As SEUNGKWAN, earlier on this year lost a fellow good friend MOONBIN, producer WOOZI hoped that SEUNGKWAN could find his peace and comfort through this song, with the sounds of the soft melody and the emotions overfilliing the lyrics.

Title: AKDONG MUSICIAN - 얼음들 (MELTED) (Romanized)!
About:
The original title of Melted, “얼음들,” literally means “ices” although you can notice Koreans do not use the plural-indicating suffix, “들,” for a lifeless thing; this means Akdong Musician intentionally used it to refer to another that can have it.
As the Korean word for “adults” (어른, eoreun) is similar in pronunciation to “ice” (얼음, eoreum), ice is used as a metaphor in the song to describe the coldness of adults and the harsh reality of the “real world” that they inhabit; “ices” are actually “adults.”
The music video serves an important role in explaining more details about the song. Watch the music video first and try to guess the meaning. The explanation is in the final annotation.

But sadly, the woman was not or probably was never belong to this world. One day, she asks the main character to brought her to the sea, she loves the sea. They rent a boat, sailing, both of them. The woman said that he has to sing and make music for her although she’s gone, the man was confused. Suddenly, there was a huge wave, the main character was trying to save the woman, but failed. She fell, drown, after she has gone, there are a lot of white flowers floating in the water. That’s the end of their not-so-clear love story

Lyrically, DEAN waxes poetic about his commitment to his lover, ready to make the ultimate sacrifice for them, even in the most tumultuous throes of their relationship. After the song’s release, DEAN commented on his Instagram post, “Maybe every time I see 4:44 I’m reminded of it.” WINNER reference aside, 4:44 is also an angel number, and coincidentally, the number of years after which DEAN has released a song (4).

Title: Someone to Stay!
About:
Speaking in an interview Tim revealed the inspiration behind the song:
I wrote most of “Someone to Stay” when I was on a missions trip in Cambodia. Seeing poverty and brokenness around me to that scale was really eye-opening and made me realize that nobody should ever have to be alone like that. I started noticing it a whole heap more in Los Angeles as well (where I recorded the whole album). The city is so prosperous yet there are so many people on the streets. It’s just really sad and the song is about how I felt seeing all of that.

Title: Square Dance!
About:
This song has political roots while also serving as a diss towards Canibus.
In fact, the song may be a direct response to Canibus’s song “Draft Me”, in which Canibus threatens an unnamed rival and expresses a desire to fight in the war on terror, even including a quote by George Bush at the end of the song. In “Square Dance”, Eminem attacks Canibus and delivers an anti-draft, anti-war, anti-Bush message.
The beat sounds kind of similar to “Bad Influence”, an earlier Em song with a beat made by the Bass Brothers. Jeff Bass again shows up in the composer credits on “Square Dance”, but Em appears to have taken the lead on creating this “Square Dance” beat.

Title: Day-O (Banana Boat Song)!
About:
“Day-O (The Banana Boat Song)” is a single from Harry Belafonte’s 1956 album Calypso is an adaptation of a vernacular Jamaican work song. This version of the song was adapted by Barbadian singer, Jamaica Farewell or Irving Burgie.
The song hearkens back to an era when the banana trade thrived in Jamaica before the predominance of banana republics. The call-and-response and repetition used in the song speak to the monotony of the work of loading bananas on ships in the Caribbean.
“Day-O” reached #5 on the Billboard Charts in 1957, and remains a famous song cited in a number of more current pop culture references such as the possession scene in Tim Burton’s Beetlejuice (1988) and Lil Wayne’s “6 Foot 7 Foot.”
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AQXVHITd1N4

Title: 사람 (People) Pt.2!
About:
“사람 (People) Pt.2” is the sequel to the song “사람 (People)” off Agust D’s mixtape, D-2. It marks the second collaboration between Agust D and one of South Korea’s most beloved female singers, IU. It serves as the first single for his album, D-DAY.
Over a deceptively simple beat, Agust D contrasts IU’s smooth vocals using a melodic rap style. Where “사람 (People)” reflected on the characteristics of how people change, Pt.2 delves into the emotional loss they feel to make that kind of change within themselves.
Agust D contemplates the give-and-take of loneliness and loves both, describing its conditionality and frailty, as well as how people deal with the loneliness that follows its loss.

The song was recorded in 2011 and uploaded to BTS’s official soundcloud in February 2013, just after RM’s track, “Favorite Girl,” and the RM and Jungkook collaboration, “Like a Star”. It was later deleted from their soundcloud in July 2016.

Title: God’s Plan!
About:
“God’s Plan” is a feel-good track that discusses Drake’s future. Being who he is in the industry, he knows that people want him to fail; however, God won’t let him do that.
Another key subject of the song is the separation Drake feels that he needs to have from those outside of his inner circle. Anyone who tries to work their way into his life must have an ulterior motive to benefit themselves.
The last key subject of this song is the fact that Drake is giving back to the life he had once, honoring the people and the environment he also once was in.
The official music video shared a different sentiment though, as Drake took the budget for the video, a whopping $996,631.90, and gave it away to families, schools, charities, and more. It was directed by Karena Evans, who also hails from Toronto, and is a protégé of Director X.
Director X has had a hand in a number of Drake music videos in the past, including “Hotline Bling” and “Started From The Bottom.”

Title: GANADARA!
About:
“GANADARA” is the first release under MORE VISION, a record label created by Korean-American rapper Jay Park.
The title, GANDARA (가나다라), is the beginning of the Korean alphabet and a common order of consonants the way kids learn it—similarly to ABC in English.
The track features a South Korean superstar, singer-songwriter IU. The nation’s sweetheart is a fitting choice for a label meant to give a “better environment and life” to those Park loves.

Title: ACT III. 무제(無題) (UNTITLED, 2014)!
About:
Untitled, 2014 is the TITLE track or the lead single for G-Dragon’s 2017 EP called, Kwon Ji Yong. Why 2014? Since this album, as said by G-Dragon is his autobiographical album, 2014= 1988 + 08 + 18 (his birthday, August/18/1988). The song is about his ex lover (speculated to be Kiko Mizuhara). G-Dragon regrets separating with his ex, he has done a lot of mistakes and so has she. In the Chorus GD is willing to sacrifice everything that he owns just to be with his past lover again.
This song can have a double meaning about a girl, but can also be disguised as a letter to his younger self. A letter trying to reconnect with a childhood he’s leaving behind as he goes into his 30’s and longing to reconcile with the real and younger person he’d abandoned and lost touched with to make way for the superstar G Dragon persona he’d become for all these years. A song meant to comfort, reassure and sum up experiences/ feelings as he closes this era of his life. A song to reclaim and rediscover his true identity as just Jiyong.

Title: Смарт (Smart)!
About:
8 августа 2018 года без каких-либо анонсов Глеб выпускает новый сингл вместе с экранизацией. О том, что готовится выход клипа, стало известно ещё в середине июля, из-за появившихся в сети видео со съёмок.

Данный трек фанаты наименовали «Я не хочу отношений».
Но как оказалось, видео со съёмок и вышедший клип — совершенно разные работы. Возможно, данная экранизация является промо-видео к концертам в Москве и в Питере, а полноценная работа выйдет позже, вместе с альбомом. Также от опубликованного ранее сниппета ничего кроме инструментала не осталось.
17 августа 2018 года песня была выгружена на Apple Music. Там же была и представлена альтернативная обложка к треку.

Title: Polaroid Love
About:
This lovely, melodic song has a very special place in ENGENES (ENHYPEN’s fans) hearts, as it was made as a gift for them.
“Polaroid Love” was unveiled for the first time very surprisingly—live in the encore at the ENHYPEN’s show [EN-CONNECT : COMPANION] on November 19, 2021, at the KBS Arena Hall in Seoul, South Korea. The show worked as an in-person and also live-streamed online fan meeting.
Before the song was set to be released on DIMENSION : ANSWER album, ENHYPEN gave a small spoiler during their V Live telling about the soon-to-be official release.

Title: Take Two
About:
“Take Two” is a digital single by the South Korean boy group BTS for their 10th year anniversary as part of their annual Festa celebrations. The acoustic pop song is dedicated to their fandom, ARMY, celebrating their decade of history.
“Take Two” is a reference to takes in cinematography. Takes are usually defined by time, and therefore, the first ten years of their career is “take one.” With the song, BTS are starting the second take of their career, and they are inviting their fans to join.

Title: Mask!
About:
On “Mask,” Dream reflects about his past fights with mental disorders like depression and ADHD while acknowledging how they affect him in his current life. The song’s title plays with the lyrics as a shield, hiding Dream’s insecurities while he fights these mental illnesses. He confirmed the title on March 14 when replying to a fan via his defunct private Twitter account, dreamhangout.
The day before, Dream first teased his fans that confirmed he was working on a new song via said Twitter account.
“Mask” aims for a rock beat while maintaining classic pop elements from Dream’s collaboration with producers Banrisk and Perish Beats called “Roadtrip.” Following the immense success of this February 2021 hit, “Mask” is the second collaboration between the three artists.
The song is also Dream’s second song Twitter user @camruna designed cover art for. They had previously designed the cover art for “Roadtrip” under a different name, @acaicamc.

Title: 오늘 취하면 (WINE)!
About:
In her first chart-topping track, “Wine,” Suran reflects on the memories of a person’s past relationship in a glass of wine through emotional lyrics yet groovy rhythm. The South Korean indie singer worked together along BTS member Suga and Changmo to produce the trendy hip-hop, R&B track infused with electro pop and a futuristic base.
Through Korean media outlet, Sport Chosun, Suran thanked both Changmo and Suga for their participation in this track.
When I was working on some music by myself last year, I was having a hard time and I was feeling depressed. It was at that time that I started talking with Suga about music, and we ended up working together and that’s how ‘Wine’ was created. I’m grateful to him because I received a lot of strength and energy from working with him, and the song came out so well. And I’ve always been a fan of Changmo, so I was very happy when he agreed to feature on my track after listening to the song. I’m grateful to him for elevating the track to another level with his rapping. (Translation Source)

Title: Young K (DAY6) - 잘 자라 내 사람아 (goodnight, dear) (English Translation)!
꽃 길 (FLOWER ROAD)!
About:
“Flower Road” is a special single released by Kpop boy group, BIGBANG. The song is a medium tempo track with lyrics written by T.O.P and G-Dragon. Through the bittersweet lyrics, the members say this is not the end and they will be back when the flowers bloom again.
The single comes right after four members of the group – T.O.P, G-Dragon, Daesung, and TAEYANG – entered the military for their mandatory service. The last member, Seungri is also planning to enlist this year after his solo promotions. Due to their mandatory enlistment, the group will reunite in 3 years and be on stage together again.
Immediately upon release, the song climbed up various music charts and hit #1 on Melon, Genie, Bugs, Mnet, Naver, and Soribada, leading to an All-Kill on real-time charts. The song also hit #1 on Worldwide iTunes Charts. It also topped Billboard’s World Digital Song Sales Chart.

Title: BBIBBI (삐삐)!
About:
IU’s digital single “삐삐 (BBIBBI)” celebrates the singer’s 10th anniversary since her debut.
With a playful, shifting percussions and a soft blend of harmonies, the track is IU’s first song that falls in the alternative R&B genre.
“BBIBBI” is the phonetic sound a bell makes in warning of something crossing some sort of boundary. The lyrics of the song express a pleasant, cheerful message to those who cross the line in personal relationships. In a society where people have little regard for the personal bubble of others, IU writes about the need for respect and privacy in modern society.
On Cafe Daum IU further explains the song:
The song conveys the message of throwing a cheerful and simple warning at those who have crossed the line. More important nowadays than ever, is to not restrict or decide for others based on one’s own standards, but for equal and independent individuals to have healthy ties. The song tells the story of people living in the modern age.
Billboard published an article following the song’s release, saying:
IU is seen emulating a variety of different roles, such as languishing around in high-fashion or dancing around in retro looks, and throwing away newspapers and looking out from a window with binoculars while wearing a Gracy Kelly-style scarf as if she’s peering out for paparazzi. As she sings about not caring about others and saying that it’s “not my business,” she coyly stares down the camera and shows off the different sides to herself.

Title: SLOW DANCING IN THE DARK!
About:
On “SLOW DANCING IN THE DARK,” Joji continues the lovelorn narrative from his previous single “YEAH RIGHT” over a dark, ambient instrumental.
This time around, he uses the metaphor of slow dancing in the dark to characterize a failing relationship—just like a slow dance where you can’t see your partner’s face, his relationship with this person is intimate but ultimately impersonal.
Joji first previewed the song on May 12, 2018, at the Blurry Vision Festival. He announced its official release on September 7th, along with teaser photos from the music video.

Title: 정말 사랑했을까, 정말 사랑했었다 (Did You Really Love Me? I Really Loved You)!
Truth Hurts!
About:
On Lizzo’s breakthrough single, she flaunts her confidence after breaking up with an unfaithful lover. Despite coming out in 2017, “Truth Hurts” gained traction after being included in the 2019 Netflix film Someone Great, and reached #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 in September 2019.
Lizzo told People that she considered quitting music after the song came out, but her family and collaborators encouraged her to continue with her career, allowing her to reap the benefits and watch the song become a hit.

Title: Lucid Dreams!
About:
On “Lucid Dreams,” Juice WRLD explores the mental anguish he feels after experiencing a break-up.
Lucid dreaming refers to a state of consciousness where a person is aware they are dreaming. With enough experience, a dreamer can slowly begin to control the contents of their dreams. Juice uses the idea of controlling a lucid dream as an analogy for his mental state; to avoid the pain of seeing his ex-lover in his dreams, Juice seeks to replace them—for good.
Juice’s frequent collaborator, Nick Mira, handles the production of this track. The melody of this track samples the intro of Sting’s March 1993 track “Shape Of My Heart.”
During the week ending October 6, 2018, the track peaked at #2 on the Billboard Hot 100 chart.

Title: Perfume
About:
“Perfume” is about an ex who the singer misses whilst still being close with, and becoming jealous when she starts to move on – which causes him to develop a bitter attitude towards his desired person and their new boyfriend, although it doesn’t seem like her new relationship will succeed. The song’s rock beat includes the electric and bass guitars' lines playing simutaneously at a moderate tempo.
“Perfume” was first revealed on June 12, 2021, on band frontman Wilbur Soot’s Twitch channel as a snippet with unfinished lyrics. Band member Ash Kabosu also teased it in his September 2021 vlog, where Wilbur performed “Perfume” on the omnichord and revealed new lyrics to the first verse. Later, on October 2021, Wilbur played the song on the omnichord again.
The song was briefly removed from Pebble Brain but Minecraft streamer and Wilbur’s friend, TommyInnit, persuaded the band to keep it on the final release.
On the day of the song’s release, Genius released an article breaking down each part of the song.

Title: Guilty Conscience!
About:
In this classic song, Eminem and Dr. Dre play the Evil and Good conscience respectively (wait, Dre is the good one?) of three separate people who are about to commit violent or sexual crimes. Dre wins the first battle whereas Eminem wins the second. In the third case, the two agree that the bad thing to do is actually the “morally right” thing to do.
In “Angry Blonde”, Eminem explains how he came up with the idea for this song:
Dre and I were in the gym one day, and we was talkin' about song concepts and shit. Dre said that we should do a song together called “Night ‘n’ Day”, where everything he was sayin', I was sayin' the complete opposite. So I thought about it, went home that same night, and wrote it.

Title: Somebody That I Used To Know!
About: Having burned through the Australian charts late in 2011, Gotye’s sad tale of lost love continued to climb the charts on US soil in 2012 and 2013. The emotionally charged “Somebody That I Used To Know” details a mutually ended relationship in which one person feels a pain that the other refuses to feel.

Title: BTS - 소우주 (Mikrokosmos) (English Translation)!
About:
“소우주 (Mikrokosmos)” is the 3rd song on BTS' 2019 album Map of the Soul: Persona. The melodic song marvels at the endlessness every human individual possesses.
The title Mikrokosmos references the ancient Greek philosophy of microcosm, the perception of viewing humans as their own little world or mikros kosmos. Humans are observed in relation to the universe, and, when the universe was first studied, kosmos translated closer to the modern ideal of order. In a broader sense, microcosm studies the harmonious relationship of each individual in parallel to everything else, which continues BTS' narrative of self discovery and persona.
The concept of “little universes” unique to each individual has been around since 2018, during the era of Love Yourself 結 ‘Answer.’

Title: 흠칫 (Hmm-cheat)
About:
“흠칫 (Hmm-cheat)” is the title track of Crush’s 3rd album wonderego. This song is a fun and upbeat track that focuses on enjoying the night and letting go of inhibitions.
The lyrics celebrate women and encourage them to indulge in the moment, have fun, and enjoy themselves without restraint. It’s a celebration of letting go and embracing the joy and excitement of the night.

Title: Fabulous
About: In “Fabulous” Sharpay expresses her want for a summer full of luxury and relaxation while lounging by the poolside at Lava Springs. She sings with Ryan and the Sharpettes as backup.

Title: Rainy Days
About: “Rainy Days” is the first joint effort between Boogie and Eminem. On the track, they share their thoughts on their current positions and goals in hip-hop.

Title: 다음 겨울에도 여기서 만나 (Hello Antenna, Hello Christmas)!
Oh my god (English Version)!
About: The title track in (G)I-DLE’s minialbum “I trust”. It was released on April 6, 2020. A fantasy-like mood and bold production are led by a vintage piano sound and strong 808 bass. MTV described it as a “dark, trap-infused song with lyrics that riff on contrasts — light and dark, purity and sin — to communicate the idea that true divinity comes from knowing and trusting yourself.”

Title: Die For You!
About:
On “Die For You” The Weeknd croons about the conflicting feelings and pain that comes with letting go of someone he still has intense feelings for.
Many fans speculate the song was written about Abel’s then ex-girlfriend, supermodel Bella Hadid following rumors, that the two ended their relationship close to Starboy’s official release due to their conflicting work schedules. Additionally, Abel admitted to Zane Lowe in an interview that the song was finished a mere week before the album’s release, raising more suspicions that he’d written the song about a recent split.
On 11/25/2021, the fifth anniversary of Starboy, a music video was at last released for the track.
In February 2023, The Weeknd released a remix of the track featuring Ariana Grande, their first collaboration since their hit remix of Abel’s track Save Your Tears.

Title: ZICO (지코) - 너는 나 나는 너 (I Am You, You Are Me) (English Translation)!
About:
For his solo single “I Am You, You Are Me” the rapper showcases a different side of himself with the mid-tempo R&B track.
A bumpin' beat, subtle bass line and electronica flourishes back Zico’s smooth croons to highlight that the 23-year-old really has a soothing voice.
The plot of the music video is simple and sweet, where Zico finds himself continuously matching clothes with a teller behind a counter at a convience store. Soon, they realize it’s not a coincidence.
Throughout the track, he touches on the ways a couple connects at a young age, from physically to adolescent beliefs in tarot cards and horoscopes.

Title: SEVENTEEN - 하품 (Yawn) (English Translation)!
About:
“하품 (Yawn)” is a b-side track from SEVENTEEN’s eleventh mini album, SEVENTEENTH HEAVEN.
Released on the 23rd of October, 2023. The album came with a total of seven tracks. This b-side track is sung by the performance team of SEVENTEEN.
“하품 (Yawn)” gives off a soft melodly labelled as a ballad that came with heartwarming poetic lyrics. Producers WOOZI, created this song dedicated to one of his fellow members SEUNGKWAN, hoping that he could find comfort from this piece during his break, as SEUNGKWAN shared his feelings about it before the music video was released during an interview.
As SEUNGKWAN, earlier on this year lost a fellow good friend MOONBIN, producer WOOZI hoped that SEUNGKWAN could find his peace and comfort through this song, with the sounds of the soft melody and the emotions overfilliing the lyrics.

Title: goodnight n go!
About:
In the lead-up to Sweetener, Ariana teased “goodnight n go” through a snippet on her Instagram story and later posted the title on Twitter.
The song draws heavy inspiration from Imogen Heap’s “Goodnight and Go.”, appropriating it for Sweetener by modifying the production and some lyrics. Ariana has stated on Instagram that Imogen was one of her primary inspirations as an artist, and made her who she is today.

Title: Daylight
About:
In the final song on her album Lover, Taylor Swift sings about struggling through previous relationships, beginning to doubt her ability to find true love, until she meets someone who brightens her life in a new way.
She refers to being unlucky in love in the past, but when she meets her new partner, she only wants to focus on them and their happiness together.

Title: 그때의 나, 그때의 우리 (When We Were Two)!
Real Life
About:
The Weeknd begins his 2015 album, Beauty Behind the Madness, with advice from his own mother on how his life was going to destroy him over time.
In this intro, we find Abel opening up about mistakes and claiming them as such, rather than in the storytelling aspect he has utilized during his career.
The song’s pre-chorus is reminiscent of the bridge on Michael Jackson’s “Billie Jean”,
People always told me be careful of what you do And don’t go around breaking young girls' hearts And mother always told me be careful of who you love
And be careful of what you do cause the lie becomes the truth
Abel also mentions his relationship with his mother on the grandiose Echoes of Silence track “The Fall.”

Title: 소나기 (Downpour)!
About:
The final single from I.O.I, Downpour is a euphonic ballad produced by SEVENTEEN’s Woozi.
The song tells the year-long story of I.O.I, their hardships since their trainee days, the memories they have cherished, and the achievements they’ve reached as a project group. The 11 girls poured out their hearts to perform this song for the first and last time at their concert, ‘TIME SLIP’ which sold out a few moments after ticketing was open. Thanks to the fans, their achievements and the love and support they’ve received, I.O.I remains one of the greatest project groups in the history of K-pop.

Title: I See a Dreamer!
About:
“I See a Dreamer” is a tribute to Minecraft speedrunner Dream. The title is a play on his name and the word “dreamer,” or a person with bold ideas. In the song’s case, the “bold ideas” are Dream’s popular speedrunning tactics from his YouTube series, Minecraft Manhunt. He uses them to trick the hunters, or people hunting down Dream in the hopes to kill him before he can defeat the Ender Dragon and win his round in the series.
Every illustration in the YouTube video is a different iconic moment from these manhunts, including jumping from a tower of blocks with frost walker boots (which is also depicted in the song’s cover art), and hopping across lava with wooden boats.

Title: The Invitation!
About:
Nick Cannon and company released this track in response to Eminem’s verse on Fat Joe’s “Lord Above,” itself a response to statement Nick Cannon made in an interview where he stated he wanted to physically fight Eminem while Cannon was dating Mariah Carey. On “Lord Above,” Em raps:
I know me and Mariah didn’t end on a high note
But that other dude’s whipped, that pussy got him neutered
Tried to tell him this chick’s a nutjob ‘fore he got his jewels clipped
Almost got my caboose kicked
Fool, quit, you not gon’ do shit
I let her chop my balls off too ‘fore I lost to you, Nick
The title, “The Invitation,” is an actual invitation – Cannon is inviting Eminem to appear on his MTV show, Wild'N Out. The art for the track has large print with the song’s title, written over a picture of the Wild'N Out set. The art also includes the header which states:
EMINEM – You Are Cordially Invited By THE BLACK SQUAD
The two have had a contentious relationship since Nick Cannon and Mariah Carey got married in 2008. Allegedly, Eminem and Mariah Carey had a brief relationship in 2001 that did not end well, and, upon the marriage, brought Nick Cannon into the fray. Eminem dissed the couple in the song “Bagpipes from Baghdad,” which led to Carey’s response in “Obsessed,” which ultimately led to Eminem’s diss track in 2009, “The Warning.”

Title: I Remember Everything!
About:
I Remember Everything is one of three colaborative tracks on Zach Bryan’s third album. Parts of the song were teased on both Twitter and Tiktok.
pic.twitter.com/nTjomE7A1I
— Zach Bryan (@zachlanebryan) June 14, 2023

Title: Ride!
About:
“Ride” is about the journey of life. Throughout the song, Tyler’s lyrics indicate he feels like dying is easy, but finding someone or something to live for is life’s challenge, or “ride.” The song contains heavy reggae influences.
The song also contains callbacks to various twenty one pilot songs, including “Heavydirtysoul,” “Lane Boy,” and “Ode To Sleep.”

Title: Hello!
About:
Almost five years after her groundbreaking 21, Adele examines the past and tries to right her wrongs on “Hello.” On the surface, the song appears to be about a break-up from long ago, but the song can also be interpreted as Adele’s attempt at reconciliation with her former self. In a tweet, Adele called 21 a “break-up record” and 25 a “make-up record.”
Adele revealed the broad inspiration for the song:
No, it’s not about anyone specifically. It’s about friends, ex-boyfriends, it’s about myself, it’s about my family. It’s also about my fans as well.
A day before the release, Zane Lowe interviewed Adele about her future goals, touring, the album, and the “Hello” music video. Although “Hello” was released on October 23, 2015, a portion of the song was first featured during a commercial break on the UK’s X Factor on October 18, 2015.
The accompanying music video broke the Vevo record for the most views in 24 hours with 27.7 million total. Taylor Swift previously held the record with her video for “Bad Blood”, which had 20.1 million views in a single day. “Hello” was directed by Xavier Dolan and was the first music video to be shot by IMAX cameras.
In the US, “Hello” debuted at number 1 on the Hot 100, selling a whopping 1,112,000 downloads in its first week, almost doubling Flo Rida’s previous sales record (636,000 with “Right Round”.) The track significantly broke various streaming records after being replayed 61 million times in its first week.
In an interview with i-D, Adele contextualizes “Hello”:
The song is about hurting someone’s feelings but it’s also about trying to stay in touch with myself, which sometimes can be a little bit hard to do,“ she explains. "It’s about a yearning for the other side of me. When I’m away, I really, really miss my life at home. The way that I feel when I’m not in England, is…” (she pauses) “desperation. I can’t breathe anywhere else.” Why? “I dunno. I’m so attached to my whole life here. I get worked up that I’m missing out on things. So ‘Hello’ is about wanting to be at home and wanting to reach out to everyone I’ve ever hurt – including myself – and apologize for it.

Title: BTS - 좋아요 (I Like It) (English Translation)!
About:
“Like” (좋아요; also known as “I Like It”) is a song by BTS. It was released on June 12, 2013 and appears as the sixth track for their debut single album 2 Cool 4 Skool. A remixed version was released on February 12, 2014 and serves as the second track from the repackage of their second mini album Skool Luv Affair Special Addition.
This song has a Japanese version featured in their first Japanese single “No More Dream” and in their first Japanese studio album Wake Up.

Title: All for Us!
About: “All For Us—from the HBO Original Series Euphoria” is a remix of the original song written, produced and performed by Labrinth for his upcoming studio album. This version was introduced in Euphoria’s eighth episode and first season finale “And Salt the Earth Behind You,” with lead character Zendaya providing vocals.

Title: Yesterday
About:
“Yesterday” is the most covered song in history, with over 2,000 versions having been recorded. A spare, haunting song of lost love from the early Beatles catalog, it was sung perfectly by a young Paul McCartney.
McCartney dreamed the entire melody before he arranged the music with George Martin. According to Rolling Stone:
‘It fell out of bed,’ Paul McCartney once said about the origins of ‘Yesterday.’ ‘I had a piano by my bedside, and I must have dreamed it, because I tumbled out of bed and put my hands on the piano keys and I had a tune in my head. It was just all there, a complete thing. I couldn’t believe it. It came too easy.’
The working lyrics Paul used to fit the tune before he wrote the actual words started with “Scrambled eggs / Oh my baby how I love your legs / Not as much as I love scrambled eggs.” Picking up on that famous trivia, Jimmy Fallon eventually developed the rest of “Scrambled eggs” and recorded it with Paul.

Title: Sunday Candy!
About:
The first single off The Social Experiment’s project Surf. Chance appears all over the track powered by vibrant live instrumentation – rapping and singing throughout what seems to be an ode to Chance’s grandmother and her lifestyle and personality.
For many black Americans, grandmothers are the staple matriarchs of the family, and the foundation of identities.
Artwork by Orlando Hays
(note: the candies in the bowl are life savers – not peppermints)
The music video was released April 12th 2015 (check the behind the scenes video here, and an overhead view of the action here):

Title: Poison!
About: In “Poison”, BBD’s debut single after their group New Edition went on hiatus, the boys are warning all dudes out there to be careful about the ho’s they spit game at. It became their first of two top 5 singles in a row in the US. It also charted well in the UK, New Zealand and The Netherlands.

Title: きくお (Kikuo) - 愛して愛して愛して (Love Me, Love Me, Love Me) ft. 初音ミク (Hatsune Miku) (Romanized)!
About:
“Aishite Aishite Aishite” is an original song written by Kikuo. This song is about a girl, with a case of Good Child Syndrome, who demands love and attention from other people as an effect of a cursed collar she wears. As she grows up, the collar’s effect on her becomes stronger, believing that the people whom she demands from are not enough.
Another interpretation is that it is about a girl who wants love from her parents. Her parents want her to do well in school and do not give her the love she so desires. The collar she wears is a metaphor, as it a symbol of her parents' expectations. As she grows up and her body changes, the collar stays the same size just as what her parents want out of her doesn’t change either.

Title: AKMU - 어떻게 이별까지 사랑하겠어, 널 사랑하는 거지 (How can I love the heartbreak, you’re the one I love) (English Translation)!
No Make-Up (Her Vice)!
About:
Kendrick doesn’t think girls need make-up to look sexy – just a brain…
The first of three songs dedicated to a prostitute named Keisha that Kendrick sympathizes with. Followed by Keisha’s Song and verse two of Sing About Me
The reliance on makeup shown also serves as a parallel to other substances that are constantly abused like alcohol and drugs. The makeup represents how people cover themselves up instead of facing the real world similarly to how people will will attempt to drink away their pain instead of facing things head on. Kendrick shows this through the bridge:
Ladies and gentlemen
And you ain’t gotta get drunk to have fun
You ain’t gotta get drunk to have fun
You ain’t gotta get drunk to have fun

Title: RADWIMPS - なんでもないや (Nandemonaiya) (Movie Version) (Romanized)!
NCT DREAM - 제자리 걸음 (Starry Night) (English Translation)!
개별로 (Fxxked Up)!
About:
“Fxxked Up (개별로)” is the third track from SOMI’s EP GAME PLAN. The song was planned to be part of SOMI’s debut album XOXO, but it was discarded to be released at another time to have all the spotlight and not be overshadowed by the other songs on the album.
The song was featured on Somi’s Youtube Series “I AM SOMI”.
Somi explained that she wrote the song about a call where this person was rude towards her and which she didn’t understand what this person was saying.
She said in an Instagram live that this is her favorite song that she has done and she want it to released it at the right time.
‘개별로’ was the second song that Somi has teased in her YouTube series, doing it first with “Watermelon”.

Title: Linked Horizon - 紅蓮の弓矢 (Guren no Yumiya) (Romanized)!
About:
“紅蓮の弓矢” (“Guren no Yumiya,” translated as “Crimson Bow and Arrow” and promoted as “Feuerroter Pfeil und Bogen”) is the second track on Linked Horizon’s second studio album, Shingeki no Kiseki, but was first included in his second single, Jiyuu e no Shingeki.
It was famously used as the first opening theme of the anime series Attack on Titan.

Title: Wonderwall!
About:
“Wonderwall” was released as the third single off of Oasis' classic album What’s The Story? (Morning Glory). It was supposedly written for Noel Gallagher’s then-girlfriend, Meg Matthews, as he told NME in 1996, “It’s about my girlfriend, Meg Matthews.” Bandmembers Bonehead and Guigsy seconded that in the documentary Live Forever, the Ups and Downs of Britpop. However, after Gallagher divorced Matthews in 2001 he said the song was not about Matthews: “[the song was] about an imaginary friend who’s gonna come and save you from yourself.” Some people believe it is about Noel talking himself (he is his own imaginary friend) out of leaving Oasis (or leaving life!). Others believe it is about the relationship between Noel and Liam and some say it is about his (then) imaginary friendship with Paul McCartney (they would later become friends in the mid-to-late 1990s).
The song’s title was appropriated from the 1968 film Wonderwall – from Psychedelia to Surrealism, which George Harrison provided the soundtrack (called Wonderwall Music) for. Before Noel had his “George Harrison revelation,” the song was titled “Wishing Stone.”
In 2006, it was included in the “Best Of”-compilation Stop The Clocks and in 2010 it was included in the singles collection Time Flies… 1994-2009.

Title: No Make-Up (Her Vice)!
About:
Kendrick doesn’t think girls need make-up to look sexy – just a brain…
The first of three songs dedicated to a prostitute named Keisha that Kendrick sympathizes with. Followed by Keisha’s Song and verse two of Sing About Me
The reliance on makeup shown also serves as a parallel to other substances that are constantly abused like alcohol and drugs. The makeup represents how people cover themselves up instead of facing the real world similarly to how people will will attempt to drink away their pain instead of facing things head on. Kendrick shows this through the bridge:
Ladies and gentlemen
And you ain’t gotta get drunk to have fun
You ain’t gotta get drunk to have fun
You ain’t gotta get drunk to have fun

Title: Welcome To MY World!
About: Welcome To MY World is the first pre-release track from aespa’s 3rd Mini Album MY WORLD SM Entertainment said this in their description for the song “Welcome To MY World is an alternative pop song that features dreamy guitar riffs, a grand orchestration in the second half of the song.” The song will feature the main character in aespa’s universe naevis.

Title: 슝! (Shoong!)!
About:
Making it their first ever collaboration, here’s TAEYANG and BLACKPINK’s LISA on “Shoong!” The track is the second song on his April 2023 mini-album, Down To Earth.
“Shoong!” is a light hip-pop anthem about how they’ll run as fast as a car just to get back to their partner. The songs instrumental compliments the duos powerful vocals and raps.

Title: Another Day of Sun!
About:
“Another Day of Sun” is the first song in the film, composed by Justin Hurwitz and written by Benj Pasek and Justin Paul, sung by the entire La La Land cast.
The song is a choreographed party in broad daylight on the usually irritating, jammed LA highway – an extremely, amazing theatrical depiction of magic in the mundane.
The cast starts in their cars, in gridlocked L.A. traffic – and break into a choreographed troupe format dance.
The song’s featured singers tell personal “origin stories” that loosely foreshadow the plot of the movie, setting up the main characters' motivations as struggling performers also trying to make it in L.A.
The lyrics, too, foreshadow the entire movie, talking about inspirations and motivations for struggling actors and artists – with all their shimmering hopes and dreams – and the entire desperate nature of these desires.

Title: Graduation
About:
“Graduation” is Juice WRLD’s contemporary take on the early 2000’s pop anthem, “Graduation (Friends Forever)” by Vitamin C. It features a trap-style beat and revived lyrics with Juice WRLD interpolating the hit track.
The music video and song was released on August 30, 2019, after an Instagram story teasing the song.

Song: I Don't Understand But I Luv U
Title: I Don’t Understand But I Luv U!
I’m Unhappy!
About:
“I’m Unhappy” is the fifth track on aespa’s third mini-album, MY WORLD. The song is the second song to have its own track video after the release of, “Welcome to MY World.” Additionally, the song is one of the few future releases from the group that they performed in their SYNK : HYPER LINE fan meeting.
In “I’m Unhappy” aespa sing about their troubles living an unhappy life as they compare themselves to others that live happy lives, they then eventually put an end to this emotion. A catchy, and simplistic electronic pop plays throughout the song, giving off a gloomy attitude to the listener.
For the track video, each member portrays a different struggle that is the cause to their unhappiness. To be specific, NINGNING, is a cheerleader that struggles to keep up and be perfect, WINTER is bullied and ignored by others. Then, KARINA is stuck with a variety of troubles in an asylum while GISELLE fails to fit in and is considered an outcast.

Title: Lovey Dovey!
About: love song

Title: We Are Number One!
About:
This song is from the popular children’s show “Lazy Town.” In 2016 it became a meme along with the character Robbie Rotten and the show.
The song is about the main antagonist, Robbie Rotten, getting help from several lookalikes of himself to become more evil.
‘We are Number One" grew quickly in the meme community, and a Go Fund Me for Stefan Karl Stefánsson’s Year of Healing was quickly noticed. Stefan has been diagnosed with pancreatic cancer, and due to financial burden, needs donations for treatment. The Go Fund Me has raised over 100k and Stefan has begun chemotherapy. The creation of this meme has helped Stefan more than ever imaginable. Unfortunately, he lost the battle against it, and he died on August 21, 2018.

Title: Love & War!
About:
“Love and War” by Kodak Black first surfaced as a one minute CDQ snippet that leaked on a SoundCloud post on December 6, 2019.
In November of 2021, the song would start to gain traction on TikTok because of the lyrics from the chorus, bringing in over 300,000 videos by the time of the release in late 2021.
The popular piano beat was produced by Pompano Puff and J Gramm, two producers who have both worked with Kodak previously. J-Gramm did not previously have his producer tag on the snippet version.
Kodak’s chorus was used on a Shorline Mafia track named “On the Low” in July 2020. Despite the use of the chorus, in December 2021, Kodak unexpectedly released the track using the same lyrics.

Title: Cocoa Butter Kisses!
About: “Cocoa Butter Kisses” is about Chance turning into a man who repulses his mother. He misses the days when he still had his innocence and his mother used to give him sweet kisses.

Title: Dive
About:
In an interview with GQ magazine for its March cover, Ed let the writer listen to five songs before anyone else and described “Dive” as:
Sheeran openly admits that all the love songs on the album are about Seaborn, and “Dive” returns to the themes of vulnerability, a sense of devotion and emotional reflection, using the classic Fifties and early Sixties soul/Stax ballad arpeggio and time sequence.

Title: 오늘이 마치막인 것처럼 (Lasting like the last day)!

Song: 다시는 사랑한다 말하지 마 (Feat. RM of BTS)
Title: 다시는 사랑한다 말하지 마 (Don’t ever say love me)!
You’re the Inspiration!
About:
Peter Cetera was originally contacted by producer David Foster who told him Kenny Rogers wanted Cetera to write a song for him. Foster and Cetera spent two hours coming up with the basic structure, then Cetera finished the lyrics over the next two weeks while in Italy. However, Rogers passed on the song, so Cetera brought it to his band Chicago.
The song was released as the third single from their album 17 and was a huge hit, reaching #3 in the US in early 1985 and was also a top 40 hit in the UK, Sweden, New Zealand and The Netherlands.

Title: Circle of Life!
About:
Lebo M’s arresting and enthralling Zulu chant opens the movie and calls to the citizens of the Pride Lands to come to Pride Rock. As the sun rises, the animals of the Pride Lands awaken from their slumber and begin to journey across the landscape. The music gently swells behind Carmen Twillie’s vocals until, suddenly, Pride Rock comes into view and we hear the first chorus.
At Pride Rock, Rafiki the mandrill sangoma arrives and warmly greets Mufasa, the King of the Pride Lands. The two go to greet Sarabi, the Queen of the Pride Lands, who is cradling her tiny cub Simba between her paws. Rafiki blesses the leonine prince and the music becomes gentle and reverent. Blessing completed, Rafiki lifts the cub over his shoulder and carries him out onto the promontory of Pride Rock as the expectant animals wait below. As Rafiki lifts the cub high into the air to present him to his citizens, the music rises triumphantly again as the gathered animals rejoice at the sight of their new prince. The camera slowly zooms out, showing us the sheer size of the throng of animals, before a closing drumbeat takes us to the opening title card of the movie.
A “pop version” by Elton John is featured on the soundtrack and became a single.

Title: 3:55 FREESTYLE!
About:
8 апреля 2020 года в своём твиттер-аккаунте Фладда намекнул на новый трек:
Впервые трек был исполнен 11 апреля 2020 года на онлайн концерте «Маятник Фуко»:
27 апреля состоялась премьера трека и клипа!

Title: Hate Sleeping Alone!
About:
Bonus Track (only on iTunes) off of Take Care.
Here, Drake details a complicated relationship that he’s staying in just because he hates sleeping alone.
This song also contains back-up vocals from Trey Songz.
Trivia: The production on this track samples Club Paradise, one of the tracks Drake used to build up to the release of Take Care.
https://youtu.be/EfGOqrXcm_Y

Title: Pop Style (Single Version)!
About:
“Pop Style” is a Jamaican term meaning “ballin’” or “showing off.” The term was famously used on Althea and Donna’s “Uptown Top Ranking”, a Reggae one hit wonder which reached number 1 on the UK charts in 1978.
While Jay Z only shows up for two lines, Drake and Kanye drop numerous references to popular culture. Drake name checks Channing Tatum and Justin Timberlake, while Kanye drops the infamous “I’mma let you finish but…" line from his 2009 VMA run in with Taylor Swift.
This is the first time Drake has collaborated with Kanye West and Jay Z as “The Throne,” and the first time all three rappers are featured on the same song. Drake previously collaborated with Kanye on “Find Your Love”, “Forever”, and “Blessings”, while he linked up with Jay Z for “Light Up,” “Off That,” and “Pound Cake / Paris Morton Music 2.” An alternate version of “Pop Style” without Kanye or Jay Z premiered on OVO Sound Radio on April 9th.
Although they released a collab album called Watch The Throne in 2011, this is Jay and Ye’s first time performing under the moniker “The Throne.”
The collaboration between Drake and The Throne may have been inspired by a line from “6PM in New York”:
Yeah, boy, you rappin' like you seen it all
You rappin' like the throne should be the three of y'all
“Pop Style” was one of two singles released on April 5, 2016, alongside “One Dance.”

Title: 이불킥 (Blanket Kick/Embarrassed)!
About: “이불킥 (Blanket Kick/Embarrassed)” is the 10th track on BTS' first studio album, Dark&Wild.

Title: CHEN (EXO) - 사월이 지나면 우리 헤어져요 (Beautiful Goodbye) (English Translation)!
Marry Me!
About:
“Marry Me” is the song by Thomas Rhett, which appears on his 2017 album, Life Changes. According to CountryFanCast, the song has been chosen as Rhett’s third single from the album, following the success of the first single, Craving You, and the second single, Unforgettable.
As a single, the song peaked at #1 on Billboard’s Country Airplay chart.

Title: Tinnitus (Wanna be a rock) (돌멩이가 되고 싶어)!
Bad Bad Bad!
About: On “Bad Bad Bad,” Thug and Lil Baby team up for a track where they brag about all the luxurious brands they can afford to buy for women and themselves.

Title: Woodpecker No. 1!
About: 6 minutes and 42 seconds of pure audio bliss

Title: Toss a Coin to Your Witcher!
Singer: Sonya Belousova, Giona Ostinelli, & Joey Batey
About:
“Toss a Coin to Your Witcher” tells the story of Geralt of Rivia and how he fought a sylvan, a half-goat half-man, in the second episode of the series titled “Four Marks.” In the show, it was composed by Jaskier, Geralt’s companion, played by Joey Batey.
Jaskier spins the real events to paint Geralt in a heroic light, hoping to win over the common folk and ease the distrust and contempt with which Witchers are met in the Northern Kingdoms.

Title: NCT DREAM - 북극성 (Never Goodbye) (English Translation)!
About: This R&B ballad song has clam percussion sounds, Rhodes piano and Chimes. It’s about waiting for you at the same place like a Polaris. The members, MARK, JENO, JAEMIN & JISUNG participated in the rap-making.

Title: 에피소드 (Episode)!
About: ‘Episode’ by Lee Mujin is a nostalgic song about a past relationship, framed as series of memories or ‘episodes’. It starts with the narrator recalling first dates and loving conversations, before heading more into the heartache of the breakup and the ensuing loneliness.

Title: DAY6 - 한 페이지가 될 수 있게 (Time of Our Life) (English Translation)!
About:
“Time of Our Life” by DAY6 is the title track of their 5th EP “The Book of Us: Gravity”. This song is an uplifting and empowering anthem about seizing the present moment and making the most out of life. It encourages listeners to let go of worries and anxieties and focus on creating memorable experiences and memories together.
The lyrics of this vibrant and anthemic song tell a story of embracing the fleeting nature of time, and cherishing the memories made along the way. With its dynamic instrumentation, powerful vocals, and heartfelt lyrics, they deliver a powerful message about living life to the fullest and appreciating every precious second.
The song was written and produced in the key A♭ Major, with a tempo of 175 BPM.

Title: BTS - 전하지 못한 진심 (The Truth Untold) ft. Steve Aoki (English Translation)!
About:
“전하지 못한 진심”, the 3rd track on Love Yourself: 轉 Tear, translates to “sincerity that couldn’t be delivered.” It references the smeraldo flower, a fictitious flower that BTS has been associated with on more than one occasion. Similar to how different flowers can mean different things, the smeraldo means “the truth I couldn’t tell,” according to the only flower shop that claims to sell it.
There are similarities between the lyrics of the song and “La Città di Smeraldo”, an Italian tale about a man who hides in his house because he’s afraid of showing himself. As he watches from the window, he falls in love with a woman stealing flowers from his garden to sell them. Trying to help her, he grows a special flower, the smeraldo, so that she can earn more money from it, but the woman dies before she is able to see it or meet the man. Several quotes from the original story are written in the teaser posters for the Love Yourself series.

Title: Discord
About: “Discord” is a song about a draconequus of the same name from My Little Pony: Friendship is Magic who attempts to overthrow Equestria and wreak havoc upon their world.

Title: Window Shopper!
About:
Off the soundtrack for Get Rich Or Die Tryin' (not to be confused with the major label debut of the same name), 50 delivers another report on the spoils of success and the negative attention it raises from his peers
Though 50 sings the entire track apart from the intro, the lyrical and instrumental structure qualify it as a rap song nonetheless
In the version of the hook used here, Fif namechecks 4 of his enemies; other versions (both dirty and radio edits) omit the names

Title: London Boy
About:
“London Boy” is most likely an ode to Swift’s boyfriend as of the song’s release, British actor Joe Alwyn. Other songs on the album, including the title track, are assumed to be about their relationship.
Having mostly escaped the public eye during her relationship with Alwyn, Swift finally shares some of the most memorable experiences she’s enjoyed.
This track is notable for using an interpolation of the rhythm on Cautious Clay’s “Cold War,” making Clay only the second artist ever to be interpolated on one of Swift’s songs (after Right Said Fred on “Look What You Made Me Do”).

Title: Tomboy!
About:
“Tomboy,” is the second track of Princess Nokia’s 2017 album 1992 Deluxe. The song is about Princess Nokia being proud of her body and owning her sexuality. In an interview with Mass appeal Princess Nokia talked about Tomboy and said,
By falling in love with all your flaws. I’ve done fallen in love with all of mine. I see my existence as a big fuck you to conventionality. Sure I have little titties and a phat belly, but that doesn’t make me less of a goddess, less of an aphrodisiac.

Title: IU - Blueming (English Translation)!
About:
“Blueming"s motivation came from 심수봉(Korean singer song writer)’s ‘백만송이 장미(A Million Roses)’
The concept of this song is ‘Waiting a miracle to a impossible phenomenon, 'bloom a blue rose and achieve love’”. The combination of ‘blue’(symbol of gloom) and ‘bloom’(symbol of hope) also explains the subject of the song.
The following is the official introduction of the song(translated).
One of my favorite love song is 심수봉’s “백만송이 장미(A Million Roses)
To me, expressing a single rose as ‘Achieving love with practice without a hateful feeling’ seems poetic and beautiful than any other metaphors of love.
The period when I bloom the most roses in a short time is after I fell in love.
Right before confessing our heart is when we use every word in the universe except only one crucial word, to express our emotions to our lover,
when we feel the phone vibration one by one as an earthquake that shakes my everyday life without mercy,
when the words that we mentioned without filtering becomes a love poem.
The awkward style of the sentences pours out in our head, giving a doubt ‘Did this sentence really came from my head?’ At that period, love explodes inside me so strongly that it gives me the confidence that ‘I can bloom millions of roses easily.’ Inside our chat, I’m positive that for this once, I can bloom a blue rose just like my color,
a ‘miracle’ just like the meaning of the rose,
the achievement that is almost impossible.
Even though I had never made it…/

Title: 신호등 (Traffic Light)!
About:
Lee Mujin personifies the anxiety of a young adult in “Traffic Light,” a summery folk jazz opening that signals his major debut. Lee compares himself to a yellow traffic light, which does its best, even if it does not last long. At 20, he will make raw choices without guidance, trying to establish his place as both a musician and a person.
“I want to talk about the chaotic state of mind that young people face in the first stages of entering society. That sense of chaos builds up day by day,” Lee told The Korea JoongAng Daily.
Lee wrote “Traffic Light” for homework at the Seoul Institute of the Arts, where he is currently an undergraduate. One other assignment is “I’m Sorry, Professor,” which he performed as part of a group.

Title: 봄여름가을겨울 (Still Life)
About:
About four years after the release of single, “꽃 길 (FLOWER ROAD)”, BIGBANG is back with a new song “Still Life”. This is also BIGBANG’s first single as a quartet.
The introductory part of the lyrics that talks about the flow of time and the cycle of the season from spring to winter, the lyrical harmonics emphasized in the latter half, the light guitar performance of the introductory part, the warm arrangement element that complements it, the charm of this song is it’s about making people think about the path they have taken in their lives after listening.
BIGBANG members evoke the memories of their youth through vocals, as if they were talking about the youth that everyone experienced, and the gentle and beautiful melody flows into their hearts, giving people peace of mind.

Song: 흔들리는 꽃들 속에서 네 샴푸향이 느껴진거야
Title: 흔들리는 꽃들 속에서 네 샴푸향이 느껴진거야 (Your Shampoo Scent In The Flowers)!
IU - 에잇 (eight) ft. SUGA (English Translation)!
About:
This digital single is a band-based pop rock with a blend of analog emotions and trendy sound. IU and SUGA are 28-year-old musicians of the same age, and they attract the listeners' ears with their balanced blend of unique personalities as said two people melts their synergy into the song.
The song talks about adults forgetting their past, their own-self, their dream, who they really are and the memories of their childhood and teenage years as they become swept away with rushing life, accepting themselves as ones they never were.

Title: Jaurim - 스물다섯, 스물하나 (Twenty Five, Twenty One) (Romanized)!
옥탑방 (Rooftop)
About:
“옥탑방 (Rooftop)” is the groups first song as quartet after the departure of member Kwang-jin. Released as a part of their FLY HIGH PROJECT #2, the song expresses feelings from one lover to another. The song contains dembow beats infused with reggae, giving a softer upbeat sound in contrast to the groups general rock sound which they’re known for.
After the end of promotion for the song, it saw a sudden raise in charts thanks to an anonymous article posted on a popular forum. The song went on to become the groups first chart topper since debut. The group also went back to music shows after the sudden popularity of the song and gained their first ever music show win. The sleeper-hit song won the “Best Rock” award on Melon Music Awards and “Discovery of the Year” in band category in Gaon Chart Music Awards.
In an interview with Billboard, member Seunghyub expressed why the song resonated with so many listeners:
It’s an easygoing listen, and when you hear it, it’s a song that you can feel the lyrics. Maybe it’s because for Korean people it’s what the public as the whole is feeling right now.

Title: Lee Mujin - 비와 당신 (Rain and You) (Romanized)!
i hate u, i love u!
About:
Olivia O'Brien and Gnash pair up to create this conflicting love story. Gnash simply described the track as:
Missing someone sucks, but it’s important to feel.
This is not the only time that this duo have worked together. Gnash has produced some of Olivia’s tracks and it’s safe to say that there could be more from them in the future.

Title: 기억을 걷는 시간 (Time Spent Walking Through Memories)!
About:
“기억을 걷는 시간(Time Walking on Remembrance)” is a song written by Jong-Wan Kim of NELL, from their album Separation Anxiety.
The speaker misses his separated lover and everything around him remind the person he love even from some sound, air, a leaf.
The song was awarded prizes ‘Rock Music Award’ from ’Mnet Asian Music Award’, ‘Best Rock Band Award’ from ‘Golden Disk’ and Jong-Wan Kim who wrote the song win an award for ‘Best Songwriter’ from ‘Mnet Asian Music Award’.
With the song, NELL became popular and established itself.

Title: SUNMI - 가시나 (Gashina) (English Translation)!
About:
“Gashina” (Hangul: 가시나) is a song recorded by South Korean singer SUNMI. The track marks SUNMI’s first release since the disbandment of Wonder Girls and the expiration of her contract with JYP Entertainment.
The song is dance hall-style synthpop with Eastern-style influences. It was a commercial success selling over a million copies. The song was nominated for multiple year end awards including Golden Disc Awards, Seoul Music Awards and Melon Music Awards. The song won SUNMI, Best Style in Music Award at the 19th Mnet Asian Music Awards.

Title: Winter Bear!
About:
“Winter Bear” is an English-language ballad from BTS member V. Alongside a self-directed music video featuring footage from his travels, the prevailing theory is that it was released as a tribute to his late grandmother who passed in September 2016.
A bear, of course, hibernates in the winter, sleeping for many months peacefully. This represents his wishes for her to rest in peace, but it also subtly hints that he wishes for her to return one day.
Incidentally, the track marks 1000 days of the phrase “I purple you.“ The unofficial color of ARMY, he defines purple as representing trust and long-lasting love since it comes at the end of the rainbow.

Title: My Love Mine All Mine!
About:
In “My Love Mine All Mine,” Mitski recognizes the beauty of her love and asks the Moon to capture it, for it to shine down and preserve even after her passing. It was the third single on Mitski’s seventh studio album, The Land is Inhospitable and So Are We, following “Bug Like an Angel” and the double-sided “Star b/w Heaven.”
The song garnered mass popularity through TikTok—which helped it gain over a million videos in less than two months. The track peaked at number 26 on Billboard’s Hot 100, marking Mitski’s first time in the chart.

Title: Ride Monologue!
About: This monologue was written by Lana Del Rey, and is spoken by her at the beginning and end of the music video of Ride.

Title: The NeverEnding Story!
About:
“The NeverEnding Story” is the end theme of the 1984 film The Neverending Story. The film is, in turn, an adaptation of the novel Die unendliche Geschichte (1979) by Michael Ende.
In keeping with the film title, the song does not have a definite beginning or ending per se: It fades into verse one, and fades out during the coda, as if the song were part of a loop. It was composed by disco pioneer Giorgio Moroder, with lyrics by Keith Forsey. Forsey was a disco drummer and songwriter who also penned songs for the films Ghostbusters (1984), Beverly Hills Cop (1984), and The Breakfast Club (1985).
Limahl and Moroder recorded French and English versions of the song at a studio in Munich. The English version features soprano vocals by American singer Beth Anderson, while the French version (translated by Pierre-André Dousset) features French pop singer Ann Calvert.

Title: Feel This Moment!
About:
“Feel This Moment” is a song by American rapper Pitbull, featuring guest vocals from Christina Aguilera. The song was released as the fourth single from Pitbull’s album Global Warming, and marks the first collaboration between the two.
Lyrically, the song is about living life to the fullest and enjoying each and every moment.

Title: FTISLAND - 사랑앓이 (Love Sick) (With 김나영 (Kim Na Young)) (Romanized)!
About: Love Sick (ft Kim Na Young) is a remake of FTISLAND’s debut song for their 10th anniversary. It has been made into duet with Kim Na Young and is pre-released for their 10th anniversary album as a gift for fans who have been with the band since debut in 2007.

Title: Starlight
About:
“Starlight” is South London superstar Dave’s first single of 2022 and the first release since the drop of his number one sophomore studio album We’re All Alone In This Together, released in July 2021.
The track samples Canada-based Indonesian duo The Macarons Project’s 2017 rendition of Frank Sinatra’s 1964 classic, “Fly Me To The Moon”.
He first teased the track during an Instagram Live on February 12 2022, where he also showcased the production of an instrumental he has been working on. This quickly became viral with fans demanding it be released. Dave teased a short clip from the music video at his first London show as part of his tour on the 28th February at the O2. Dave posted the trailer on the March 1st 2022, alongside the title and the March 3rd 2022 release date, amidst the second day of his London O2 W.A.A.I.T.T Headline tour.

Title: Endless Fashion!
About:
Endless Fashion is the 11th track on Lil Uzi Vert’s third studio album, the “Pink Tape”. This song represents the two rappers' third song together. In this song, Lil Uzi and Nicki mimic Eiffel 65’s flow and interpolates the instrumental on their breakout hit single, “I’m Blue”, while Nicki complements Uzi’s performance through her smoother vocals. Throughout the song Uzi employs a hyper-melodic tone to flex their superior fashion. One week before the launch of Pink Tape, Uzi made a sweeping statement claiming to have better fashion than “A$AP Rocky”
Lil Uzi Vert Says They're 'Flyer Than ASAP Rocky Nowadays'
Complex
During a red carpet interview, Uzi shouted out a number of their key influences, including Mike Jones and ASAP Rocky.

Title: 나 에게 로 떠나는 여행 (Trip to Myself)!
drivers license!
About:
“drivers license” is the first single and third track on Olivia Rodrigo’s debut album SOUR. It is a possible love ode about Rodrigo moving on from her previous relationship with Joshua Bassett, who is now supposedly dating Sabrina Carpenter, by doing the one last thing that she promised him—getting a driver’s license. The song showcases a brilliant balance of unfiltered outpouring and graceful specificity. Its accompanying music video spins a vivid portrait of Rodrigo as she drives aimlessly, longing for an ex-love.
The song was teased several times before its release in January 2021. On July 23, 2020, Olivia posted a snippet of the song on Instagram with the caption “wrote dis the other day. vv close to my heart. gonna call it drivers license I think lol.” She then posted another two snippets in December 2020, captioning them with “soon” and “sooner.” On January 4, 2021, she announced the song as her debut single.

Title: 행복했던 날들이었다 (Days Gone By)!
About:
Day6 “Days gone by” is the 2nd track on the album “Remember us: Youth part 2”. The song has a 80’s nostalgia gives a mellifluous and vibrant feel to the album.
The track was released on December 10,2018

Title: 발걸음 (A Day For Love)!
About: Yukika said in her album commentary that she received this song long before she received her debut song, NEON, and she felt like this song was meant for her.

Title: 개화 (開花) (Lost One)!
About:
The Epik High member Tablo’s interpretation of the song is mainly directed to what he wants to say to many trainees.
In a recent interview he stated that his main inspiration for writing and composing “Lost One” were artists such as BTS, who respect and are inspired by Epik High:
There have been a few people who’ve said that they started dreaming of making music because of our music. In particular, I once watched a video someone sent me where members of BTS said that they started dreaming of making music after hearing Epik High’s song “Fly”. I’ve seen a few others say that over the years as well. That was what was in my head.
“There are a lot of trainees in our world today, not only just in the entertainment industry,” he continued.
We’re still like trainees in terms of being fathers and husbands too. So I think it’s something I’m saying to those people.
Tablo also mentioned that, advice from seniors in an industry is usually positive, and contains messages like “You can do it,” or “You can do well.” To this, he said:
I think it’s a shame that I couldn’t only talk about good things if I were to say something to those who start to dream after seeing me, because there are so many painful things as well on the path that I’ve walked. At the same time, I think that these words could also seem like a comfort.
If our children ever want to follow the path we’ve taken, I’d start by playing this song for them.

Title: Love Me or Leave Me!
About: “Love Me or Leave Me” comes about following the demise of a relationship and the girls are reflecting on the relationship and wondering where they went wrong. The girls pose an ultimatum for the lover, either to man up and love her, or to ultimately walk away from everything, leave her and end the relationship forever.

Title: Broken Melodies
About: “Broken Melodies” is the second track on NCT DREAM’s “ISTJ – The 3rd Album” The song was released in June 19th 2023, 28 days before the full album released. Mostly calming and with an exciting, high energy chorus, ‘Broken Melodies’ is about relationships (romantic or not) over long distances, the feeling that everything is ‘wrong’ without those relationships close to them, and the yearning of the singers to be closer to the people they love.

Title: 사랑은 가슴이 시킨다 (Love from a Real Heart) (2023)!
제주도의 푸른 밤 (The Blue Night of Jeju Island)!
맨정신 (SOBER)!
About:
“D” is the sixth single album by South Korean band Big Bang, and the third from their MADE Series.
It contains two songs: “If You” & “Sober”
The first poster of D was released on June 26, with the name of the title track, and the date of album release. The second poster with the second title track was released on the next day June 27. The two new songs were released on July 1, and the physical album will be released on the 7th. The release is supported by a live countdown on Naver Starcast on 30 June, 11 pm.

Title: XO Tour Llif3!
About:
“XO Tour Llif3” is a chronicle of Uzi’s relationship issues with ex-girlfriend, Brittany Byrd, and substance abuse. It’s delivered over bassy and upbeat production but addresses topics like suicide and betrayal. Ultimately, it’s a narrative mixed with strong emotional dialogue, outlining a big fight between them and their girlfriend.
LUV premiered the track in London while opening for The Weeknd’s Starboy: Legend of the Fall 2017 world tour, following the song’s leak. The title pays homage to The Weeknd’s label XO, in light of the performance.
Eternal Atake’s “P2” is a continuation of XO Tour Llif3. Uzi raps about moving on from heartbreak and continuing their career, problem-free. P2 is the outro to Eternal Atake, excluding two bonus tracks.

Title: Revenge
About:
“Revenge” is a song that is dedicated to his late friend, Jocelyn Flores, who committed suicide in the bathroom of his hotel room while visiting him. This is the the only single released to promote his debut album, 17.
This song was first previewed on Twitter on May 9th and released officially on X’s SoundCloud titled as “garette’s REVENGE” on May 18th.
X posted a Snapchat about the video saying,
For those of you that saw that video on my Twitter, I produced that..all of it..completely original.

Title: Good Morning
About:
Kanye’s intro to his third album, Graduation, is a wake-up call to himself and the world. It also gives Kanye a chance to show off his polished lyrical abilities.
The song is part of a larger metaphor: The album Graduation is Kanye’s “graduation” in the rap game. “Good Morning” is told as if Kanye is waking up on his Graduation Day, reminiscing about his college days and finally receiving his “rapping degree” in the third verse. The school/college theme is very present throughout the song.
Kanye samples Jay-Z’s “The Ruler’s Back” and Elton John’s “Someone Saved My Life Tonight” (both artists have been featured on one of Kanye’s songs).
On a side note, as if writing and producing this track wasn’t enough, Ye also developed a creative storyline for a video – despite the song never being released as an actual single.
The music video for “Good Morning” is essentially the result of Japanese pop artist Takashi Murakami bringing to life the various designs he had composed for Graduation’s album artwork through the use of cel-shaded animation.

Title: Higher
About:
According to songwriter Bibi Bourelly, “Higher” was written in only 20 minutes. She spoke to Vice about the song:
Everything happens for a reason. Working with Rihanna has changed my life. The 20 minutes it took me to write “Higher” is actually what changed my life, because, even though we wrote “Bitch Better Have My Money” first, she heard that one first.
The intoxicating ANTI track first surfaced in March 2015 as a pair of Instagram previews posted by Rihanna while drunk on whiskey at 5:00 AM.
Rihanna compared the song to “a drunk voicemail” in Vogue’s April 2016 cover story:
You know he’s wrong, and then you get drunk and you’re like, ‘I could forgive him. I could call him. I could make up with him.’ Just, desperate.

Title: YB - A Flying Butterfly (나는 나비) (English Translation)!
About:
“A Flying Butterfly (나는 나비)” is a song from the 2nd album of YB (also known as Yoon Do-hyun Band), “Why Be?”.
It’s a motivational song that explores the journey of personal growth and transformation. The lyrics use the metaphor of a caterpillar transforming into a butterfly to convey the idea of breaking free from limitations, pursuing dreams, and embracing freedom.
The song was covered multiple times, for example :
- 2016 KBS Song Festival sung by BTS’s Jungkook, Twice’s Jihyo, Seventeen’s Mingyu and Dokyeom, GOT7’s BamBam and Yugyeom, GFriend’s Yuju and Eunha, DIA’s Chae-yeon.
- Immortal Songs 2 sung by Ailee
- The liar and his lover OST sung by Red Velvet’s Joy

Title: 그대만 있다면 (If I have you only) (My love)!
Home Sweet Home
About:
“Home Sweet Home” is track #5 on Mötley Crüe’s 1985 LP Theatre of Pain.
Although so-called “power ballads” existed prior to this song, it’s probably the first one that influenced an entire generation of rock bands to record at least one power ballad for release as a single on each record; virtually every band emulated “Home Sweet Home.”
Originally, Elektra Records didn’t want to release the song due it sounding too different from the band’s previously released albums. After threatening to leave, the record label released Theatre of Pain with the song included. However, the band had to finance the video for MTV on their own and it became history: the video stayed on the MTV daily request chart for over 3 months.

Song: 시대유감 (時代遺憾) (2024 aespa Remake Ver.)
Title: aespa - 시대유감 (時代遺憾) (Regret of the Times) (2024 aespa Remake Version) (English Translation)!
사랑하는 법을 몰라서 (Because I Don’t Know How to Love) (2023)!

Song: 몇 번의 여름 (여름날 우리 X TOIL, Gist)
Title: 몇 번의 여름 (After a few summers)!
Boogieman!
About:
Gambino conjures a new version of the traditional ‘bogeyman’ used to scare children into behaving. The “boogieman” is a portmanteau explained here. He uses this image to illustrate racial fear in the United States, specifically involving African Americans. The refrain seemingly takes a critical view of the police:
With a gun in your hand, I’m the boogieman
This mystical, almost witchdoctor-like view perception is a strongly criticised African American stereotype. Notably, the image also parallels the album cover art.

Title: Linked Horizon - 心臓を捧げよ! (Shinzou wo Sasageyo!) (Romanized)!
About:
“心臓を捧げよ!” (“Shinzou wo Sasageyo!,” translated as “Dedicate Your Heart!”) by Linked Horizon is the opening theme for the second season of the popular anime Attack on Titan.
The title is meant to go hand-in-hand with the famous salute in both the anime and the manga, where the Survey Corps put a fist to their heart. The title tells you to not just dedicate your heart in some poetic way, rather to literally take your very being and dedicate it to something. Source

Title: Angel Eyes
Singer: Cast Of “Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again”

Title: i love you!
About:
“i love you” is a soft acoustic ballad that explores the struggles and feelings from a complicated relationship. The song’s chorus features vocal harmonies by her brother, FINNEAS.
On May 13, 2018, she teased a 15-second snippet of the demo to her fans. This snippet was re-posted to her Instagram story on January 2, 2019, where she confirmed it to be the twelfth track on her debut album. However, it was replaced by “listen before i go,” becoming the thirteenth track instead.
When asked by Radio Energy for her favorites on the album, Eilish listed “i love you” as one of them, citing the track as, “still one of my favorite songs I’ve ever written in my life.”
In an interview with PopBuzz, Eilish confirmed that the final three song titles on the album were intended to form a sentence, beginning with “listen before i go,” then “i love you,” ending on “goodbye.”
The sentence was initially supposed to begin with a track titled “i dont know, i just wish i wasnt breathing,” but Eilish stated that the song was never completed, and therefore could not be included on the record.
“i love you” was the longest track of Eilish’s discography at 4 minutes and 52 seconds, but was overtaken by the title track of her sophomore album Happier Than Ever, which is 4 minutes and 58 seconds long, just 6 seconds longer.
Many have also pointed out similarities between the chorus of “i love you” and Leonard Cohen’s iconic song, “Hallelujah”.
The song’s Spotify artwork was done by @junkie.cowboy on Instagram.

Title: Growing Pains III!
About:
Long awaited trequel to Logic’s highly popular song “Growing Pains”. Growing Pains was on “Young Broke & Infamous” and Growing Pains II was on “Young Sinatra”.
6ix hinted that he, Tae Beast, & Shkye Hutch produced this track.
In this video you can hear Logic recording the beginning of the first verse:

Title: TOMORROW X TOGETHER - Blue Spring (English Translation)!
Lost
About:
“Lost” is the eleventh track of Frank Ocean’s critically acclaimed debut studio album, channel ORANGE.
The protagonist in the song is a drug supplier who uses his girlfriend as a drug mule. His lover gets lost in the intensity of the jet-setting lifestyle of drug trafficking and the subsequent money & lavish gifts that come along with it. He believes that one day she will live a normal life away from this criminal lifestyle, but it must come after ‘one last smuggle,’ which leads to another ‘one last smuggle.’
Ocean released “Lost” as the fourth single from channel ORANGE on December 17, 2012, five months after the album’s release. The single achieved RIAA Gold certification on January 20, 2017.
The official music video, directed by Francisco Soriano, appeared on Ocean’s Tumblr in a since-deleted post on April 12, 2013, with the caption:
summer 2012

Title: The Astronaut
About:
BTS‘ oldest member Jin turns a new leaf in his solo career with “The Astronaut” before completing his military service, which is mandatory in South Korea.
Lyrically, the song sees the Jin comparing his artist-fans relationship to to an intergalactic travel, showing that they made him who he is today. It is a story about him who is aimlessly wandering, and fans who helped him find his dream. The same sentiment is expressed in the track’s official video clip, where he interprets an alien astronaut that discovers the Earth after crashing on its surface.
Jin first announced the song’s release on October 15, 2022, during BTS' Yet to Come in Busan concert. Five days later, he shared a text message exchange with Coldplay’s leader, Chris Martin, on their Twitter account, revealing the band’s involvement in the song’s creative process. The song was originally written by the band, however, after collaborating with BTS on the September 2021 single “My Universe,” Chris Martin decided to gift the song to Jin, who added verses in Korean.

Title: On The Ground
About:
Five months after THE ALBUM, BLACKPINK is back “in your area” with New Zealand-born vocalist ROSÉ’s “On The Ground.” The song is the group’s second solo release after rapper JENNIE’s November 2018 single “SOLO.” It is also the leading track from ROSÉ’s debut solo two-track EP, R.
Complemented by a simple guitar and an EDM-inspired beat, “On The Ground” sees ROSÉ reflecting on her life as a global K-pop superstar and she realizes what really matters in her life already lies within herself.
