“Thunder” peaked at #4 on Billboard’s Hot 100, and scored the band their first song to surpass 1,000,000,000 (one billion) views on YouTube.

Title: Drunk Texting!
About:
In “Drunk Texting,” Chris and Jhené play the role of two people in a relationship numbing their feelings with alcohol, only to drunk text their lover to come over.
“Drunk Texting” marks the first official collaboration between Chris and Jhené. However, Jhené did contribute vocals to May 2012’s “Don’t Wake Me Up.” and June 2012’s “2012.”

Title: Sunday Morning
About:
“Sunday Morning” is the fourth single from Maroon 5’s debut album Songs About Jane The song is another mid-tempo pop-rock jam about a relationship, which seems to be a featured topic on Maroon 5’s debut.
The song was quite successful. Although the song only peaked at number 31 in the US, it topped the charts in Belgium and reached the top forty in Australia, Hungary, Ireland, the Netherlands, New Zealand, South Korea, and the UK.

Title: Underneath the Tree!
About:
“Underneath the Tree” is a song by Kelly Clarkson, from her sixth studio album and first Christmas album, Wrapped in Red. it is a Christmas-themed song that sings of gratitude for companionship during the holidays, in which the beloved is being referred to as the only present needed “underneath the tree”. Accompanied by various instrumental sounds, the song prominently incorporates a Wall of Sound treatment along with sleigh bells and bell chimes to resonate a holiday atmosphere.
“Underneath the Tree” has been praised by music critics, who considered it as the main highlight of Wrapped in Red and lauded its potential to be a holiday classic. They also complimentarily compared it to the 1994 modern Christmas standard “All I Want for Christmas Is You” by Mariah Carey.

Title: (Every Time I Turn Around) Back in Love Again!
Let It Go
About:
“Let It Go” is the 5th song from the 2013 Disney animation Frozen, about the queen of Arendelle, Elsa, with winter powers and her struggles to find her place in a world that fears her and a life she’s afraid of.
The Lopezes (a husband and wife team) and “Let It Go” won the 2013 Academy Award for Best Original Song, beating out both Happy and The Moon Song. As well as doing the songwriting for Frozen, the Lopez’s are also well known for their work on the musical The Book Of Mormon.

Title: Made You Look
About:
“Made You Look” was released on September 10, 2002 as the lead single for Nas' sixth studio album God’s Son. The single peaked at #9 on Billboard’s US Hot Rap Songs chart and #32 on the Billboard Hot 100.
Built around a breakbeat from the Incredible Bongo Band’s “Apache,” “Made You Look” established Nas' direction following his feud with Jay-Z, showing that he could continue his momentum following the release of his critically acclaimed album Stillmatic in 2001. Nas was joined by Jadakiss and Ludacris on the official remix.

Title: Too Much!
About:
“Too Much” finds Drake reconnecting with Sampha to create one of the most introspective and personal songs of his career to date.
Drake details his anxiety about reaching his goal of being the best rapper in the game and brings fans into his home by discussing family issues he’s currently dealing with.

Song: Text Me Merry Christmas (Feat. Kristen Bell)
Title: Text Me Merry Christmas!
Christmas Without You
About:
“Christmas Without You” is the festive promotional single, released through Atlantic Records, in preparation for the Christmas season. The song debuted on Spotify Top 200 at position #197.
Mike Wass, a writer for Idolator described the song as a “feel-good festive anthem” with a “loved-up chorus” and later declaring it as one of the best modern Christmas anthems.
Jerrett Franklin, a writer for Nashville Music Reviews, states “she even manages to hit a whistle note, which is very Mariah Carey-esk”

Title: Santa’s Coming for Us!
About:
Sia gets in the Christmas spirit on “Santa’s Coming For Us,” the lead single off her first Christmas LP, Everyday Is Christmas. The track marks Sia’s first release under her Atlantic Records deal.
The music video stars different actors from, among other things, IT, Stranger Things and Gossip Girl.
The song hit #1 on the US Adult Contemporary Radio Charts.

Title: Santa, Can’t You Hear Me
About: The Voice coaches Ariana Grande and Kelly Clarkson sing of not wanting any of the Christmas festivities because they didn’t get what they wanted, and they beg for Santa to give them what they wanted because otherwise they don’t see a point in Christmas.

Title: Like It’s Christmas!
About:
“LIke It’s Christmas” is obviously a Christmas single from Jonas Brothers, which was released on November 8, 2019. The instrumental consists of bells with upbeat drums, and a repeating acoustic and electric guitar pattern.
On November 1, 2019, exactly a week before the song’s release, the band replied to a fan page account’s tweet, which revealed the artwork of the song, which only showed the brothers' faces. Three days later, on November 4, they announced the song’s title and release date.
A lyric video was published to the band’s YouTube channel on December 6, 2019, which was nineteen days before Christmas.

Title: Mistletoe
About:
This is the lead single from pop sensation Justin Bieber’s second studio album, Under the Mistletoe.
After the release of the 2015 album Purpose, the single was given an alternate cover.

Song: Oh Santa! (Feat. Ariana Grande, Jennifer Hudson)
Title: Oh Santa! (Remix)!
About:
“Oh Santa!” is a holiday collaboration between Mariah Carey, Ariana Grande, and Jennifer Hudson and a remix of Carey’s 2010 single of the same name.
The song premiered the same day as the Apple TV+ original holiday program, Mariah Carey’s Magical Christmas Special, which the song is featured in.

Title: come out and play
About:
“come out and play” is Eilish’s fifth release since the release of her EP, don’t smile at me, leading up to her debut album WHEN WE ALL FALL ASLEEP, WHERE DO WE GO?, released on March 29, 2019.
The track will also be included in the Japanese Import version of Eilish’s debut album.
The song was written for Apple’s 2018 holiday ad campaign, “Holiday – Share Your Gifts”, as shared by Eilish’s brother and producer Finneas in a Tweet:
Billie and I got sent an incredibly early version of this commercial when they were still putting it together and we wrote this song while watching it. Beyond honored to be a part of this wonderful piece
The advertisement features a young woman who is creative but is too afraid to share her creativity to the world. This is not the first time Eilish has partnered with Apple, as she was one of Apple’s Up Next artists in 2017.
To launch the ad, Eilish performed the original track in an Apple Store in Santa Monica, California, together with her brother Finneas.
The track, which was not anticipated by fans, unlike some of the singer’s latest releases, was first teased when Eilish’s team took to Twitter to announce she would be taking over Zane Lowe’s World Record feature on Apple Music’s Beats 1 radio station on Tuesday 20th November, 2018.

Title: Candy Cane Lane
About:
“Candy Cane Lane” is the second song of Sia’s all-original seasonal LP Everyday is Christmas. Lior Molcho, director of the claymation for Sia’s holiday album commented:
The music was incredibly inspiring, as I listened to the songs, vintage Christmas movies like Rudolph The Red-Nosed Reindeer kept popping in my head.

Title: White Christmas (1947 Re-Recording)!
About:
“White Christmas” is an Irving Berlin song reminiscing about an old-fashioned Christmas setting. According to the Guinness Book of World Records, the version sung by Bing Crosby is the best-selling single of all time, with estimated sales in excess of 50 million copies worldwide.
Accounts vary as to when and where Berlin wrote the song. One story is that he wrote it in 1940, in warm La Quinta, California, while staying at the La Quinta Hotel, a frequent Hollywood retreat also favored by writer-producer Frank Capra, although the Arizona Biltmore also claims the song was written there. He often stayed up all night writing — he told his secretary, “Grab your pen and take down this song. I just wrote the best song I’ve ever written — heck, I just wrote the best song that anybody’s ever written!”
This hit song also made an appearance in Berlin’s ever-popular Christmas movie, “Holiday Inn” where it was awarded the 1943 Academy Award for Best Original Song.

Title: Wish You Were Here!
About:
“Wish You Were Here” is the title track on Pink Floyd’s 1975 album. The song’s lyrics encompass writer Roger Waters‘ feelings of alienation from other people, drawing particular inspiration from his old friend Syd Barrett, the founder of Pink Floyd who left the band due to mental health issues. The song forms part of a concept album focusing on absence and disenchantment with the music industry. The song “Shine on you Crazy Diamond (Parts 1-9)” was already an homage to Syd, while this one also grieves his absence. The other two songs on the album (“Welcome to the Machine” and “Have a Cigar”) express the band’s newfound distaste for the pressures of the music industry, pressures that they feel helped cause Syd to crack, thus tying the album together as a coherent piece. David Gilmour and Roger Waters collaborated to write the music.
This was a rare case of the Pink Floyd primary songwriters Roger Waters and David Gilmour mutually collaborating on a song – they rarely wrote together. Gilmour had the opening riff written and was playing it in the studio at a fast pace when Roger Waters heard it and asked him to play it slower. The song built from there, with the pair writing the music for the chorus and verses together, and Waters adding the lyrics.
The song reflected the feeling of the band while they were recording the album. Waters felt they were not putting a full effort into the recording sessions.
When this song starts, it sounds like it is coming from an AM radio somewhere in the distance. It represents the distance between the listener and the music.
At 42 seconds, you can also hear Richard Wright cough. When he heard the song later, it inspired him to quit smoking.
While not a single upon release, it earned this status with a version recorded on the live album Pulse, where the cover depicted two lost souls staring into fish bowls.

Title: We Wish You a Merry Christmas!
Christmas Tree Farm
About:
“Christmas Tree Farm” is a Christmas single by American singer-songwriter Taylor Swift. The song was first announced on Good Morning America on December 5, 2019. It was later confirmed by Swift’s marketing team, Taylor Nation, that the song was titled “Christmas Tree Farm.”
It is Taylor’s first Christmas-themed music since her EP, The Taylor Swift Holiday Collection, which was released back in October 2007.
In a video published on her social media platforms, Taylor herself revealed that the song was written on the weekend before its release.
Christmas tree farms played a significant role in Taylor’s “magical childhood.” Back in March 2009, she spoke to Rolling Stone about her unusual upbringing:
We all had jobs [in the farm]. Mine was picking the praying mantis pods off of the trees, collecting them so that the bugs wouldn’t hatch inside people’s houses.

Title: My Only Wish (This Year)
About: “My Only Wish (This Year)” is a Christmas song Britney recorded back in 2000. It was released originally in the Christmas compilation album Platinum Christmas.

Title: Cozy Little Christmas
About:
“Cozy Little Christmas” is Katy Perry’s second Christmas song after “Every Day is a Holiday”, which was released as promotion for H&M in 2015. Perry wrote the song about spending Christmas with her family in Copenhagen and the lyrics talk about not needing gifts as love is more important.
The song was released as an Amazon Music exclusive in 2018, with the song intended to be released to all platforms in the near future, which happened in 2019, when the song was released digitally.

Title: Merry Christmas Everyone!
About: One of many perennial Christmas classics in the UK, “Merry Christmas Everyone” actually had its original 1984 release delayed by 12 months in order to avoid clashing with “Do They Know It’s Christmas?”, which Shakin’ Stevens contributed to.

Song: All I Want For Christmas Is You (SuperFestive!)
Title: All I Want for Christmas Is You!
About:
“All I Want For Christmas Is You” is a holiday classic, and an uptempo love song. Its narrator declares she doesn’t care about Christmas presents or lights; the best holiday present she can have is to be with the one she loves.
Released in 1994 as the lead single from Mariah Carey’s first holiday album and fourth studio album, Merry Christmas, the song has since become the Christmas standard. It has sold more than 14 million copies globally since its debut, making it one of the best-selling singles ever.
Over time, countless artists have covered the song, including Miley Cyrus, John Mayer, Shania Twain, Ariana Grande, Fifth Harmony, Demi Lovato, Cee Lo Green, Lady A; even Kylie Minogue featuring Mumford and Sons.
Perhaps the most famous cover artist of the song is…Mariah herself. In 2011, Carey re-recorded the song as a duet with Justin Bieber. A year later in 2012, Carey recorded a fun version with classroom instruments and children for The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon. In 2019, she even uploaded a new “Make My Wish Come True” version of the track to her YouTube channel.
The song’s influence has extended beyond music, too. In 2015, Carey released a book with the same title as her holiday staple, acknowledging the song’s longevity:
When I wrote ‘All I Want For Christmas Is You’ it was my dream for it to become a classic Christmas song. I am so proud of the song’s impact as it continues to create memories for fans each year. I am thrilled to be able to bring the story of the song to new generations of families with the picture book.
She also released a movie called Mariah Carey’s All I Want For Christmas Is You in 2017.
The song’s legacy remains unparalleled. It charts every holiday season since its original release, and is estimated to have earned Carey $60 million in royalties. The song is also referred to as “the biggest Christmas song of all time”, and was named the #1 holiday song of all time by Billboard. The song’s immense popularity has earned Mariah the title ‘Queen of Christmas.’ In 2017, this was a title she wasn’t exactly sure about:
I don’t accept that name because I feel like it’s [too much]. I humbly thank them and I do have an extraordinary love for the holiday season, and it is the best time of the year.
However, in 2022, she tried to trademark the title, but was unsuccessful in doing so.
After 25 years, the track finally reached No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2019, becoming Mariah’s 19th song to do so and making the singer the solo artist with most Billboard Hot 100 chart-toppers. It also sets a new record of the longest run to No. 1 on the chart, surpassing Elton John’s re-recording of “Candle in the Wind.” It has since returned to the top of the charts in 2019, 2020, 2021, and 2022, making Mariah the first artist to ever have a number one song in four different decades.

Title: Our 25th Birthday!
About:
The name is a continuance of Dave’s birthday series. Predating versions are “My 19th Birthday” and “My 24th Birthday”.
Dave’s 25th birthday is the 5th of June; the day this song dropped, and Cench’s was the day before it. The EP dropped exactly as one ended and the other began. They combine this fact into the name of this song.

Title: Warm on a Christmas Night
About:
Warm on a Christmas Night is an iterative rendition of the famous song Warm on a Cold Night by Honne released on September 4th, 2014. This song features a violins, piano, acapella vocals of Andy Clutterbuck and some cheeky jingle bells in the chorus
Asides from that, it includes some slight changes to suit the Christmas theme like replacing reindeer instead of rabbit and “Cold” with “Christmas”
The original song has plenty of replay value including a remix featuring Amine.
Merry Christmas Honne :)

Song: All I Want For Christmas Is You

Title: With You This Christmas
About:
Adding to Why Don’t We’s evergrowing Christmas saga, is “With You This Christmas,” released in November 2019. It follows 2016’s “You and Me at Christmas” and their 2017 EP A Why Don’t We Christmas as the band’s third segment of a Christmas release.
Whilst “With You This Christmas” is about the December holiday, band member Corbyn Besson told fans on Twitter that the song draws inspiration from the band’s love for Thanksgiving.
The song marks a big first in the band’s career as all of the band wrote the lyrics and member Daniel Seavey is the sole producer on the song. It was written during the band’s Europe 8 Letters Tour in October 2019.

Song: You Make It Feel Like Christmas (Feat. Blake Shelton)
Title: You Make It Feel Like Christmas!
About:
The title track of Gwen Stefani’s christmas album You Make It Feel Like Christmas is a happy love song that features Gwen Stefani’s boyfriend Blake Shelton.
Stefani told Billboard how after releasing her personal 2016 album This Is What the Truth Feels Like, that she wanted to put out a Christmas album.
It was really a whim. The way it works is you have to get it done really early, and I had no idea that I would be able to write so quickly, because I’m such a slow writer. It just came right out. I did six songs in three sessions."

Title: Loneliest Time of Year!
About: I love how this song is all about people who can’t celebrate Christmas with family’s or friends because there either homeless or cant afford things. we should know there out there.

Title: Christmas Lights
About:
Marked by a somber mood, Coldplay’s anthem for Christmas is one that metaphorizes the lights and decorations of the festive season as beacons of warmth and hope for those feeling left in the cold during Christmas. The song follows a first-person perspective of a person mourning the verbally violent breakup of a relationship on Christmas Eve, with the heartening spirit of the holidays to guide him and others to a happier and merrier Christmas, as it should be.
Originally released in December 2010 as a surprise, straight from the recording sessions that would spawn the technicolor marvel known as Mylo Xyloto, “Christmas Lights”, for its festive themes, has been a recurring staple of Christmas celebrations for Coldplay fans each December.

Song: Baby It's Cold Outside (with Michael Bublé)
Title: Baby, It’s Cold Outside!
About:
“Baby, It’s Cold Outside” was originally written by Frank Loesser in 1944 and has subsequently been covered countless times during the festive period, perhaps most famously by Dean Martin and Marilyn Maxwell in 1959.
In the late 2010s, the track has faced backlash over its forceful nature towards the female protagonist, largely instigated by an American radio station banning the song in support of the #MeToo movement.

Title: Kiss You This Christmas
About:
“Kiss You This Christmas” is the lead single from A Why Don’t We Christmas – EP.
The song was released on November 21st in 2017, with the EP dropping just two days afterwards, and the track’s official music video dropping another two days later on November 25th.

Title: My Only Wish (This Year)!
About: “My Only Wish (This Year)” is a Christmas song Britney recorded back in 2000. It was released originally in the Christmas compilation album Platinum Christmas.

Title: Naughty List
About: “Naughty List” marks Liam and Dixie´s first collaboration. The song was released on October 30th. Many fans were disappointed when he announced that Dixie was featured on the song. This is Liam’s second Christmas song, after All I Want (For Christmas) in 2019.

Title: I Want That
About: “i want that ” is a song from the album heat that is made by G-idle a popular global girl group

Title: Here With Me
About:
“Here With Me” is about wanting to spend your life with someone you love whilst doing everything you can to keep them them around. It sheds light on the subject of spending time apart and it works as a comfort song because the main point through the song is that the person you love is there with you.
It gained over 90,000 thousand On Tik Tok and on 56,000 thousand SoundCloud within the first few weeks.
It is also one of d4vd’s most popular songs of all time, with it having over 30 Million across all platforms as of November 2022.

Title: vampire
About:
“vampire” doubles as the lead single from Olivia Rodrigo’s sophomore album, GUTS, as well as her comeback single following the release of her debut album, SOUR, in 2021. A small number of lyrics for the track were steadily released on her website, merchandise, and through her social media prior to the song’s full release.
Rodrigo’s lyrics describe a boy that took advantage of, lied to, and ultimately used her. The references to a vampire in the song’s title, lyrics, and cover art are used as a metaphor to further illustrate this person who sucks the life out of the narrator, leaving her feeling powerless and drained. The song touches upon the themes of vulnerability, self-discovery, and the importance of liberating oneself from toxic relationship dynamics.

Title: Overdrive
About:
“Overdrive” was found on the music streaming application Trebel by fans in July 2020, 7 months before the song’s release.
On February 11th, 2021, Conan uploaded an unknown countdown to his website, and a link to pre-save unknown music. The next day, he released a teaser on his social media accounts for a new song coming the next week.
The song’s name was revealed in another teaser two days before its release.
Lyrically, the song is about living in the moment, and experiencing love without any worries about what will happen next.

Title: Body (Remix)!
About:
This is the remix to Russ & Tion Wayne’s viral hit “Body” featuring a star studded list of rappers including ArrDee, 3x3’s ZT & E1, Darkoo, Bugzy Malone, Russ' blood brother Buni and New York’s Fivio Foreign—the only American rapper on this track.
The track sees the 9 artists trading bars about their expensive lifestyles, girls and taking shots at their opps—with the various 3x3 members dissing sworn rivals OFB several times.
This is the second time Russ & Tion have released a remix to their songs following April 2019’s “Keisha & Becky (Remix)” that went viral—arguably a bigger hit than the original—thanks to a new verse by then up-and-coming rapper Aitch.

Title: Welcome to the Black Parade!
About:
This song is on My Chemical Romance’s third studio album, entitled The Black Parade. The song, as well as the rest of the album, follows The Patient, a man who is dying, through death and beyond.
MCR frontman Gerard Way said in an interview:
I’d like to think that when you die death comes for you however you want, and I feel that it’s your strongest memory—either from childhood or adulthood. […]
For this particular character […] called ‘The Patient,’ his strongest memory is of being a child and his father is taking him to this parade.
So when death comes to him, it comes in the form of a Black Parade.

Title: Señorita
About:
“Señorita” is a steamy track about an ambiguous relationship. The song marks the second collaboration between Shawn Mendes and Camila Cabello — they previously collaborated on November 2015’s “I Know What You Did Last Summer.”
Cabello and Mendes first revealed that they were working on new music together in a December 2018 Instagram post of them together backstage at iHeartRadio JingleBall and commenting “IKWYDLS part 2 ?” and “IKWYDLS PART 2!!!” respectively. According to Camila, Shawn came up with an idea for this song in the end of 2018, but it took some time before they could work on it.

Title: Bad Boy
About:
On “Bad Boy,“ Juice WRLD and Young Thug reunite for an upbeat and quick-moving track in which the pair liken themselves to 1995 comedy Bad Boys‘ main cast, Will Smith and Martin Lawrence, boasting about their reckless lifestyles while touching on their items and wealth.
An early version of the track was originally leaked on January 4, 2020, and followed with the leak of this version on July 7, 2020 with a new and updated Juice WRLD verse.
Additionally, the track was first previewed in behind the scenes footage from the Cole Bennett directed music video for the track. The video was originally intended to release alongside the track on November 16, 2020, but both the track and video’s release were delayed for unknown reasons.
Despite the complications, on December 19, 2020, Lyrical Lemonade directly mentioned in a tweet the two main actors of Bad Boy and accompanied it with a picture of Juice and Thugger, hinting at an official release in the future. Cole Bennett retweeted the Lyrical Lemonade tweet later the same day, and the next day announced that he “has good news“ and accompanied it once again with a picture of Juice and Thugger.
On December 25, 2020, Cole Bennett once again took to his Twitter to confirm the title of the track alongside announcing the release date of January 15, 2021 with a six-second teaser video:

Title: HandClap
About:
As a neo soul/indie pop song, it had peaked at #5 on the US Alternative Songs Billboard.
Lead singer of Fitz and The Tantrums, Fitz, claims that HandClap is “a primal tale of love and lust, a call to arms in the late hours of the night."Fitz has also stated "it’s the X-rated version of ‘Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind’ happening on a dance floor.”

Title: (If You Want Love) Lower Your Expectations!
About: This song is about how people tend to make a list of idealistic, contradictory and sometimes unachievable traits to seek out in a romantic partner, and then later wonder why they haven’t found that person yet. Bo goes on to say that you cannot expect someone to be perfect in every way otherwise it will be impossible to be happy with anyone. Humans are flawed beings yet all of them deserve to love and to feel loved, therefore you must love despite flaws people may have.

Title: Call Me Maybe
About:
“Call Me Maybe” is a song by Canadian pop icon Carly Rae Jepsen. It initially served as the lead single for her original second studio album Curiosity, which was cut down to a 6 track EP just days before its release. After the immense success this song had, managing to top the Billboard Hot 100 chart the week of June 23rd, 2012; as well as being named “Song of the Summer” for that year by several publications, she embarked on a quick journey to make her second full-length studio album (and first worldwide release) Kiss, for which Call Me Maybe also served as the lead single.
Lyrically, the song is about a crush. The writing is straightforward and visual, with lines such as “Ripped jeans, skin was showing”. The simplicity of the lyrics, as well as their relatability, makes the song easy to latch on to and enjoy.
Sonically, the song is mainly held together by a four-on-the-floor style drum pattern, as well as string sounds that shine from the very start of the track until the end, where they slow down and get pitched to bring the energy to a stop. The song has been described as being “Bubblegum Pop”, referring to it’s light and sugary feel.

Title: Mad at Disney
About:
On “Mad at Disney”, Salem changes the ending of her life story, from the usual “And they lived happily ever after!” to “And she lived forever deceived!”, that says, Disney taught her to dream of true love, and growing up she realized that in real life it’s a lie.
The song went viral on Tik-Tok, where they made videos of themselves dressed as Disney princesses, which so far has 1.7 million videos on the platform.

Title: If We Ever Broke Up
About: Mae Stephens Wrote the song If We Ever Broke Up to tell the story of Stephens' telling her ex-boyfriend’s father how his son treated her throughout their relationship

Title: Eyes Closed
About:
“Eyes Closed” is about being in a new relationship but still having feelings for your ex.
The Weeknd contributed to the song’s melody, as Halsey clarified in a direct message to a fan. “Eyes Closed” debuted on Beats 1 radio on May 4, 2017 and is Halsey’s second single from hopeless fountain kingdom.
On May 19th, Halsey returned to her roots of Room 93 by releasing a stripped version of her track “Eyes Closed” and promoted the song on her Twitter shortly after.
On June 1st, Halsey performed the whole hopeless fountain kingdom album for VEVO: Presents. Eyes Closed is found below:

Title: Dance The Night
About:
“Dance The Night” is an upbeat disco dance track by Dua Lipa. The song served as the lead single for Greta Gerwig’s Barbie soundtrack and was released on May 26, 2023 ahead of the film’s release in July. Dua Lipa made her debut acting performance in the film as Mermaid Barbie.
The song was used for a scene in the movie during Barbie’s blowout party in which all the Barbies and Kens break into choreographed dance.

Title: pick up the phone
About:
“Pick Up the Phone” details Travis, Thug and Quavo’s unfortunate situation. While they all want to be faithful and stay committed to this girl, she won’t show her love in return, refusing to pick up the phone—even while she’s not busy.
“Pick Up the Phone” is a bonus track off Young Thug’s mixtape, Jeffery, as well as the 11th track off Travis Scott’s Birds In The Trap Sing McKnight.
This song was originally a collaboration between Young Thug and Quavo, with Starrah singing the hook. Travis was in the studio when Young Thug recorded the song, and he wrote the bridge for Young Thug (early versions of this song exist where Travis sang the bridge instead).
Travis then began playing his altered version at clubs and called it his own single without notifying Young Thug. Travis teased the song for months before he finally leaked it on his own SoundCloud because the labels would not release it. An agreement was made that the song would be released on iTunes later that day as a joint single between Thug and Travis.
“Pick Up the Phone” and “goosebumps” are certified platinum by the RIAA as of January 11, 2017.

Title: I’m Yours!
About:
Jason Mraz’s most-successful chart hit – throughout 2008 and 2009, it topped singles charts in Israel, New Zealand, Norway and Sweden while reaching top 5 in Australia, Belgium, Canada, Denmark, Italy, the Netherlands and Switzerland. In Mraz’s native United States, it topped several airplay charts but only hit a peak of #6 on the Billboard Hot 100, nevertheless it proved its longevity on the chart with a total of 76 weeks, the Hot 100’s longest-consecutive chart run at the time.
“I’m Yours” has a simple reggae-like sound arranged in the very popular I-V-vi-IV chord progression.

Title: My Stupid Heart!
About: The song “My Stupid Heart” by Walk off the Earth is about a couple who keeps going back to each other despite the pain they have caused each other. The lyrics describe the internal struggle of both parties – they know they should let go and move on, but their hearts keep leading them back to each other.

Title: Barbie Dreams!
About:
On “Barbie Dreams,” Nicki pays homage to The Notorious B.I.G., delivering her take on his classic 1994 track “Just Playing (Dreams).”
Unlike Biggie, who rapped about his attraction to popular female R&B singers, Nicki decides to roast prominent male artists and athletes. It’s all in good fun, though, as most of the rappers Nicki puts down are friends and collaborators. A few of them are even featured on the album Queen, including Lil Wayne and Swae Lee.
Nicki previously used the “Just Playing (Dreams)” beat on her 2007 track “Dreams” from her mixtape, Playtime Is Over.
In an August 2018 episode of her Beats 1 Queen Radio show with Zane Lowe, Nicki broke down the track, saying:
Reading the reaction to ‘Barbie Dreams’ is hilarious. Everyone is saying how disrespectful [the song] is and that’s why I fucking love it. ‘Barbie Dreams’ is gonna go down in history. Shout outs to B.I.G. you are still inspiring rappers every day. This man is still inspiring me every day. He’s one of the first rappers who had a sense of humor. I love dope writers who can write bars and make you laugh. Eminem [after that] too. I think [humor] shows a person’s IQ and [wittiness].

Song: Angel Pt. 2 (Feat. JVKE, Charlie Puth, Muni Long)
Title: Angel Pt. 2!
About:
The song “Angel Pt. 2” by Jimin (지민) featuring Charlie Puth, JVKE, and Muni Long explores the themes of self-destructive behavior, letting go of someone for their own good, and accepting one’s flaws in relationships.
The chorus emphasizes the protagonist’s warning to an angelic figure not to get too close to them. The line “I’ll pull you down eventually” suggests that the protagonist believes their own struggles and negative tendencies will ultimately have a negative impact on the angel. The mention of “losing wings” symbolizes losing innocence or purity, implying that the protagonist’s influence can tarnish something beautiful.
In JVKE’s verse, he reflects on moments of strain in the relationship and hints at personal issues that have affected their behavior. He admits to saying hurtful things and acknowledges that forgetting the past is necessary to move on. The repetition of “no conversation” suggests a desire to avoid further complications.
Muni Long takes over the chorus, reinforcing the idea that people like the protagonist have a tendency to break beautiful things, potentially causing damage to the angelic figure.
Charlie Puth’s verse brings a sense of acceptance and resignation. He advises being cautious when moving on, highlighting the potential darkness one might encounter. He also acknowledges that the relationship wasn’t meant to last forever and believes that someone else will love the angel better. The line “it’s time to let me go” indicates a realization that holding on is not healthy for either party involved.
The pre-chorus, performed by JVKE and Muni Long, suggests that the protagonist understands their imperfections and doesn’t want to disappoint or hurt the angel. They encourage the angel to leave and find happiness elsewhere, expressing a desire for the angel’s well-being and acknowledging that they themselves are not perfect.
Overall, “Angel Pt. 2” delves into the complexities of relationships, exploring the self-awareness of one’s flaws and the need to let go in order to protect someone from potential harm. It demonstrates a bittersweet mixture of love, acceptance, and the recognition of personal limitations.

Title: 24K Magic
About:
“24K Magic” is a braggadocious party anthem described by Bruno Mars as an “invitation to the party” of his third album of the same name. It combines modern-referencing lyrics (like “#blessed”) with 80s-style production helmed by Shampoo Press & Curl and The Stereotypes.
The official video for the track dropped October 7, 2016. It’s the perfect complement to the party anthem, an opulent dance-fest, with Mars joined by an incredible looking squad living it up in Los Angeles and Las Vegas.
The song won the Grammy for “Record of the Year” at the 2018 Grammys held on January 28, 2018.

Title: Marvin Gaye!
About:
Charlie Puth uses ‘Marvin Gaye’ as a euphemism for sex in this song, featuring his friend Meghan Trainor.
While performing the track at the 2015 American Music Awards, the two famously locked lips onstage sparking many relationship rumors.

Title: Lilith!
About:
Lilith, the fourth track off Halsey’s fourth album If I Can’t Have Love, I Want Power. Halsey confirmed that “Lilith” was the first song written for the album in a Youtube interview with the album’s producers on September 1st, 2021.
This track was first hinted at when the @iichliwp Instagram account posted an oil painting by Dante Gabriel Rossetti titled Lady Lilith.
In the Bible, Lilith is also translated as a type of owl, explaining the owl present in the visual album.

Title: Freaky Friday!
About:
“Freaky Friday” is a song that involves a crossover between Lil Dicky and Chris Brown.
The first verse is Lil Dicky speaking as Chris Brown and the second verse is Chris Brown living as Lil Dicky. Both artists choose different things to take advantage of while living the other person’s life.
The song is a nod to the movie Freaky Friday that was released in 1976 and remade in 2003. The movie created this premise where a girl and her mother switch places in order to experience each other’s lives.
Lil Dicky released the music video on his 30th birthday, which gave him high hopes for its success.

Title: Hold On Tight
About: “Hold On Tight” is a song recorded by South Korean girl group aespa. It was released on March 30, 2023 as part of the soundtrack of Jon S. Baird’s 2023 biographical drama film, Tetris. The track samples two nineteenth-century Russian folk songs more commonly known as themes from the Tetris games – “Kalinka”, written by Ivan Larionov, who is credited on the track due to the sample, and “Korobeiniki”. It was written by Aaron Hibell, Boy Matthews, Dave LaBrel and Georgia Ku and produced by Hibell.

Title: You Know Me Too Well!
About:
This song explores the sexually-driven relationship between the narrator and his partner and his struggle to overcome his dangerous, impulsive and lustful attraction. The narrator reveals a sense of loneliness which is filled or, at least, numbed by the sexual nature of this relationship.
The narrator is aware of the fact the the two are incompatible in every way other than sexually and that, although the relationship is not healthy, he continues to find solace in it and becomes hooked – like a drug.

Title: Good Mourning!
About:
“Good Mourning” is a short interlude at the halfway point of Halsey’s sophomore album, hopeless fountain kingdom. It suggests a change within the narrative of the album, referencing to the sun coming up on the horizon bringing some new feelings.
The singer muses about Luna (Halsey’s character in hopeless fountain kingdom). Halsey hinted that the song is from the perspective of the House of Aureum and their fear over her relationship with Solis Angelus, or ‘White Nite’.
The song is partially spoken which was confirmed by Halsey confirmed via Twitter to be her 12 year old brother, Dante, with the ending being sung with resonating chorus.

Title: How Deep Is Your Love!
About:
“How Deep Is Your Love” is a deep house dance track featuring uncredited vocals by Ina Wroldsen.
Harris previewed the song on June 21st 2015, during the Electric Daisy Carnival in Las Vegas, a few weeks before its eventual release as a stand-alone single. When asked why the song wasn’t part of a larger project, Harris told KISS-FM he “wanted to have a song out in the summer.”
“How Deep” was a massive international hit, charting top ten in over 20 countries and reaching #1 in 10 countries.
The surreal, dream-like music video, directed by Emil Nava, features Sports Illustrated swimsuit model (and Kendall Jenner bestie) Gigi Hadid.

Title: Paper Planes!
About:
This penultimate song on Kala marries a deceptively airy beat with a song about tourist-murdering passport-counterfeiting thieves. The song can be read as the clichéd story of immigrants or the owner of an arms industry. The title subtly refers to transit visas (from Latin, charta visa means “paper that has been seen”).
It samples the 1982 Clash song “Straight to Hell”.
The song climbed into to the Top 10 of the US Hot 100 in mid-2008 after being featured in the trailer for the movie Pineapple Express, and then later used in the soundtrack for 2008 film Slumdog Millionaire.

Title: Anti-Hero
About:
Described by Taylor herself as “one of my favorite songs that I have ever written”, “Anti-Hero” is the scintillating lead single from Midnights, her tenth studio album. Largely serving as a parallel to 2019’s “The Archer”, the song shows a darker self-perception from Swift than usual, and goes into detail about what she feels are her personal failures.
The track’s title and track number were revealed in the sixth video of her TikTok game Midnight Mayhem With Me on October 2nd, 2022. The next day, Swift released a ‘Behind The Song’ video to discuss the meaning and purpose behind the track, saying she “struggle[s] a lot with the idea that my life has become unmanageably sized", and described the song as a “guided tour" of the things she hates about herself.
On Halloween night of 2022, the song was announced to have debuted at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100. Marking her ninth number one single and fifth number one debut, “Anti-Hero” joined nine other songs from Midnights in constructing the entire top 10 – the first time ever in Billboard history.

Title: How Do I Say Goodbye
About: “How Do I Say Goodbye” is a touching song in which, as explained by Dean Lewis, the vocalist is mourning his dying father, which honestly is not a particular theme we come across often. But as presented in this case, the addressee, i.e. Dean’s dying father, was someone who had a constant presence throughout his entire life His father meant so much to him that the prospect of the addressee passing is something which the singer cannot conceptualize. And such is this song’s thesis sentiment, i.e. Dean’s emotions being such that he can’t even conceive ‘how to say goodbye’ to his dad. Moreover, he is using the opportunity to let him know, most frankly put, that he doesn’t want him to die. And yes, by the time all is said and done, even if not stated forthrightly, it can be ascertained that the addressee is indeed the vocalist’s father. Accordingly Dean’s mom, who is still around, also plays a role in it all. And one thing the singer does assure his passing father of is that he will take care of her in his absence. Of course no matter what, Dean is never going to forget his dad. How can he, when he beholds his features even while looking in the mirror? So again, if nothing else, this song is unique in that we have a vocalist identifiable mourning his pops.

Title: Shivers
About:
“Shivers” is the third single released from Ed Sheeran’s fourth solo studio album, = (Equals).
Sheeran first previewed the song by playing a snippet during his August 19 Instagram livestream. The track was officially announced by Sheeran just a few weeks later on September 2, one week before it and the music video were released.

Song: I'm Not Here To Make Friends
Title: I’m Not Here To Make Friends!
About:
“I’m Not Here To Make Friends”, together with “Unholy”, is perhaps a stand-out track on Sam Smith’s Gloria album. Sam performed it flawlessly on the BBC 1 Graham Norton Show on 27th January 2023. The song’s Calvin Harris-produced beat is insistent, another track on the album for dancing.
Sam has often commented on the negative aspects of fame; how difficult it is to build a relationship with a potential lover, when so many barriers, not least being in the public eye and year-long world-wide tours, have to be overcome.

Title: Talking to Myself!
About:
First premiered at the Maximus Festival in Buenos Aires, Argentina on the 6th of May 2017, Talking To Myself is the third track off One More Light.
This song is primarily aimed at Chester from his wife Talinda’s point of view. The song relates the thoughts of a loved one while they watch their other half battle his (or her) inner struggles and yet are helpless to do anything.
Musically, the song has been described as being reminiscent of The Killers.
The official music video was released on July 20th, 2017. Later that day, lead singer Chester Bennington was found dead by apparent suicide.

Title: World’s Smallest Violin!
About: “World’s Smallest Violin” was released as the 11th track in AJR’s album, “OK ORCHESTRA” (released March 26th 2021)

Title: Before You Go!
About:
“Before You Go” is about the aftermath of someone close committing suicide, including feeling like you are responsible for their death. Throughout the track, Lewis repeatedly questions whether he could have done something to prevent it.
Lewis teased the track by posting a snippet on his social media accounts one day before its release.

Title: Vicious!
About:
On “Vicious,” Sabrina sings in an angry way about this vicious person who manipulated her saying that he loved her. Carpenter emphasizes in each line how this person is different from how everyone believes and how he deceived her by saying that he loved her and left without remorse.
Prior to its release, Carpenter teased the song by posting a pre-save link and sharing a snippet of the song on TikTok. She revealed the track title and release date via a Spotify playlist.

Title: forget me too!
About:
Searching for closure in past relationships, Machine Gun Kelly asks for a fresh start on “forget me too.”
A snippet of the song was first shared in January of 2020 by producer and blink-182 drummer Travis Barker. The release of the Halsey-assisted track arrives shortly after MGK confirmed his relationship with actress Megan Fox. Referenced on “banyan tree (interlude),” the two celebrated their romance with personalized tattoos:
Like, you just got my initials tattooed on you
I got your nickname tattooed on me
The pop-punk anthem details MGK’s desire for stable love over temporary affection.

Title: don’t miss me!
About:
don’t miss me is the lead single off Rosinkranz’s album, CR1*. It explores committment issues on the cusp of a potential new relationship. Claire has said that this song is about her own issues committing to significant others.
don’t miss me has also inspired a tiktok trend where people upload photos of themselves next to the lyrics of the song.

Title: Photograph
About:
“Photograph” is the 5th radio single from x. Ed said about it that “it will be the one that will change my career path”, and hopes it will sell the album “even if the rest of the album is shit”.
The song talks about trying to preserve their love in a picture so that the romance’s beauty will remain with them forever.
Ed wrote this song about his ex girlfriend Nina Nesbitt.

Title: Circles
About:
“Circles” is the fourth single from Post Malone’s third studio album, Hollywood’s Bleeding. The track continues the heartbreak theme from “Goodbyes” as he struggles to maintain a strong relationship with this girl. Like “Goodbyes,” “Circles” fell victim to leaks before official announcements. A snippet of the track originally surfaced online in July of 2019, with the full song being leaked online later in August.
In 2018, Post released the song “Stay” in a very similar fashion—the song was debuted on April 4, 2018, during his first Bud Light Dive Bar Tour show in Nashville, Tennessee. Like “Stay,” “Circles” features a more unique instrumental compared to the majority of Post’s discography, and is sonically similar to his 2016 track, “Hollywood Dreams / Come Down.”
An official teaser was shared on August 5, 2019, and the full song was later debuted by Post during his Bud Light Dive Bar Tour performance on the same day in New York. During his performance at the Bud Light Dive Bar Tour, Post said that the track would be released the next week, leading many to believe it was supposed to be released on August 16. However, the song was not released on its original expected release date. In an Instagram post on August 28, 2019, Post announced the song would be released on August 30, 2019, alongside the song’s cover art.

Title: Viva La Vida
About:
Revered as an essential rock song of the 2000s, “Viva La Vida” tells the story of a monarch who’s lost his kingdom. In another aspect, the song is also seen as a narrative on the French Revolution given the historical and biblical references in the lyrics. The instrumental is based around a string and piano background, led by an orchestral drum beat.
The title derives from the Spanish phrase “Long Live Life,” and takes its name from Frida Kahlo’s last painting before her death. During a March 2008 interview with Rolling Stone, frontman Chris Martin explained the reason for naming the song and album in honour of Kahlo:
She went through a lot of shit, of course, and then she started a big painting in her house that said ‘Viva la Vida.’ I just loved the boldness of it.
This song debuted to massive success and acclaim, peaking at number one on both the Billboard 100 and UK Singles Chart. Coldplay received several awards for the track, including Song of the Year at the Grammy Awards in February 2009.
The chorus has musical similarities to Joe Satriani’s instrumental piece, “If I Could Fly.” Satriani sued Coldplay for plagiarism—they settled out of court. The band Creaky Boards also accused Coldplay of taking the song’s melody from their record, “The Songs I Didn’t Write,” however, Coldplay denied the allegations.
Coldplay released two music videos for this song. Hype Williams made the official video, which premiered in August 2008, depicting the band against a backdrop of Eugène Delacroix’s painting, Liberty Leading the People. The alternate music video dropped alongside the first one but was directed by Anton Corbijn and starred Chris Martin as a king.

Title: Thinking Out Loud
About:
“Thinking Out Loud” is Sheeran’s smash hit about lifelong love. Though he was just 23 when he wrote it, he imagines the love lasting well into his later years.
The message resonated with fans, and it shattered several records—it was the first song to spend a full year in the UK Top 40, and the first song to reach 500 million streams on Spotify (the service also deemed it the top “first dance” song in the world).
Sheeran wrote the song about his then-girlfriend Athina Andrelos. He calls it the only happy song on the album x, on which he strived for a more serious vibe, working extensively with Rick Rubin.
Sheeran co-wrote “Thinking Out Loud” with Welsh folk singer Amy Wadge, and his friend and long-time collaborator Jake Gosling produced the track. Chris Leonard engineered it, and played guitar.
The bass and drums are very close to Marvin Gaye’s “Let’s Get It On"—Sheeran has even mixed a little of the "Let’s Get It On” lyrics into his life performances:

Title: Don’t Look Back in Anger!
About:
“Don’t Look Back in Anger” was released as the fourth single off of (What’s The Story) Morning Glory?, February 19th 1996. In 2006, it was included in the “Best Of”-compilation Stop The Clocks and in 2010 it was included in the singles collection Time Flies… 1994-2009.
Noel Gallagher also admits that he was under the influence of illegal substances when he wrote the song and to this day he claims he does not know what the lyrics mean.
In late May 2017, the song became a symbol for the Manchester’s spirit and resistance following the terrorist attacks during an Ariana Grande concert, when a crowd of Mancunians started singing it, at the end of a minute of silence to honour the victims of the attack.

Title: My Love Mine All Mine
About:
In “My Love Mine All Mine,” Mitski recognizes the beauty of her love and asks the Moon to capture it, for it to shine down and preserve even after her passing. It was the third single on Mitski’s seventh studio album, The Land is Inhospitable and So Are We, following “Bug Like an Angel” and the double-sided “Star b/w Heaven.”
The song garnered mass popularity through TikTok—which helped it gain over a million videos in less than two months. The track peaked at number 26 on Billboard’s Hot 100, marking Mitski’s first time in the chart.

Title: Lucky You!
About:
“Lucky You” marks the first collaboration between Eminem and Joyner Lucas, whom people have pointed out, shares many characteristics with Em in terms of rhyme and flow.
Their verses are polar opposites, a thematic structure often employed by Joyner in his songs to show opposing perspectives of the same topic (see “Ross Capicchioni” and “I’m Not Racist”).
Here, Joyner comments on how he hasn’t received any awards, although he is steadily gaining recognition. For instance, the video for his song “I’m Not Racist” went viral in 2017.
Em’s verse, on the other hand, is about how he’s at the top in terms of awards and status but is being overlooked in the current rap scene. This is a recurring theme throughout this album as well as other 2018 releases, such as the remix of “Chloraseptic.”

Title: Legends Never Die
Singer: League of Legends & Against The Current
About:
“Legends Never Die” is a song made by Riot Games in collaboration with Against the Current. It’s the anthem of Worlds 2017, a tournament where the best League of Legends teams in the world compete against each other. It takes place in China, with the finals taking place at the Beijing National Stadium.
The League of Legends champions Ashe, Garen and Lee Sin can be seen on the cover of the song. These champions also feature in the WORLDS 2017 animation.
Ashe was declared the “champion of season 7” for being played extensively both in the competitive scene and solo queue. Riot Games also created a limited edition Championship Ashe skin for this occasion, which is shown in the picture above.

Title: Dancing in the Moonlight!
About:
Keyboardist/songwriter Sherman Kelly writes on his official website that this mellow song may have had a violent origin:
On a trip to St. Croix in 1969, I was the first victim of a vicious St. Croix gang who eventually murdered 8 American tourists. At that time, I suffered multiple facial fractures and wounds and was left for dead. While I was recovering, I wrote “Dancin In The Moonlight” in which I envisioned an alternate reality, the dream of a peaceful and joyful celebration of life. The song became a huge hit and was recorded by many musicians worldwide. “Dancin In The Moonlight” continues to be popular to this day.

Title: Counting Stars
About:
The first track on OneRepublic’s third studio album Native, “Counting Stars” was inspired by lead singer Ryan Tedder’s writing sessions with Beyonce in the Hamptons. The song has sold over ten million copies in the United States alone.
The annotations on the left suggest a literal positive view of life and religion, an affirmation of care for his partner, a rejection of materialism and hope for the future.
Alternatively, it could be an ironic view of a questionable religious cult. Hence, the singer feels ‘something so right doing the wrong thing, something so wrong doing the right thing’. He’s ‘doing what he’s told’ mindlessly, without questioning. The crocodile in the music video could be a representation of the serpent. The cult leader is a dishonest bully who provokes convulsions in one woman. There are shady undertones to this lyric, depending on interpretation. A clever conundrum.

Song: The Last Of The Real Ones
Title: The Last of the Real Ones!
About:
“The Last of the Real Ones” is a love song that uses celestial imagery to illustrate the gravity of the narrator’s devotion. It is the third single and second track from Fall Out Boy’s upcoming seventh album, M A  N   I    A. The single was released one month after the band announced that the album would be delayed, and it was written the week after the announcement.
The music video is a direct continuation of “Young and Menace”, and appears to share many similarities to the music video for Kanye West’s 2007 single “Flashing Lights”. It was later revealed in an interview with The Charlotte Observer that these similarities were intentional.

Title: Stereo Hearts!
About: The Papercut Chronicles II’s lead single features Adam Levine, the lead singer of Maroon 5.

Title: Lucky Strike
About:
“Lucky Strike” is the ninth track on Troye’s sophomore album Bloom.
The title references one of the world’s best-known cigarette brands, with its distinctive red-on-green bullseye design created by famous industrial designer Raymond Loewy, who also designed the Campbell’s Soup label and the logos for Shell Oil and Coca-Cola.
Here, Sivan meets a boy who tastes just like the classic regular-sized (70 mm), non-filtered cigarette, and is getting addicted to this love affair.

Title: I Love You So
About: “I Love You So” is about falling in love with someone who isn’t ready for a serious relationship. The love interest in the song isn’t ready to be exclusive and eventually the narrative voice realizes he needs to get over her and move on even though he loves her very much.

Title: Creep
About:
According to Thom Yorke, “Creep” tells the tale of an inebriated man who tries to get the attention of a woman to whom he is attracted by following her around. In the end, he lacks the self-confidence to face her and feels he subconsciously is her.
When asked about “Creep” in 1993, Yorke said:
I have a real problem being a man in the ‘90s… Any man with any sensitivity or conscience toward the opposite sex would have a problem. To actually assert yourself in a masculine way without looking like you’re in a hard-rock band is a very difficult thing to do… It comes back to the music we write, which is not effeminate, but it’s not brutal in its arrogance. It is one of the things I’m always trying: To assert a sexual persona and on the other hand trying desperately to negate it.
Jonny Greenwood said the song was in fact a happy song about “recognizing what you are”.
Notable covers include those by The Pretenders, Carrie Manolakos, Macy Gray, Lea Michele and Dean Geyer, as part of Glee, Vega Choir, Prince, The Bluecoats Drum and Bugle Corps, and most recently, Kimberly Nichole, who performed a very strong cover of this song on the 2015 season of The Voice.
Despite, or perhaps because of, the popularity of the song, Radiohead grew to hate “Creep” and have rarely played it live since the OK Computer tour. “My Iron Lung”, written in 1994 and later released on The Bends, is about the song.

Title: Everglow
About:
The most personal and emotionally-charged song on Coldplay’s enigmatic A Head Full of Dreams, “Everglow” expresses memory and loss as an ever-lasting “glow.”
Most likely inspired by his own divorce from actress Gwyneth Paltrow, songwriter Chris Martin stated during an interview with Zane Lowe:
To me, [the song] is about — whether it’s a loved one or a situation or a friend or a relationship that’s finished, or someone’s passed away — I was thinking about, after you’ve been through the sadness of something, you also get this everglow. That’s what it’s about.

Song: Enemy (from the series Arcane League of Legends)
Title: Enemy!
About:
“Enemy” is the lead single and fifth track for League of Legends‘ inaugural animated series, Arcane. The track features vocals from Imagine Dragons and JID, the former of which previously collaborated with Riot Games Music Team on “Warriors,” a song written and recorded as the anthem for the 2014 League of Legends World Championships.
As a primary artist, “Enemy” marks the band’s third collaboration with a rapper throughout their entire history, with JID joining the likes of Kendrick Lamar and Lil Wayne.
Imagine Dragons also released a solo version of the song, which features a bridge performed by lead singer Dan Reynolds in replacement of JID’s verse.

Title: Tangled in the Great Escape!
About:
I wrote this about one of my best friends who got hooked on some pretty serious drugs. It got to the point where I was so scared that if he didn’t get cleaned up soon, I knew he was going to die. I felt so powerless because I am always on tour and there’s no real way for me to help someone like this from so far away. I flew out to Detroit to do some co-writing with my friend B-South (Brian Southall). We had done a lot of work together on our side project Isles & Glaciers, so I hit him up to see if he’d be down to make some more songs. By the time I got back to San Diego we had written 5 songs in 5 days, but this was the one that fit the album the best. I asked our friend Jason Butler from the band letlive. to sing on this song for two reasons. One being that I have a lot of respect and admiration towards him as a singer and as a person. Two being that he is straight edge, and I thought maybe he could relate to the lyrics and topic in some way. Special thanks to Jason for bringing this song to life.
-Vic Fuentes

Title: We Are Young!
About:
“We Are Young” was the lead single from Fun’s second album Some Nights. It hit #1 on the Hot 100 for 6 straight weeks (from March 17 to April 21, 2012). Lyrically, the song describes a wild night out and the necessity for good friends to be there for you – even at your worst and completely drunk.
The song was released in the fall of 2011, but a quick cover of it by Glee in December brought it to the attention of a larger audience and kick-started the song’s ultimate success. An appearance in a Super Bowl ad finally gave Fun.’s own version of the song the spotlight it deserved, with it flying to number 3 on the charts after the commercial first aired. Since its release some have also argued that “We Are Young” became not only a breakthrough for fun., but for indie pop as a mainstream genre.

Title: Maps
About:
“Maps” was released as the lead single from Maroon 5’s fifth studio album, V.. During it’s release, the song broke records, logging 1,800 spins on the radio on its first day (or 17 million people listening). After debuting at 14 on the Billboard Hot 100, it peaked at 6, becoming one of three top tens from V.
Lyrically, the song talks about searching for a past love. Although Levine’s been there for his lover, his lover hasn’t always been there for him.

Title: Natural
About:
“Natural” is Imagine Dragons‘ third single after the release of Evolve, following their collaboration with Kygo on “Born to Be Yours”; it is the lead single for their fourth studio album, Origins. The single’s artwork features the same artist from Evolve, Beeple.
According to Dan Reynolds, the song is about becoming a little ruthless and calloused with time as you realize you have to fight and stand up for yourself. This song became the anthem for 2018’s ESPN College Football season and was even featured as Amazon Music’s and Shazam’s Song of the Day.

Title: Beggin’!
About:
“Beggin'” is the funk-rock version of The Four Seasons’s 1967 homonymous soul-rock song.
This cover was presented in November 2017, during the X-Factor live show, and then it was included in Chosen EP: the song got only the 37th position in italian FIMI singles charts.
In June 2021, following the band’s Eurovision victory, the song started to gain attention, especially on TikTok: “Beggin'” reached the top position on Spotify Global Top 50 Chart in July and it was the second most streamed song in the world on Spotify in 2021’s summer.

Title: Dirt!
About: Bones is calling out all the fake streamlined rappers who rhyme about petty lies, while the verse is an ode to the gritty aspect of an underground rapper.

Title: Fix You
About:
“Fix You” is the fourth track and second single from Coldplay’s third album X&Y. It was written by Chris Martin for his then-wife, Gwyneth Paltrow, to comfort her after her father passed away.
The song has been covered numerous times, including notable renditions by the Young@Heart chorus in the award-winning 2007 documentary, as well as pop icon Sam Smith, and Korean septet BTS.

Title: Youngblood
About:
On “Youngblood,” 5SOS sing about the push and pulls of a relationship. In this song, we see 5SOS move further away from their defining sound from previous albums and see the growth and maturity in their songwriting.
“Youngblood” is the title track from 5SOS' third album released on June 15th, and was an instant grat when you pre-ordered the album on iTunes.

Title: Bohemian Rhapsody!
About:
Widely considered to be one of the greatest songs of all time, “Bohemian Rhapsody” was the first single released from Queen’s fourth studio album, A Night at the Opera. It became an international success, reaching #1 in seven countries and peaking at #9 in the United States. Seventeen years after its initial release, “Bohemian Rhapsody” re-entered the pop charts in the US, peaking at #2 after being featured in the 1992 hit movie Wayne’s World. In 2002, the song was listed at #1 in a Guinness World Records poll as Britain’s favourite single of all time—ranking higher than four Beatles tracks and “Imagine” by John Lennon.
Complex and operatic both musically and lyrically, “Bohemian Rhapsody” (like Led Zeppelin’s “Stairway to Heaven” and The Eagles‘ “Hotel California”) has attracted endless fan theories and commentary. The surviving band members have claimed that the narrative is based on the Faust legend; critics have found possible sources in opera and Freddie Mercury’s personal biography; but like any good piece of art, it’s open to interpretation.
The word “Bohemian” seems to refer to a group of artists and musicians from the 19th century, known for defying convention and living with disregard for standards; as opposed to the region of Bohemia in the Czech Republic. Meanwhile the term “rhapsody” (derived from the Greek: ῥαψῳδός or rhapsōidos for a reciter of epic poetry, or a rhapsodist) is a piece of classical music with distinct sections that are played as one movement. Rhapsodies often feature dense themes or narratives.
As of December 2018, “Bohemian Rhapsody” is the most streamed song of the 20th century.
“Bohemian Rhapsody” has got a very diverse song structure. It includes:
• Intro (0:00-0:49)
• Ballad section (0:49-2:37)
• Brian May‘s’ guitar solo (2:37-3:05)
• Operatic verse (3:05-4:07)
• Hard-rock verse (4:07-4:54)
• Coda (4:54-5:55)

Song: When Will My Life Begin? (From "Tangled" / Soundtrack Version)
Title: When Will My Life Begin?!
About:
“When Will My Life Begin?” the first song featured in the 2010 Disney movie Tangled, is the classic “I want” song, which appears in every Disney movie in which the protagonist is considered a Disney princess.
In this case, Rapunzel wants to change the boring life she leads in a tower because of her “mother” and wants to see the “floating lights” that appear every year on her birthday.

Song: How Long Will I Love You
Title: How Long Will I Love You
About:
“How Long Will I Love You” is a song by English recording artist Ellie Goulding from Halcyon Days (2013), the reissue of her second studio album, Halcyon (2012). Released on November 10, 2013, as the second single from the reissue, “How Long Will I Love You” is a cover of the lead single from The Waterboys' fifth studio album, Room to Roam (1990).
“How Long Will I Love You” is also included on the soundtrack for the 2013 film About Time.

Title: No Surprises
About:
“No Surprises” is the fourth and final single from Radiohead’s third album, OK Computer. It was the first song to be recorded for the album, and the band actually stuck with the original recording: Singer Thom Yorke is quoted as saying that
“We did endless versions afterwards […] and they were all just covers of the first version. So we gave up and went back to [the original].”

Song: RISE (Feat. The Glitch Mob, Mako & The Word Alive)
Title: Rise!
About:
Telling the quintessential teenage story of refusing to grow up, “Rise” is a summer-ready house pop single in Jonas Blue’s signature upbeat, tropical style. With bright and bouncy synthesizers over upbeat piano chords, the song has the makings of a summer hit.
Jonas Blue originally reached out to Nebraskan pop-rap duo Jack & Jack after hearing them on an interview with a Los Angeles radio station and decided they were the perfect vocalists for his next hit.

Title: Sk8er Boi
About:
“Sk8er Boi” is a song from Avril Lavigne’s debut album Let Go which was released on Aug. 27, 2002 when Avril was seventeen years old.
This song is about a skater boy that liked a girl who was too stuck up and too worried about her friends' opinions to admit that she liked him back. This was a decision she went on to regret as the skater boy became a successful musician whereas the girl ended up a single mother living alone.
The song did not perform as strong commercially as Lavigne’s previous single from Let Go, which was “Complicated,” but still peaked at #10 on Billboard’s Hot 100 chart.

Title: Homage
About: “Homage” is just a song about how most, if not all, of Mild High Club’s music is inspired by people before them. Thus they are paying ‘Homage’ to those who inspired them with this song.

Title: The House of the Rising Sun!
About:
The Animals' version of the American folk song is considered one of the 20th century’s British pop classics. While the original version was sung in the character of a woman led into a life of degradation, the Animals' version is told from the view of a young man who follows his father into alcoholism and gambling ruin.
Although the date and author of the song are unknown, some musicologists have said that it resembled ballads of the 16th century, and could very easily have derived from one of that time. As a popular folk song, the oldest record of “House of the Rising Sun” in reference to a song was 1905, and it was first recorded in 1933 by an Appalachian group. Other early recordings include Woody Guthrie’s version from 1941 and Bob Dylan in 1961. The version by the Animals, however, is by far the most popular, and Dylan is often annoyed when it is assumed that he covered that song from them.
Versions of the song have been recorded by many notable artists including Lead Belly, Joan Baez, Ramblin' Jack Elliot, Dolly Parton, Waylon Jennings, Nina Simone, Adolescents, The Ventures, Duane Eddy and Five Finger Death Punch. The song is often heard in the soundtracks of popular TV shows (The West Wing and Supernatural) and movies (Suicide Squad).

Title: Man on a Mission
About: Futuristic is taking the opportunity to tell the listener his story, his struggle, his road to fame. He talks about all the issues he had to deal with in order to make it to the stage that he is at, even turning women down, in order to be able to concentrate on his music and what he wants to do in life. Futuristic is hoping that one day he will be great, judging on the effort that he puts into his music and the amount of time and hard work that he puts in.

Title: The Phoenix
About:
OVERVIEW
“The Phoenix” is the opening track on Fall Out Boy’s comeback album Save Rock and Roll. Many interpret the song to be the band’s statement regarding the end of their hiatus: they started it because they felt “burnt out”, but now they rise out of these ashes like a phoenix.
The song is unique in that it is the only Fall Out Boy song that premiered with its music video – up until the point of its release, fans had never heard the song. This was done to help promote the start of the Young Blood Chronicles, a series of videos based on each song from the record.
The song features the London Symphony Orchestra in the intense background of the song. This background was suggested by Patrick Stump, who had been influenced by the sound of the fourth movement of Dmitri Shostakovich’s “Leningrad” symphony:
http://youtu.be/Xbru9UcCLQQ?t=1h6m8s
RECEPTION
“The Phoenix” was well received by fans, which caused Fall Out Boy to use the song as the opener for the “Save Rock and Roll” tour as well as “Monumentour”. It peaked at #80 on the Billboard Hot 100 and appeared on other charts. In the UK, it topped the “Rock and Metal” chart.
SINGLE
MUSIC VIDEO
http://youtu.be/5hDZbroaQDc?t=13s
NOTABLE LIVE PERFORMANCES
[As the opener to the Save Rock and Roll tour]
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_JWsMorK1cE
The band wore masks as a nod to the song “Car Radio” by tour mates Twenty-One Pilots.
Victoria’s Secret Fashion Show
USES IN POPULAR CULTURE
The song features in the Gameplay Trailer of WWE 2K14, the first WWE game released under the 2K label.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=D99l1aGJCtA

Title: Basket Case
About:
“Basket Case” spent five weeks at the top of the Modern Rock Tracks chart, becoming arguably Green Day’s signature song of the pre-American Idiot days.
It’s about Billie Joe Armstrong’s struggles with anxiety; before he was diagnosed with a panic disorder years afterward, he thought he was going crazy. Armstrong commented that at the time:
The only way I could know what the hell was going on was to write a song about it.

Title: Sweet Child O’ Mine!
About:
“Sweet Child O’ Mine” is the 9th track of Guns N’ Roses’ debut album Appetite for Destruction. The song became GNR’s most successful single, topping the Billboard Hot 100.
The guitar solo was ranked as the 37th greatest solo of all-time by Guitar World. Amazingly, it was recorded in one take.
The lyrics were written by frontman Axl Rose as a dedication to his then-girlfriend Erin Everly. Once he heard guitarist Slash noodling downstairs, Axl found the musical backing he needed.
In 2015, the Australian website MAX TV revealed the song’s striking similarity to “Unpublished Critics,” a song released in 1981 by the band Australian Crawl. Duff McKagan, bass player for Guns N' Roses during the recording of “Sweet Child O' Mine,” said the similarities were startling. Speaking to Radio.com in 2015:
It is pretty stunning. But we didn’t steal it from them! I swear [puts his hand on his heart], I never heard that song until a couple of days ago."

Title: Champagne Supernova
About: The epic rock ballad, “Champagne Supernova,” was released as the sixth and final single off (What’s The Story) Morning Glory?. In 2006, it was included in the “Best Of”-compilation Stop The Clocks.

Title: Wonderwall
About:
“Wonderwall” was released as the third single off of Oasis' classic album What’s The Story? (Morning Glory). It was supposedly written for Noel Gallagher’s then-girlfriend, Meg Matthews, as he told NME in 1996, “It’s about my girlfriend, Meg Matthews.” Bandmembers Bonehead and Guigsy seconded that in the documentary Live Forever, the Ups and Downs of Britpop. However, after Gallagher divorced Matthews in 2001 he said the song was not about Matthews: “[the song was] about an imaginary friend who’s gonna come and save you from yourself.” Some people believe it is about Noel talking himself (he is his own imaginary friend) out of leaving Oasis (or leaving life!). Others believe it is about the relationship between Noel and Liam and some say it is about his (then) imaginary friendship with Paul McCartney (they would later become friends in the mid-to-late 1990s).
The song’s title was appropriated from the 1968 film Wonderwall – from Psychedelia to Surrealism, which George Harrison provided the soundtrack (called Wonderwall Music) for. Before Noel had his “George Harrison revelation,” the song was titled “Wishing Stone.”
In 2006, it was included in the “Best Of”-compilation Stop The Clocks and in 2010 it was included in the singles collection Time Flies… 1994-2009.

Title: HEY CHILD
About:
“HEY CHILD” is a song by the American rock band X Ambassadors, released on April 19, 2019 as a single from their third studio album ORION.
The track seems to be a touching letter to an old friend, who the narrator has been doing bad actions with during their teen years.
As they separated, the character interpreted by Sam Harris has become more mature and he has formed a family, whereas the other man has kept on committing crimes and eventually he has gone to jail. Harris, however, is aware of his former friend’s struggles, so he encourages him not to lose his hope and to improve himself.

Title: Defying Gravity!
About:
This show-stopping tune that closes Act I is perhaps the most well-known anthem from Wicked and it’s considered to be the show’s signature song.
Elphaba is infuriated upon discovering the Wizard of Oz and Madame Morrible’s scheme to oppress the Animals and strip them of their sentience. She escapes, but in order to prevent Elphaba from telling the truth to the citizens of Oz, Madame Morrible spreads the news that Elphaba is now “wicked”. This only boosts Elphaba’s determination. Glinda tries to calm her friend down, but Elphaba learns that she should trust her instincts, go off on her own, and do what she believes in.
Photo of the West End production, Matt Crockett
The first of seven notes of the song “Over the Rainbow” from the 1939 film The Wizard Of Oz are woven a as motif into many of the musical’s songs including this one. This suggests the obvious relationship between The Wizard of Oz and Wicked, which is true, as Wicked is its prequel.

Title: Let It Be!
About: One of The Beatles' many iconic ballads, written by McCartney. The level of repetition in the lyrics suggests this song was written quickly and through emotional inspiration — similar patterns can be seen in songs like “Hey Jude”.

Title: HUMBLE.!
About:
On the ironically braggadocious track “HUMBLE.” Kendrick Lamar challenges his competition. It was released a week after the first promotional single, “The Heart Part 4,” with a music video directed by Dave Meyers and The Little Homies. It features religious imagery mixed with urban life, such as Kendrick in priest’s robes and a moving re-enactment of Leonardo Da Vinci’s painting The Last Supper—juxtaposing this with the concept of humility in the chorus.
On a religious note, the song could also be a reference to the verse James 4:7.
So humble yourselves before God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you."
—Thus linking to April 7th, the date Kendrick told the industry to have their shit together.
Mike WiLL Made-It produced the beat. It features a deep piano riff and a banging 808 bass line. This style of production is a departure from the sound of Kendrick’s previous jazz-influenced project, To Pimp a Butterfly. It seems to reference the character Deebo’s theme song from the popular comedy movie Friday (1995). Kendrick is also shown riding through the neighborhood on a bicycle in the music video, similar to Deebo in Friday.
The song debuted at No. 2 on the Billboard Hot 100 on April 22, 2017, making it not only the highest charting song for Kendrick, but also one of the highest debuting hip-hop singles since “Love the Way You Lie” by Eminem. The song later hit No. 1 on the chart on June 5, 2017, making it Lamar’s first solo No. 1 song and second No. 1 song overall on the charts.

Title: Hotel California!
About:
One of the most mysterious and widely speculated songs in rock history, “Hotel California” is best described in the words of its creators. As Don Henley explained in the Daily Mail dated 9/11/2007:
Some of the wilder interpretations of that song have been amazing. It was really about the excesses of American culture and certain girls we knew. But it was also about the uneasy balance between art and commerce.
Though the annotations to the left do their best to decipher Henley & co.’s words, it’s important to keep in mind a quote (and classic malapropism) from Eagles member Glenn Frey: “Vaguery is the primary tool of songwriters.”
In response to the insinuation that the first working title of the song was “Mexican Reggae,” Don Felder laughed and responded:
Yes, that’s right. It wasn’t really a title. When I first wrote all the music for it, I put it on a cassette with about 16 or 17 other song ideas, another one was what later became “Victim of Love,” and gave copies of the cassette to Joe Walsh, Don Henley, Glenn Frey, and Randy Meisner. At the time I said, “If there’s anything on this cassette you like you want to work on, call me and let me know.”
And so Henley said, “I like that song that sounds like a Mexican reggae.” That was his description of what it drew in his mind. And later we started talking about it, and he came up with the framework lyrically of the hotel being a physical structure called the Hotel California, which there is no real Hotel California other than the one that’s down on Sunset here, the Beverly Hills Hotel is the artwork on the front of the cover.
During the 58th Grammys in February 2016 the Eagles were presented with their Grammy from 1977 for Album of the Year because they didn’t attend the Grammys in ‘77. This took place during a commercial break just after they and Jackson Browne finished a tribute to Glenn Frey.

Title: Smells Like Teen Spirit
About:
