Considered by many to be the ultimate grunge anthem, “Smells Like Teen Spirit” was the lead single for Nirvana’s second studio album and major label debut, September 1991’s Nevermind. The song’s success and omnipresence in the early ‘90s brought the band unrivaled popularity. Eventually, Kurt Cobain grew tired of it and removed it from their live set as often as possible.
Rock history recalls Kathleen Hanna from Bikini Kill scrawling the phrase “Kurt Smells Like Teen Spirit” in spray paint on a wall, from which Kurt took the title. Some say Kurt took it as a call for a revolution, in reference to the Bikini Kill-led riot grrl movement; but according to producer Butch Vig, Kurt just thought the phrase “was stupid,” and it didn’t really mean anything at all. However, it was later revealed that Hanna was referring to the brand of deodorant worn by Kurt’s then-girlfriend and Hanna’s then-bandmate Tobi Vail.
In a 1994 interview with Rolling Stone, Cobain said:
I was trying to write the ultimate pop song. I was basically trying to rip off the Pixies. I have to admit it. When I heard [them] for the first time, I connected with that band so heavily I should have been in that band—or at least in a Pixies cover band. We used their sense of dynamics, being soft and quiet and then loud and hard.
It was one of the last tracks written and recorded for the album, and the final version is comprised of the second take out of just three takes, according to Montage of Heck and other sources.
“Smells Like Teen Spirit” was voted #9 on Rolling Stone’s list of the 500 Greatest Songs of All Time.

Song: Moves Like Jagger (Feat. Christina Aguilera) (Studio Recording From The Voice Performance)
Title: Moves Like Jagger!
About:
“Moves Like Jagger” was first performed on an episode of The Voice, a singing competition which Adam Levine and Christina both judge. It peaked at number one on the Billboard Hot 100, becoming Adam Levine & co’s second number one (after “Makes Me Wonder”).
The video opens with black-and-white footage of a theater. This is followed by classic footage of Mick Jagger, during which Michael Parkinson asks the iconic Rolling Stones frontman how long he plans to make music. “I don’t know. I never thought I’d be doing it for two years even,” a young Jagger says, as the opening whistles of the Maroon 5 song kick in, throughout which a slew of Jagger look-alikes are shown dancing up to a central microphone, interlaced with archival footage of Jagger himself and shots of bandmembers James Valentine, Jesse Carmichael, Michael Madden and Matt Flynn rocking out on their instruments. When Aguilera makes her brief appearance, she fits right into the retro feel with mascara-heavy lashes and a floppy hat.

Title: Time Bomb!
About:
Despite it’s upbeat, ska punk vibe, “Time Bomb”’s subject matter is actually quite dire. The meteoric ascendance of a young boy to prominence within a gang followed by his equally swift demise as he is replaced via murder.
The first verse’s lyrics were taken from “Motorcycle Ride” off the Let’s Go album.
“Time Bomb” is the second single from Rancid’s third album, …And Out Come the Wolves; it reached number 8 on the Billboard Modern Rock Tracks. It was later featured on the multi-band compilation album CBGB Forever. As well as being featured in S1E4 of Netflix Original show Everything Sucks!.
“Time Bomb” was featured in the fifth episode of the Gilmore Girls, “Cinnamon’s Wake.” In the 19th episode of Season 4, “Afterboom,” a character’s band performed a cover. It was also used in the 2005 movie Fun with Dick and Jane.
“Time Bomb” was released in the Rancid 01 track pack for Rock Band 2.

Title: Shut Up and Dance!
About:
“Shut Up and Dance” was the lead single from Walk The Moon’s 2014 album, Talking Is Hard.
The single was a major success, and gave Walk the Moon their first top ten pop hit.
Inspired by a true experience and memories of high school, the song makes a deliberate attempt to recreate the bouncy, quirky feel of early 80s pop-rock.
This was one of the last songs they made for the album, written in LA with assistance from Ben Berger and Ryan McMahon of Captain Cuts.

Title: Sleep Well Beast!
About:
It feels like we’re in this period where there’s a tendency to just want to hibernate when things get really fucking weird. It’s an escape to try to sleep through it, but the beast for me isn’t a negative thing: it’s the future. We’ve all got kids, and when I see all of our kids… They’ve got a challenge ahead of them, but I feel positive about the future. The beast is like, wait until the youth wakes up. It’s an abstract thing.
Matt Berninger
What Makes Mary Timony a Guitar God, According to Sleater-Kinney, Sadie Dupuis, and More
Pitchfork
"It's an imaginary place... it's a fantasy," says a 27-year-old Mary Timony, swirling a paintbrush. The year is 1997, and Timony is on MTV's "Indie Outing" explaining the title of her band Helium's new album, The Magic City, while adding strokes to a ceramic teapot.

Title: Bodak Yellow!
About:
On “Bodak Yellow,” Cardi brings up her past as a stripper and how she has cemented her legitimacy in the rap game with her peerless work ethic. It’s the first single she released with Atlantic Records since signing to the label in 2017.
The chorus and Cardi’s flow throughout the song is reminiscent of Kodak Black’s August 2014 song, “No Flockin.” The title’s also a play on Kodak’s name as “Bodak” alludes the “B” in “Bloods” as Cardi is a member of the Bloods gang, and “Black” is changed to “Yellow” as she’s a yellow bone or light-skinned.
In an August 2017 Billboard interview, Cardi revealed that she wrote the lyrics to the song while listening to the beat on an airplane:
I heard the beat […] on the airplane, I kept writing to it.

Title: I Just Wanted You to Know!
Take Me to Church!
About:
“Take Me to Church” is the debut single by Irish recording artist Hozier. It has become an international hit, reaching #1 in 12 countries, and has been certified five times platinum in the US. “Take Me to Church” was nominated for a Grammy Award for “Song of the Year” in 2015.
Despite its widespread and international success, Hozier was a struggling musician when he recorded the song. And in fact, it wasn’t until the music video — a critique of Russia’s anti-LGBT+ policy, which some audiences have found controversial — went viral that the song attracted attention.
“‘Take Me to Church’ is essentially about sex,” Hozier said in an interview with New York Magazine in March 2014, adding that “it’s not an attack on faith.”
It’s about sex and it’s about humanity, and obviously sex and humanity are incredibly tied. Sexuality, and sexual orientation — regardless of orientation — is just natural… The song is about asserting yourself and reclaiming your humanity through an act of love.
The song has also garnered attention from musicians around the world — it was, for example, covered on The Voice, and director David LaChapelle released an interpretive dance video of “Take Me To Church”, featuring British Royal Ballet veteran Sergei Polunin performing choreography by Jade Hale-Christofi.

Title: Take On Me!
About:
“Take on Me” is a synthpop song that combines various instrumentation that includes acoustic guitars, keyboards, and drums. It was released as a-ha’s debut single in September 1984 to huge success in their homeland of Norway.
The song was then remixed and re-released internationally, but it wasn’t until its groundbreaking, award-winning music video of pencil-sketch animation combined with live-action shot of a girl dragged into a comic book world came out four months later that the song’s success was repeated around the world.
“Take On Me” hit #1 in several countries (including the US in late 1985) and eventually sold over 7 million copies worldwide, making it one of the best-selling singles of all-time. And its video won eight awards at the 1986 MTV Video Music Awards.

Title: The Scientist
About:
“The Scientist” is a very mellow song about trying to analyze a broken relationship.
The song’s melody is inspired by “Don’t Look Back in Anger” by the Oasis, especially the piano part.
The music video is famous for its reverse chronology (which required Chris Martin to learn the song backwards in order to make it look like he was singing forwards as he walked backwards…). Although the video fits the song well, the idea for the video came to Director Jamie Thraves independently; he then waited for the perfect song to match his concept.

Title: Walking the Wire!
About:
“Walking the Wire” possesses the signature Imagine Dragons bass-heavy, meaningful, pulse racing sound. The track is about overcoming hardships and struggles in a rocky relationship. Knowing that they can succeed means they can get higher up.
The track was released as the second and last promotional single in preparation for the release of their album Evolve.

Title: No One Else Like You
About:
This song is included as track number 3 on the OST of the 2013 romantic music drama, Begin Again).
Vocals on this song are provided by Adam Levine of Maroon 5 fame.

Title: Murder on My Mind!
About:
“Murder on My Mind” is YNW Melly’s breakout song. It depicts a dark, upsetting story of Melly shooting a close friend and holding him in his arms as he slowly lost consciousness and died. Melly also mentions his previous jail time served for gun charges and drug possession. The shooting he refers to in this song happened in 2016 or earlier.
In an interview with HotNewHipHop, Melly described how he had written this after being charged with three counts of aggravated assault with a deadly weapon and one count of discharging a firearm in public and had been locked up for a year. In the interview he said how all 240 inmates and the staff enjoyed his song and that’s how he knew it’d be a hit.
Following the release of the track on Soundcloud in 2017, the track’s lyrics supposedly violated Melly’s probation for his prior charge; sending him back to prison.
Over a year after the release of the audio, Melly released the official music video directed by the popular director Gabriel Hart. The video gave a demonic and creepy vibe while vividly showing the fictional crime scene Melly describes.
A month after the release of the video, the song started gaining traction hence Melly released “Mischief on My Mind”, a clean, radio-edited version of “Murder on My Mind”.
“Murder on My Mind” was soon followed up by a sequel, “Mind on My Murder,” which depicts the same events of this song from the victim’s perspective. It is subsequent to the album’s tracklisting.

Song: I Was Born To Love You
Title: I Was Born to Love You!
About: First appearing as a disco/dance track on Freddie Mercury’s 1985 solo album Mr. Bad Guy, the song was later re-worked by the remaining members of Queen and included as a rock song, more in line with the band’s established and accepted style, on their album Made in Heaven (1995), which would be their final studio album.

Title: A Sky Full of Stars
About:
One of Coldplay’s most hyped tracks ever, described by attendees of Coldplay’s secret Ghost Stories shows as “wonderfully produced” and “upbeat, happy, makes you want to stand up and dance,” “A Sky Full of Stars” is the centerpiece of Ghost Stories, bringing the emotional treadmill to an explosive climax.
The track was released as Coldplay’s second single from Ghost Stories, after “Magic,” with the track premiering on 29 April 2014.
EDM artist Avicii was a primary producer on the track and Chris Martin later revealed in an interview that he asked the producer to play the piano parts.

Title: It’s My Life What Ever I Wanna Do!
What’s Up?!
About:
“What’s Up” was the second single from the 1992 album Bigger, Better, Faster, More! by 4 Non Blondes. It shot the band into international stardom, topping the charts in seven countries, reaching #2 in three more (including the UK) and reaching the top 20 in the US, making them the first openly lesbian group to reach the top 40 there.
Bassist Christa Hillhouse recalled hearing Linda Perry working on the song for the first time:
For a short time, Linda had quit her job and she was living with me in this little 2-bedroom flat in San Francisco. She wrote the song when she was in a room down the hall. I was in my bedroom having sex, and I stopped because I heard her playing that song. I remember running down the hall and saying, ‘Dude, what are you playing? I like that.’ We had a lot of rock, thrashy stuff back then, but Linda always would pull her ballads out. I remember being struck by it. She kept looking at me, going, ‘Does this sound like something? Am I plagiarizing someone?“ I said, 'Finish the song, it’s beautiful.’ It caught on at our shows right away, people really liked it.
The song was titled “What’s Up”, despite the phrase not appearing in its lyrics, to avoid confusion with the Marvin Gaye song “What’s Going On”.
In 1994, DJ Miko covered the song with British vocalist Louise Gard. His dance club version found its biggest success in Sweden and New Zealand. In the US, it peaked at #58.
“What’s Up” is both remembered as one of the “Top 100 One-Hit Wonders” of all time and conversely one of the “Worst Songs Ever”.
It found new life in the 2010s thanks to a remake by SLACKCiRCUS titled “Fabulous Secret Powers” using characters from the 80s cartoon He-Man and the Masters of the Universe.

Title: BLACKPINK - Kill This Love (Romanized)!
About:
“Kill This Love” is the title track for BLACKPINK’s second EP of the same name.
This is the highly anticipated release following their last Korean mini album SQUARE UP in 2018. It features intense lead brass electro-pop and marching band snares. The song is propelled by BLACKPINK’s charismatic rap and vocals.
In the first full group teaser, the looks caught attention for the resemblance with Tomb Raider even noticed by the official twitter of Tomb Raider, who replied to an article about it.
YG Entertainment, BLACKPINK’s label, spoiled that the new dance choreography was made by four world-class choreographers and was expected to be more dynamic than the choreography of any song that the group has introduced.

Title: Last Night on Earth!
About:
Singer Billie Joe Armstrong explained to Q magazine May 2009 that “‘Christian’s Inferno’ is like a subway ride to hell and you kind of end up in outer space with this” song. Bassist Mike Dirnt added: “It’s the most unapologetic love song.”
This song is a piano-led number. Armstrong learned the piano before recording 21st Century Breakdown and a lot of the songs were written on it. Bassist Mike Dirnt told The Sun April 24, 2009: “There are parts of this record, which are the most melodic thing we’ve ever written.”

Title: One More Night
About: “One More Night” is a song performed by American band Maroon 5. The song was released as the second single from their fourth studio album, Overexposed.

Title: Bad Liar
About:
According to co-writer Justin Tranter, “Bad Liar” is about “trying to hide magical feelings for someone new, but not being able to.”
The track is the latest single from Selena Gomez released on May 18, 2017. Although it is not yet linked to any project, it could be a first single for one.
The track interpolates the bassline of Talking Heads‘ 1977 classic “Psycho Killer."
For more on the lyrics, check the official premiere article here:
Selena Gomez Premieres The Lyrics To Her New Single "Bad Liar"
Genius
"Your touch like a happy pill / But still all we do is fear."

Title: Centuries
About:
“Centuries” is the lead single from from Fall Out Boy’s sixth studio album. It’s a grandiose track about the fight to make a lasting artistic impression.
In an interview, Pete Wentz explains:
Our band came from the suburbs of Chicago. The blandest place. Nothing comes out of there, no one would have ever said that a rock band would come out of there and go on to play arenas. ‘Centuries’ goes back to that David versus Goliath idea. Even U2 had to start somewhere…
Change terrifies a lot of us. ‘Centuries’ really bangs on that. It’s like, just because you’re being told that what you’re doing sucks, that you’re weird or whatever, that doesn’t mean that you’re not a leader, creating something new. Leaders attract a lot of arrows, you know?
The annotations on this page were made by bassist Pete Wentz, as confirmed in a tweet by him:
Login on Twitter
Twitter
Welcome back to Twitter. Sign in now to check your notifications, join the conversation and catch up on Tweets from the people you follow.

Title: Apocalypse
About:
In “Apocalypse,” Greg acknowledges the pain his new lover has suffered in her past relationships. He’s urging her to let go of heartbreak and the fear of feeling let down, and to embrace the highs and lows of this new relationship.
Greg discussed the beauty and danger that comes with letting go of fear and fully indulging in any relationship on his 2017 interview coupdemain:
You go into love, and you’re pretty much going to get hurt at some point, it’s just kind of the way it is. But if admitting that the person is worth it, that they’re worth the pain, they’re worth the trouble and it doesn’t matter because you just want them, and anything else that comes after that is another side? I really believe in that.

Title: death bed (coffee for your head)!
About:
“death bed (coffee for your head),” released alongside Some Boring, Love Stories, Pt. 2, is a collaboration between Powfu and beabadoobee. The song samples “Coffee” from London artist beabadoobee. In the song, Powfu reminisces on memories with a supposed former lover.
The song delivers two themes of one being the narrator writing this as a tribute to a breakup, or the narrator writing this to address his lover because of upcoming death.
“death bed (coffee for your head)” was first released on February 9, 2019, to all streaming services, but eventually taken down due to Powfu using an unauthorized sample. It was re-released a year later on February 7, 2020, to all streaming platforms. It gained popularity after being featured in many videos on the video platform TikTok.

Title: Freaky Friday!
About:
“Freaky Friday” is a song that involves a crossover between Lil Dicky and Chris Brown.
The first verse is Lil Dicky speaking as Chris Brown and the second verse is Chris Brown living as Lil Dicky. Both artists choose different things to take advantage of while living the other person’s life.
The song is a nod to the movie Freaky Friday that was released in 1976 and remade in 2003. The movie created this premise where a girl and her mother switch places in order to experience each other’s lives.
Lil Dicky released the music video on his 30th birthday, which gave him high hopes for its success.

Title: Love of My Life!
About:
This song is written about Mary Austin, with whom Freddie had a long-term relationship. They met in 1970, and Freddie referred to her once as the ‘love of his life’, despite engaging in relationships with men later on.
Austin and Mercury lived together for seven years and remained best friends until his death. In Mercury’s later life, she became his personal assistant, and after his death was the inheritor of the bulk of his estate.

Title: Believer!
About:
“Believer” thunders around a percussive backbeat and expressive vocals from Dan Reynolds to bring about a message of embracing pain, and using it as a tool for personal growth. It marks the end of Imagine Dragons' self-imposed hiatus that started in 2016. The song acts as the title for their documentary surrounding LGBT youth and the Mormon church.
The song was released on the first of February as the lead single for their third studio album, Evolve, and was featured in a commercial for the Nintendo Switch that premiered on Super Bowl LI. The song was also featured in the first trailer for Kenneth Branagh’s Murder on the Orient Express remake.
Nearly two years after its release, the band released a remix of the song alongside Lil Wayne during their 2019 National College Football Championship halftime performance.

Title: All of Me!
About:
“All of Me” is the third single from and sixth track on John Legend’s album Love in the Future (2013). Legend wrote it along with Toby Gad.
It has become one of Legend’s most popular songs, earning an 8x Platinum RIAA certification and appearing on top of 9 different countries' charts (including the US Billboard Hot 100).
Legend’s performance was nominated for Best Pop Solo Performance at the 2015 Grammys.
Legend has said in interviews that the song was inspired by his passionate love for model Chrissy Teigen, to whom he got engaged in 2011 and married in 2013.

Title: I Know There’s Gonna Be (Good Times)!
About:
The xx’s own Jamie xx released his solo debut In Colour, grabbing Popcaan and man-of-the-moment Young Thug for an assist with “I Know There’s Gonna Be (Good Times).”
The song was met with universal acclaim from multiple publications and featured prominently on year-end lists. The track ranked second on PopMatters' 90 Best Songs of 2015, third on Pitchfork’s top-100 tracks, tied-for-ninth on the Village Voice’s Pazz & Jop poll, and was selected as one of Stereogum’s 80 best songs of 2015.

Song: Tell Me If You Wanna Go Home
Title: Tell Me If You Wanna Go Home
About:
Sung by Keira Knightley, track number 2 on the OST album of 2013 movie Begin Again features a poppy, peppy, jingly sound that gets instantly caught in your head.
The song has two versions, this one and the rooftop mix. The latter features a smashing extended outro from Hailee Steinfeld who plays Violet Mulligan in the movie. Point to be noted, the rooftop mix eschews the first part of the song and begins straight from verse 2.

Title: High and Dry
About: The second single from Radiohead’s second album, The Bends. “High and Dry” was released on a double A-side with the album’s opening track, “Planet Telex.”

Title: I Will Always Love You!
About:
“I Will Always Love You” was covered by Whitney Houston and recorded for her 1992 film The Bodyguard. The song was originally written by Dolly Parton and released on June 6, 1974 as the second single from her thirteenth solo studio album, Jolene.
Houston’s cover has since become one of the best-selling singles of all time and contributed to The Bodyguard being the top-selling soundtrack of all time. Kevin Costner, Houston’s male opposite and one of the film’s co-producers, is actually responsible for suggesting this song for Houston to cover in the film’s first track release. He brought the original song to composer David Foster, who rearranged it as a pop ballad for Houston.
It topped the Billboard Hot 100 for fourteen consecutive weeks; at the time, this was a record for longest number one. It also reached #3 on the Hot 100 after Houston’s death in 2012.

Title: Back in Black!
About: “Back In Black” is the title track off AC/DC’s first album with Brian Johnson, and became its third single. The song is a tribute to deceased frontman Bon Scott. When Brian Johnson was told to write a song, the members said, “It can’t be morbid – it has to be for Bon and it has to be a celebration.” And so they wrote it from Bon’s perpective. The song has since become a mainstay of their concerts, and the riff is one of the most iconic in rock music – for instance, it was the first song Kurt Cobain learned to play, at the age of 14.

Title: Somewhere Only We Know!
About:
“Somewhere Only We Know” is a song by English alternative rock band Keane, officially released as the lead single from their debut album Hopes and Fears.
Keane lead vocalist Tim Rice-Oxley revealed in 2004 of the meaning of the song:
It’s about being able to draw strength from a place or experience you’ve shared with someone. I think it’s an idea a lot of people can relate to.

Title: XO Tour Llif3!
About:
“XO Tour Llif3” is a chronicle of Uzi’s relationship issues with ex-girlfriend, Brittany Byrd, and substance abuse. It’s delivered over bassy and upbeat production but addresses topics like suicide and betrayal. Ultimately, it’s a narrative mixed with strong emotional dialogue, outlining a big fight between them and their girlfriend.
LUV premiered the track in London while opening for The Weeknd’s Starboy: Legend of the Fall 2017 world tour, following the song’s leak. The title pays homage to The Weeknd’s label XO, in light of the performance.
Eternal Atake’s “P2” is a continuation of XO Tour Llif3. Uzi raps about moving on from heartbreak and continuing their career, problem-free. P2 is the outro to Eternal Atake, excluding two bonus tracks.

Song: A Step You Can’t Take Back
Title: A Step You Can’t Take Back
About: This song is placed near the start of the film, as Gretta (Keira Knightley) is dragged up on stage and performs a halfhearted acoustic version of it. Suicidal record producer Dan (Mark Ruffalo) happens to be in the audience and imagines a more fully produced version, which is what we hear on the soundtrack album.

Title: BTS - Life Goes On (English Translation)!
About:
This nostalgic song blurs alternative hip-hop sound with acoustic guitar and synth-pop. The lyrics offer comfort in a time when the world faces the pandemic.
The story behind the song begins from BTS declaring that “even in the face of this new normality, our life goes on and imparts a message of healing to fans and to the world.”
The theme “life goes on” was mentioned several times while BTS shared the process behind the album through vlogs over the months.
“Life goes on, let’s live on” was also the main message of their speech at the 75th UN General Assembly, given just a few months before the song release. The group appeared there as they’ve worked previously with UNICEF on the “LOVE MYSELF” campaign, this time giving the message of “let’s live on in a new world, breaking free from despair and supporting one another in warm solidarity.”
The song serves as the leading track and the second promotional single from BTS' fifth studio album, BE.
The single was announced by BigHit Entertainment via their official SNS and Weverse accounts on October 30, 2020.
The live debut stage took place during the American Music Awards on November 22, 2020.

Song: Wake Me up When September Ends
Title: Wake Me Up When September Ends!
About:
“Wake Me Up When September Ends” is the only song on American Idiot that has no direct connection to the album’s story arc.
Green Day lead singer Billie Joe Armstrong wrote this about his father, who died of oesophagal cancer on September 1st, 1982. The title allegedly comes from what 10-year-old Billie Joe Armstrong told his mother after his father’s funeral. He had locked himself in his room and when his mother knocked on the door, he replied, “Wake me up when September ends”.
This was originally supposed to be on Shenanigans, but Billie wasn’t emotionally ready to record it, so it was put onto American Idiot.

Title: Bad Day
About:
This song is saying that even if you have a bad day once in a while, things will get better. In Europe, it was released in 2005 and became a hit when Coca-Cola used it as the theme song for an advertising campaign on the continent. It topped the Italian and German charts and was #2 in England for 3 weeks. In America, Powter’s album was released in 2006, and this song became a hit when the TV show American Idol started using it as a theme for the “goodbye video” for the departing contestants who were voted off the show on the fifth season.
Daniel Powter was named by Billboard as the top one-hit wonder of the 2000s for this song. The magazine defined a one-hit wonder as an act whose second hit did not reach the Top 25 and only included acts from 2000 to 2007.
Powter is from British Columbia. When this hit #1 in the US, Powter became the first Canadian to top the US charts since Nickelback did it with “How You Remind Me” in 2002.
This was revealed to be the most played song in the UK over the last five years, in a survey announced in July 2008 by the British Performing Rights Society. The runner-up was Kelly Clarkson’s “Because Of You” and in third place came James Blunt’s “You’re Beautiful”.

Song: You're Gonna Live Forever in Me
Title: You’re Gonna Live Forever in Me!
About:
“You’re Gonna Live Forever In Me” holds the promise of a legacy. John considers the impact that a special person has had on his life, claiming that “parts of [him] were made by [them].”
John’s personality is a product of the subject’s companionship, and John promises that influence will never die. In between a whistling intro and outro, Mayer invokes images of mass-extinction and “destiny” to prove his commitment.
The track was included in the first batch of songs released off the album, alongside “Moving On And Getting Over,” “Changing” and “Love On The Weekend.” The album was released in sets of four tracks – sets which were released monthly.

Title: Radioactive
About: The first song on Imagine Dragons' debut album Night Visions gives off a revolutionary vibe, but leaves what is being changed up to interpretation. Some argue it is about nuclear war, others say it is about Wall Street and similar protests, and some think it is really talking about the end of the world. Regardless, it is a powerful song bound to inspire debate on its meaning.

Title: Hey Jude!
About:
Originally titled “Hey Jules,” named after John Lennon’s son Julian, this song was written by Paul McCartney to comfort him during John and Cynthia’s divorce. John Lennon stated that he thought the song was written as a form of blessing from Paul McCartney for his and Yoko’s relationship. They were touched by McCartney’s act and Cynthia said she would always remember his “gesture of care and concern in coming to see us.”
While it was being recorded, producer George Martin voiced his discontent with the abnormal song length. Coming in at over seven minutes long–with four of those minutes comprising the outro–Martin expressed the opinion that radio DJs would not play the song. Lennon responded with: “They will if it’s us.”
Once released, the song performed absolutely fantastically. With its seven-minute runtime, it became the longest single to top the British music charts in its day. In the United States, the song spent nine weeks at #1, making it the best-performing Beatles single. Additionally, at the time it tied for the record of longest-sitting chart-topping single.
The reaction to the song did not wane. It stayed as The Beatles' best performing U.S. single and is considered by many music critics as being one of the best songs of all time. Rolling Stone lists it at #8. It sits at #10 on Billboard’s 55th Anniversary countdown. In addition, the song is the most referenced musical performance.

Title: Time Is Running Out!
About:
The third track off of Absolution, and the second single. It was one of the last songs recorded for the album. The single’s B-Side is The Groove.
Like most of Muse songs, it’s a song adressed to another, but this another and the narrator are both unintentified. The overall lyricism of the song is pretty vague, but we clearly see that Bellamy describes here a kind of weird, attracting, but unhealthy relationship.
The lyrics are still in the general theme of the album, which are the apocalypse, the feeling of emergency, madness/crazyness, and finally religion.
Althrough this song is probably not about religion, you can definitely feel the madness/illness of the narrator and the feeling of emergency.
The music video, even if pretty cool, doesn’t really makes any sense. It takes place in a large room with some kind of military chiefs sitting behind it. During the choruses, pre-choruses and the bridge, they dance around and become kind of crazy, which links with this theme of the song and the album.
The scene is also an obvious reference to the War Room from the classic Kubrick’s movie Doctor Strangelove, in which dignitaries are trying to prevent the launching of a nuclear warhead during the Cold War.
This can be linked to the feeling of emergency, very present in the movie, but the explaination that the director of the music video (John Hillcoat) choosed this decor because it looked cool has most probabilities.

Title: Immortals!
About: The 10th track of American Beauty/American Psycho, “Immortals” first appeared on the soundtrack for the 2014 Disney animated comedy superhero film Big Hero 6. Fall Out Boy was asked to create the song after learning about the movie’s plot in which a young boy loses his older brother to a super-villain. He recovers from his grief by assembling a team of super-heroes, including a robot created by his deceased brother, to apprehend the villain.

Title: Stand By Me
About:
Released in 1961, this song was inspired by the spiritual hymn “Lord Stand By Me”. It has since been recorded into more than 400 other versions.
It was ranked the 122nd out of Rolling Stones “500 Greatest Songs of All Time”. In 2012 it ranked as one of BMI’s top 5 most performed songs of the 20th century, with more than 11.6 million performances.
When it debuted in 1961, it reached the Billboards #1 position in “Hot R&B Singles.”

Title: I Love You 3000
About:
“I Love You 3000” is inspired by box office record breaking and the highest grossing film of all time, Avengers: Endgame. The song makes many references to the film, one notably being;
‘Cause you’re my Iron Man
And I love you 3000
This is direct reference to a scene in the film where Tony Stark’s daughter says “I love you 3000” to him in response to Tony, “I love you tons”.

Title: Phoenix
About:
In this song Rocky looks deep into his career, the affects it’s having on him, and how things could’ve been much worse off. He looks at society as a whole, and at the people that compose it, and observes how we have/can destroy one another with nothing more than our words.
As annotated in the outro, the Phoenix is seen to be a symbol of rebirth, this hinting at the possibility that Rocky is trying to reinvent himself.

Title: Don’t Stop Me Now!
About:
“Don’t Stop Me Now” is one of Queen’s masterpieces, a song that’s aged well with time and landed third on Rolling Stone’s list of the Top 10 Queen Songs. It’s a simple song, an energetic song that sees lead singer Freddie Mercury on a high that he wants to stay in, letting nobody pull him down.
Critically, the song performed well, releasing as a single after the release of album Jazz. Despite reaching number 9 on the UK charts and later going Platinum, it only hit 86 on the US charts. Time has only increased its popularity, as it is often included in “Greatest Hit” compilations and stands as their second most played song on Spotify with over 1.25 billion plays as of March 2022.

Title: I Will Always Love You!
About:
“I Will Always Love You” was covered by Whitney Houston and recorded for her 1992 film The Bodyguard. The song was originally written by Dolly Parton and released on June 6, 1974 as the second single from her thirteenth solo studio album, Jolene.
Houston’s cover has since become one of the best-selling singles of all time and contributed to The Bodyguard being the top-selling soundtrack of all time. Kevin Costner, Houston’s male opposite and one of the film’s co-producers, is actually responsible for suggesting this song for Houston to cover in the film’s first track release. He brought the original song to composer David Foster, who rearranged it as a pop ballad for Houston.
It topped the Billboard Hot 100 for fourteen consecutive weeks; at the time, this was a record for longest number one. It also reached #3 on the Hot 100 after Houston’s death in 2012.

Title: Christmas Saves the Year!
About:
“Christmas Saves the Year” is twenty one pilots‘ first Christmas song. It was released after their Christmas-themed Chipotle Challenger Series Twitch stream playing Fortnite on December 8, 2020, that Tyler Joseph, Josh Dun, and Mark C. Eshleman of Reel Bear Media organized to raise money for the Make-A-Wish Foundation, and raised roughly $18,000. The song was released at the end of the Twitch stream in the form of a visualizer music video. Immediately following the stream, the song and visualizer video were made available on music-streaming services.
The song was teased as a surprise gift to the fans on Tyler’s Instagram story the day prior, calling it a “stocking stuffer.”
A claymation music video was later released on December 8, 2021, to celebrate the song’s one-year anniversary.

Title: I Wish It Could Be Christmas Everyday!
About: An instant Xmas classic that you’ll hear on the radio every year in the UK. Frontman Roy Wood wrote and produced the song, whose wintery music video was actually filmed in August.

Title: Wonderful Christmastime
About: Wonderful Christmastime was recorded by Paul during the sessions for his album McCartney II. It was originally issued as a single not found on any album. It was later included as a bonus song on the 2011 reissue of McCartney II, as well as the 1993 reissue of the Wings album Back to The Egg. Despite having been a solo single, the music video for this song features the members of Paul’s band Wings.

Title: Warriors!
About:
“Warriors” is a song written and recorded by American alt-rockers Imagine Dragons.
The band wrote and recorded the song alongside Riot Games for the 2014 League of Legends World Championships. The event is a tournament hosted by Riot Games for high-profile League of Legends, competing to determine the best team in the world.

Title: Destination Freestyle (feat. King Vvibe)
About:
After months of requests from fans, Crash Adams finally released a studio version with the then unknown man who dropped a freestyle verse while the duo were on the Santa Monica Pier.
The duo would later admit that they initially did not release a studio version because they simply “forgot to get his at”. Thankfully, a follower dropped his at and they were able to reach out to the rapper, who turned out to be King Vvibe.

Title: Now and Then!
About:
“Now And Then” was originally a demo written and recorded in 1977 by former Beatles' member John Lennon. It is the final song released under the name of The Beatles, featuring contributions from the entire quartet—including Lennon and George Harrison, even years after their passing.
The song was initially intended for release in 1995 during the remastering sessions for The Beatles’ Anthology, but could not be completed due to technology limitations of the time preventing proper vocal extraction. Decades later, with the help of cutting-edge AI software, Ringo Starr and Paul McCartney were able to properly separate the vocal tracks from John Lennon’s original demo and place them over their own musical recordings, so that each of the four band members could contribute to this final song.

Title: Yesterday!
About:
“Yesterday” is the most covered song in history, with over 2,000 versions having been recorded. A spare, haunting song of lost love from the early Beatles catalog, it was sung perfectly by a young Paul McCartney.
McCartney dreamed the entire melody before he arranged the music with George Martin. According to Rolling Stone:
‘It fell out of bed,’ Paul McCartney once said about the origins of ‘Yesterday.’ ‘I had a piano by my bedside, and I must have dreamed it, because I tumbled out of bed and put my hands on the piano keys and I had a tune in my head. It was just all there, a complete thing. I couldn’t believe it. It came too easy.’
The working lyrics Paul used to fit the tune before he wrote the actual words started with “Scrambled eggs / Oh my baby how I love your legs / Not as much as I love scrambled eggs.” Picking up on that famous trivia, Jimmy Fallon eventually developed the rest of “Scrambled eggs” and recorded it with Paul.

Title: She’s Gone!
About:
Originally released as a “sleeper hit” in 1973, the duo re-released “She’s Gone” in 1976 after scoring a hit with “Sara Smile”, this time around peaking at 7 on the Billboard Hot 100.
By the second release of “She’s Gone”, the duo had made a name for itself, and the song received much praise, with it being called both “beautiful” and “fabulous.”
The song describes a situation where either John or Daryl can’t come to terms that their past lover really is gone, and won’t be coming back. The song was originally released without a music video, but that didn’t mean that there wasn’t one. The duo had filmed a music video in 1976, but when the TV station in Philadelphia saw what they had created, they refused to air it because it was so outlandish, with Daryl in woman’s sandals and a robe, and John wearing a sleeveless tuxedo. In 2007 , John uploaded their original music video on YouTube, where you can still watch it.

Song: The Man Who Can't Be Moved
Title: The Man Who Can’t Be Moved!
About: “The Man Who Can’t Be Moved” paints a picture of the most dedicated man in the world, who has run out of options to find the girl that left him. He has resorted to simply living on the corner where he first met her–in hopes she will return to him.

Title: Rich Love
About:
OneRepublic collaborated with the Swedish EDM duo Seeb on this song, released after Ryan’s comeback from an extended break during the promotion of their 2016 album Oh My My.
Musically, it features the chiming electronic melodies typical of Seeb’s productions; lyrically, it deals with the understanding that love and being able to have fun with your friends and familiars is more important (that is, it makes you “richer”) than running after money.

Title: X!
About: 21 Savage teams up with fellow Atlanta rapper Future for the ominous track “X.” On the track, the two emcees rap about their ex-girlfriends and past endeavors, flexing on them with their money and luxurious lifestyles, all over an eerie, high bass instrumental.

Title: Goodbye Yellow Brick Road!
About:
The fourth track on Elton John’s 1973 double album of the same name, and the second single from the album. Written by Elton and his co-songwriter Bernie Taupin, “Goodbye Yellow Brick Road” is about wanting to give up a life of opulence for a simpler life away from all the glitz and glamour. Since Elton himself was living deep in the high life at the time, it can be said that this message is more relevant to Bernie than Elton, for he was often the guy that preferred to keep it low key compared to his more famous partner.
It is one of Elton John’s most famous and commercially successful singles, topping the Canadian RPM 100 Top Singles Chart and peaking at #2 on the US Billboard Top 100. It’s also considered one of the best songs he’s ever made. In 2022, it was used as the trailer song for Marvel’s Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania Trailer.

Title: Zombie!
About:
The Cranberries describe the violence in “The Troubles,” the decades-long conflict in Northern Ireland between nationalists (mainly self-identified as Irish or Roman Catholic) and unionists (mainly self-identified as British or Protestant).
Dolores O'Riordan wrote the song during the band’s English tour in 1993 in memory of two young boys, Tim Parry and Johnathan Ball, who were killed in an IRA bombing in Warrington, England.
“This song is our cry against man’s inhumanity to man, inhumanity to child,” she said.
During a performance of “Zombie” in January 1994 at The Astoria in London, Dolores O'Riordan said:
This song is our cry against the violence in London, against the war in the north of Ireland. And we want it to stop.

Title: Hymn For The Weekend!
About:
Coldplay’s Queen B duet is an ode to enjoying a significant other you love and cherish. In an interview, Martin described the origins of the collaboration and its sound:
I thought I’d like to have a song called “Drinks on Me” where you sit on the side of a club and buy everyone drinks because you’re so fucking cool. I was chuckling about that, when this melody came – “drinks on me, drinks on me” – then the rest of the song came out. I presented it to the rest of the band and they said, “We love this song, but there’s no way you can sing ‘drinks on me.’” So that changed into “drink from me” and the idea of having an angelic person in your life. Then that turned into asking Beyoncé to sing on it.
It’s one of four collaborations on the album; the others are Tove Lo on “Fun”, Gwyneth Paltrow on “Everglow”, and a Barack Obama sample on “Kaleidoscope.”
Apparently, Beyoncé also made their album 90% better.
She made our album 90% better. And we were already 20% good. So now we’re like 110% the band we used to be. You do the math.
Before the album’s December 4, 2015 release date, it was previewed on Twitter on November 24, 2015. The official music video was uploaded on the 29th of January.

Title: Creep!
About:
According to Thom Yorke, “Creep” tells the tale of an inebriated man who tries to get the attention of a woman to whom he is attracted by following her around. In the end, he lacks the self-confidence to face her and feels he subconsciously is her.
When asked about “Creep” in 1993, Yorke said:
I have a real problem being a man in the ‘90s… Any man with any sensitivity or conscience toward the opposite sex would have a problem. To actually assert yourself in a masculine way without looking like you’re in a hard-rock band is a very difficult thing to do… It comes back to the music we write, which is not effeminate, but it’s not brutal in its arrogance. It is one of the things I’m always trying: To assert a sexual persona and on the other hand trying desperately to negate it.
Jonny Greenwood said the song was in fact a happy song about “recognizing what you are”.
Notable covers include those by The Pretenders, Carrie Manolakos, Macy Gray, Lea Michele and Dean Geyer, as part of Glee, Vega Choir, Prince, The Bluecoats Drum and Bugle Corps, and most recently, Kimberly Nichole, who performed a very strong cover of this song on the 2015 season of The Voice.
Despite, or perhaps because of, the popularity of the song, Radiohead grew to hate “Creep” and have rarely played it live since the OK Computer tour. “My Iron Lung”, written in 1994 and later released on The Bends, is about the song.

Title: Be Around Me!
About:
In the opening track, and third single off his sophomore album, Will Joseph Cook describes the feeling of falling in love.
Before releasing the song, he posted a short promo on his YouTube channel stating just this.

Title: Come and Get Your Love!
About:
In April 1974, the Native American members of the group Redbone basked in the success of their million-selling gold single “Come and Get Your Love.” The single was a Top-Five hit on the U.S. Billboard Hot 100 chart and hit #1 in several U.S. cities across the country.
ICTMN spoke with Pat Vegas, the group’s bassist, and only founding member still playing under the Redbone banner.
A Top-Five single—what did that kind of success mean for you as a Native artist in those days?
It was something I had already envisioned in my head. I had always loved Native American chants and music and I knew If I worked hard I would come up with something that would appeal to everybody.
How did the song “Come and Get Your Love” come about?
Lolly called me up at 3 am; we had just come back from Philadelphia from doing a tour and he said “Pat, can you come over? I think I have a great idea for a song. I want you to come and help me write it.” I was pretty tired and I said, “Oh man, I am not going to come over there and work on this unless we write together.” He said of course. My girlfriend and I went over there at 3 AM and we listened to it – it was nice, but it wasn’t structured. I think it was 11 minutes long. I sat in the music room and worked on it, structured it with a bassline and cut it to 6 minutes. We recorded it in the studio, and when it came out, there was only one name on it – Lolly Vegas. (Laughs) I said, “Wait a minute man, I got up at 3 in the morning with you, that’s my bassline!”
When the record came out they wouldn’t play it because they said it was too long.
Michael Atkinson, the head of promotions at CBS-Epic, came down to Whiskey-a-Go-Go when we were performing with Ronnie Milsap, and he said, “Pat If you go into the studio and break this down to 3 minutes, you’ll make it to number one.” We worked on it, mastered it and I cut it down to 2:56. I said to Lolly, look what we’ve done and he said, “I don’t hear anything different.” (Laughs) He didn’t even notice it was shorter.

Title: Young and Beautiful!
About:
From the soundtrack for the 2013 film version of The Great Gatsby. The song was also featured in the second trailer released for the film, and used throughout the film as a motif of Gatsby and Daisy’s relationship.
According to Del Rey:
It was an honor to work with Baz Lurhmann on his amazing adaptation of one of the most extraordinary books ever written. The movie is highly glamorous and exciting; Rick Nowels and I were thrilled to write the song for the film.
“Young and Beautiful” was penned by Lana and the film’s director, co-writer, and co-producer Baz Luhrmann, for the soundtrack. Del Rey wrote an earlier version of the song for her Paradise EP, but re-worked it with Luhrmann after he heard it and decided it’d work great with the movie. The song served as the film’s kickoff single, and an “orchestral single” featuring an orchestra was also released. Its motif, as well as samples of it, can be heard throughout the film and its soundtrack.
A jazz version of the song, created by the Bryan Ferry Orchestra, can be heard in the background of the scene that introduces Daisy.

Title: Dancing in My Room
About: The song has become popular widely due to the TikTok exposure that the artist has exposed it to. Several other creators have also helped in its exposure to increase its popularity.

Song: When Can I See You Again? (From "Wreck-It Ralph"/Soundtrack Version)
Title: When Can I See You Again?!
About:
The main song for the soundtrack of the Wreck-It-Ralph movie, “When Can I See You Again?” tells about the eternal friendship, and people whom it hurts to say goodbye.
This song is dedicated to inseparable friends, and by extension, Ralph and Vanellope.

Title: Popular Song!
About:
This song is about bullied, unpopular students eventually becoming a successful and making a “popular” song while the bullies are stuck at dead end jobs. The final message of the song is that popularity is ultimately not riches but being yourself.
Ariana Grande was featured on this track, which was then added to Ariana’s album, Yours Truly. The original version of this song was featured on Mika’s album in 2012. Grande’s part was originally done by singer Priscilla Renea, who helped pen the original. Mika and Grande were connected via his label. He hadn’t heard of her previously (at that point, she was a relative unknown in the music world, and more closely associated with her character on Nickelodeon’s Sam & Cat).
Mika wrote this song after seeing the Broadway musical Wicked—this is his version of the song “Popular,” sung by Glinda the Good Witch. He told Huffington Post:
I went to see “Wicked” and I thought, ‘Glinda’s actually being mean.’ So I thought it would be nice to take the song and write my own version of it because I don’t like what she was saying in that part of the show.
Mika invited Wicked composer Steven Schwartz over to his house in London to listen to the song, and he instantly loved it.
This song became an anti-bullying anthem, which Mika intended. As a kid who was bullied growing up, this song’s message was very personal for him. He told Windy City Times:
That is what it’s about but it is more than that. Part of it is about bullying but it also about the music industry. We strive to hard to create something but we are still that same thing that we started off as. It definitely has some of my favorite lyrics on the whole album. ‘Standing on the field with your pretty pom poms now you are working at the movies selling popular corn.’

Title: She Will Be Loved
About:
“She Will Be Loved” is the third single released from the band’s 2002 debut album, Songs About Jane. The song peaked at number five in the US (becoming their second top ten there), number four in the United Kingdom and number one in Australia (for five consecutive weeks).
As of June 2014, the song has sold more than three million copies in the United States.

Title: Stronger
About:
The biggest hit and second single from Kanye’s third studio album, Graduation, “Stronger” samples French electronic music duo Daft Punk’s “Harder, Better, Faster, Stronger,” which in turn samples Edwin Birdsong’s 1979 track, “Cola Bottle Baby.”
Timbaland helped Kanye a little with the drums on the track.

Title: Here’s to Never Growing Up!
About:
This song is about teens and young adults who want to be able to do things that adults do (like drinking and partying) but never having to deal with the consequences. Hence, never wanting to grow up.
“Here’s to Never Growing Up” was the lead single from Lavigne’s self-titled fifth studio album. It was released on April 9, 2013.
One of the writers on the song was Lavigne’s ex-husband Chad Kroeger of Nickelback.

Title: In the Meantime
About:
In “In the Meantime,” Spacehog sings about equality and overall human decency. It appeared as the first track on Spacehog’s first studio album, Resident Alien, and gained quick notoriety due to its #1 spot in the U.S. Billboard Mainstream Rock charts.
The track also had a popularity spike in the early to middle months of 2023, due to its appearance in Marvel Studios' May 2023 film, Guardians of the Galaxy, Vol. 3. Its first appearance was in the trailer for the film in early December 2022.

Title: Poetry: How Does It Feel Now???!
About:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8XQVU85mNnk
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fZoMzWraS_c

Title: Better Together
About:
This is the first track on Jack’s album In Between Dreams which was released in February 2005.
The song samples some from “Heart and Soul” by Hoagy Carmichael.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6EaytUFTDDs

Song: Come and Get Your Love (Re-Recorded) [From "Guardians of the Galaxy"]
Title: Come and Get Your Love!
About:
In April 1974, the Native American members of the group Redbone basked in the success of their million-selling gold single “Come and Get Your Love.” The single was a Top-Five hit on the U.S. Billboard Hot 100 chart and hit #1 in several U.S. cities across the country.
ICTMN spoke with Pat Vegas, the group’s bassist, and only founding member still playing under the Redbone banner.
A Top-Five single—what did that kind of success mean for you as a Native artist in those days?
It was something I had already envisioned in my head. I had always loved Native American chants and music and I knew If I worked hard I would come up with something that would appeal to everybody.
How did the song “Come and Get Your Love” come about?
Lolly called me up at 3 am; we had just come back from Philadelphia from doing a tour and he said “Pat, can you come over? I think I have a great idea for a song. I want you to come and help me write it.” I was pretty tired and I said, “Oh man, I am not going to come over there and work on this unless we write together.” He said of course. My girlfriend and I went over there at 3 AM and we listened to it – it was nice, but it wasn’t structured. I think it was 11 minutes long. I sat in the music room and worked on it, structured it with a bassline and cut it to 6 minutes. We recorded it in the studio, and when it came out, there was only one name on it – Lolly Vegas. (Laughs) I said, “Wait a minute man, I got up at 3 in the morning with you, that’s my bassline!”
When the record came out they wouldn’t play it because they said it was too long.
Michael Atkinson, the head of promotions at CBS-Epic, came down to Whiskey-a-Go-Go when we were performing with Ronnie Milsap, and he said, “Pat If you go into the studio and break this down to 3 minutes, you’ll make it to number one.” We worked on it, mastered it and I cut it down to 2:56. I said to Lolly, look what we’ve done and he said, “I don’t hear anything different.” (Laughs) He didn’t even notice it was shorter.

Title: You’re Beautiful!
About:
“You’re Beautiful” is a song written by James Blunt, Sacha Skarbek, and Amanda Ghost for Blunt’s debut album, Back to Bedlam. This song talks about Blunt’s ex-girlfriend, whom he saw on the London Underground with her new partner. Blunt told Oprah:
It’s about seeing my ex-girlfriend on the subway in London with her new man, who I didn’t know existed. She and I caught eyes and lived a lifetime in that moment, but didn’t do anything about it.
Rolling Stone put the song at #7 of its list of the 10 most annoying songs of all time.

Title: Since You Been Gone
About:
Originally a song by Russ Ballard, Rainbow released a cover of the song on their 1979 album Down to Earth, which would become both more popular than the original version of the song and one of Rainbow’s most popular songs. This arrangement of the song has much more of a hard rock feel than the original, and features considerable instrumentation and vocal rhythm differences, though the overall structure of the song was not changed.
Brian May, guitarist of Queen, covered this song live with the Brian May Band, as something of a tribute to drummer Cozy Powell, who played with both Rainbow when recording their version of “Since You Been Gone,” as well as with the Brian May Band.

Title: Mr. Blue Sky
About:
In a BBC Radio interview, Lynne talked about writing “Mr. Blue Sky” after locking himself away in a Swiss chalet and attempting to write ELO’s follow-up to A New World Record:
It was dark and misty for 2 weeks, and I didn’t come up with a thing. Suddenly the sun shone and it was, ‘Wow, look at those beautiful Alps.’ I wrote Mr. Blue Sky and 13 other songs in the next 2 weeks.
“Mr. Blue Sky” was the second single off the Out of the Blue album and peaked at number 6 in the UK and number 35 in the US.

Title: Smile (Living My Best Life)!
About: Smile is just a feel good song about living your best life and not giving a fuck about the haters.

Title: Happy Face
About: This song is about the toxic positivity that we see in the world. It can also refer to how famous stars (Ex: Actors) are forced to put on a smile when it comes to being on the red carpet. They never get to show their genuine emotions. If they did, it’d be rare.

Song: Can't Help Falling in Love (Bonus Track)
Title: Can’t Help Falling in Love!
About: A tender ballad about being unable to resist falling in love, “Can’t Help Falling in Love” is one of Elvis' most famous and romantic songs. Originally recorded to tie along with his movie, Blue Hawaii, the song has since been covered by various other artists, from U2 and Britney Spears to twenty one pilots. Rolling Stone ranked it as the 5th best Elvis song of all time, and it was also ranked as the 50th most popular wedding song by Billboard magazine.

Title: Exit Music (For A Film)!
About:
Radiohead was in the middle of their 1996 tour when they were approached by director Baz Luhrmann to record a song meant for his upcoming film, Romeo + Juliet.
The band was shown the final thirty minutes of the film and were instantly driven to inspiration. According to Thom Yorke, “When we saw the scene in which Claire Danes holds the Colt .45 against her head, we started working on the song immediately.”
The result was “Exit Music (For A Film)”, a chillingly gentle song that bursts into a haunting crescendo of emotion. Because of its inclusion on OK Computer, it was withheld from the film’s soundtrack and instead a remix of “Talk Show Host” took its place.
Appropriately, the song plays during the film’s closing credits.

Title: She Bangs The Drums!
About: “She Bangs The Drums” was released as the third single off of The Stone Roses.

Title: Closer!
About:
“Closer” is a millennial romance anthem that celebrates youth and heartbreak. It features vocals from singer-songwriter Halsey and Chainsmokers member Andrew Taggart, marking the first time the Chainsmokers feature their vocals on a track—Taggart previously performed backing vocals for the chorus of “Roses”. It is also their first collaboration with Halsey.
At the 2016 MTV VMAs, both the duo and Halsey performed “Closer”. The duo teased the performance as “new level” on Twitter. This was the second time that “Closer” was performed at Madison Square Garden — the first being at Halsey’s sold-out Badlands Tour show on August 13th, 2016 where The Chainsmokers performed as guests.
In September 2016, after “Closer” had already reached the summit of the Billboard Hot 100, Isaac Slade and Joe King, respectively the lead singer and guitarist of The Fray, were given additional songwriting credits on the song due to the similarity of its chorus to the verses of their 2005 breakout hit, “Over My Head (Cable Car).”
On the March-25-dated chart, “Closer” tied LeAnn Rimes Cibrian’s 32 week record in the Billboard Hot 100 top 10. The singer held it for 20 years – beginning December 13, 1997 – with her single “How Do I Live.” She congratulated the hitmaker duo on Twitter by singing a mashup of “Closer” and her own song.
On September 15, 2018, the song was certified Diamond by the RCAA, verifying that the song had sold an equivalent ten million units in the United States alone.
Related: How Blink 182’s “I Miss You” Inspired The Chainsmokers And Halsey’s “Closer”

Title: Faded
About:
Originally an instrumental from August 2014 titled “Fade,” Alan released this updated version in December 2015 under the slightly-altered name “Faded,” with ISELIN providing vocals.
“I’m still just an eighteen year old from Bergen in Norway,” Walker told Genius. Until “Faded” blew up, he was “more concerned about [his] homework and lack of focus in studies than on having a song that was topping the charts in many territories.”
I’m just really humble and grateful for the response and feedback this track has gotten. Unimaginable, if that’s possible to say. Just unreal!

Title: On My Way!
About:
“On My Way” was created by DJ Alan Walker to celebrate the first year anniversary of “PUBG Mobile”, teaming up with Sabrina Carpenter and Latin artist Farruko. On March 21, players were able to hear the song while they’re waiting in the “PUBG Mobile” lobby or while driving a car during a match.
On March 13, the “PUBG Mobile” team took to twitter to tease the arrival of the song
Alan Walker also tweeted a promotional photo for the song and game the day after the release
In “On My Way”, the theme centers around abandonment. Sabrina and Farruko sings about their decision to leave for “the greater good” hinting that the people they left behind might not understand that it’s for the best.

Title: The Nights
About:
“The Nights” by Avicii is an ode to good fun and also to the singer’s father. The “pirate-y fight” (as described by the writer Nicholas Furlong) sound to the song was inspired by the bars in Ireland, where they play folk music which is upbeat and easy to dance (and drink) to.
This song is also Avicii’s contribution to the soundtrack of EA Sports‘ FIFA 15.

Song: All Falls Down (Feat. Noah Cyrus, Digital Farm Animals)
Title: All Falls Down!
About:
“All Falls Down” is a self-reflection of Kanye’s personal battle with consumerism. The track was the third single from his debut album, The College Dropout. The song peaked at number seven on the Billboard Hot 100 and was nominated for Best Rap/Sung Collaboration at the 47th Grammy Awards.
Kanye released an earlier version of this song as “Self Conscious (Poetry-Style)"on Freshmen Adjustment and performed a lesser-known spoken word version on “Def Poetry Jam” of this song also known as from Freshmen Adjustment

Title: Day 1 ◑
About: “Day 1” talks about loyalty and being with someone right from the beginning, perhaps even talking about first love that lasts forever.

Title: Something Just Like This
About:
“Something Just Like This” is about a relationship that doesn’t need to be superhumanly perfect.
On February 21, 2017, Spotify teased the song by displaying a banner advertisement on their main page. It was subsequently released on February 22, 2017, following Coldplay and The Chainsmokers' surprise performance at the BRIT Awards. However, Coldplay frontman Chris Martin teased it in a tweet one month prior:
As Forbes reported, the official lyric video broke the record for the most views in a single day on Youtube:
According to a YouTube representative, the “Something Just Like This” lyric video racked up over 9 million views in just its first 24 hours of availability, a new all-time record.
The song is the second single released from the Chainsmokers' debut album, Memories…Do Not Open, after the lead single “Paris.”

Title: Hate Sleeping Alone!
About:
Bonus Track (only on iTunes) off of Take Care.
Here, Drake details a complicated relationship that he’s staying in just because he hates sleeping alone.
This song also contains back-up vocals from Trey Songz.
Trivia: The production on this track samples Club Paradise, one of the tracks Drake used to build up to the release of Take Care.
https://youtu.be/EfGOqrXcm_Y

Title: Stay With Me!
About:
“Stay with Me” sees Smith yearning for a lasting relationship after a one-night stand leaves them craving more. One of their biggest hits, it’s gone #1 in countries like the United States, Canada, and Ireland, and even won two Grammy awards.
Written with Jimmy Napes and Tourist in Old Street, London studio, Smith claimed “the song just flowed out of us so naturally.” When Tourist played the first three chords, Napes quickly added a drum layer, and the rest of the track took only 30-40 minutes to write.
Upon the track’s release, listeners noticed similarities between “Stay With Me” and Tom Petty’s 1989 hit “I Won’t Back Down.” Smith’s team said that this was purely coincidental, but agreed to give Petty co-writer credits, and Petty stated that he has no ill feelings for Smith.
The music video was directed by Jamie Thraves, who also directed the music videos for Smith’s “Money On My Mind,” Radiohead’s “Just,” Coldplay’s “The Scientist,” by and Death Cab for Cutie’s “I Will Follow You into the Dark.” It features Sam walking down the street in Hackney, London, among other settings.

Song: Feels (Feat. Pharrell Williams, Katy Perry, Big Sean)
Title: Feels!
About:
“Feels” is a lighthearted summer song which unites previous “Heatstroke” crooner Pharrell Williams, “Open Wide” rapper Big Sean, and new collaborator Katy Perry.
Calvin announced the track alongside a teaser through Twitter:

Title: Cake By The Ocean!
About:
“Cake by the Ocean” is the debut single by DNCE. The song appears on two projects: it serves as the lead single from the band’s debut extended play, SWAAY, which was released in October 2015, and also appears on their self-titled debut studio album, which was released in November 2016.
The track was first premiered on New York radio station Z100 and on Complex on September 14, 2015.
Jonas told Complex the track was “a total breakthrough” for the band, propelling the “offbeat pop project,” while Complex called it “one part swagging 70’s, one part buttery R&B.” The track’s title was a quote said by producers Mattman & Robin, who were actually referring to the drink “Sex on the Beach”.
The song includes playful lyrics from DNCE’s lead singer, Joe Jonas, who sings about getting wild in a romantic relationship by making cake-related references, as “cake” could be used as slang for the sexually-charged lyrics that he croons. The other members of the band include JinJoo Lee on the guitar, Cole Whittle on the bass and keyboards, and Jack Lawless on the drums, who are involved alongside Mattman & Robin in providing Joe with a dance-pop and funk-pop style.

Title: Waiting For Love
About:
“Waiting For Love” is the third single off Avicii’s second LP Stories, the song released in the US on May 22, and in the UK on June 7. Avicii previewed the song live alongside co-producer Martin Garrix at Ultra Music Festival.
The vocalist of the song is the lead singer of band Cherry Ghost, Simon Aldred. The song talks about the singers' character desperately waiting and trying to find somebody to love. The song’s music video features an elderly man with dementia who goes out on a journey to seek out his lost wife.
The song is extremely successful and peaked at #1 in Austria, Belgium, Norway, and Sweden. In addition to that, it peaked at #13 in Germany, #15 in Italy, #37 in Spain and #41 in the UK.

Title: Malibu Nights
About:
“Malibu Nights” serves as the title track for Malibu Nights, as well as the closure. The song is about someone’s thoughts after a breakup.
“Malibu Nights” was teased through the release of the album trailer published on 6 September 2018.

Title: Perfect Symphony!
About: Days after the song reached number 1 in United States, Australia, and his native country United Kingdom, Ed Sheeran collaborates with Italian tenor singer Andrea Bocelli for the second remix of the hit ballad-song “Perfect”. This version is a classical “remix” of & and samples the hit track that features Ed Sheeran singing in Italian at the third chorus with Andrea (which also sings in Italian in the second verse and second chorus).

Title: Toca Toca!
About: “Toca Toca” is a song from Romanian band, Fly Project. It was released on 18 September 2013 as a standalone single through Roton Records. The song is a bilingual latin eurodance track, sung in Spanish and English with house influences. The track was an international success, reaching #1 in Poland and within the top 10 in Romania and France. It is certified gold in Italy.

Title: The Spectre
About:
“The Spectre” is a remastered vocal version of Walker’s earlier track, “Spectre”. It was revealed at Walker’s concert “Alan Walker is Heading Home” in Bergen, Norway, on December 21 and 22.
Alan Walker said of the song in an interview with Dance Music Northwest:
The Spectre, it is a newer version of my old song Spectre. It’s like what I did with Fade to Faded.
Alan Walker also said in a press release:
The reactions and feedback from people have been truly amazing, I’m very excited that it’s now being released officially. It’s a song that I specifically want to dedicate to my core fans who’ve been following me since the start.
In September 2023, The Spectre became the epicenter of a viral meme, the “Smurf cat” or its original Russian name, “Shailushai” (Шайлушай; “little scoundrel”)—videos depicting a realistic artwork of the popular cartoon character, The Smurfs, with the song used in the background. Walker took notice of this and eventually incorporated it into his shows in Southeast Asia during the period.
Two official alterations of the track had been made so far: a sped-up version in July 2022, a special slowed-down version during the Smurf cat craze. A follow-up track, “Spectre 2.0”, was released in November 2023.

Song: Hate You + Love You (Feat. AJ Mitchell)
Title: Hate You + Love You!
About: “Hate You + Love You” is a song by American DJ trio Cheat Codes featuring American singer-songwriter AJ Mitchell, released through Epic Records on February 5, 2021, as the eighth single from their debut studio album Hellraisers, Pt. 1 (2021).

Title: Everything Black
About:
“Everything Black,” featuring Mike Taylor, was produced by Unlike Pluto, and released on Monstercat on March 10, 2017. The song was originally previewed on Episode 143 of the Monstercat Podcast.
The song’s release also comes in conjunction with the announcement of his newest tour, under the same name.
On YouTube, the Monstercat visualizer is solid black (per the song name).

Title: How to Love!
About:
Tha Carter IV’s third single. In line with his previous efforts, this acoustic track is a genre bend for the rap game. Once again he’s lamenting a lost love, but in stark contrast to its mirror image How to Hate, this track is sweet and nurturing. Apparently it only takes five tracks for Weezy to have a change of heart.
Check out BigGhost’s Carter IV Review nahmean?

Song: Hey Mama (Feat. Nicki Minaj, Bebe Rexha & Afrojack)
Title: Hey Mama!
About:
This song is a ballad dedicated to Kanye West’s mom Donda West and was written in 2000. It later appeared in its final version on Kanye’s 2005 album Late Registration.
West debuted the song on Oprah with his mother in the audience. Donda liked it so much she actually made it her ringtone at the time.
Sadly, Donda passed away in 2007 of cosmetic surgery complications, and Kanye understandably broke down in tears attempting to perform this song at his next concert, but not before he had the opportunity to perform the song live for her in their kitchen in Chicago.
On the 10th anniversary of Donda West’s passing on November 10, 2017, a group of Kanye fans organized an event titled “Hey Mama Day”. The event aims to encourage fans to listen to the song with the hope of it reappearing on the Billboard Hot 100. It’s motivations were also to combat the release of Taylor Swift’s album Reputation on the anniversary, which many percieved as an intentional move in the long-standing Swift-West feud.

Title: Niggas in Paris!
About:
Kanye West, XXL Magazine, October 2010:
I am where art meets commercial. The sweet spot between the hood and Hollywood. Having a conversation with Karl Lagerfeld and Jay-Z within the same hour. When we’re in Paris dressing all crazy at fashion shows, we listening to Jeezy. Jeezy in Paris, that’s what it is
This song gains thematic weight from the long, rich history of African-Americans in Paris and the rest of France, dating back to the early nineteenth century. Paris has historically been a place where black artists could go to escape vicious racism and gain the appreciation denied them in their homeland. See a short sample list of folks who have done so here.

Title: Warm on a Cold Night!
About:
HONNE’s first ever song, an ode to love, was penned by Andy in the dark in under two hours.
HTV: You wrote “Warm on a Cold Night” in the dark, is this a common theme when you make music?
HONNE: It is yes. Not only musically but also visually too. We love the vibe of a poorly lit/dark room at night especially for writing. I feel it’s a time/place you can truly be yourself. Plus, we all know that the best things happen at nighttime.
— interview with Hunger TV
Upon release, it hit the top spot on Hype Machine, a site measuring a song’s popularity with music blogs, foreshadowing a bright future for the indie electrosoul duo.
Note the radio static at the end of the song. The pair’s next single “All in the Value” begins with five seconds of radio static, suggesting that the two songs' lyrics are connected and detail a continuous story: where “WOACN” describes a couple in love and “AITV” takes place a year after the same couple has broken up.

Title: The Middle
About:
“The Middle” marks the first time Zedd has collaborated with country singer Maren Morris, but he has teamed up with production duo Grey various times before, on songs such as “Starving” and “Adrenaline.”
Zedd first introduced this song in a one-second teaser snippet on Twitter as his first release of 2018. The song was released in full on January 24, 2018.
On the song, Morris sings about trying to find a compromise between her and a lover in order to save a relationship.

Song: Let Me Love You (Feat. Justin Bieber)
Title: Let Me Love You!
About: “Let Me Love You” is Biebs' ode to a girl who seems ready to put an end to their relationship. Over smooth tropical production from DJ Snake, Bieber pleads with his lover to stick around and receive his love. This subject matter has become a popular topic in Justin’s music as seen on 2015’s Purpose and his feature on Major Lazer’s 2016 hit “Cold Water.”

Title: ily (i love you baby)!
About: The lyrics of “ily (i love you baby)” are actually a complete repurposement of the chorus from Frankie Valli’s 1967 smash hit “Can’t Take My Eyes Off You.” With Emilee Flood’s voice, Surf Mesa transformed the track into a new 21st century hit.

Song: This Is What You Came For
Title: This Is What You Came For
About:
Calvin and Rihanna return to the club in “This Is What You Came For.” It’s their third collaboration, following “We Found Love” and “Where Have You Been.”
It makes sense that Harris is eager to get Rihanna’s vocals on this track. Their first collaboration in 2011 launched Harris into mainstream popularity. It was also a boon for Rihanna, becoming one of the best-selling singles in history. Harris had recently tried to lend a track to RiRi’s ANTi, though it didn’t pan out.
Harris and Rihanna recorded this song in New York, while Rihanna was on tour. Harris said they made this track in one all-night session, working until 8 in the morning. Rihanna didn’t hear the final version until they were backstage at Coachella in 2016. Harris told BBC 1:
She’s got the iconic voice. Like, the first time that I heard her sing ‘We Found Love’—I had listened to the guide vocal for so long. I lived with it. I was like, it can’t get any better than this, even if it is Rihanna. And then I heard Rihanna and it was like I was already listening to the radio, it was like I was listening to the top 40. Her voice is out of control. The tone, how distinctive it is, the way she carries it, like every little phrase, annunciation. There’s nothing like her. Stylistically, she can’t be touched.
On July 13th, 2016, Taylor Swift’s reps confirmed that she co-wrote the song under the name Nils Sjoberg, and she and Calvin agreed to keep it a secret. The “her” in the song is likely a reference to Taylor, who was Harris' girlfriend at the time of release. Swift was spotted wearing the cover art weeks before the single’s release, and some fans speculated that she sang the “oohs” in the chorus, which was later confirmed by her team.

Title: Сансара (Samsara)!
About:
Сингл Басты «Сансара» был представлен в преддверии большого концерта в Олимпийском. В записи песни приняли участие музыканты, творчеством которых, исходя из официального описания на YouTube, дорожит команда Gazgolder. В числе этих артистов оказались Диана Арбенина, Александр Ф. Скляр, SunSay, Ант и Скриптонит.
Трек «Сансара» посвящен преемственности поколений, ценности семьи и значимости человека. Главный посыл песни — даже после смерти, человек остается жив в нашей памяти, делах и поступках.
Видео на эту песню набрало более 25 миллионов просмотров. Сама песня стала одним из главных концертов хитов Басты и самым популярным его треком в Apple Music за всё время.

Title: Rise!
About:
Telling the quintessential teenage story of refusing to grow up, “Rise” is a summer-ready house pop single in Jonas Blue’s signature upbeat, tropical style. With bright and bouncy synthesizers over upbeat piano chords, the song has the makings of a summer hit.
Jonas Blue originally reached out to Nebraskan pop-rap duo Jack & Jack after hearing them on an interview with a Los Angeles radio station and decided they were the perfect vocalists for his next hit.

Title: I Know Who I Am!
About:
Yes i know who
I am , she know that GOD AlLmithy is her creator , so no room for fear , Ture Christ JESUS amen

Song: Way Back Home (feat. Conor Maynard) [Sam Feldt Edit]
Title: Way Back Home (Sam Feldt Edit)!
About: Way Back Home is a song by South Korean singer SHAUN. The track was originally released on June 27, 2018 as part of his second extended play Take. The remix by Dutch DJ Sam Feldt, released on December 21, 2018, features vocals from English singer-songwriter Conor Maynard.

Title: Narcos!
About:
On “Narcos,” Migos pay homage to South American cities, culture, and drug lords. The lyrics describe drug dealing whilst drawing several similarities with the popular Netflix original series of the same name, Narcos, which is centered around Colombian drug lord Pablo Escobar.
Whilst the Migos' make multiple references to Latin countries, the official sample is taken from “Espoir” by Les Difficiles de Pétion-Ville, a song from Haïti.

Title: Titanium!
About:
