Sia and David Guetta’s massive track “Titanium” soared as the fourth single from Guetta’s Nothing but the Beat. Visceral, tall lyrics accompanying daring vocals and addictive production, “Titanium” acts as an anthem against the critics and the bullies, equating vicious criticisms to bullets.
The song was originally written for Mary J. Blige among other artists (including Katy Perry and Alicia Keys). Blige recorded the song but instead of releasing her version, Guetta used Sia’s original demo without her permission (complying with Katy’s suggestion). It soon became a hit, and Sia acknowledges the choice as a major benefactor in her songwriting career.
Chartwise, it was extremely successful, becoming a top ten hit in 17 countries and moving 3.8 million units in the United States alone.

Title: Wake Me Up!
About:
“Wake Me Up” is the lead single for Avicii’s first studio album #True. It features Aloe Blacc, who wrote the lyrics on a plane flying from Geneva, Switzerland, to L.A.
Like many other Avicii hits, this song was first introduced at the Ultra Music Festival with the live performance of a bluegrass band, swaying so far away from the typical EDM spectrum. It infuses Irish folk music with house beats, with vocals from American soul singer Aloe Blacc.
The song spent 53 weeks on the Billboard charts (21 of them being in the top 10), making it the first Dance/Electronic song to stay over a year on that chart. It never reached the #1 spot, though; it only peaked at #4.
The song also broke all of Avicii’s personal records of sales, topping music charts in 22 different countries as well as breaking the top 10 mark in 6 others. Commercially, the song has been a bigger hit than his previous “Levels” that only peaked at #60 on Billboard’s Top 100 and only spent 20 weeks on the charts.

Title: Love Is Gone!
About:
“Love is Gone” is an emotion-laden track from bass powerhouse duo SLANDER, capturing one’s denial in the face of a doomed relationship. Dylan Matthew, who appeared on tracks like 2018’s “Hate Being Alone”, supplies ethereal vocals, while Scott and Derek segue seamlessly from a siren-heavy, dubstep build to wispy, uplifting future bass.
The track is the duo’s first to have a full-length music video, created in collaboration with 3D-rendering artist Roboto, and appears on the first compilation of SLANDER and NGHTMRE’s label Gud Vibrations.

Title: Whatever It Takes!
About:
While the fast-paced verses of “Whatever it Takes” propels Imagine Dragons into new territory, the big and booming chorus retains a sense of their signature sound.
The song is about being the best at what you do no matter the obstacle, a theme found to be prevalent on the album.
The song was released as the first promotional single from EVOLVE after official singles, “Believer” and “Thunder.”

Title: Bad and Boujee!
About:
“Bad and Boujee" is a trap anthem about making money and spending time with women who have expensive taste. “Boujee” is an intentional misspelling of “bougie,” which is slang for bourgeois, and refers to the materialism of society’s middle class.
This Metro Boomin-produced track marks the first collaboration between Northside/North Atlanta’s Migos, minus Takeoff, and Philly’s Lil Uzi Vert.
“Bad and Boujee” is Migos' highest-charting track yet, as well as their first to top the Billboard Hot 100 chart.

Title: Rather Be!
About:
The fourth single from Clean Bandit’s album New Eyes was written by the band along with songwriter Jimmy Napes and reportedly inspired by Rosie Gaines' “Closer Than Close”. The band enlisted Jess Glynne on the vocals after Grace Chatto heard her on “My Love” and thought “this voice is perfect for the song that we’ve written.”
“Rather Be” was a huge commercial success, debuting at number one on the UK charts and was the highest and fastest selling single of January 2014. In February 2014, the song shot past Daft Punk’s “Get Lucky” record with 1.09 million plays in a week on UK Spotify.

Title: Believers
About:
“Believers” is the self titled track on Mayday’s latest album released on July 16, 2013 by Strange Music.
WRITTEN BY: Benjamin Miller, Bernardo Garcia, Aaron Eckhart, Gianni Perocarpi PUBLISHED BY: Songs of Snake and Bat (BMI) obo Songs of Snake and Bat, Sunrise To Sunset Soundworks, Indigo Stew, Fat Sugar And Salt / Snake and Bat Music (ASCAP) obo Snake and Bat Music, Indigo Hue VOCALS BY: Bernz & Wrekonize GUITAR PLAYED BY: Plex Luthor KEYS PLAYED BY: Plex Luthor PRODUCED BY: Plex Luthor & Gianni Ca$h

Title: Shape of You!
About:
Ed Sheeran released “Shape of You” together with “Castle On The Hill.” The two songs were the first releases from his third album, ÷ (Divide), following in the mathematical footsteps of his + (Plus) and x (Multiply) albums.
“Shape Of You” is based around a tropical house-inspired lead melody and dancehall beat. A rhythm is given by xylophones, guitars, and percussions. The style and rhythm have been noted as close to the Sia successes, “Cheap Thrills”, which has caused some controversy. Ed has denied receiving inspiration from the track. Just like “Cheap Thrills”, however, “Shape of You” was written with the intention of it being recorded by Rihanna, but Sheeran later decided to keep it for himself.
In the song, Sheeran sings about meeting someone at a bar and falling in love with her; a love distinct from those in much of his previous work as it’s more focused on the physical than the emotional.
Sheeran had teased the song through a tweet that he had posted on January 5, 2017. The video had a colon that would then merge into a human body, referencing the “body”-inspiration of the song.
As of August 29, 2017, “Shape Of You” set a Billboard record, spending 33 straight weeks in the Top 10 of the Hot 100 chart.
On September 22, 2017, the track became the most streamed song of all time on the Spotify plaform at 1.318 billion plays. Sheeran passed Drake who previously held the record with “One Dance.”
The song won a Grammy Best Pop Solo Performance at the 2018 Grammys held on January 28, 2018.
On January 24th, 2019, “Shape Of You” was certified Diamond by the RIAA, selling over ten million units.

Title: Be Kind
About:
“Be Kind” is an empowering song that questions the motives of a significant other in a relationship. Instead of fully committing to the relationship, the person seems to be searching for something new. Amidst all of this, Halsey reassures this person she’d be there to support them. It’s likely this song is about rapper G-Eazy who Halsey previously dated. It was reported that G-Eazy cheated on Halsey, which could serve as inspiration for this song.
The song marks the first collaboration between the two popstars. Rumors of the song surfaced online on April 24, 2020, with the artists confirming the song less than a week later.

Title: Blueberry Faygo!
About:
“Blueberry Faygo” was expected to be the first single from Seattle rapper Lil Mosey in advance of his upcoming project, The Land of Make Believe. It was later added onto Mosey’s debut album, Certified Hitmaker, near the end of February 2020.
The song’s title is a reference to a flavor of Faygo, a soft drink company commonly referenced in hip-hop culture in relation to drug use, specifically, the abuse of lean and codeine.
While the song was never officially previewed by Mosey, it suffered a partial leak on June 5, 2019. In the months before the song officially released, the leak gained traction thanks to TikTok, and uploads of the leak saw millions of views across multiple platforms. However, the officially-released version of the track featured a new second verse which had only been briefly previewed by the verse’s recording studio.

Title: 3 Musketeers!
About:
“3 Musketeers” is ppcocaine’s first official released single since her breakout hit, “DDLG,” and on this track features NextYoungin—ppcocaine’s close affiliate. Via TikTok, ppcocaine went on to talk about how her parents didn’t approve of her sexuality and other topics that impacted the song’s illicit meaning and lyrics.
ppcocaine first previewed “3 Musketeers” on June 26, 2020, through a now-deleted Instagram post, then released the song weeks later on July 7, 2020.

Title: Who Do You Love?!
About:
“Who Do You Love?” is a song by American hip hop recording artist YG from his debut studio album My Krazy Life (2014). It was released on February 20, 2014, as the third single from the album. The song, produced by frequent collaborator DJ Mustard, features a guest appearance from Canadian rapper Drake. The song has since peaked at number 54 on the US Billboard Hot 100 chart.
YG has stated that the inspiration of the song came from a Lil Boosie song of the same name. The song was originally intended as the first single off of “My Krazy Life” but YG chose to go against the label due to him preferring to not rely on Drake for success. The song initially leaked New Year’s Eve 2013 after a fan impersonating a DJ tricked YG into emailing him the song.

Song: Get Lucky (feat. Pharrell Williams and Nile Rodgers)
Title: Get Lucky!
About: The lead single from Daft Punk’s fourth and final studio album, Random Access Memories, “Get Lucky” was first teased in snippets on Saturday Night Live. It peaked in the top 3 in every country it charted in and, because of its performance in the United States, got the duo their first song to reach the top 10 on the Billboard charts. According to an interview with Pitchfork, the original version featured the Wurlitzer electronic piano, but because of the revisions, took 18 months to complete from start to finish.

Song: Way Back Home (feat. Conor Maynard) [Sam Feldt Festival Mix]
Title: Way Back Home (Sam Feldt Edit)!
About: Way Back Home is a song by South Korean singer SHAUN. The track was originally released on June 27, 2018 as part of his second extended play Take. The remix by Dutch DJ Sam Feldt, released on December 21, 2018, features vocals from English singer-songwriter Conor Maynard.

Title: Boom, Boom, Boom, Boom!!!
About: “Boom, Boom, Boom, Boom!!” is a 1999 party anthem from Dutch Eurodance group Vengaboys, released as the lead single from The Party Anthem. Lyrically, the song is about sex.

Title: One Kiss
About:
“One Kiss” is the second single released by Calvin Harris in 2018. It was preceded by “Nuh Ready Nuh Ready,” which came out two months prior.
A header image on Harris' Facebook page from March 20, 2018 revealed that the song was forthcoming when the QR code in the image was scanned.
Harris officially announced the track on Twitter on April 2, 2018, four days before its release.

Title: Lovesick Girls!
About:
“Lovesick Girls” is about yearning for love, in spite of being fully aware that the pursuit could end in heartbreak.
On September 28, 2020, “Lovesick Girls” was officially confirmed as the leading track off BLACKPINK’s first full-length Korean record THE ALBUM. Many fans had concluded that a week prior to the announcement after they found the file name to the first album teaser was “LSG_teaser.jpg” on the girl group’s official website.
On September 27, 2020, BLACKPINK released THE ALBUM concept videos with a snippet of “Lovesick Girls”, which could be recognized by Shazam, a song identifier app.

Title: Cold Water!
About:
“Cold Water” is about coming together to face adversity and to say that you have your partner’s back no matter what the situation is.
Diplo and Justin Bieber hinted about the song through some cryptic tweets. Diplo then announced to Fact Mag that “Cold Water” would be premiered on his Beats 1 radio show Lazer Sound “sometime Summer 2016.”
In early July, Justin announced their collaboration on the project with a tweet:
On July 20th, in his final teaser for the track, Justin began releasing pieces of the lyrics to “Cold Water” in the form of direct messages to his fans on Twitter who used the #ColdWater2Days hashtag.

Title: Cradles
About: In “Cradles,” Sub Urban takes us into his fantasy world in which adults are free to act and speak as they like. He compares this to the ways of innocent children who haven’t learned to mask their true identities. Reflecting on the reality of adulthood, he notes that we carry an unspoken obligation to mask ourselves. Lest we come across as rude, brash, or even crazy, we filter our true thoughts before speaking aloud and suppress our desires instead of acting them out.

Title: walk but in a garden
About: LLusion first used this beat to make a lo-fi remix of Comethazine’s hit song ‘Walk’ and it is titled, ‘if Comethazine made lofi hip hop (Walk)’ on Soundcloud.

Song: Summer Days (feat. Macklemore & Patrick Stump of Fall Out Boy) (시몬스 침대 광고 삽입곡)
Title: Summer Days!
About:
“Summer Days” is a song by Dutch producer Martin Garrix, featuring American rapper Macklemore and singer Patrick Stump of Fall Out Boy, and it is the first collaboration between the three artists. The song was released on 25 April 2019. The music video for the song was released on 22 May 2019
The song, made “to help get you into that summer state of mind”, tells the classic story of summer love and carefree days. The song’s distinctly catchy chorus, featuring Patrick Stump’s signature falsetto complemented by a relaxing guitar riff, stands in contrast to the energetic, synth-laden verses of Macklemore.
The song was unveiled on 20 April 2019, all three artists shared a picture with the caption “I got this feeling on a Summer day”, and a few days later the song got released.

Title: Slide!
About:
“Slide” details the story of those who just want to enter into a richer person’s life so they can live like they’re more affluent. It arrived on February 24, becoming the first original song released by Scottish DJ/producer Calvin Harris in 2017. It features vocals from Quavo and Offset of the Atlanta trap trio Migos and singer-songwriter Frank Ocean, marking the first collaboration between Harris and either of the two features.
The single was officially announced on February 21 via Calvin’s Twitter after several snippets were teased by Harris on Snapchat.
On June 2, 2017, a second version of the song featuring an intro by Quavo and an alternate verse by Takeoff leaked on Audiomack.

Title: Darkside!
About:
“Darkside” is the second chapter of the World of Walker trilogy. The single, by Norwegian DJ, Alan Walker the sequel to ‘All Falls Down" and features Au/Ra and Tomine Harket.
The lyrics are about a relationship in which the lines of what it is are blurred, it’s not a romance, but it’s not a friendship. Both Au/Ra and Tomine Harket talk about experiencing everything and just letting what happens happen.
The song was later re-released on Walker’s first studio album Different World.

Title: Dear Mama!
About:
“Dear Mama” is the song that made it OK to admit: even if they kill people and sell drugs, deep down, thugs are all sensitive mama’s boys.
It was the third hip-hop act ever (after Grandmaster Flash and the Furious Five’s “The Message”“ and Public Enemy’s album, Fear of a Black Planet) to be placed in the Library of Congress.
Tupac’s mom, Afeni Shakur, died on May 2nd, 2016.
Watch Tupac talk about this song in the video below:
Look around you in this studio right now. I have women working on my music. They understand where I’m coming from. So does my mama. I always play my music for her before it comes out. Why do you think I wrote ‘Dear Mama’? I wrote it for my mama because I love her and I felt I owed her something deep. (2Pac, 1995)
To see annotations on this song by the cast and author of the Broadway musical Holler If Ya Hear Me, which tells a powerful story of today using Pac’s music, see here
In Eminem’s tribute letter 2Pac he states that even though he had a bad relationship with his Mom; “I could put ‘Dear Mama’ in and damn near be in tears.”

Title: There For You
About:
“There For You” is a song by Dutch DJ and producer Martin Garrix with Australian singer, Troye Sivan. Lyrically, the song is about supporting each other and being by your lover’s side. In a series of tweets, Troye Sivan stated that the lyrics, for him personally, are about his fans who have stuck with him since day one.
The song was premiered live during Martin Garrix’s set at Coachella 2017, 22nd April.

Title: Hymn For The Weekend!
About:
Coldplay’s Queen B duet is an ode to enjoying a significant other you love and cherish. In an interview, Martin described the origins of the collaboration and its sound:
I thought I’d like to have a song called “Drinks on Me” where you sit on the side of a club and buy everyone drinks because you’re so fucking cool. I was chuckling about that, when this melody came – “drinks on me, drinks on me” – then the rest of the song came out. I presented it to the rest of the band and they said, “We love this song, but there’s no way you can sing ‘drinks on me.’” So that changed into “drink from me” and the idea of having an angelic person in your life. Then that turned into asking Beyoncé to sing on it.
It’s one of four collaborations on the album; the others are Tove Lo on “Fun”, Gwyneth Paltrow on “Everglow”, and a Barack Obama sample on “Kaleidoscope.”
Apparently, Beyoncé also made their album 90% better.
She made our album 90% better. And we were already 20% good. So now we’re like 110% the band we used to be. You do the math.
Before the album’s December 4, 2015 release date, it was previewed on Twitter on November 24, 2015. The official music video was uploaded on the 29th of January.

Title: Don’t Let Me Down!
About:
Daya asks her boyfriend to save her and prove that the good boys don’t hide away.
“Don’t Let Me Down” is the first single The Chainsmokers released since their single “Roses” achieved chart success. Andrew Taggart said of the track:
I’m super proud of it. I think it’s one of the best songs I’ve written and produced. I think people are going to be pretty blown away by it and it’s very different than “Roses”, which is nice.
The production is frantic and chord-heavy, with a final-drop switch up.

Title: Don’t Touch My Hair!
About:
“Don’t Touch My Hair” sees Solange setting limits to the degree in which she’ll compromise her beliefs and identity to satisfy other people.
Asking to touch a Black woman’s hair is a racial microaggression masquerading as a compliment. In a patriarchal, white-dominated society, it denies black women respect, consent and agency over their own bodies. Solange’s “Don’t Touch My Hair” can be read as an explicit rejection of this behavior, as a simple establishment of boundaries, or as a powerful statement of personal identity.
In a personal essay posted in the month of the track’s release, Solange broke down the hostilities she faces when enjoying herself in predominantly white spaces:
You and your friends have been called the N word, been approached as prostitutes, and have had your hair touched in a predominantly white bar just around the corner from the same venue. You know that people of colors’ ‘spaces’ are attacked every single day, but many will not be able to see it that way.

Title: Tie Me Down
About:
“Tie Me Down” is a single by American electronic music producer Gryffin, featuring singer-songwriter Elley Duhé. The track was released on August 3, 2018.
The lyrics come from the point of view of one partner in a relationship, who feels that their paramour has become entangled in a darker side of their life, and wants to bring them back in.

Title: Beautiful Now!
About:
“Beautiful Now” is the 2nd radio single off of Zedd’s True Colors.
Jon doesn’t know what’s going to happen or how long it will last, but right now everything is right.
Just because they don’t know how or why they met, doesn’t mean they should pass up this amazing opportunity and live like they’re Beautiful Now.
Partial resemblance to his song “Ungrateful Eyes”.

Title: Rockabye!
About:
“Rockabye” is a dancehall song about all of the single and struggling mothers in the world. The track is the second single released from Clean Bandit’s second studio album What Is Love?, and their first single as a trio after violinist Neil Milan left the band earlier in the same week. It features guest vocals from Jamaican rapper Sean Paul and English singer Anne-Marie.
The song’s lyrics describe how single mothers bear hardships to raise their children and give them the best possible life, and the upbeat music turns that theme into a motivational track.
“Rockabye” was leaked onto the internet prematurely on the 20th October and was officially released the day after.

Title: Perfect Symphony!
About: Days after the song reached number 1 in United States, Australia, and his native country United Kingdom, Ed Sheeran collaborates with Italian tenor singer Andrea Bocelli for the second remix of the hit ballad-song “Perfect”. This version is a classical “remix” of & and samples the hit track that features Ed Sheeran singing in Italian at the third chorus with Andrea (which also sings in Italian in the second verse and second chorus).

Title: New Thang
About:
This song is about Redfoo trying to get a girl to be his “new thang.” That is, he doesn’t want anything serious. He just wants good sex, and to have a good time.
Most of the song is just thinly veiled sexual metaphors, which is actually pretty cool. Because it means that you can get down and dirty to it, but you can play it in front of younger people and it’s mostly okay for them to hear
The music video chronicles his attempts to pick up hot ladies, thwarted at each hook by the sax man. During the last hook, Redfoo learns to plays this bit on the sax, so he’s successful in getting all the girls.

Title: iPad
About:
Initially teased at the end of The Chainsmokers’ performance of their previous single “High” at One Vanderbilt’s SUMMIT in New York City, “iPad” is the second single from The Chainsmokers’ upcoming fourth studio album.
Speaking on the song in an Instagram post prior to the song’s release, Drew Taggart said the song served as a journal for him, letting him express his feelings of isolation. This personal nature is reflected in the fact that “iPad” only has two songwriters: the members of The Chainsmokers themselves.
The song was a commercial success for the duo, marking The Chainsmokers’ twentieth top 10 song on Billboard’s Hot Dance/Electronic Songs chart, debuting and peaking at #6 on the chart.

Title: On My Way
About:
“On My Way” was created by DJ Alan Walker to celebrate the first year anniversary of “PUBG Mobile”, teaming up with Sabrina Carpenter and Latin artist Farruko. On March 21, players were able to hear the song while they’re waiting in the “PUBG Mobile” lobby or while driving a car during a match.
On March 13, the “PUBG Mobile” team took to twitter to tease the arrival of the song
Alan Walker also tweeted a promotional photo for the song and game the day after the release
In “On My Way”, the theme centers around abandonment. Sabrina and Farruko sings about their decision to leave for “the greater good” hinting that the people they left behind might not understand that it’s for the best.

Title: Whiskey Lullaby!
About: “Whiskey Lullaby” is the title of a country song composed by Bill Anderson and Jon Randall. It was first recorded by Brad Paisley as a duet with Alison Krauss on Paisley’s 2003 album Mud on the Tires, and released on April 12, 2004, as that album’s third single, and the eleventh chart single of Paisley’s career. The song reached a peak of number three on the Billboard Hot Country Singles & Tracks (now Hot Country Songs) charts, and 41 on the Billboard Hot 100. The song won the 2005 Country Music Association Song of the Year award. It is one of Paisley’s three songs certified Platinum by the Recording Industry Association of America, the others being “She’s Everything” and “Then”.

Title: Light It Up!
About:
“Light It Up” is the 5th song off of Young Thug’s debut album, So Much Fun, and it is the first of four songs on SMF produced by Pi'erre Bourne. This song was first previewed by Young Thug on fellow YSL artist Lil Duke’s birthday, and it dropped along with the rest of the album on Thug’s own birthday.
“Light it up” can refer to sparking up some drugs, putting on jewelry, or shooting up a block.

Title: Anti-Hero!
About:
Described by Taylor herself as “one of my favorite songs that I have ever written”, “Anti-Hero” is the scintillating lead single from Midnights, her tenth studio album. Largely serving as a parallel to 2019’s “The Archer”, the song shows a darker self-perception from Swift than usual, and goes into detail about what she feels are her personal failures.
The track’s title and track number were revealed in the sixth video of her TikTok game Midnight Mayhem With Me on October 2nd, 2022. The next day, Swift released a ‘Behind The Song’ video to discuss the meaning and purpose behind the track, saying she “struggle[s] a lot with the idea that my life has become unmanageably sized", and described the song as a “guided tour" of the things she hates about herself.
On Halloween night of 2022, the song was announced to have debuted at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100. Marking her ninth number one single and fifth number one debut, “Anti-Hero” joined nine other songs from Midnights in constructing the entire top 10 – the first time ever in Billboard history.

Title: Bangarang!
About:
The breakthrough hit for EDM producer Skrillex (born Sonny John Moore), reaching the top 10 in Australia and Belgium. Despite lower chart positions in those countries, it was also a platinum-certified single in New Zealand, Sweden and the United Kingdom.
Sirah, the featured rapper, was also in Skrillex’s previous track “Weekends”.

Title: WTF (Where They From)!
About:
Rap legend Missy Elliott returned with her first single in 7 years, and it’s a platinum-certified hit. She enlists her old friend, longtime collaborator, and fellow Virginia native Pharrell Williams for the beat and second verse on this bouncy dance track.
The song peaked at #22 on the Billboard Hot 100 and #8 on the Billboard Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Charts. The incredible music video has amassed 50 million views (as of April 2017) on Youtube and takes Missy’s quirkiness up another notch.

Title: Sing Me to Sleep!
About: If you like the song, you’ll love the official live performance, search for it on youtube :)

Title: Solo!
About:
On “Solo,” Frank touches on various aspects of being alone, such as self-love or waiting for a call from your lover. It’s not entirely clear if this song is specifically about experiences he’s had.
The song’s title has two interpretations. Throughout the song, Frank sings about how he is alone, or solo, but, through the chorus Frank explains that he’s “so low” emotionally and that he needs to “get high.”

Title: Instruction!
About:
“Instruction” is released as the third single from Jax Jones’s debut studio album. The samba-infused dance hall anthem is a collaboration with American pop singer, Demi Lovato and British rapper Stefflon Don.
The song is a summer banger with heavy drum assist where Lovato sings about self empowerment.
The cover art of the single follows Jones' theme of using household products, this one being inspired by Sriracha sauce.

Title: Satisfied!
Singer: Original Broadway Cast of Hamilton & Renée Elise Goldsberry
About:
“Satisfied” — whose lines and title draw inspiration from an actual letter Angelica wrote to Hamilton — reinforces one of the most important themes of Hamilton, that our interpretation of events depends on who’s telling the story. One of the most innovative songs in the show, here we start at Angelica’s speech at Eliza and Alexander’s wedding, then the whole set—actors, props, even the turntables operating in the floor—dramatically “rewind” to the scene of their meeting in “Helpless,” which then gets retold from Angelica’s perspective. Not to be upstaged by a literal stage, Renée Elise Goldsberry gives an absolutely powerhouse performance as Angelica Schuyler trying to sublimate her feelings for Alexander into her feelings of duty toward her family.
Miranda says of the song:
The lyrics to “Satisfied” — in which Angelica Schuyler recounts how Hamilton and her sister Eliza met and married — are some of the most intricate I’ve ever written. I can’t even rap them, but Renee Elise Goldsberry, who plays Angelica — that’s her conversational speed. That’s how fast she thinks. You really get the sense that Angelica’s the smartest person in the room, and she reads Hamilton within a moment of meeting him.
Miranda wrote “Satisfied” while working on the short-lived TV series Do No Harm, so technically, he wrote this song while already at work. (Maaan, the man is non-stop.)
The notion of satisfaction and Hamilton’s difficulty in attaining it speaks not only to his general restlessness and driven nature but also explicitly foreshadows the eventual duel whose goal is, of course, satisfaction after offense.
The song’s chord structure is also a half-time version of the insistent, driving progression in “My Shot” and similar numbers (for the theory nerds, that’s i-III-iv-vi-V).
And the rising and falling piano melody is somewhat evocative of the beat for Biggie’s “Notorious Thugs”.

Title: Mackie Messer!
About:
“Die Moritat von Mackier Messer”, known in English as “The Ballad of Mack the Knife” or “Mack the Knife”, was a song written by Bertolt Brecht & Kurt Weill for the play ‘Die Dreigroschenoper’ (The Threepenny Opera).
According to this article from openculture.com, “The song…was written in a rush only a few days before the August 31, 1928 Berlin premiere, after the actor who played MacHeath complained that his entrance wasn’t grand enough. Brecht wrote the words overnight and asked his collaborator, the composer Kurt Weill, to set them to music. The song is modeled after the Moritat (from “mord” meaning murder and “tat” meaning deed), a kind of medieval ballad traditionally sung by traveling minstrels recounting the crimes of notorious murderers.”
The lyrics translated into English

Title: AM/PM!
About: Scrim and Ruby embody the Lil Half Cut and Yung Slumber personas, and talk about suicidal tendencies, drug addiction, emptiness, and the wish for death.

Title: Just What I Am!
About:
Released October 2, 2012, this was the first track released off of Kid Cudi’s studio album, Indicud. The song is produced by Cudi himself.
The title is an allusion to Exodus 3:14 where God tells Moses “I am who I am.” This connection is draw upon throughout the song. Cudi and Chip use the phrase as a justification for their weed use: they love to get high, but that’s just who they are, and since God made them this way, there can be nothing wrong.
In 2016 “Just What I Am” was certified Platinum.

Title: Anti-Hero!
About:
Described by Taylor herself as “one of my favorite songs that I have ever written”, “Anti-Hero” is the scintillating lead single from Midnights, her tenth studio album. Largely serving as a parallel to 2019’s “The Archer”, the song shows a darker self-perception from Swift than usual, and goes into detail about what she feels are her personal failures.
The track’s title and track number were revealed in the sixth video of her TikTok game Midnight Mayhem With Me on October 2nd, 2022. The next day, Swift released a ‘Behind The Song’ video to discuss the meaning and purpose behind the track, saying she “struggle[s] a lot with the idea that my life has become unmanageably sized", and described the song as a “guided tour" of the things she hates about herself.
On Halloween night of 2022, the song was announced to have debuted at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100. Marking her ninth number one single and fifth number one debut, “Anti-Hero” joined nine other songs from Midnights in constructing the entire top 10 – the first time ever in Billboard history.

Title: Lie to Me!
About: “Lie to Me” is the third track on 5 Seconds of Summer’s third album, Youngblood. It was first played at their promotional “5SOS3 Tour” in Stockholm, Sweden. The music video for “Lie to Me” was released on January 18, 2019 and their acoustic version was released on February 1, 2019. On December 21, 2018, they released a remix of the song featuring Julia Michaels.

Title: Turn Down For What!
About:
“Turn Down for What” is a single by DJ Snake & Lil Jon released on December 18, 2013. It was co-produced by both artists and co-written by Tchami. The song and its viral music video, featuring a guy who manages to break things and do other weird things with his crotch, popularized the use of the phrase.
This is also the 13th track on the soundtrack of Furious 7.
The original demo version contained a Redman sample saying: “It’s the countdown, bang the underground”

Title: Знаешь что мне нужно (You know what I need)!

Song: Sex Apeal (Max Farenthide Radio Edit) (Feat. Mike W.)
Title: SEX APPEAL!
The Bad Touch!
About:
Over a sample of Pet Shop Boys‘ “In the Night”, Jimmy Pop finds himself making a sensual appeal to women everywhere about the art of intercourse.
This song later went on to define Bloodhound Gang’s legacy, becoming their biggest charting hit and cementing them as a pop band in Europe and specifically Germany. This song would be referenced and remixed, noticeably in Eminem’s 2000 hit “The Real Slim Shady.”

Title: Work Hard, Play Hard!
About:
Arguably the most notorious and renown songs off the “O.N.I.F.C” album. This song has a simple meaning, the more harder one grinds the more fun that person can have. Wiz amplifies this message by talking about his jewels and success.
OFFICIAL ARTWORK COVER:
OFFICIAL MUSIC VIDEO:

Song: Bomba (Klass Club Mix) (영화 '검사외전 강동원 댄스' 삽입곡)
Title: Bad Bunny & Bomba Estéreo - Ojitos Lindos (English Translation)!
Don’t Wake Me Up
About:
“Don’t Wake Me Up” finds Chris clinging on to a past love, which he can now only experience while he’s asleep. The intro, spoken by Priscilla Renea, attests to this. While Chris is asleep, he is at his happiest with this girl, and he pleads to never be woken up because he doesn’t want to return to reality, where he’s no longer with this girl.
“Don’t Wake Me Up” is an upbeat EDM track co-produced by Benny Benassi, and was released as the fourth single from Fortune. The track was initially recorded for Madonna’s MDNA album; however, didn’t make the cut due to “time constraints.”
“Don’t Wake Me Up” charted well around the world, and was a top 10 hit in the United States.

Title: Careless Whisper!
About:
Although officially released by Wham!, this was George Michael’s first breakaway single. The song explores the guilt felt by George after he cheats on a partner, and his inability to “dance” with them in the same way after this betrayal.
Despite the song’s ubiquity in pop culture since its arrival on the scene in 1985, George Michael has confessed to having no particular connection to the situation this song portrays. He wrote the song to pass the time on a bus, when he was just seventeen. In his 1991 memoir, Bare, Michael admits he regrets that it
was not an integral part of my emotional development… It disappoints me that you can write a lyric very flippantly—and not a particularly good lyric—and it can mean so much to so many people. That’s disillusioning for a writer.
Later in an interview with People magazine, he made similar remarks about the song.
I’m fed up with ‘Careless Whisper.’ I don’t know why it made such an impression. Is it that so many people have cheated? I have no idea. But it’s ironic that I wrote it when I was 17 and didn’t know much about anything. Certainly nothing much about relationships.

Title: Lonely Together!
About:
“Lonely Together” is about Rita’s struggle with a collapsed relationship her drunk mind wants her to revive, although she knows there won’t be romantic love anymore. However, remaining a friendship with that person would make her feel lonely, so she attempts a one night stand.
The song features British singer-songwriter Rita Ora and is part of Avicii’s comeback EP Avīci.
On June 28, 2017, Rita and songwriter Ali Tamposi performed an acoustic version of the song at an intimate gig hosted by NOTION Magazine. There were no hints given that the song was a collaboration between her and Avicii.
A month later, Avicii previewed a snippet of the studio version in an Instagram post.
In the 2018 MTV VMAs, “Lonely Together” took the award for Best Dance Video.

Song: Feliz Navidad (펠리스 나비다) (I Wanna Wish You A Merry Christmas)
Title: Feliz Navidad!
About:
“Feliz Navidad” es una canción navideña billingüe, con éxito histórico en varios países alrededor del mundo; hispanos y angloparlantes.
Esta canción se ha convertido en una de las canciones de Navidad más descargadas y transmitidas en los Estados Unidos y ha sido declarada por ASCAP como una de las 25 mejores canciones de Navidad más interpretadas y registradas en todo el mundo.

Title: Wonderful Christmastime!
About: Wonderful Christmastime was recorded by Paul during the sessions for his album McCartney II. It was originally issued as a single not found on any album. It was later included as a bonus song on the 2011 reissue of McCartney II, as well as the 1993 reissue of the Wings album Back to The Egg. Despite having been a solo single, the music video for this song features the members of Paul’s band Wings.

Title: 24K Magic!
About:
“24K Magic” is a braggadocious party anthem described by Bruno Mars as an “invitation to the party” of his third album of the same name. It combines modern-referencing lyrics (like “#blessed”) with 80s-style production helmed by Shampoo Press & Curl and The Stereotypes.
The official video for the track dropped October 7, 2016. It’s the perfect complement to the party anthem, an opulent dance-fest, with Mars joined by an incredible looking squad living it up in Los Angeles and Las Vegas.
The song won the Grammy for “Record of the Year” at the 2018 Grammys held on January 28, 2018.

Title: pink skies!
About: From where the photo used as the cover art for kinda was taken, Paul Klein has spent many times alone after watching the sunset. He’s said it was on the very slab of concrete in the picture where he found the inspiration for “pink skies.”

Song: Ignite (2016 League Of Legends World Championship)
Title: Ignite!
About:
“Ignite” is a vocal edit of its eponymous instrumental version which was released on April 7, 2017. This vocal version features South Korean singer Seungri and Norwegian singer-songwriter Julie Bergan, the latter of whom previously performed with Walker on his remix of “Legends Never Die.”
Walker debuted the song during his performance at Coachella 2018, stating that it “will be released very soon.” The song was officially announced on May 5, 2018, along with a “prologue” video.

Title: Happy Now
About:
On “Happy Now,” singer-songwriter Elley Duhé sings about an ex who left her, wondering if they are really happy with what they did.
The song was first teased in August 2017 in an interview with Billboard. The song was initially titled “Are You Happy Now,” featured Norwegian singer Sigrid, and was played to Billboard during the same interview—along with the demo version of “The Middle” with Sarah Aarons’s vocals.

On “1-800-273-8255,” Logic shows that suicide is never the answer to anyone’s problems. He wants people to know that there is always help available, such as the National Suicide Prevention Lifeline, whose phone number is the title of this song. Although 9-8-8 became the new phone number for the lifeline since 2022, the original phone number still continues to remain available. In fact, the song was released in partnership with the NSPL.
In an interview with Genius, Logic said that this song details the story of someone calling the NSPL wanting to commit suicide but after a conversation with the NSPL representative, the caller soon after decides he wants to live.
Logic teased the song various times on both Twitter and on Instagram stories as well.

Title: better off!
About:
On “better off,” Ariana dissects her relationship and comes to realize her partner isn’t good for her. Originally, the song wasn’t going to be included on Sweetener as Ariana felt it was “too honest about what [she] was afraid of happening” to her relationship at the time. She added it to the album after “the people in [her] life” changed.
It has been speculated that “better off” is about Ariana’s relationship with Mac Miller, which ended three months before the song’s release. Ariana had previously denounced their relationship as “toxic,” and editor’s notes for Sweetener on Apple Music expressed similar sentiments, saying “she exits a toxic relationship in ‘better off’.” However, according to Kaydence, she wrote the song and introduced it to Ariana. Ariana recorded the song on July 19, 2016, one week before the announcement of her breakup with Ricky Alvarez. While the song was not written about Ariana’s experience, her explanation for it being a late addition to Sweetener suggests the song was representative of her relationship with Miller.

Title: Cotton Candy Clouds
Dessert
About: “Dessert” is a dance electronic song by American singer and producer Dawin. Released in 2015.

Title: Here Without You!
About:
The band’s second biggest song, behind “Kryptonite,” is an anthem aimed at a missing or lost lover, designed to straddle the distance between them and communicate just how desperately the central figure misses their soulmate.
Brad Arnold said:
Here Without You" was a song that I wrote for my now-ex-wife. I didn’t write it for her, I guess, she was just mainly the inspiration for the thought process. And it was really kind of about all of us, in a way. The song’s about being away from someone, or missing them. And it really doesn’t matter if you’re here without them for all day or all month. It’s about the loneliness and missing of somebody. But in a way, people take that as a little bit of a sad song, and I kind of meant it as a happy song, because it’s talking about being here without you, but she’s still with me in my dreams. And tonight, it’s only you and me, so the song was really about that dream. And being in a state of peace, because you’ve got that person there with you in your sleep. In that way I kind of meant for it to be a little bit of a happy song.
The track reached number 5 on the US Billboard charts, and sold over 2 million copies in the US. The track featured an emotional video made up of shots of the band playing the track live in the studio.

Song: This Girl (Kungs Vs. Cookin' On 3 Burners)
Title: This Girl!
About:
Inspired by Psalm 37:24, “This Girl” is the story of Lauren comparing her spiritual life to a physical journey, specifically in how she finds God.
Though he may stumble, he will not fall, for the LORD upholds him with his hand.
Like most songs, “This Girl” gets its title from the chorus. Lauren explains that it goes deeper than that, saying it “holds hands” with the nickname that her grandfather used to call her growing up: “That Girl.”

Title: Body (Remix)!
About:
This is the remix to Russ & Tion Wayne’s viral hit “Body” featuring a star studded list of rappers including ArrDee, 3x3’s ZT & E1, Darkoo, Bugzy Malone, Russ' blood brother Buni and New York’s Fivio Foreign—the only American rapper on this track.
The track sees the 9 artists trading bars about their expensive lifestyles, girls and taking shots at their opps—with the various 3x3 members dissing sworn rivals OFB several times.
This is the second time Russ & Tion have released a remix to their songs following April 2019’s “Keisha & Becky (Remix)” that went viral—arguably a bigger hit than the original—thanks to a new verse by then up-and-coming rapper Aitch.

Title: Stairway to Heaven!
About:
“Stairway to Heaven” is one of Led Zeppelin’s most famous recordings, with many considering it the greatest rock song of all time. It tells the story of a greedy woman who is overly optimistic about her unpromising future.
The song resonated with young listeners, opening up a door to a different realm of spirituality and transporting them into a more mystical view of life. The vagueness of the lyrics also allows listeners to interpret the song in their own way, adding to the magic and mystery of the track.

Title: Why Do I?!
About: Why Do I? Is a song by Unknown Brain and Bri Tolani which asks why or what you’re liking is a normal or good thing to like, though this song seems to sound loving but, it tells about lots of emotion and feelings toward the ones you love, but you are liking the most abnormal things in life.

Song: Taki Taki (Feat. Selena Gomez, Ozuna, Cardi B)
Title: Taki Taki!
About:
“Taki Taki” is a DJ Snake produced song influenced by Reggaeton, featuring a trio with Latin roots: Ozuna, Cardi B and Selena Gomez, singing in both Spanish and English.
In the song, the artists talk about dancing and the possibility of how love could go much farther through dancing and teasing. The phrase ‘Taki Taki’ has no official meaning, and it is believed that is used in the song just for better sound.
The rumors of this joint song first surfaced on 25 August 2018 when DJ Snake posted a photo on social media showing director stools for the four artists (plus Cardi’s daughter), teasing the collaboration between them.
The song was confirmed to be about 6 minutes prior to its release. But in fact the song only lasts for 3:32 after the release. Whether the song has ever been 6 minutes long before may never be truly resolved.
“Taki Taki” was featured in Just Dance 2020.

Title: Fire & Desire!
About:
On “Fire & Desire,“ Drizzy expresses his dedication to one woman, who’s outside his grasp, already committed to another man.
She and Drizzy have already spent some time together, so he knows that they have a deep connection. The girl doesn’t know if she’s ready to leave her current man for Drake, however.
The song shares a name and subject matter with Rick James and Teena Marie’s classic duet from 1981, “Fire & Desire.” The song is about reminiscing over a former lover.

Title: Love You More
About: A bonus track off of Encore, Eminem goes into further detail about his and Kim’s unstable relationship. Most of the songs about he and Kim up until this point were just hateful towards her, but this song tries to show why they are the way they are. The main point is that no matter how fucked up the two of them are, there is this undeniable love between them that keeps them together.

Title: Only a Fool Would Say That!
About: This is a realist retort to the naïve idealism of some of the political movements of 1972. At the same time, the fool is the only one in a king’s court that can speak the truth, so you could read this song from either direction.

Title: It Ain’t Me!
About:
“It Ain’t Me” is an uptempo tropical house breakup song. It features Selena Gomez on vocals. In the song, Selena breaks up with someone by asking who will take care of him—when that person isn’t her. Actor Vin Diesel is mistakenly credited as a background vocalist, when what he actually did was an in-studio cover version of the track with Kygo himself in the booth; adding several ad-libs during the beatdrops.
The track is the first single from the second studio album by Norwegian EDM producer Kygo. The song was released on Kygo’s official Vevo, after previews on Selena’s Instagram.

Title: Young Hearts Run Free!
About:
“Young Hearts Run Free” was written and produced by David Crawford and originally recorded by Candi Staton in 1976.
Crawford penned the lyrics of the song after having a conversation with Staton over lunch. In a 2010 interview between Wes Enzinna and Candi Staton, Staton said: “I ended up with this guy, Jimmy James. He was a pimp, and he was very difficult to leave. And he was very threatening and abusive. And I left him, and after I left him— just before I left him, actually, David Crawford and I just had a long conversation. And I would notice that he was taking notes, and he said, ‘You know, I’m gonna write you a song. I’m gonna write you a song that’s gonna last forever.’ Those were his exact words. And so he did. He wrote “Young Hearts Run Free,” and it’s been around for almost four decades.”
“Young Hearts Run Free” spent a week on the Hot Soul Singles chart and reached number twenty on the Billboard Hot 100 chart in 1967.
Kym Mazelle’s cover of the song was featured in Baz Luhrmann’s 1996 film, Romeo + Juliet. In the film, Mercutio (played by Harold Perrineau) lip-syncs to Mazelle’s version of the song in drag.
In 1999, Staton recorded a new dance version of the song. It peaked at #29 in the UK.

Title: All You Need Is Love
About:
Originally a non-album single that ultimately ended up on the Magical Mystery Tour album, “All You Need is Love” was created for and performed on Our World, the first live, international television production.
https://vimeo.com/176129699
This song has become one of Lennon’s most iconic hippie anthems, alongside such classics as “Give Peace A Chance” and “Imagine”.
The opening orchestrated part is also the opening of the La Marseillaise, the French National Anthem. Obvious reference to the French, the self proclaimed world true “lovers”.
Among the background singers were Mick Jagger, Donovan and Keith Moon.

Song: Baby Don't Hurt Me (feat. Coi Leray)
Title: Baby Don’t Hurt Me!
About: David Guetta premiered his new single “Baby Don’t Hurt Me” featuring Anne-Marie and Coi Leray during his set at Ultra Music Festival 2023. The song samples Haddaway’s hit “What Is Love?”. Introducing the song, Guetta said it is from Coi Leray’s new album, however it is unknown whether it will also be on Anne-Marie’s third studio album.

Title: Came Here For Love
About: “Came Here For Love” is the second collaboration between English artists Sigala and Ella Eyre. The first being “Good times” — released two years prior in 2015.

Song: That's What I Like (feat. LKY Young)
Title: That’s What I Like!
About:
“That’s What I Like” is the fourth song on 24K Magic by Bruno Mars. As the album’s second radio single, it took off after Bruno’s performance of it at the 2017 Grammy Awards.
In the song, Bruno tells a girl all the things he likes. These include beach houses, extravagant food, expensive cars and jewelry, and more. In doing so, he implies that the girl can have it all with him.

Title: Ritual
About: Ritual is a collaboration between EDM artist Marshmello and singer Wrabel. The song details the protagonist finding a partner who loves them and helps them repair their distrust for relationships due to past breakups.

Title: That Funny Feeling!
About:
“That Funny Feeling” is the 17th track on Bo Burnham’s May 2021 Netflix special, Inside. It is a reflection on aspects of the modern age that Bo feels peculiar about: ironies; contrasts; inappropriate pairings; the hopelessness of observing what’s going on around us—all set to calm, acoustic coffeeshop music.
In the context of Inside as a whole, this song occurs during the second act—the darker part of the special—in which Burnham is more overtly reckoning with despair, both introspective and external, e.g., the demise of the planet at the hands of mankind.

Title: Who Run It!
About:
On March 18, 2018, Herbo visited the K104FM radio station and dropped a freestyle over Over Three 6 Mafia’s, ‘Who Run It’.
After hearing the short freestyle, Drake was so impressed that he requested that Herbo make and release a full version.

Title: Shut Up
About:
Stormzy looks to DJ XTC’s old school grime beat, “Functions on the Low,” to drop a freestyle in the park, detailing his influence in the grime scene. It was so well-received that he re-recorded it in the studio, releasing it as the B-side to the “Wicked Skengman 4” single. It later went platinum in March 2017 following a relatively successful campaign to get the song to Christmas number one in the UK; the song peaked at number eight.
Stormzy also performed the song live prior to the titular fight at Anthony Joshua vs Dillian Whyte with adapted lyrics.
The song’s most memorable lines, the first eight bars, are directed at Wiley’s brother Cadell, who had been sending for Stormzy throughout 2015 and accusing him of riding off Wiley’s success. Stormzy also addresses the comments by Big Narstie, J Spades and Link Up TV’s Remel London about how he was a “backup dancer” for Kanye at the BRIT Awards.

Title: Born to Be Yours!
About:
“Born to be Yours” is a track by Imagine Dragons and Kygo released in the wake of lead singer Dan Reynold’s unofficial divorce with Aja Volkman. The song marks only the sixth time the band has ever collaborated with another artist alongside the likes of “Sucker for Pain,” “Hands,” “Fear,” the “Thunder / Young Dumb & Broke Medley” with Khalid, and the “Radioactive (Remix)” with Kendrick Lamar.
The song is an international exclusive for Imagine Dragons' fourth studio album, Origins, and was originally teased by Kygo on his instagram with this picture and the caption: 🎶

Title: HIGHEST IN THE ROOM!
About:
On “HIGHEST IN THE ROOM,” Travis discusses his relationship with his then-wife and mother of his child – Kylie Jenner – and his self-proclaimed title as the most intoxicated person in the place he is in.
This song was first teased by Kylie on April 22, 2019, when it played in an advertisement for her Kybrows cosmetic product. Following this, on May 3, 2019, Travis performed a portion of the song during his headlining set at JMBLYA 2019, and then again a week later, during his set at Rolling Loud Miami.
In late June 2019, Travis performed a large portion of the track, during his Morocco performance and on August 31, 2019, he previewed Lil Baby’s verse during a live show.
On September 30, 2019, Travis revealed the single’s final release date and cover art in an Instagram post. Coincidentally, two days before the scheduled release of “HIGHEST IN THE ROOM” Travis and Kylie reportedly called it quits on their relationship.
The song was officially released on October 4, 2019, along with accompanying visuals. However, the final version did not include the teased Lil Baby feature. It eventually was released on the song’s remix in December of 2019.
The track also became Scott’s second single to top the Billboard Hot 100 chart, and first to debut in the peak position. “HIGHEST IN THE ROOM” was certified 4x Multi-Platinum by RIAA on December 1, 2020.

Title: Borró Cassette!
About: In “Borró Cassette” Maluma describes in a catchy danceable song the struggle of trying to connect with the girl he hooked up with the night before, but sadly she does not want to remember that night.

Title: i hate u, i love u!
About:
Olivia O'Brien and Gnash pair up to create this conflicting love story. Gnash simply described the track as:
Missing someone sucks, but it’s important to feel.
This is not the only time that this duo have worked together. Gnash has produced some of Olivia’s tracks and it’s safe to say that there could be more from them in the future.

Title: Takeaway!
About:
“Takeaway” is a collaboration between American DJ and production duo The Chainsmokers and American musician Illenium, featuring Lennon Stella as a vocalist.
The song name was accidentally revealed by The Chainsmokers through an Instagram story in February 2018.
The original snippet of the song, with Sorana having the female vocals, was played live at Ultra Miami 2019 during the closing performance from the Chainsmokers, and Illenium was brought on stage to help debut it live. However the finalised and extended version played later at Ultra Europe 2019 replaced Sorana with Lennon Stella, who will be supporting The Chainsmokers on their upcoming World War Joy tour.
On July 17, the track, and its subsequent music video, was teased.

Title: 2U!
About:
“2U” is a romantic ode to a woman who is worth the chase and time she demands.
On June 6, 2017, Justin teased the release of the song on Twitter. The promotion of the song was later followed by a series of post on social media, in which Victoria Secret models danced to it. After the release, those videos turned out to originate from Jerome Duran’s lip-sync music video. Furthermore, Guetta sent a few lucky fans the song’s full lyrics in advance of its premiere.
The song is the first official collaboration of the French DJ and the Canadian pop star, the latter of which has already been dominating the charts with DJ Khaled’s “I’m The One” and his remix of Luis Fonsi’s “Despacito.”
The single soared up to #1 on several charts, conforming a recent comment made by the chief of the label Justin is signed to, Def Jam Recordings' CEO Steve Bartels:
The remarkable thing about him right now is that he could be having the song of the summer without it being his own record

Title: Lost In The Night
About: They are lost in the night

Title: 2U!
About:
“2U” is a romantic ode to a woman who is worth the chase and time she demands.
On June 6, 2017, Justin teased the release of the song on Twitter. The promotion of the song was later followed by a series of post on social media, in which Victoria Secret models danced to it. After the release, those videos turned out to originate from Jerome Duran’s lip-sync music video. Furthermore, Guetta sent a few lucky fans the song’s full lyrics in advance of its premiere.
The song is the first official collaboration of the French DJ and the Canadian pop star, the latter of which has already been dominating the charts with DJ Khaled’s “I’m The One” and his remix of Luis Fonsi’s “Despacito.”
The single soared up to #1 on several charts, conforming a recent comment made by the chief of the label Justin is signed to, Def Jam Recordings' CEO Steve Bartels:
The remarkable thing about him right now is that he could be having the song of the summer without it being his own record

Title: Time Bomb
About:
Despite it’s upbeat, ska punk vibe, “Time Bomb”’s subject matter is actually quite dire. The meteoric ascendance of a young boy to prominence within a gang followed by his equally swift demise as he is replaced via murder.
The first verse’s lyrics were taken from “Motorcycle Ride” off the Let’s Go album.
“Time Bomb” is the second single from Rancid’s third album, …And Out Come the Wolves; it reached number 8 on the Billboard Modern Rock Tracks. It was later featured on the multi-band compilation album CBGB Forever. As well as being featured in S1E4 of Netflix Original show Everything Sucks!.
“Time Bomb” was featured in the fifth episode of the Gilmore Girls, “Cinnamon’s Wake.” In the 19th episode of Season 4, “Afterboom,” a character’s band performed a cover. It was also used in the 2005 movie Fun with Dick and Jane.
“Time Bomb” was released in the Rancid 01 track pack for Rock Band 2.

Title: Girls Like You (Remix)!
About:
The existence of the remix of the original “Girls Like You” was initially confirmed in a tweet by the band:
Leaving the original vocals & production intact, Cardi adds a Bronx-flavored verse to the pop record, rapping about having danced for dollars & being the life of the party among other things.
The remix also gained its own music video featuring several idolized women across an array of fields.
In September 2018, the song topped the Billboard Hot 100, becoming Cardi’s third #1 single, and Maroon5’s fourth. The song dethroned the uber-popular “In My Feelings”, by Drake, which led the pack for ten weeks.
On February 11, 2019, “Girls Like You” logged its 33rd week in the top 10 on the Billboard Hot 100, becoming the sixth single in the chart’s history to stay in the top 10 for that long. The song currently matches Ed Sheeran’s 33-week run of “Shape of You” in the top 10.

Title: Back To You
About:
“Back To You” is the second song to be released by Louis Tomlinson as a solo artist following the December 2016 release of “Just Hold On.” On the day this song dropped, Louis told Heat this song isn’t closely related to him, because the whole album is very personal so it was nice to take the pressure off with this song. He also said in the same interview that the song represents “the most extreme situation of that kind of relationship”.
On June 24 Louis hinted at the release of a single in a tweet. Later that day he announced the title and revealed that Bebe Rexha and Digital Farm Animals would be featured in the song in another tweet. Following this announced that it would be released at the end of July before confirming the rumored release date of July 21 and releasing the single art. Later he revealed a lyric from the song before sharing clips of the song on his Instagram story and on Twitter.
“Back to You” has been certified Gold in the UK and Platinum in the US.

Title: Make You Mine
About: While “Make You Mine” was originally released in 2014, the track quickly gained attention as it became part of a viral TikTok thread. Soon after, it took social media by a storm, notably entering Spotify’s United States Viral 50 chart and Spotify’s Pop Rising playlist. In late May of 2019, “Make You Mine” was initially picked up by SiriusXM Venus radio station, which led the band to release a new radio edit of the song in early June.

Title: Good Together
About: Good Together is Shy Martin’s own first debut single. It was released on 10 November 2017 and is listed under the genres pop, electro-pop and indie.

Song: Bad Boy (with Wiz Khalifa, bbno$, MAX)
Title: Bad Boy!
About:
On “Bad Boy,“ Juice WRLD and Young Thug reunite for an upbeat and quick-moving track in which the pair liken themselves to 1995 comedy Bad Boys‘ main cast, Will Smith and Martin Lawrence, boasting about their reckless lifestyles while touching on their items and wealth.
An early version of the track was originally leaked on January 4, 2020, and followed with the leak of this version on July 7, 2020 with a new and updated Juice WRLD verse.
Additionally, the track was first previewed in behind the scenes footage from the Cole Bennett directed music video for the track. The video was originally intended to release alongside the track on November 16, 2020, but both the track and video’s release were delayed for unknown reasons.
Despite the complications, on December 19, 2020, Lyrical Lemonade directly mentioned in a tweet the two main actors of Bad Boy and accompanied it with a picture of Juice and Thugger, hinting at an official release in the future. Cole Bennett retweeted the Lyrical Lemonade tweet later the same day, and the next day announced that he “has good news“ and accompanied it once again with a picture of Juice and Thugger.
On December 25, 2020, Cole Bennett once again took to his Twitter to confirm the title of the track alongside announcing the release date of January 15, 2021 with a six-second teaser video:
BAD BOY 1/15/21 pic.twitter.com/r6JvUZfe2u
— Cole Bennett (@_ColeBennett_) December 25, 2020

Title: See You Again
About:
“See You Again” is a tribute to the late Furious actor Paul Walker who died tragically in November of 2013 after his car crashed and burst into flames in Valencia, CA. The first verse is from the perspective of Vin Diesel and the Furious 7 cast members, while the second is from Paul Walker.
Its popularity grew quickly after the release of the film Furious 7, and the music video eventually became the first hip hop song to reach 1 billion YouTube views. It topped the Billboard Hot 100 for twelve weeks, becoming the longest-running rap number one since Eminem’s “Lose Yourself.”
This marks Wiz Khalifa’s second release from the Furious 7 soundtrack, his first being “Go Hard or Go Home” featuring Iggy Azalea.

Title: Paint The Town Red
About:
“Paint The Town Red” is released on August 4, 2023 as the lead single from Doja Cat’s upcoming fourth-studio album that is expected to be released on September 22nd. On the hip-hop and jazz-fused song she samples “Walk On By” by Dionne Warwick over a smooth beat. She raps about the people who’ve disliked and hated her for her change in appearance.
The term, paint the town red, is an expression in American slang which means to go on a reckless debauch or to be wildly extravagant. This metaphor is used to apply on bonfires which (not literally) paint the sky or scenery red. (Source: The Guardian)

Title: Surround Sound!
About:
“Surround Sound” is the first collaboration between Atlanta artists JID, 21 Savage, and Baby Tate. It was first teased as a song between JID and DJ Scheme in a tweet on April 5, 2020.
On September 20, 2021, Barry Johnson, one of JID’s managers, teased the song’s imminent release when he posted a short clip of JID and Savage from the filming of the music video.
On January 10, 2022, JID officially announced the song’s new title and release date.
The first version of this song leaked in late-August 2021 with the title “Houses.” In November 2021, the official version leaked.

Song: STAR WALKIN' (League of Legends Worlds Anthem)
Title: STAR WALKIN’!
About:
“STAR WALKIN'” is a single by Lil Nas X, which he teased several times through Twitter and TikTok as early as May 2022. The song is the anthem of the League of Legends 2022 World Championship. The first time fans heard the full song was as an encore on the Long Live Montero tour in support of his MONTERO album, which started on September 6th, 2022.
On September 15th, 2022, Lil Nas announced a partnership with the multiplayer video game League of Legends to promote both “STAR WALKIN'” and the game’s 2022 World Championship. The company behind League of Legends, Riot Games, released a video wherein LNX acts as “Lil Nas Xecutive” and becomes the president of the company, playing “STAR WALKIN'” throughout their offices.
Initially slated to be released on September 23rd, the single’s release date moved up one day for unconfirmed reasons.

Title: Sunflower!
About:
“Sunflower” marks the second collaboration by Post Malone and Swae Lee. It serves as the second single for Post Malone’s 2019 album, Hollywood’s Bleeding, and is part of the soundtrack for the 2018 animated film Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse. The track would end up breaking the record for most weeks spent in the top 10 of the Billboard Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs Chart after notching its 45th week.
Sunflowers are a symbol of loyalty and longevity because of the flower’s ability to retain its beauty in the face of environmental factors that would leave other flowers wilted. The sunflower in this song represents a girl—a woman who’s shown persistence, despite a lack of affection shown to her. However, sunflowers also deplete nutrients from soil much quicker than other plants and can inhibit the growth of other plants. Post Malone’s verses could imply that the person he’s with is in constant need of help and attention, yet he is unable to tend to them as much as they would like.
This mystery girl ends up being the subject matter for both Post Malone and Swae Lee. In their verses, the girl seems to be trying much harder in the relationship than either of the two stars. However, even after all of the poor treatment the girl has received, she remains by Post and Swae’s side.
Post first teased the song at The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon on October 1, 2018. He then confirmed on Facebook that the song would include Swae Lee. Two snippets from the lyric video were shown on both of their Instagram accounts on October 15, 2018.

Title: Mood Swings!
About:
“Mood Swings“ sees Pop Smoke reuniting with Bronx rapper Lil Tjay for a slow-moving track. The two take turns dropping references to their women and expensive items to boast about their lifestyle with fame.
Pop and Tjay made this track the same night they made their October 2019 hit “War,“ a track which was featured on Pop’s 2020 mixtape, Meet The Woo 2.

Title: INDUSTRY BABY
About:
Lil Nas X recruits Louisville native Jack Harlow for “INDUSTRY BABY,” a boisterous song where the duo rap about their fame and success, blowing up, and their newfound statuses in the rap game. The title plays on the term “industry plant,” which are people backed by a major label at the start, but play into an image of being organic and all by themselves.
The song was first teased on October 22, 2020 with a snippet showing Nas X dancing to the song with producer duo, Take a Daytrip. A demo version of the song leaked in full late June 2021, using a slightly different instrumental and containing no feature. A week before its release, the rapper tweeted a promise, claiming he would drop “INDUSTRY BABY” once the June 2019 track, “Panini,” hit 400 Million views on YouTube.
The music video hit the goal shortly after and Lil Nas announced the song on July 19th, through a trailer portraying him being sentenced to jail in relation to his Nike court case. The trailer revealed the release date, producers, and Jack Harlow as a featured artist.

Song: I'll Be Missing You (feat. Faith Evans, 112)
Title: I’ll Be Missing You!
About:
Puff Daddy’s tribute to his friend The Notorious B.I.G. was written, recorded, and released less than three months after Biggie’s death, and became one of the top-selling rap singles ever.
The beat is sampled from the The Police song “Every Breath You Take” about which Puff said that he had used it because it is one of his favourite tracks.
The chorus, sung by Faith Evans, the widow Biggie left behind, is an interpolation of its chorus. The intro to the album version includes an interpolation of the classical music piece “Adagio for Strings” by Samuel Barber.

Title: Agora Hills
About:
In “Agora Hills,” Doja Cat evokes the complex feelings of wanting to publicize a love that must be kept private out of necessity, and whatever that necessity may be. Throughout the track, Doja expresses her love, and even mentions her marriage.
The track’s title has been floating around for months before being unveiled as the second single from Doja’s fourth studio album, Scarlet. It samples Troop’s 1989 hit “All I Do Is Think of You.”
“Agora Hills” is a misspelling of the Los Angeles suburb of Agoura Hills — Doja Cat’s childhood neighbourhood — but actually refers to ‘agoraphilia’, which is “the love of public life, crowds, and activity.” In the field of sexology, it is “a fetish for having sex in public places.”

Song: I Like You (A Happier Song) (Feat. Doja Cat)
Title: It’s Not Like I Like You!!!
About: “It’s Not Like I Like You!!” is a song by Static-P and Amree. Released in 2016, it has gotten popular due to its use in various animatics and AMVs, with the original video amassing over 29 million views on YouTube. The lyrics show the relationship between a boy and a girl who also happens to be a tsundere. (“Tsundere” is a Japanese term referring to people in anime or even real life who initially show a cold and temperamental side of themselves to someone, but are actually really warm, friendly, and kind)

Song: I'm the One (Feat. Justin Bieber, Quavo, Chance the Rapper, Lil Wayne)
Title: I’m the One!
About:
“I’m the One” features a star-studded line-up of artists in especially braggadocio spirits, with Justin Bieber, Chance The Rapper, Lil Wayne and Quavo boasting to the women in their lives that they are “the only one” for them. “I’m the One” is Khaled’s second single from his 2017 album Grateful, following the February release of “Shining.”
The collaboration was teased throughout videos and pictures via the Instagram profiles of the featured artists, showing the making of the music video.
The song debuted at No.1 on the Billboard Hot 100 chart and UK Official Singles Chart. It became the second song to debut at #1 on the Hot 100 in 2017 making it Bieber’s second feat and Khaled’s first.

Title: lovely!
About:
The highly anticipated “lovely” is the second song on the 13 Reasons Why: Season 2 soundtrack. It premiered in Zane Lowe’s World Record feature on Apple Music’s Beats 1 radio station on April 19th 2018.
The track was produced by Eilish’s brother FINNEAS, who was with Billie and Khalid when writing the song. The lyrics of the track focus on the duo’s attempt to overcome depression. Eilish told Lowe:
We called it [lovely] because the song was sort of really freaking depressing so then it’s like oh, how lovely. Just taking everything horrible like you know what this is great. I’m so happy being miserable.
Despite being released as a single, the song was included on the official soundtrack for season two of the Netflix hit-show 13 Reasons Why. “lovely” can be heard in the last episode, “Bye”, in which the show’s main character, Clay Jensen, finally copes with the death of Hannah Baker and decides to part ways with her.
Eilish shared her excitement about “lovely” being included on the show’s soundtrack on her podcast groupies have feelings too on Apple Music’s Beats 1 radio station:
And what’s really insane about it, like, if you look at the soundtrack it’s like Selena Gomez, who produced the show, and then, me!
The track was also featured on season one, episode two of Pretty Little Liars: The Perfectionists.

Title: Take a Step Back!
About:
“Take A Step Back,” a pivotal track in the distorted trap trend. Notable for its distorted 808s and vocals, staples of X and Ski’s early careers. This is one of the most popular collaborations between the pair.
This song was originally released May 15, 2016 and was later put on Ski’s “You Will Regret” mixtape; released on June 30, 2017.

Title: Kiss Me More!
About:
“Kiss Me More” marks Doja’s first collaboration with SZA and is an introduction to Doja’s highly anticipated third studio album, titled Planet Her. The song was initially teased during an interview with V Magazine on March 5, 2021.
Doja had also cryptically hinted at a collaboration between the two in a tweet drawing attention to the artists she follows, which included SZA.
Following them for a reeeeaaaaasssoooonnn ;) Guess why.
— I’m Doja (@DojaCat) January 5, 2021

Song: Empire State Of Mind (Feat. Alicia Keys)
Title: Empire State of Mind!
About:
The Blueprint 3’s third single is a love song to Jay-Z and Alicia Keys' home city of New York, which is also known as “the Empire State.” The title alludes to the Billy Joel ballad, “New York State of Mind,” and Nas‘ “N.Y. State of Mind.”
“Empire State of Mind” stands as one of Hov’s most successful singles. It was the first Jay-Z-lead single to ever top the Billboard “Hot 100,” sitting at #1 for five consecutive weeks (also being the last number #1 in the 2000s). It has been certified 5x Multi-Platinum by the RIAA for sales of over 5.5 million in the United States. The track was nominated for three Grammy Awards, winning “Best Rap Song” and “Best Rap/Sung Collaboration.”

Song: What It Is (Block Boy) (Feat. Kodak Black)
Title: What It Is (Solo Version)!
Lovin On Me
About: First teased on social media in October 2023, ‘Lovin On Me’ serves as the potential lead single for a currently unnamed fourth studio album from Jack Harlow.

Title: death bed (coffee for your head)!
About:
“death bed (coffee for your head),” released alongside Some Boring, Love Stories, Pt. 2, is a collaboration between Powfu and beabadoobee. The song samples “Coffee” from London artist beabadoobee. In the song, Powfu reminisces on memories with a supposed former lover.
The song delivers two themes of one being the narrator writing this as a tribute to a breakup, or the narrator writing this to address his lover because of upcoming death.
“death bed (coffee for your head)” was first released on February 9, 2019, to all streaming services, but eventually taken down due to Powfu using an unauthorized sample. It was re-released a year later on February 7, 2020, to all streaming platforms. It gained popularity after being featured in many videos on the video platform TikTok.

Title: Too Good at Goodbyes!
About:
“Too Good at Goodbyes” tells of a turbulent personal relationship, and is Sam Smith’s first solo release since their 2015 James Bond theme “Writing’s on the Wall.” The song is the lead single from their sophomore album, The Thrill of It All.
In a Beats 1 interview, Sam explained that only four songs on the album are about them, and “Too Good at Goodbyes” is one of them. They told Zane Lowe how the song was inspired by a real relationship:
[The song is] about a relationship I was in and it’s basically about getting good at getting dumped. […] I’m still very, very single. I think I’m even more single than I was when I released In the Lonely Hour.
They also said,
At the time I was trying to convince myself I was tough, to protect myself. I was gearing up for what I knew would happen. I’m really not like that at all.
In an interview with Nick Grimshaw on BBC Radio 1 on the morning the song dropped, Nick introduced Sam by saying that “the vibes around Sam have definitely changed,” and Sam agreed. They explained that the album, due in November, is a tribute to the family and friends who supported them over the years, focusing on ‘their lives — their ups and downs —, rather than Sam’s own.

Song: Angel Pt. 1 (Feat. Kodak Black, NLE Choppa, JVKE, Muni Long)
Title: Angel Pt. 1!
About:
“Angel Pt. 1” is a poignant collaboration between Kodak Black, NLE Choppa, Muni Long, JVKE, and Jimin, on the soundtrack of the tenth installment of the Fast and Furious franchise, Fast X. The song is the first track on the album with a music video, which features the artists on the track.
The track explores the intricate dynamics of love and sacrifice in the face of a turbulent environment. With soulful vocals and introspective lyrics, the artists caution against the consequences of getting too close to darkness.
This emotionally charged track encapsulates the bittersweet struggle between the need for connection and the destructive nature that can ultimately break beautiful things.

Title: Boss Bitch
About:
On December 14th 2019, Doja Cat announced via Instagram that she was releasing a music video for a new track called “Boss Bitch” from the Birds of Prey soundtrack.
The caption on the post read:
Shooting for @birdsofprey music video / my new song #bossbitch is out on all platforms now!
However, she soon edited the caption, leaving the song’s release date uncertain.
The song was eventually released January 23, 2020.

Title: Better Now
About:
On “Better Now,” Post reminisces about a past relationship.
He believes that his emotions mirror those of his ex. They are likely both masking the fact that they still have feelings for each other in an attempt to “win the breakup.”

Title: Dilemma!
About:
A hugely popular song from 2002 which appeared on both Nelly’s Nellyville and Kelly Rowland’s Simply Deep.
This song is arguably the most successful, ‘biggest’ song of both Nelly’s career and Rowland’s solo career (aside from her featured vocals in David Guetta’s “When Love Takes Over”).
It was Nelly’s first #1 single in countries such as the UK, Ireland, Australia, Belgium, Germany and the Netherlands. It followed on from the success of his previous single “Hot in Herre”.
It was – and remains to be – Rowland’s highest selling single in the US and the UK, as it has sold over 935,000 copies as of November 2017.

Title: Lot to Learn (No Rap Version)!
About:
This song appears on the TMRW EP, the follow-up to “YSTRDY.” Christopher is asking if someone will always be there to be the peanut butter to his jelly.
He’s admitting he doesn’t know everything, and is willing to do what it takes to learn.
He also addresses a girl who seems to be hiding an inner sadness inside of her.
Christopher has a theory about why this song is popular. He told NRJ Sweden: “The reason why it’s been so successful is because it’s relatable. I feel like everyone kind of feels that way. For me, it was something I wanted to say. And I think people agreed. People thought the same way.”
Christopher has a clear memory of how he wrote “Lot to Learn.” Christopher to NRJ Sweden: “I’ll sit down on the piano and come up with a chorus. For ‘Lot to Learn,’ that’s kinda how I did it. I sat down on the piano and then kinda wrote the chorus and then made the beat and then did the other stuff afterwards.”
