Although Eilish has made public her struggles with depression, the song isn’t necessarily about her life experience, but could be an artifact of her songwriting process. Eilish shared with Genius' own Rob Markman that when writing, she often impersonates other people and other people’s thought and problems:
You don’t have to go through anything to write about it, or you can go through everything and write about something else. It’s literally like telling a story, you can write about something you’ve done, you can write about something your friend’s done, or you want to do, or you used to do, or your mom’s done.
At her first show in Toronto during the 1by1 tour, she talked about what the song is
“Basically it’s like, um it’s about, it’s just about depression, and what can come out of depression, which is not pretty good. […] When this comes out, I don’t want it to break you down, I want it to bring you to me and I don’t know, be like a mental hug, I guess. [… ] What I’m really trying to do is let my words be your words and stay ours.” (She continues to talk about how her fans are the reason she is here and how much she loves them).
In an interview with Radio Energy, Eilish listed “listen before i go” as “—one of my favorite songs I’ve ever written in my life”.
In an interview with PopBuzz, Eilish confirmed that the final three song titles on the album were intended to form a sentence, beginning with “listen before i go”, then “i love you”, ending on “goodbye”.
The song’s Spotify artwork was done by @_italiyana on Instagram.

Song: Take Me Where Your Heart Is
Title: Take Me Where Your Heart Is
ICB!
About: This song is a tribute to Ian Curtis, the lead singer of Joy Division, the band that gave origin to New Order; the title “ICB” being an initialism for “Ian Curtis Buried”.

Title: Finesse (Remix)!
About:
As the dust started to settle from his monstrous comeback, Bruno Mars returns for more heat with fellow chart topper, Cardi B. The Bronx rapper drops bars woven between Bruno’s crooning over a 90’s style beat. The original track was released on Bruno’s 2016 album 24K Magic, which spawned three Hot 100 singles. With his throwback star power and Cardi’s fire bars, the track is destined for success as well.
On Instagram, Bruno revealed how “the one and only” Cardi’s verse came to be and gave her some advice:
I met Cardi B at 3 am after my show in LA backstage where we recorded her verse for Finesse. She walked in the room and she was everything I’d hope she’d be. Never change cardi! Don’t let this crazy music business change who you are. You posses something that can’t be taught. You’re a true star. Thank you for putting your energy and love all over this record and video.
Cardi has recently collaborated with many artists, accumulating millions of streams and breaking records along the way, so it’s likely that this remix will be another addition to her successes. She went to Instagram to convey her gratitude.
The song debuted in the #35 spot and then jumped up to #3 the following week on Billboard’s Hot 100 chart.
They performed during the 60th Grammy Awards on January 28th, 2018, mashed up with a verse of Cardi B’s “Bartier Cardi.”

Title: Can’t Feel My Face!
About:
This song personifies drug addiction, likening it to the love of a woman and has been described as “classic pop” by critics, comparing its sound to that of Michael Jackson.
This track also marked the first collaboration between The Weeknd and powerhouse pop producer Max Martin.
“Can’t Feel My Face” is the third single from The Weeknd’s second studio album; it leaked along with “In The Night” and “Acquainted,” (which was leaked as an incomplete track referred to as “Girls Born in the ‘90s”) on May 25, 2015. The song later premiered live at Apple’s WWDC, in conjunction with the announcement of the Apple Music service.
“Can’t Feel My Face” was The Weeknd’s first solo pop hit, without the aid of Ariana Grande or the Fifty Shades of Grey film. As such, it marked the completion of The Weeknd’s four-year transition from niche alternative R+B to bonafide pop stardom. The song eventually reached #1 on the Billboard chart for three nonconsecutive weeks.

Song: Santa Claus Is Comin' to Town
Title: Santa Claus Is Comin’ To Town!
Someday at Christmas
About: This Song was written by Stevie Wonders in 1967, during the Vietnamese War, to protest against the war.

Title: You Deserve It All
About:
“You Deserve It All” is the fifth song on The War Is Over (2017), Josh Baldwin’s third studio album.
In an editorial published on NewReleaseToday, Josh Baldwin says:
“You Deserve It All” is a corporate worship song that proclaims the worthiness of King Jesus. The song calls people back to the core message of salvation and our response to the saving power of Jesus.

Title: Last Christmas
About: “Last Christmas” is a song by Wham! It’s been widely covered by a multitude of artists, including Taylor Swift and Jimmy Eat World The song reached No. 1 in 2 countries and reached No. 2 in eight countries. It tells the story of a failed relationship and the sadness that the holiday triggers.

Song: Have Yourself A Merry Little Christmas
Title: Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas!
About: “Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas” is a song written in 1943 by Hugh Martin and Ralph Blane and introduced by Judy Garland in the 1944 MGM musical Meet Me in St. Louis. Frank Sinatra later recorded a version with modified lyrics. In 2007, ASCAP ranked it the third most performed Christmas song during the preceding five years that had been written by ASCAP members. In 2004 it finished at No. 76 in AFI’s 100 Years…100 Songs rankings of the top tunes in American cinema.

Title: This Christmas
About: Single by Chris Brown which was produced to support the released of the 2007 movie of the same name, of which Brown features in.

Title: Rest Assured
About:
The 5th track on Eric B. & Rakim’s 4th and last studio album as a duo: Don’t Sweat the Technique.
The song samples “Life Could” by Rotary Connection.
A classic Rakim song talking about his bread and butter: his abilities in the rap game while proving every word he spits is true.
Also in this song Rakim compares himself to the classic agent 007- or, James Bond- of course instead of securing people, he secure the rap game.

Title: Happier Than Ever
About:
The titular song from Billie Eilish’s sophomore album of the same name was first teased in the February 2021 documentary, The World’s a Little Blurry. In a clip from the documentary, Eilish had this to say about the track:
The whole song is just more like nothing even specific that they did, you’re just not happy being with them. Can’t even explain it.
In a February 2021 interview on Eilish’s documentary, The World’s a Little Blurry, the film’s director, R. J. Cutler, hinted that the unreleased songs that appeared in the documentary may end up on Eilish’s album:
…and you see them writing material that perhaps you’ll see on a later album.

Title: Christmas Blues
About:
The title track of Claudio’s debut Christmas album features GRAMMY award-winning Canadian singer-songwriter The Weeknd, marking the pair’s first collaboration together. The sultry R&B track, which GQ Magazine ranked as the sixth best new Christmas song of 2020, sees the artists crooning over missing a loved one on Christmas Day, therefore having “Christmas blues.”
While the artists hadn’t yet musically collaborated together, Claudio was announced as an opener for The Weeknd’s “After Hours World Tour” in February 2020, with the tour originally scheduled to take place in mid-2020 before the COVID-19 pandemic struck the United States.

Title: I’ll Be Home for Christmas!
About: This song is a recreation of Bing Crosby’s “I’ll Be Home For Christmas.” This version includes more words and phrases than the original.

Song: Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas (feat. Esperanza Spalding)
Title: Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas!
About: “Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas” is a song written in 1943 by Hugh Martin and Ralph Blane and introduced by Judy Garland in the 1944 MGM musical Meet Me in St. Louis. Frank Sinatra later recorded a version with modified lyrics. In 2007, ASCAP ranked it the third most performed Christmas song during the preceding five years that had been written by ASCAP members. In 2004 it finished at No. 76 in AFI’s 100 Years…100 Songs rankings of the top tunes in American cinema.

Title: O Holy Night
About: Placide Cappeau (October 25th, 1808 – August 8th, 1877) was a French poet and the author of the poem, “Minuit, chrétiens” (1847), set to music by Adolphe Adamand known in English as the carol “O Holy Night” or “Cantique de Noël”.

Title: The Twelve Days of Christmas!
About:
“The Twelve Days of Christmas” is an English Christmas carol that enumerates each day by the gifts given on each of the twelve days of Christmas.
Frederic Austin was known to popularize the arrangement of the song we know today in 1909.

Title: Mr. Right Now!
About:
On “Mr. Right Now,” 21 Savage and Drake trade bars about introducing their women to luxury lifestyles and boasting their respective sexual prowess. They drop references to multiple R&B staples, including TLC, Keith Sweat, and SZA.
21 Savage has often expressed his love for R&B. He frequently covers songs by legendary and modern R&B artists on his Instagram story.
Metro Boomin provides a melodic instrumental built around a carnivalesque sample.

Song: Favorite Time Of Year (Feat. Tori Kelly)
Title: Favorite Time of Year!
About: favorite time of year byIndia.Arie & Joe Sample

Title: The Christmas Song
About:
“The Christmas Song” (commonly subtitled “Chestnuts Roasting on an Open Fire” or, as it was originally subtitled, “Merry Christmas to You”) is a classic Christmas song written in 1944 by musician, composer, and vocalist Mel Tormé (aka The Velvet Fog), and Bob Wells. According to Tormé, the song was written during a blistering hot summer. In an effort to “stay cool by thinking cool”, the most-performed (according to BMI) Christmas song was born.
“I saw a spiral pad on his piano with four lines written in pencil”, Tormé recalled. “They started, "Chestnuts roasting…, Jack Frost nipping…, Yuletide carols…, Folks dressed up like Eskimos.‘ Bob (Wells, co-writer) didn’t think he was writing a song lyric. He said he thought if he could immerse himself in winter he could cool off. Forty minutes later that song was written. I wrote all the music and some of the lyrics.”

Title: I Don’t Want To Be Alone For Christmas!
About:
“I Don’t Want to Be Alone for Christmas” is a leaked cover by Ariana Grande. The song was released when she reissued her 2013 Christmas EP Christmas Kisses.
Ariana recorded the song in 2013 for her first Christmas EP Christmas Kisses. Sadly the song did not make the cut. The song was then suppose to be released on the re-release of the EP in 2014 along with “Santa Tell Me.” The song was leaked on PerezHilton.com.

Title: Fall in Love at Christmas (Extended Radio Version)!

Song: lonely christmas (Feat. Justin Bieber, Poo Bear)
Title: Lonely Christmas!
About:
This song is about a guy losing his significant other before Christmas. He then gets lonely and starts to become sad. He then believe his life sucks. He finally ends up working with a 21 year old vlogger, and gets to live with an old guy and a hot guy. Little does he know, his life still sucks.
(thanks dude)

Title: Meet Me Inside!
Singer: Lin-Manuel Miranda, Leslie Odom, Jr., Anthony Ramos, Christopher Jackson & Original Broadway Cast of “Hamilton”
About:
If you needed reminding that Hamilton was barely out of his teens for the majority of the Revolution, here we have a very adolescent and daddy-issues-laden musical number.
Hamilton’s hot-blooded ambition and sensitivity to his illegitimacy clash with Washington’s cool-headed diplomacy and tendency to be thoughtlessly patronizing. This is historically accurate to the sometimes oil-and-water clash of these two, very different men:
Washington was prudent and reserved, with excellent judgment. He sought conciliation and accepted compromise. Hamilton was brilliant and decisive, but prone to rashness. When he believed himself right (which was nearly all of the time), he gave no quarter and could not keep quiet.

Title: Is It a Crime?!
About: Is It a Crime? peaked at #49 on the U.K. Singles chart and #55 on Billboard’s R&B chart, staying on the chart for 7 weeks in 1985.

Song: When You Wish Upon A Star (Feat. Barbra Streisand & Chris Botti)
Title: When You Wish Upon a Star!
About: This song is a song found in Disney’s ‘Pinnochio’. It is commonly seen as Disney’s theme song, and the melody plays before every Disney movie in the Disney intro.

Title: Drunk in Love!
About:
Beyoncé brings her husband Jay Z aboard for their first collaboration since 2013’s “Part II (On the Run)” continuation of their first collab “‘03 Bonnie & Clyde”.
“Drunk” seems to be a more sexualized continuation of their first track together, “Crazy in Love.” Over Detail’s blaring beat, the lyrics repeatedly touch on female and marital sexuality, and the effects of alcohol.
Bey described her entire self-titled album as “much freer than anything I’d done in the past,” but none seemed more free than “Drunk In Love” with its freestyled lyrics, studio fun, and improvised beach music video:
We were in Miami for Jay’s concert, and it was just the two of us, on the beach, amazing weather, and one outfit! It’s beautiful in its simplicity. If you want something to feel real and urgent, you can’t overthink it.
Kanye West hopped on the official remix featured on the deluxe version of the album, but the song’s influence stretched to several other remixes and covers from The Weeknd, Lil Wayne, Future, T.I., Ed Sheeran, and more.

Title: End of the Road!
About:
In this song, one of the Boyz know that his time with his girl is coming to an end, but he isn’t ready to let go of her.
“End of the Road” was Boyz II Men’s first ever #1 single, reaching #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 charts for 13 straight weeks, which was a record at the time. This song also won 2 Grammys for “Best R&B Performance by a Duo or Group with Vocals” and “Best R&B Song”.

Title: All Mine!
About:
Opening with church-like organ music, “All Mine” ironically deals with infidelity.
To “let the genie out of the bottle” means to allow something to happen that cannot then be stopped – like controlling one’s desires while in a committed relationship. Ye goes on to list several high profile celebrities who have been caught out cheating and speculates about their motives.
While this track was ultimately called “All Mine,” Ye Tweeted after the release of the song that it was initially going to be called “Medulla Oblongata” – the component of the brain partially responsible for regulating respiration and circulation. Reference to this can still be found in the lyrics.

Title: Grenade
About:
“Grenade” is the second single from Bruno Mars' debut album Doo-Wops & Hooligans.
In an interview with Idolator, Bruno explained the backstory of how the song was created and what it meant for him to sing the song:
I was with my friend Benny Blanco (very well-known songwriter) and he was playing me some songs, and he played me this song that had a lyric like [the one in “Grenade”]… he said, this band is not signed, this is a CD that wasn’t released. I said to Benny, I can relate to that so much, I want to take that [song] and make it my own. He was in contact with the dude, and I started writing my version, basically. It’s a heartbreaking, heartbreak song, and I think everyone can relate to that. You’re so in love with this woman and you don’t understand, “ What am I doing wrong? What am I not giving to you? I’ll go as far as putting as putting a bullet in my brain for you, and why can’t I get that kind of love in return?”

Title: SLOW DANCING IN THE DARK
About:
On “SLOW DANCING IN THE DARK,” Joji continues the lovelorn narrative from his previous single “YEAH RIGHT” over a dark, ambient instrumental.
This time around, he uses the metaphor of slow dancing in the dark to characterize a failing relationship—just like a slow dance where you can’t see your partner’s face, his relationship with this person is intimate but ultimately impersonal.
Joji first previewed the song on May 12, 2018, at the Blurry Vision Festival. He announced its official release on September 7th, along with teaser photos from the music video.

Title: God is a woman!
About:
“God is a woman” is the second single from Ariana Grande’s fourth studio album, Sweetener, which refers to the pleasures of sex and, consequently, encourages the message of female sexual liberation and domination. The track was teased in her “no tears left to cry” music video, and was hinted again in the “no tears left to cry (BTS – Part 2)” video.
Through her interview with Time Magazine, the track is described as an “anthemic, sultry banger.” The production is described as having “Grande’s voice layered so that it sounds like a choir, but really, is only her, multiplied.”
According to Ariana during an interview with Jimmy Fallon on May 1, 2018, “God is a Woman” is one of her grandma’s favorite tracks.
The track was speculated to be released on July 20, 2018, following Grande’s “20th of each month” release schedule for the album. At midnight on July 12th, Grande tweeted that she would be releasing the song and music video early as a surprise.

Title: Smokin Out The Window
About:
“Smokin Out The Window” is the third single from Silk Sonic’s debut album An Evening with Silk Sonic. The song was first teased on the duo’s Rolling Stone issue, posted in August 2021.
The track’s release date was announced in an Instagram post made by Silk Sonic on November 1, 2021, where they teased the music video.

Title: Cheapest Flight
About:
A song about being swept away from love that was thought to be stable and whole.
After stress and usual emotion fallout; Prep shows us a more optimistic view in which you can ‘fly’ away from your love/lover.
As the further away you are from the love that will hurt you; the safer, clearer and more cheerful you will become.

23 of course refers to Michael Jordan’s jersey number – and this song is all about his signature shoe brand, Jordans…
Mike WiLL finally dropped off his highly anticipated lead single from his Interscope debut.

Title: Easy On Me!
About:
“Easy On Me” marked Adele’s return after a six-year hiatus. The song is the lead single off her fourth studio album 30. In an October 2021 BBC Radio 1 interview, Adele revealed that it was the first song written for the album, as she started it back in 2019. The song sees the singer speak about her fraught childhood, her lost marriage and the lessons learnt and unlearnt about family, love and abandonment along the way.
Following recent teases of her long-awaited fourth album on October 5, 2021, Adele took to Twitter, announcing that she would be dropping a brand new song on October 15th, sharing the teaser trailer to this track. The first verse of the song was played during Adele’s Instagram Live on October 9, 2021.
Upon its release, “Easy On Me” broke the world record for the most streamed song in 24 hours, with 24 million streams. This unseated the previously record-holders BTS for their song “Butter.”

Title: Take a Bow!
About:
“Take a Bow” is about Rih’s not wanting to rekindle her relationship with an unfaithful ex. She’s tired of hearing his excuses and wants to let him know it’s over for good.
The song (along with “Disturbia”) was of two new songs recorded for Good Girl Gone Bad: Reloaded. The song debuted at #53 and peaked at #1—marking the second largest leap to number one in the history of the Billboard charts—a feat that only Maroon 5’s “Makes Me Wonder” surpasses.

Title: Stronger Than I Was!
About:
This sympathetic song provides an insight into Eminem’s relationship with ex-wife Kim, and how he apologizes for acting how he did towards her in the past. The song is the polar opposite of the track “Kim” from The Marshall Mathers LP album.
Moreover, Em, at least in the first two verses of the song, sings instead of rapping—which has only happened few times before:
Yo, I can’t sing, I feel like singing; I wanna fuckin' sing!
It is not clear in which perspective the first two verses are written, whether from Em’s or Kim’s point of view, and nothing was ever said about this matter either. Probably, this was left purposely vague.

Title: Dark Fantasy!
About:
Preceded by the darkest period of Kanye’s life, which culminated in the infamous Taylor Swift incident, this conceptual album digs deeper into the dark side of fame.
This track mainly showcases the side of fame people first think of, but Nicki’s intro and the mourning hook indicate we’ll hear something different.
Although a music video wasn’t released, it’s the first track featured in Kanye’s full-length film Runaway.

Title: Comfortably Numb!
About:
Pink’s emotional journey into the past is both interrupted and heightened when his management breaks into the hotel room and has a doctor inject him with a drug that will snap him out of his drug-induced malaise, ensuring that he can still perform at a concert later that evening.
Roger Waters wrote the lyrics inspired by an experience where he was injected with tranquilizers before a 1977 concert in Philadelphia:
That was the longest two hours of my life, trying to do a show when you can hardly lift your arm.

Title: Redbone
About:
“Redbone” is about paranoia and infidelity in a relationship. As a slang term, “redbone” distinguishes a lighter-skinned black person of mixed race. Bino fears that his efforts to keep this woman satisfied will drive her away.
In a Genius video, Ludwig Göransson, the song’s producer, recreated “Redbone” and explained that it was heavily inspired by Funkadelic, George Clinton’s 1970s psychedelic funk band. According to Göransson, Gambino even sent him Funkadelic’s music as a reference point for the production, which was created mostly on live instruments.
Gambino first revealed “Redbone” at his Pharos concert experience, but it officially premiered on BBC Radio 1 as Annie Mac’s Hottest Record. The track received worldwide recognition shortly thereafter, shooting to #12 on Billboard’s Hot 100 list. It got a second wind almost four months later when parodies of the song turned it into a meme.
“Redbone” was featured in the opening credits of Jordan Peele’s horror film, Get Out. In a February 2017 interview with Hip Hop DX, Jordan revealed why he picked the record for the movie:
Well, first of all, I love the “Stay woke”–that’s what this movie is about. I wanted to make sure that this movie satisfied the black horror movie audience’s need for characters to be smart and do things that intelligent and observant people would do.
The song won a Grammy for Best Traditional R&B Performance at the 2018 Grammys held on Jan. 28, 2018.

Title: Happy!
About:
Pharrell made the world “Happy” in 2014 with this feel-good anthem. The song soared to #1 in 35 countries—it was the best selling song of 2014.
Pharrell wrote and produced “Happy” for the soundtrack of Despicable Me 2—it also became the lead single of his second studio album, G I R L. The song appears in a scene in the film where the lead character Gru dances through streets with joy because he has fallen in love.
Pharrell told W Magazine that “Happy” came after he unsuccessfully pitched Despicable Me 2 producer Chris Meledandri on some other songs he’d written.
After nine different songs, recorded fully, they were like, ‘No, no, no, no.’ So I went back and wrote ‘Happy.’ I didn’t have the melody, just the chorus. For 20 minutes after I finished, I was jumping around the room. I told Chris to listen to the song in his car, that if he didn’t like ‘Happy,’ I didn’t know what to give him.
Originally, Pharrell intended Cee Lo Green to sing the song—but things didn’t work out. He told Howard Stern:
Cee Lo Green wanted to do it—and he did do it. He sounded amazing on it. He burns my version! The powers that be, at the time, did not see it fit for him. Some folks on his team just felt that the priority should be on his album at the time, so they elected not to do that song. It’s not his fault; he was totally down with it. He was one of the most gracious people about it when ‘Happy’ came out
“Happy” is repeated 56 times over the course of the song. 62% of the “Happy” is dedicated to its catchy chorus—about 20% more than the average pop song.
The first-ever 24-hour music video was made for “Happy”—it follows 400 different people enjoying life while they walk around LA.

Title: Baby Powder
About:
Following the release of her debut single “Medallion” in February 2020, Jenevieve returns with “Baby Powder,” released on a month later on March 25, 2020.
Throughout the track, Jenevieve sings about the sadness of relationships as she recounts her first love and subsequent heartbreak. The song samples Japanese singer-songwriter Anri’s 1982 track “Last Summer Whisper”, which results in a dreamy mix of contemporary R&B and neo-soul.
Additionally, a music video accompanies the song, which reached 10 million views after one year to the day.

Title: You Come First!
About:
“You Come First” is an upbeat pop track which had been performed live months prior to it’s release, but originally without Saweetie’s feature.
The song sounds innocent enough upon first listen and is all about putting your partners needs before your own, but has an underlying sexual theme with many sexual references.

Title: Weight in Gold
About:
“Weight In Gold” is a single from Gallant preceding his debut album. The track was released on June 30, 2015 and was featured as the first-ever World First track on Apple Music’s Beats 1.
In an interview with HungerTV, Gallant explains:
It’s about just really feeling like you’re weighed down, in a really dangerous way. I know that when I was writing it I had something really specific in mind, personally. It was my instinct to shut everything off but with that song I tried to let myself feel a little bit, just to feel even more vulnerable, that’s how I approached writing it. I would say it’s one of the most special songs I’ve written for myself, it dared to let people into my life a little bit more with using using extreme encryption or metaphor.

Title: Motorbike
About:
“Motorbike” was released as the third single from Poppy’s fifth studio album Zig. The song is a female empowerment anthem that celebrates women on motorcycles, inspired by the 1968 romantic drama film The Girl on a Motorcycle starring Marianne Faithfull. Poppy described the song as being about “power and being in control”, but also about “being sexual and fun and okay with femininity”. It was released alongside a music video that shows Poppy in a leather tracksuit riding a motorcycle, interspersed with suggestive shots of her dancing and posing with the motorcycle.
The single was initially teased on September 5, 2023 with stills from the music video on Instagram. Poppy then announced the release date of the song the following day, accompanied by photos of her posing with a motorcycle taken from the music video shoot. It was released on September 12, 2023.

Title: Summer Too Hot
About:
“Summer Too Hot” is a bouncy track detailing Chris’ plans to “wet up” the summer. The track was released as the lead single from Chris’ upcoming eleventh studio album and announced via Instagram on June 17, along with a short snippet and the album art.
Chris first teased new music on June 17 via Instagram, stating:
If yall thought I wasn’t going to release a single for the summer …. Think again
He also announced that his upcoming eleventh album would be “just the right amount of magic to digest,” moving away from 30+ albums such as October 2017’s Heartbreak on a Full Moon.

Title: More!
About: This is a single released independently by the British duo The Citrus Clouds.

Title: Starboy!
About:
“Starboy” is The Weeknd’s realization of his mega-stardom. Transforming from mysterious Canadian act to Billboard mainstay. Abel has come a long way, but with negative consequences in his journey to the top, he has developed mixed feelings towards fame.
“Starboy” potentially marks a new era for The Weeknd. On the release date of the song, Abel posted an Instagram picture with the caption “r.i.p ‘@abelxo,’” marking the turn of his character and sound.
The song features production from French electronic duo Daft Punk, marking the first collaboration between The Weeknd and Daft Punk, and the latter’s first single since “Give Life Back To Music” was released in early-2014. A month before release Abel and Daft Punk had spent time together in the studio, and it was obviously so fruitful he decided to make one of their collaborations the album’s lead single.
“Starboy” is the title track and lead single from The Weeknd’s third studio album, released on November 25th, 2016.

Title: Locked Out Of Heaven
About:
“Locked Out of Heaven” is a new wave, funk and reggae rock song about the rapturous feelings brought about by a relationship infused with positive emotion as well as good sex. Several music critics noted that the song is influenced by a number of bands, with The Police being most cited. Mars admitted that he was influenced by the band to write the song.
It received generally positive response from most music critics; his vocals were praised, being called “smooth” and “sweet,” while its sound was lauded, with the song being called “interesting” and a “musical evolution”. The song was a commercial success, topping the Billboard Hot 100 for six consecutive weeks and the Canadian Hot 100 for three consecutive weeks, while also charting inside the top-ten in 20 countries.

Title: Moonshine
About:
A song off Bruno Mars' sophomore album, Unorthodox Jukebox, about his drinking.
As you listen to it, you’ll find some innuendos/double entendres integrating his drinking experiences and his experiences with a woman.

Title: Honey
About:
“Honey” is Kehlani’s second release since January 2017’s SweetSexySavage, the first being her G-Eazy collaboration “Good Life.” The song, written for her lover, sees Kehlani using romance as an escape.
Kehlani teased her new music on Instagram in early October 2017 and told fans to comment honey emojis if they wanted to hear a new track. She then posted the cover art and a SoundCloud link to the song. About four days later, “Honey” was available on all other streaming services as well.
She first performed the song live at Billboard’s Women in Music 2017. The official music video was released a few days later, on December 4th.

Title: Double Fantasy!
About:
“Double Fantasy” is a reference to a relationship that is based on physical and materialistic pleasures, and the Weeknd and Future know that it’s not morally right or socially acceptable. It serves as a sonic companion to The Idol, a series co-created by the Weeknd and Euphoria creator Sam Levinson which explores similar themes.
In his verse, Abel identifies that his lover thinks they are right for each other, but he knows that the relationship is wrong. In his verse, Future adds his own perspective, describing a relationship where both parties know it’s wrong but indulge in it anyway. He talks about their physical intimacy and indulging in materialistic pleasures.
The Weeknd originally played a snippet of the song on Instagram Live on April 10, 2023. The song would later be performed at Coachella 2023 on April 14, where Abel was featured alongside the song’s co-producer, Metro Boomin. The single was officially released on April 21, 2023.

Title: Wait a Minute!
About: Arguably the most sophisticated and lively song on the album, this neo-funk number is extremely catchy. Following in her brother’s footsteps, Willow is experimenting with her own spirituality and growing into a young adult. We follow her into the crevices of her brain, as she lets us in on this transitional point in her life through her unique sound.

Title: LET’S PRETEND WE’RE NUMB!
About:
“LETS PRETEND WE’RE NUMB” is another softer, more emotional song from X. The beat, produced by Khaed, samples parts from Yuna’s “Someone Out of Town”.
X is rapping about a girl, most likely his ex-girlfriend Cinthia, and what it feels like seeing each other after their relationship ended in a bad way. He knows that she misses him and is trying to use other guys to make X jealous. Likewise, X is trying to do the same with girls, but they aren’t giving him the same emotional connections as his ex-girlfriend. Towards the end of the song, X raps about the anger he feels when thinking about her and starts to blame himself for losing her.
On May 24th, 2023, it was announced by X’s team that Let’s Pretend We’re Numb will be released on all streaming services on Friday the 26th.

Title: Floor Seats (Text Back)!
About:
“ Floor Seats ” is a melodic rap inspired song by Cleveland artist Moxas, following the releases of singles “ OFFiT ” and “ 8 Gates Flow ”.
Moxas talks about how he has a lot on his mind about love, girls and people trying to beef with him, saying that he doesn’t feel the same way about those people and would rather steer clear.

Title: Bye Bye Bye!
About:
“Bye Bye Bye” is the introductory song of ‘N Sync’s album No Strings Attached. It is arguably the quintet’s most popular song, and also arguably has the most overt messages: they’re breaking up with a girl for being a pain in the butt.
Their classic music video exemplifies how this girl continues to play with their emotions.

Title: Bad Habits!
About:
On “Bad Habits,” Ed Sheeran reflects on past habits of constant drinking and late night partying and what these actions tend to lead to. No matter how pure Ed’s intentions are at the start, the night brings out a different side of him that garners darker results.
The vampire costume seen in the music video reflects the dark side of him and displays the ‘bad habits’ he carries with himself.
With each song on this album representing a specific moment in his life, Ed noted that “Bad Habits” derives from his desire to be fully present as a new father. Specifically, he wanted to curb habits of partying and drinking so that he could be ready to take his then-pregnant wife to the hospital whenever her water broke.

Title: Options
About: Track 9 on PARTYNEXTDOOR TWO has Party venting about a girl by addressing her sexual appeal. He’s torn because of his attraction to her but he knows she’s desirable and has options…meaning other dudes she can get with. So PND gets faded and keeps in mind he’s always an option too.

Title: Poetry: How Does It Feel Now???!
About:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8XQVU85mNnk
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fZoMzWraS_c

Title: Under the Influence!
About:
Over chilled production, “Under the Influence” finds Chris “under the influence” of Robitussin cough syrup and the impression of his lover as he croons for her to come over to his place for the night.
“Under the Influence” marks Nigerian producer Kiddominant’s second production credit on Indigo (Extended), also penning the Davido-assisted “Lower Body.”

Title: Lemonade
About:
Five Internet Money Records producers team up and enlist Gunna, Don Toliver, and NAV for “Lemonade,” the second single off of Internet Money’s debut studio album B4THESTORM. The song was released alongside an accompanying music video directed by Cole Bennett of Lyrical Lemonade.
This single was first previewed by Internet Money founder Taz Taylor during a June 2020 Instagram live. Gunna then previewed a snippet of his verse later that month.
The song then saw several leaks before surfacing in its entirety on July 14, 2020. Notably, a demo of Don’s solo version of the song leaked on June 24, 2020, revealing a verse absent from the final song.

Title: Mia Khalifa!
About:
This song was made when Smoke Hijabi misread a fake tweet as a real one from retired adult film star Mia Khalifa that was posted by the Instagram meme page trashpump.
Aqsa responded to the fake tweet with a (now deleted) video on Twitter, even showing the image of the fake tweet as well.
The song got a resurgence of popularity around late October 2018 after nyannyancosplay made a TikTok video, lip-synching to the song whilst cosplaying as Nico Yazawa from Love Live! School Idol Project. The video has since been removed from TikTok, but it can still be seen on YouTube here.

Title: BTS – 봄날 (Spring Day) (English Translation)!
About:
“봄날 (Spring Day)” is the title track of BTS’s sophomore repackage album, You Never Walk Alone. The midtempo song is usually described as either an alternative hip-hop track or a pop-rock ballad with some Brit rock influence.
The song’s lyrics give a positive light over a friend’s absence, utilizing a seasonal metaphor to deliberate on how things will get better afterward. Considered one of the greatest boy-band songs of all time by Rolling Stone, its lyricism was praised countless times by music critics since it was able to showcase longing and nostalgia in an effective way.
After the music video’s release, it quickly broke the record for the most-watched music video in 24 hours by any K-Pop group as of then, surpassing 11.8 million views. Following the album’s release “봄날 (Spring Day)” scored BTS' first ‘roof hit’ on Korea’s largest music streaming service, MelOn. Up to date, the song is MelOn’s longest-charting song in the streaming platform’s digital chart. “봄날 (Spring Day)” went on to rank #1 on eight real-time Korean music charts. The song hit #1 on the major music charts of 14 countries and placed in Spotify’s Viral 50 in 12 countries, including the US. It debuted on Billboard’s Bubbling Under Hot 100 Chart at No. 15 and was the first song by a K-pop group to reach the top 10 on iTunes US.
The song received several awards, including Song of the Year at the 2017 Melon Music Awards and a Digital Bongsang at the 32nd Golden Disc Awards. The music video was awarded with Music Video of the Year at South Korea’s biggest award show, the Mnet Asian Music Awards.

Title: Easy On Me!
About:
“Easy On Me” marked Adele’s return after a six-year hiatus. The song is the lead single off her fourth studio album 30. In an October 2021 BBC Radio 1 interview, Adele revealed that it was the first song written for the album, as she started it back in 2019. The song sees the singer speak about her fraught childhood, her lost marriage and the lessons learnt and unlearnt about family, love and abandonment along the way.
Following recent teases of her long-awaited fourth album on October 5, 2021, Adele took to Twitter, announcing that she would be dropping a brand new song on October 15th, sharing the teaser trailer to this track. The first verse of the song was played during Adele’s Instagram Live on October 9, 2021.
Upon its release, “Easy On Me” broke the world record for the most streamed song in 24 hours, with 24 million streams. This unseated the previously record-holders BTS for their song “Butter.”

Title: Summer Sixteen!
About:
This song is Drake’s announcement to the rap game that he’s on top and plans to dominate 2016. “Summer Sixteen” is split into two parts—the beat switches at the start of the second verse. The first verse rekindles Drake’s beef with Meek Mill, while the second verse addresses up and coming Toronto artists like Tory Lanez.
The song premiered on Episode 14 of Beats 1’s OVO Sound Radio. Only 15 minutes after the song’s release, Meek Mill fired back with “War Pain.” The track addresses several of Drake’s lines directly, including his Jay Z comparison.
The surprise has left hip-hop fans bemused. How did Meek get the track? According to Meek Mill, Drake’s ghostwriter told him the lyrics.
On the same OVO Sound show, Drake announced that his long awaited fourth studio album Views would be released some time in April 2016. The exact day was revealed on Episode 19 of OVO Sound Radio, as it was announced that the album would be released on April 29, 2016.
The single artwork was designed by Filip Pagowski, the creator of the COMME des GARÇONS Play logo, one of the most recognizable logos in the fashion world today.

Song: 이쁜 여자가 좋더라 (Feat. Gist, Jayci yucca)
Title: 이쁜 여자가 좋더라 (Boys Like Girls)!
Power Trip!
About:
In 2013, Cole dropped Born Sinner’s lead single just in time for Valentine’s Day. His Facebook page stated this was a continuation of “Dreams,” a track off of his second mixtape, The Warm Up.
According to Cole himself the song has a double meaning, with the song not only about his love for a girl, but his love for hip-hop and how it’s got him up all night trying to create chart topping love songs.
A “Power trip” by definition is:
A self-aggrandizing quest for ever-increasing control over others
This definition fits both “Dreams” and “Power Trip” in that Cole is so obsessed with a girl, that he’s willing to kill for her (in the music video, he actually does). It also explains his rise to stardom and how he wants to progress in the rap industry as well. He only wants to keep getting bigger and better.
Read how they mixed the track, and why it took them 10 days, on soundonsound.

Title: Agust D - 어땠을까 (Dear my friend) ft. Kim Jong Wan (김종완) (English Translation)!
About: “Dear My Friend” is the closing song on D-2, and it details the story of Yoongi’s childhood friend. With a more ballad like tone, he recalls a friend he had in his hometown Daegu that’s in jail. He asks himself whether they would’ve still been friends if things were different or if he had stopped his friend. This isn’t a hateful song, it’s more nostalgic and regretful. The Korean ‘어땠을까’ translates to ‘what would it be’, an all encompassing phrase for the track.

Title: Shutdown!
About:
With his fourth album, Konnichiwa, delayed beyond the anticipated March release, to keep his fans at bay Skepta released “Shutdown,” which he later incorporated into both Konnichiwa and The Tim Westwood Mix.
Before the official release, he teased at “Shutdown” through several Vine clips ultimately allowing Ragz Originale, co-producer, to announce the official release date.
https://twitter.com/RagzOriginale/status/571684960479907840
The song dropped just 4 days after Skepta was featured in Kanye West’s performance of “All Day” at the 2015 Brit Awards.
Drake, who thanked Skepta in the If You’re Reading This It’s Too Late album credits, is sampled on the track’s intro and outro skits. Later, the two performed “Shutdown” together at London’s Wireless festival in the summer of 2015.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BVf_If9Zqv4

Title: Love Me!
About:
On “Love Me,” Weezy, Drake, and Future talk about their women and how they care about nothing but them—as long as they love them back. It was released as the second single from Wayne’s tenth studio album, I Am Not a Human Being II.
The song was a success, peaking at #9 on the Billboard Hot 100—notably becoming Future’s first and only top ten single until he released “Mask Off” in February 2017, which peaked at #5.
The official music video premiered on February 14, 2013, on MTV Jams. In an interview with MTV News a day prior, Wayne talked about the concept of the music video:
The concept of the video is really just to create awesome scenery. A scenery that every man… anyone wanna be in […] I appreciate Drake and Future for doin’ this because I’m quite sure they’re hella busy like I am, but I ain’t never too busy to get in a bed with three women, with water everywhere.

Title: Pink Venom
About:
Ahead of their highly-anticipated second studio album, BORN PINK, BLACKPINK is enchanting with their charisma and talents on its pre-release single, “Pink Venom.”
The song kicks off with a classic hip-pop beat, with the melody being accompanied by traditional Korean instruments—including a gayageum, a pluck zither containing 12 strings. Additionally, the song’s lyrics explore the girls' evil plan of attracting others into their dangerous-yet-lovely “Pink Venom.”
The music video is YG Entertainment’s highest-budget music video, which showcases the girls in multiple dark scenes with an antique-yet-mystical feel.

Title: Pound Cake / Paris Morton Music 2!
About:
One of the most anticipated songs off Nothing Was The Same, which was originally a loose Magna Carta… Holy Grail track, features El Padrino himself. This marks the third time they’ve collaborated on each other’s albums, following 2009’s “Off That” and 2010’s “Light Up.”
The track peaked at #65 on the Billboard Hot 100 and #24 on the Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs chart.
This track serves as the outro track to NWTS. The second half also serves as a long-awaited follow-up to Drake’s “Paris Morton Music.”

Song: 내 기쁨은 너가 벤틀리를 끄는 거야
Title: 내 기쁨은 너가 벤틀리를 끄는 거야 (My Pleasure Is That You Ride The Bentley)!
Officially Missing You
About: Lead single from Tamia’s third studio album, “More”.

Title: BTS - 시차 (My Time) (English Translation)!
About: “시차 (My Time)” is the 9th song of BTS' Map of the Soul: 7 album. It is Jungkook’s solo song of the album, and it chronologically follows his emotions from when he was a 2011 trainee at Bighit to now.

Title: Stay With Me!
About:
“Stay with Me” sees Smith yearning for a lasting relationship after a one-night stand leaves them craving more. One of their biggest hits, it’s gone #1 in countries like the United States, Canada, and Ireland, and even won two Grammy awards.
Written with Jimmy Napes and Tourist in Old Street, London studio, Smith claimed “the song just flowed out of us so naturally.” When Tourist played the first three chords, Napes quickly added a drum layer, and the rest of the track took only 30-40 minutes to write.
Upon the track’s release, listeners noticed similarities between “Stay With Me” and Tom Petty’s 1989 hit “I Won’t Back Down.” Smith’s team said that this was purely coincidental, but agreed to give Petty co-writer credits, and Petty stated that he has no ill feelings for Smith.
The music video was directed by Jamie Thraves, who also directed the music videos for Smith’s “Money On My Mind,” Radiohead’s “Just,” Coldplay’s “The Scientist,” by and Death Cab for Cutie’s “I Will Follow You into the Dark.” It features Sam walking down the street in Hackney, London, among other settings.

Song: Rainy day (Feat. ASH ISLAND, Skinny Brown)
Title: Rainy Day Women #12 & 35!
About:
Dylan wanted to hire an actual school marching band for the opening track on Blonde on Blonde so that the backing music had that amateurish, “ramshackle” sloppiness – but the marching band he heard played way too strict and tight.
The studio musicians in Nashville played it like they didn’t know what they were doing – because they were good enough to do so, although some musicians claim that they smoked a “huge amount” of marijuana and “got pretty wiped out”.
Dylan biographer, Robert Shelton, wrote in his book that he was told by Phil Spector that the inspiration for the song came when Spector and Dylan heard a Ray Charles song, “Let’s Go Get Stoned”.
The song is cryptic, and found some controversy as a “drug song”. Time magazine, on July 1, 1966, wrote:
In the shifting multi-level jargon of teenagers, ‘to get stoned’ does not mean to get drunk but to get high on drugs… a ‘rainy-day woman’, as any junkie knows, is a marijuana cigarette.
Despite reaching the top 10 in the US, UK, Canada and The Netherlands, the controversy caused the song to stop being played on some radio stations. Dylan lashed back, saying:
I’m not going to play any more concerts in England. I’d just like to say that the next song is what your English musical papers would call a ‘drug song’. I have never and never will write a ‘drug song’. I just don’t know how to. It’s not a ‘drug song’. It’s just vulgar.
(from No Direction Home: The Life and Music of Bob Dylan)
The title could also be a Biblical reference, taken from the Book of Proverbs “which contains a huge number of edicts for which one could genuinely get stoned”. In particular; chapter 27, verse 15:
A continual dropping in a very rainy day and a contentious woman are alike.
(from Gathered from Coincidence: A Singular History of Sixties' Pop)
According to some sources, the song got its name after two women, a mother and daughter, came in to the studio from the rain. Dylan guessed their ages correctly as 12 and 35.
In a 2012 interview with Rolling Stone, Dylan said when asked if he ever worries about people interpreting his work in misguided ways:
It doesn’t surprise me that some people would see it that way. But these are people that aren’t familiar with the Book of Acts.

Title: Meteor Shower!
About: The song “Meteor Shower” is about a person and who they love as they go through a metaphorical of a literal meteor shower. The person speaking cares very much about the other and would sacrifice many things for them and says so throughout the song.

Song: 사이렌 Remix (Feat. UNEDUCATED KID, Paul Blanco)
Title: 사이렌 (Siren) (Remix)!
About:
The remix of Siren has surfaced March 20th.
With a strong melancholic hook from YDP Chin, his label-mate UNEDUCATED KID and the Canadian-Korean rapper Paul Blanco add each a verse reminiscing their childhood devastated by poverty and violence.

Title: Closer Than This
About:
As a beautiful heartfelt farewell, “Closer Than This” by Jimin encapsulates his message of love towards his fans, ARMY.
In the song’s lyrics, Jimin reminisces about his history with ARMY and sees that throughout every point of his musical journey, they were there. Next, he then acknowledges that although there is a pause in the time that he can physically be with ARMY, it is simply just that; a pause. It is a comma that will lead them to a new story filled with pages to write out new memories. Through this expression, Jimin shows that he is still there; his heart is closer than any pause that could try to separate them.
Jimin’s words in this song serve as his reminder to ARMY that he will always be close to them. The distance between them is only temporary and through ARMY singing this song with him, their love will span that distance and bring them closer than this.

Song: 헤어지지 못하는 여자, 떠나가지 못하는 남자 (Feat. 정인)
Title: 헤어지지 못하는 여자, 떠나가지 못하는 남자 (Can’t Breakup Girl, Can’t Breakaway Boy)!
About:
The song “Can’t Breakup Girl, Can’t Breakaway Boy” is part of Leessang’s 6th album Hexagonal. The song is featuring Jung In.
The song explores the theme of a dysfunctional relationship where both individuals find themselves unable to break up despite their lack of love for each other.

Title: Sorry Not Sorry!
About:
“Sorry Not Sorry” is an anthem towards Demi’s haters. In it, Demi confidently shows themselves off to their haters, which makes them feel annoyed that they’re (Demi) looking and feeling so good and they’re not. The track is lead single from their sixth album, Tell Me You Love Me.
The single was teased by Lovato and their team through cryptic updates on their social media accounts with the hashtag #SNS.
The song peaked at #6 on the Billboard Hot 100, which made it Demi Lovato’s highest career peak, besting their #9 peak of 2009’s “This Is Me.”

Title: m y . l i f e!
About:
On “m y . l i f e,” Cole recruits 21 Savage and Morray for a track on which they discuss the struggles of their respective come-ups and the benefits of their perseverance. They also speak on what their lives could have been like if they didn’t fight to make it out.
The instrumental of this track was first teased in Applying Pressure, the documentary for the album. Also in the short film, Cole and 21 Savage are seen having a discussion about the album, which can be considered foreshadowing his appearance on the record.
J. Cole has famously avoided features on his albums since 2014 Forest Hills Drive, turning it into a cultural phenomenon. This is notably the first of two tracks on The Off-Season that contain rapping from other artists, making them the first occurrences of a guest verse on a Cole solo album since 2013’s Born Sinner.

Title: BABYMONSTER - BATTER UP (Romanized)!
About:
“BATTER UP” is BABYMONSTER’s first digital single released on November 27, 2023. Over a catchy rap-like beat, the members sing and rap about their confidence and introduce themselves to us in song.
On November 9th, the BABYMONSTER social media accounts teased a debut poster featuring the date of the release, and on November 19 the title of the single was revealed.
BATTER UP by BABYMONSTER is created by Jared Lee (Producer), ASA (BABYMONSTER), CHOI HYUN SUK, LEE CHANHYUK (이찬혁), WHERE THE NOISE, Yang Hyun Suk (양현석), BIGTONE (빅톤) (KOR), Chaz Mishan & Dee.P (KOR). A

Title: МАМБЛ (MUMBLE)!
About:
24 июня 2018 года Фладда опубликовал тизер клипа в своём Инстаграме.
А 25 июня 2018-го уже вышел сам клип, режиссёром которого стал Рустам Курташкин.
Спустя короткий промежуток времени после выпуска микстейпа Прилунение GONE.Fludd опубликовал ещё одну композицию — по звучанию она сильно отличается.
За бит ответственен Toreno.

Title: 자격지심 (Inferiority Complex)!
About:
Kicking off with a comforting R&B welcome, “자격지심 (Inferiority Complex)” is an song by Park Kyung, that surprisingly features EUNHA. Additionally, the song is the fourth track on his 2017 Notebook EP.
With the aforementioned track, it’s accompanied by EUNHA’s heavenly vocals and Park Kyung’s heavy raps that capture the feeling of getting your love rejected, but still loving that special person no matter what.

Title: iKON - 사랑을 했다 (LOVE SCENARIO) (Romanized)!
About:
Fronted by this playful hip-pop single “Love Scenario,” their second studio LP Return features 12 tracks that were all co-written or feature lyrics by members B.I and/or Bobby. YG Entertainment lablemates, including Psy, BIGBANG’s Taeyang, and Epik High’s Tablo, also had a role in songwriting tracks alongside some frequent collaborators, including Choice37 and Teddy Park.
(I can’t type the rest because that would be plagiarizing.)

Title: Right Now!
About:
Future and producer Metro Boomin connect with Uncle Murda for an moody club banger, which is most popular for a single line: “Young Metro don’t trust you I'ma shoot you.”
“Right Now” is where Future spits the lyric for the very first time, but it has since been sampled on Drake and Future’s “Jumpman” and Kanye West’s “Father Stretch My Hands, Pt. 1.”
The line has also become a popular meme.
Musically, the song is set in the club, where Future is posted with his boys (100 shooters to be exact) and spending lots of money (100 bottles), while Murda raps about uniting Bloods and Crips, and shooting at police.
Unlike most of his songs, Uncle Murda incorporates the use of auto-tune to create a warbly flow not unlike Future’s signature delivery.
The music video, shot by Eif Rivera, was released via World Star Hip-Hop on October 28, 2015. It includes cameos from Fabolous, DJ Clue and more.

Title: Man’s Not Hot!
About:
Released after the viral success of his freestyle on the BBC Radio 1 series Fire in the Booth, British comedian Michael Dapaah converted it into an official song titled “Man’s Not Hot” under the name Big Shaq, a parody character of the British “roadmen” culture.
The song was released under Island Records on September 22, 2017.
It has since spawned an official remix featuring notable British rappers Lethal Bizzle, Chip, Krept & Konan and Jme.

Title: ZICO - 아무노래 (Any Song) (English Translation)!
About:
“아무노래 (Any Song)” by ZICO has all the makings of a genre-bending hit which debuted at number four on Billboard’s World Digital Song Sales chart, and, popular video apps aside.
Over an array of jazzy piano keys and production that builds and evolves to emulate a party hitting its groove at 2 a.m., ZICO delivers a deceptively euphoric vibe.
In reality, “아무노래 (Any Song)” walks the tightrope between its blissful backing production and existential themes. ZICO sings of fleeting youth, built-up stress, and using music as a literal, momentary escape from his worries. It is a sentiment that may get lost in translation. Rapturous and melancholic in one fell swoop, it is a brilliant example of the multiplicity capable in pop.

Title: Dynamic Duo - AEAO (Romanized)!
About:
The song “AEAO” by Dynamic Duo featuring DJ Premier discusses the challenges of pursuing one’s dreams in the face of a harsh reality. The rappers, 최자 and 개코, touch on themes such as resilience, perseverance, and self-doubt. In the first verse, 최자 describes how they had learned to pick themselves up every time they stumbled and relied on their loyalty to each other to overcome obstacles. Though they faced setbacks and struggles, they refused to lose hope and continued to pursue their dreams.
The chorus sung by 개코 expresses the desire for peace of mind and the wish to be able to share in life’s journey with loved ones until the end of time. The second verse, sung by 개코, touches on the difficulties of life, including the pressure to succeed, the daily grind, and fatigue. Despite these difficulties, the rappers recognize the importance of criticizing and supporting each other to sustain hope. They also acknowledge the inevitability of aging and the need to accept the wrinkles of life.
In the end, the sampled outro repeats that the game won’t change, that the same challenges and struggles in life will always persist. The song’s message is that even in a difficult reality, it is possible to pursue and achieve one’s dreams through perseverance, hope, and unwavering loyalty to oneself and others.

Title: Stray Kids - 특 (S-Class) (Romanized)!
About: “S-Class” is a song by Stray Kids that means a special top class, and it’s related to ‘★★★★★ (5-STAR)’. The song embodies ‘Stray Kids’ inherent novelty and challenge’ and carries the message that ‘Among the odd ones, we are the most peculiar; among the special ones, we shine the most’. The English title is “S-Class”, which means like the highest class. The song composition feels like multiple songs combined, moving beyond diverse sound sources and song forms, delivering freshness.

Song: 내 생애 가장 행복한 시간 (Feat. 허각)
Title: 내 생애 가장 행복한 시간 Pt. 2 (The Happiest Time of My Life, Pt. 2)!
BLACKPINK - 뚜두뚜두 (DDU-DU DDU-DU) (Romanized)!
About:
“뚜두뚜두 (DDU-DU DDU-DU)” is the trap-influenced title track from K-pop girl group BLACKPINK. The song was produced by YG Entertainment’s in-house producer Teddy Park, who has been behind many of K-pop’s biggest hits, including BLACKPINK’s previous singles.
In “DDU-DU DDU-DU,” the girls are unapologetically savage. They’re not shy about letting you know about the success they’ve amassed.
A teaser of the MV was released on June 13 by YG Entertainment, followed by the release of the official MV two days later, on June 15 at 6PM KST.

Title: Friends with Benefits (FWB)!
About:
KSI’s ode to friends with benefits (friends who have a sexual relationship without being emotionally involved). He has described the song as “like ‘Lamborghini’ but louder and cheaper”.
This single is notably more pop-sounding than KSI’s other songs, with electro-hop and EDM influences. KSI even stated that he was scared of how people would react, but was very happy to see positive reception.

Song: 만남은 쉽고 이별은 어려워 (Feat. Leellamarz) (Prod. TOIL)
Title: 만남은 쉽고 이별은 어려워 (Meeting is easy, parting is hard)!
The Schuyler Sisters!
Singer: Phillipa Soo, Jasmine Cephas-Jones, Leslie Odom, Jr., Original Broadway Cast of “Hamilton” & Renée Elise Goldsberry
About:
Referred to as the Destiny’s Child number of the musical, “The Schuyler Sisters” explosively introduces two of Hamilton’s major female players, Angelica and Eliza Schuyler… as well as their sister, Peggy, the Michelle Williams of the trio.
Give the modern attitude of the performers, listeners to the cast recording may hear the colloquial “werk,” particularly after Angelica’s badass line “I’m ‘a compel him to include women in the sequel;” however, the official, Miranda approved, liner notes transcribe all instances of the word as the formal “work.”
What we’re left with is an extended wordplay throughout the song between the conventional “work” and its modern cousin, to the point where it becomes impossible to parse which word is being used at any particular moment. This serves to conflate the two words/ideas—mixing exercising the mind with showing off, blending historical and modern ideas of fierceness—for the audience.
Alternatively, it creates a, perhaps conscious, dichotomy between the spoken and written word—one highlighting the sisters as contemporary women for their day, the other emphasizing their status as formidable founding mothers of our nation.

Title: NCT DREAM - 마지막 인사 (To My First) (English Translation)!
About:
It’s a song that helps us move on from our memorable first loves. It looks back on the firsts, the memories, laughter, and the very mundane things about love.
We were all so young when we first felt love. And once it ends and we grow up, we tend to hold onto the entirety of the memories of it, thinking and knowing we could’ve done better. After all that time, the only feeling we felt aside from reminisce, was regret.
We hold onto those memories because we fear the loneliness and emptiness away from those happy times of feeling free and living.
The song states that it’s okay to be awkward and make mistakes when in love, but it guides us to the conclusion that we all must let go and say goodbye to the memories that we hold onto dearly, even if it pains us to do so because sometimes, holding on hurts, especially if there’s no one to help you back up again. No one but yourself

Title: 마이웨이 (MY WAY)!
Singer: JUSTHIS, DON MALIK, Huh, KHAN, Mckdaddy & Los

Title: 한여름밤의 꿀: 다시 여름 (A Midsummer Night’s Sweetness: Summer Again)!

Song: 비 오는 날 듣기 좋은 노래 (Feat. Colde)
Title: Epik High - 비 오는 날 듣기 좋은 노래 (Rain Song) ft. Colde (English Translation)!
Stay With Me
About:
“Stay with Me” sees Smith yearning for a lasting relationship after a one-night stand leaves them craving more. One of their biggest hits, it’s gone #1 in countries like the United States, Canada, and Ireland, and even won two Grammy awards.
Written with Jimmy Napes and Tourist in Old Street, London studio, Smith claimed “the song just flowed out of us so naturally.” When Tourist played the first three chords, Napes quickly added a drum layer, and the rest of the track took only 30-40 minutes to write.
Upon the track’s release, listeners noticed similarities between “Stay With Me” and Tom Petty’s 1989 hit “I Won’t Back Down.” Smith’s team said that this was purely coincidental, but agreed to give Petty co-writer credits, and Petty stated that he has no ill feelings for Smith.
The music video was directed by Jamie Thraves, who also directed the music videos for Smith’s “Money On My Mind,” Radiohead’s “Just,” Coldplay’s “The Scientist,” by and Death Cab for Cutie’s “I Will Follow You into the Dark.” It features Sam walking down the street in Hackney, London, among other settings.

Song: 네가 없는 밤 (Feat. ASH ISLAND) (Prod. GRAY)
Title: 네가 없는 밤 (Without you)!
Counting Stars!
About:
The first track on OneRepublic’s third studio album Native, “Counting Stars” was inspired by lead singer Ryan Tedder’s writing sessions with Beyonce in the Hamptons. The song has sold over ten million copies in the United States alone.
The annotations on the left suggest a literal positive view of life and religion, an affirmation of care for his partner, a rejection of materialism and hope for the future.
Alternatively, it could be an ironic view of a questionable religious cult. Hence, the singer feels ‘something so right doing the wrong thing, something so wrong doing the right thing’. He’s ‘doing what he’s told’ mindlessly, without questioning. The crocodile in the music video could be a representation of the serpent. The cult leader is a dishonest bully who provokes convulsions in one woman. There are shady undertones to this lyric, depending on interpretation. A clever conundrum.

Title: Solo!
About:
On “Solo,” Frank touches on various aspects of being alone, such as self-love or waiting for a call from your lover. It’s not entirely clear if this song is specifically about experiences he’s had.
The song’s title has two interpretations. Throughout the song, Frank sings about how he is alone, or solo, but, through the chorus Frank explains that he’s “so low” emotionally and that he needs to “get high.”

Title: on the street!
About:
In his solo single “on the street" j-hope shares his candid feelings toward fans. The song was released as a gift and a kind of ‘goodbye’ to them, as j-hope has initiated the enlistment process for completing his military service, which is mandatory in South Korea.
The title “on the street” refers to j-hope’s roots as a former competitive street dancer and a member of a Gwangju dance crew, NEURON. That got him on the path to being recruited by BigHit in 2010, and later to becoming a member of BTS. Hope on the street is also the name of j-hope's street dancing videos series he’s publishing since 2015.
For the song, j-hope invited renowned American rapper J. Cole. The song marks their first collaboration; however, it wasn’t that much of a surprise for his fans. j-hope showed admiration for Cole’s music before—he made a shout-out to Cole’s 2011 debut studio album, Cole World: The Sideline Story, in his verse in “힙합성애자 (Hip Hop Phile)” off BTS' first full studio album, 2014's Dark & Wild. Moreover, “Born Singer,” which describes BTS' journey from trainees to rookie idols, was written by j-hope, RM, and SUGA to the instrumental of Cole's “Born Sinner.” BTS released that song on SoundCloud in 2013, just a month after their debut, but in 2022 they got clearance from the original composers (including J. Cole) and released it officially as a part of their anthology album, PROOF. Both rappers finally met during 2022 Lollapalooza, where j-hope made history, becoming the first South Korean act to headline a main stage at a US festival.

Title: DNA.!
About:
On “DNA.,” Kendrick Lamar adopts multiple viewpoints; celebrating, critiquing, and exploring his black heritage and culture. In the music video, Kendrick and actor Don Cheadle appear to trade bars, engaging in an argument using the song’s lyrics during an interrogation scene. We also learn that “DNA” stands for “Dead Nigger Association.”
Lamar is wearing a traditional Kung Fu uniform throughout the piece. In an interview with Pitchfork, Don Cheadle confirmed that he inadvertently inspired the ‘Kung Fu Kenny’ persona. Cheadle portrayed a Chinese-speaking martial artist named Kenny in the 2001 action-comedy sequel Rush Hour 2.
At the beginning of the video, the song “YAH.” can be heard echoing above—almost as if it is playing in Kendrick’s head as the camera pans down. “YAH.” comes directly after “DNA.” on DAMN.’s tracklist.
Following his initial encounter with Cheadle, who seems to be portraying a law enforcement figure, the ending of “BLOOD.” suddenly begins playing; when Cheadle turns on the lie-detector device, he begins convulsing, almost as if in a trance. This snippet comes from the video of Geraldo Rivera criticizing Kendrick’s lyrics on “Alright,” one of his most successful singles. The music video also contains snippets from “Hood Politics,” another song from Lamar’s March 2015 award-winning album, To Pimp A Butterfly.
Lastly, the song’s second verse samples a different segment of the same Fox News clip from “BLOOD.” and it’s worth noting that “BLOOD.” precedes “DNA.” on the album.

Title: 널 너무 모르고 (Don’t Know You)!
About:
“널 너무 모르고 (Don’t Know You)” is a song from Heize’s 3rd mini album You, clouds, rain.
The lyrics are about realizing that she didn’t truly understand her partner. She regrets not appreciating the little things and making him happy. She also feels guilty for not being able to give him anything in return for his love. In the end, she acknowledges that she didn’t know him well and that she didn’t give him the kind of love he wanted.

Title: 한 개도 몰라 (I don’t know a single thing)!
WE LIKE 2 PARTY
About:
“We Like 2 Party” is a single recorded by South Korean boy band BIGBANG. It was released digitally on June 5th, 2015, by YG Entertainment, as the fourth single from MADE Series, and the second from A.
“We Like 2 Party” was named one of the best music videos Of June 2015, and was described as “casual and inviting feeling the music video gives off from start to finish unscripted, ridiculous, and easily gets us into the party mood”.
The song ranked nine as the best 20 Korean singles in 2015 in Taiwan.
The “We Like 2 Party” music video was shot on Jeju Island on May 19, 2015. It features self-recorded footage from the members themselves; the members were drunk during the filming in order to have a more natural feeling for the music video, “You’ll be able to see the comfortable image of Big Bang rather than a more weighted-down Big Bang.” said a rep from the agency.
The music video showed a carefree side of Big Bang, playing, party and drinking alcohol, unlike their other songs, who have been known for their powerful image. Billboard gave a review of the song, saying that Big Bang showed the playful side of themselves, and also showed their strong friendship.

Title: Pusha Man/Paranoia!
About:
“Pusha Man/Paranoia” is a two-part tale. While “Pusha Man” finds Chance acting a fool, living the life of the rapper, “Paranoia” is a personal moment as he describes how it really feels to live in Chicago and watch loved ones getting killed left and right almost every day, especially in the summer.
When Acid Rap was re-released on streaming platforms on June 2019, this song was split in two. So, “Pusha Man” and “Paranoia” are individual songs in that version.

Song: 너는 나 나는 너 (말하고 싶은 비밀 X 이서연 (프로미스나인))
Title: ZICO (지코) - 너는 나 나는 너 (I Am You, You Are Me) (English Translation)!
About:
For his solo single “I Am You, You Are Me” the rapper showcases a different side of himself with the mid-tempo R&B track.
A bumpin' beat, subtle bass line and electronica flourishes back Zico’s smooth croons to highlight that the 23-year-old really has a soothing voice.
The plot of the music video is simple and sweet, where Zico finds himself continuously matching clothes with a teller behind a counter at a convience store. Soon, they realize it’s not a coincidence.
Throughout the track, he touches on the ways a couple connects at a young age, from physically to adolescent beliefs in tarot cards and horoscopes.

Title: NCT DREAM - 사랑은 또다시 (Love Again) (English Translation)!
Paranoid
About:
“Paranoid” is the opening track to Post Malone’s long-awaited beerbongs & bentleys. Post details his doubts about his security and safety with his friends and his rise to fame. He feels as if people could be manipulating him for his fame and fortune and begins taking more precautions to protect himself.
During his 2021 Rolling Loud performance, Post said that the song’s inspiration comes from losing trust in one of his closest friends within the music industry. Further details about who this is in reference to are unclear.
This song could also be influenced by the 2017 controversy in which infamous YouTuber, Jake Paul, uploaded a since-deleted video that initially contained footage of Post Malone’s private property after seeing his interview with H3H3 Productions in which Post criticized Paul’s single, “It’s Everyday Bro”, and then proceeded to purchase Paul’s merch for an ironic joke soon after.

Title: Closer to You!
About:
“Closer to You” by Jungkook of BTS, is an enchanting track that beautifully encapsulates the yearning to be closer to someone special. The lyrics, woven into a tapestry of emotive melodies, convey the profound emotions the protagonist harbors for their beloved.
Diving deeper into the lyrics, the protagonist tries to persuade their beloved with strong affirmations that they will always be there for them. While doing this, they do not ignore the fact that there may be adversities that could cause the affirmations to fall short. Instead of giving up, the protagonist strengthens their resolve and states that those hardships will only bring them closer to their partner.
In the lyrics brought to life by Jungkook’s powerful vocals, there is a continuous theme of a person who will always fight for their partner; someone who doesn’t care about what anyone else says and will stand strong in their love and desire. Through this theme, it can be seen that this song is a part of the exposition of the story told throughout the album, “Golden.” It is a story about a person’s journey through a relationship from falling in love to breaking up.
“Closer to You” describes the stage of falling in love through lyrics that depict the desire a person has for their prospective partner. The lyrics make sure that the protagonist’s desire is built on a strong foundation of a vow to love and support their partner through every adversity. Additionally, they attempt to remove the fear usually associated with relationship problems by stating that those issues will only bring them closer.
Released alongside 11 songs, this track is part of Jungkook’s debut album, “Golden,” and showcases Jungkook’s vocal prowess and ability to convey deep emotions. The album, well-received by fans and critics, offers a glimpse into Jungkook’s musical journey and his growth as an artist. “Closer to You” stands out as a testament to Jungkook’s artistry, resonating with many and touching the hearts of listeners.

Title: BIBI - 인생은 나쁜X (Life is a Bi…) (English Translation)!
Hate Sleeping Alone!
About:
Bonus Track (only on iTunes) off of Take Care.
Here, Drake details a complicated relationship that he’s staying in just because he hates sleeping alone.
This song also contains back-up vocals from Trey Songz.
Trivia: The production on this track samples Club Paradise, one of the tracks Drake used to build up to the release of Take Care.
https://youtu.be/EfGOqrXcm_Y

Title: I’m Not OK!
About:
This song is about her admitting to her feelings of uneasiness and discomfort about her relationship. In the live performances of this song on the ”I Used to Know Her” tour, H.E.R. explains how this generation likes to hide their true feelings and pretend like everything is fine. She wants the listeners to know “it’s okay not to be okay”.

She says everyone has their own personal battles and struggles and can feel alone while fighting through the tribulations. The important thing to remember is that you do not ever have to be alone in your struggle. She also encourages her listeners to “check on the strong friend” as much as possible.

Title: WAKE UP!
About:
At the time of this song’s release, Travis Scott and The Weeknd were in relationships with Kylie Jenner and Bella Hadid, respectively; it is likely that their relationships served as inspiration for this sexually explicit track.
This is the second collaboration between Travis and Abel on ASTROWORLD, following “SKELETONS,” which immediately precedes this song.
Genius later reported that “WAKE UP” was originally intended to be a part of The Weeknd’s March 2018 EP My Dear Melancholy,, but the song was later repurposed for ASTROWORLD because it did not suit the EP.

Title: Monster
About:
One of the most highlighted posse cuts of 2010, Kanye, Rick Ross, JAY-Z, and Nicki Minaj boast on how they’re a “Monster” on the mic. Fittingly, the song comes with a couple of horror movie references.
In a September 2010 MTV News interview, Rick Ross explained the origin behind the song:
I was actually in the studio with Kanye in Hawaii when he played the concept for me. I was blown away. I had the opportunity to sit in the studio while Nicki wrote her verse just off the record.
The song was originally released as a G.O.O.D. Friday track on August 27, 2010 before Ye replaced it with the “Runaway Love (Remix)” with himself, Justin Bieber and Raekwon. He, then, re-released “Monster” on October 23, 2010 as the third single from My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy and it peaked at #18 on the Billboard Hot 100 chart.
The song’s official music video, directed by Jake Nava, features dark, horror related imagery, with references with several horror movies, such as American Psycho and Saw. It became widely controversial on release, with medias labeling it as graphic, racist and sexist. As a result, it was banned on MTV and removed from Kanye’s YouTube account.

Title: Who Want Smoke?? (Remix)!
About:
For the remix of his breakout single, Nardo Wick enlists the help of recording artists Lil Durk, 21 Savage and G Herbo, and Cole Benett for the song’s visuals.
With a Chicago drill cadence, Lil Durk takes the opportunity to respond to those telling him to “Slide for Von” online whilst discussing the levels of violence and potential criminality involved in his lifestyle, alongside everyone else featured.
The conclusion of 21 Savage’s verse garnered viral acclaim through TikTok and other socials – accumulating 139,200 videos as of November 2021.

Title: SEVENTEEN - F*ck My Life (English Translation)!
About:
If you failed to this toxic world, let’s healing yourself by F*ck My Life, the title track from FML, the tenth mini album from SEVENTEEN.
A song based on old-school hip-hop rhythm. The sound realized by putting a conflicting codework on top of the drum sound reinterpreted with an analog sensibility is impressive. The lyrics were talking about a tired life. Be the only stupid one in this world; live with no passion, way to go, or aim. But intend to rise again and fight for this one life.
The music video conveys the song’s vibe by showing all the members facing immense conflicts in their life that look like there’s no way to solve them. But after seeing the light again, they decided to find their true selves again in other parts of this big world. Which inspired by 1998’s movie, “The Truman Show.”
Left: F*ck My Life M/V | Right: The Truman Show

Title: goodnight n go!
About:
In the lead-up to Sweetener, Ariana teased “goodnight n go” through a snippet on her Instagram story and later posted the title on Twitter.
The song draws heavy inspiration from Imogen Heap’s “Goodnight and Go.”, appropriating it for Sweetener by modifying the production and some lyrics. Ariana has stated on Instagram that Imogen was one of her primary inspirations as an artist, and made her who she is today.

Title: Bulls on Parade!
About:
“Bulls on Parade” was the second single released from Rage Against the Machine’s second album, Evil Empire.
The song discusses the subliminally oppressive leaders of the world in their never ceasing quest to conquer and kill for their own gain. The title reflects this; a bull is an aggressive animal only stopped by force, as are the world’s leading governments. These bulls are going on a parade to gain, by any means necessary.
The back of the 7-inch single states, “All sounds made by guitar, bass, drums and vocals.”
