Title: Drip Too Hard!
About:
“Drip Too Hard” is the lead single from Lil Baby and Gunna’s debut collaborative album, Drip Harder. Both the song and album title are homages to Baby and Gunna’s respective “Hard” and “Drip” series of mixtapes and albums.
The song features production from Turbo, who frequently collaborates with both artists and who also serves as executive producer of Drip Harder. Other notable collaborations by the trio are Gunna’s “Oh Okay” and “Sold Out Dates.”

Title: BLACKPINK - Kill This Love (Romanized)!
About:
“Kill This Love” is the title track for BLACKPINK’s second EP of the same name.
This is the highly anticipated release following their last Korean mini album SQUARE UP in 2018. It features intense lead brass electro-pop and marching band snares. The song is propelled by BLACKPINK’s charismatic rap and vocals.
In the first full group teaser, the looks caught attention for the resemblance with Tomb Raider even noticed by the official twitter of Tomb Raider, who replied to an article about it.
YG Entertainment, BLACKPINK’s label, spoiled that the new dance choreography was made by four world-class choreographers and was expected to be more dynamic than the choreography of any song that the group has introduced.

Title: Love Me!
About:
On “Love Me,” Weezy, Drake, and Future talk about their women and how they care about nothing but them—as long as they love them back. It was released as the second single from Wayne’s tenth studio album, I Am Not a Human Being II.
The song was a success, peaking at #9 on the Billboard Hot 100—notably becoming Future’s first and only top ten single until he released “Mask Off” in February 2017, which peaked at #5.
The official music video premiered on February 14, 2013, on MTV Jams. In an interview with MTV News a day prior, Wayne talked about the concept of the music video:
The concept of the video is really just to create awesome scenery. A scenery that every man… anyone wanna be in […] I appreciate Drake and Future for doin’ this because I’m quite sure they’re hella busy like I am, but I ain’t never too busy to get in a bed with three women, with water everywhere.

Title: RM - 들꽃놀이 (Wild Flower) ft. youjeen (English Translation)!
About:
On “들꽃놀이 (Wild Flower)” RM takes a trip to the past, his 20s, and discusses the internal struggle between fame and success while staying true to who you are.
The song works as the lead single off RM’s first full-length album, Indigo. RM started working on the song in 2019, inviting Cherry Filter’s vocalist, youjeen—his idol and South Korean rock icon—to it.
In the music video, RM is seen surrounded by beautiful nature scenery and has two sides, one in a field of wildflowers and butterflies, and one that’s in flame with nothing left but smoke clouds and destroyed trees. Near the end, RM is then rapping in his sparkly blue mic to a huge audience.

Title: NCT DREAM - 오르골 (Life Is Still Going On) (English Translation)!
Love Sosa!
About: Keef often refers to himself by the name Sosa in reference to Scarface character Alejandro Sosa, who was an international drug dealer and Tony Montana’s boss of sorts.

Title: Believe!
About:
Cher’s “Believe” is about getting over heartbreak. It pioneered the use of Auto-Tune as a recognizable vocal effect; at the time it was known as the “Cher effect”.
According to The New York Times the song took nine years to make. In 1990, songwriter Brian Higgins wrote the initial hook but was unhappy with the verses, so other writers began tinkering with the lyrics and production. Higgins tried pitching the song to various artists he wrote for over the years, but they all passed on it.
Meanwhile, the president of Warner Music UK, Rob Dickins, wanted Cher to “focus on her gay audience with a high-energy dance record.” Dickins told The New York Times that when he heard “Believe” he thought to himself, “Cher could do this chorus, especially the lyrics, with her private life the way it is. She’s gone through all these things.”
After recording the song, the producers worried Cher’s vocals sounded too robotic due to the Auto-Tune. Cher told the Times: “‘Everyone loves that song but wants to change that part of it. I said, 'You can change that part of it, over my dead body!’”
In an interview with Sound on Sound, the song’s producers actually lied about using Auto-Tune and claimed Cher’s vocal effects were created using a vocoder pedal. They were trying to keep Auto-Tune a secret, but it eventually became an industry standard.
The song became a huge international smash, landing at #1 in the US and many other countries including in Italy, Germany, and Spain. It sold over 11 million copies making it one of the best-selling singles of all time.

Title: Yet To Come
About:
“Yet To Come” introduces a heartwarming concept to BTS' anthology album, PROOF. On this light alternative hip-hop track, BTS speak about how the past may consist of the best moments in life, but the greatest moments will come of their path.
The song’s second title, “The Most Beautiful Moment,” references the groups' 화양연화(The Most Beautiful Moment In Life) album series and story concept that explains life hardships from teenage years to adulthood—the youth—which is one of the most beautiful moments in life and should be appreciated more.
The music video showcases BTS' in a variety of scenes in a primarily deserted island, while many of these scenes references the sets of their previous music videos and trailers releases.

Title: Creep!
About:
According to Thom Yorke, “Creep” tells the tale of an inebriated man who tries to get the attention of a woman to whom he is attracted by following her around. In the end, he lacks the self-confidence to face her and feels he subconsciously is her.
When asked about “Creep” in 1993, Yorke said:
I have a real problem being a man in the ‘90s… Any man with any sensitivity or conscience toward the opposite sex would have a problem. To actually assert yourself in a masculine way without looking like you’re in a hard-rock band is a very difficult thing to do… It comes back to the music we write, which is not effeminate, but it’s not brutal in its arrogance. It is one of the things I’m always trying: To assert a sexual persona and on the other hand trying desperately to negate it.
Jonny Greenwood said the song was in fact a happy song about “recognizing what you are”.
Notable covers include those by The Pretenders, Carrie Manolakos, Macy Gray, Lea Michele and Dean Geyer, as part of Glee, Vega Choir, Prince, The Bluecoats Drum and Bugle Corps, and most recently, Kimberly Nichole, who performed a very strong cover of this song on the 2015 season of The Voice.
Despite, or perhaps because of, the popularity of the song, Radiohead grew to hate “Creep” and have rarely played it live since the OK Computer tour. “My Iron Lung”, written in 1994 and later released on The Bends, is about the song.

Song: 곡예사2 REMIX (Feat. MC Sniper, Sikboy, Hash Swan, 마미손, TAKEWON, KOONTA, 2faith)
Title: 곡예사2 (Acrobat2) (Remix)!
Wonderwall!
About:
“Wonderwall” was released as the third single off of Oasis' classic album What’s The Story? (Morning Glory). It was supposedly written for Noel Gallagher’s then-girlfriend, Meg Matthews, as he told NME in 1996, “It’s about my girlfriend, Meg Matthews.” Bandmembers Bonehead and Guigsy seconded that in the documentary Live Forever, the Ups and Downs of Britpop. However, after Gallagher divorced Matthews in 2001 he said the song was not about Matthews: “[the song was] about an imaginary friend who’s gonna come and save you from yourself.” Some people believe it is about Noel talking himself (he is his own imaginary friend) out of leaving Oasis (or leaving life!). Others believe it is about the relationship between Noel and Liam and some say it is about his (then) imaginary friendship with Paul McCartney (they would later become friends in the mid-to-late 1990s).
The song’s title was appropriated from the 1968 film Wonderwall – from Psychedelia to Surrealism, which George Harrison provided the soundtrack (called Wonderwall Music) for. Before Noel had his “George Harrison revelation,” the song was titled “Wishing Stone.”
In 2006, it was included in the “Best Of”-compilation Stop The Clocks and in 2010 it was included in the singles collection Time Flies… 1994-2009.

Title: Try Me!
About:
“Try Me” is the breakout single from the diminutive Detroit sensation. Featuring chilled out production and a steady delivery from DeJ, the single got a huge bump when Drake quoted the “you should see my closet” line in an instagram post.
Peaking at #45 on the Billboard Top 100, the success of the single led to her signing with Columbia, working with Eminem, and touring with Nicki Minaj.

Title: 맛 (Hot Sauce)
About:
“맛 (Hot Sauce)” is a hip-hop song influenced by an afrobeat sound that counts as the title track in NCT DREAM’s freshman full-length album.
The trilingual song includes NCT’s well known experimental sound and showcases the group’s powerful energy as it includes a catchy chorus and a guitar strum that continues throughout most of the song. On the other hand, the lyrics of the song demonstrates the members' confidence, while in the music video NCT DREAM is seen running a taquería while being surrounded by a Mexican-inspired setting.
Upon its release, the lead single became NCT DREAM’s best performing song in streaming charts as of then, since it got an All-Kill on Korean streaming site MelOn’s Real-Time chart and became SM Entertainment’s most viewed music video within 24 hours, with 34.2 million views.

Title: 해금 (Haegeum)!
About:
The song is the lead single of Agust D’s first solo studio album D-DAY. Haegeum references the traditional Korean string instrument of two silk thread strings. The instrument is known for its extensive range of various expressions despite having only two strings, with sounds ranging from sorrowful to humorous.
Another meaning of Haegeum is the straight translation of the word in Korean (해금) meaning “To unlock”, or “to break a ban”.
Explaining the song and the writing process, Yoongi addressed it in the Diseny+ documentary Suga: Road to D-DAY:
The thing about idols is that we don’t have to commit a crime or do something controversial for people to blow little things out of proportion and judge us. I could never understand the mentality of those people. I mean, I’ve never caused trouble, but.. when an idol smokes a cigarette in a music video, this kind of thing… Let’s say I get criticized for these things at the age of 31.
I wanted to unlock what’s forbidden to us, and since a world of information is just a click away from us now, even I stopped bringing my imagination, and my thoughts into action.
So this is my message: “Let’s free ourselves from this thing".

Title: Hard!
About:
“Hard” with Tay-K and Blocboy JB is the first record released from No Jumper Records on March 19th 2018 (in affiliation with Atlantic Records). Adam Grandmaison, or better known as Adam22, is a YouTube podcaster who is known in the Hip-Hop community.
Tay-K has recently been denied bail in February 2018 and is still in prison; his vocals in this track originate from “Opp Shit”.
Tay-K’s aggressive and dark vocals combine well with Blocboy’s flow and equally daunting verse. As with many of Tay-K’s songs, he solidifies his gangster way of life and his violent aggression towards rival gangs.
On July 27th, 2018, No Jumper released the official music video on YouTube. Here, Blocboy is in school detention, where in his spiral notebook, a doodle of Tay-K begins to spit his verses and ultimately roam the school. This is similar to the video for Kodak Black’s “Patty Cake”, in which he was portrayed as an animated character because of his incarceration.

Title: Jimin - Set Me Free Pt.2 (English Translation)!
We Didn’t Start the Fire!
About:
This monster hit of a song was created in 1989, when Joel turned 40. The idea spawned from a conversation he had with a friend of Sean Lennon. The friend just turned 21 and was complaining about how crazy it was to be living in his era, therefore undermining any other time before his. This encouraged Joel to write a song that would prove that any time is filled with extremes.
Starting from 1949 (the year in which he was born), he chronicled the major events that occurred during that time in a rapid fire delivery of names, places, and cultural works.
These are his words on the subject:
I started doing that as a mental exercise. I had turned forty. It was 1989, and I said, “Okay, what’s happened in my life? I wrote down the year 1949… It was kind of a mind game. [It’s] one of the few times I’ve written the lyrics first, which should be obvious to why I usually prefer to write the music first, because the melody is horrendous. It’s like a mosquito droning. It’s one of the worst melodies I’ve ever written. I kind of like the lyric though.
Passage taken from the book, In Their Own Words by Bill DeMain (Chapter 14) where he interviewed Joel about his thoughts on the song.
The song reached #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 and became a radio mainstay.

Title: The Joker!
About:
Taken from the 1973 album The Joker, which topped the US Billboard hot 100 in 1974. First line of the lyrics is a reference to the song “Space Cowboy”, from the Brave New World album. The following lines refer to other earlier songs “Gangster Of Love” from Sailor, and “Enter Maurice” from Recall the beginning…A Journey From Eden.
Song was covered by Fatboy Slim (with vocals by Bootsy Collins), appears in the twelfth episode of the second season of The Simpsons and Johnny Depp, and is referred to in many media.

Song: BLUE CHECK (Feat. 박재범, 제시) (Prod. by Slom)
Title: BLUE CHECK REMIX!
Zoom!
About:
“Zoom” was originally previewed in a since-deleted Instagram post made by Lil Uzi Vert nearly a month after the release of his debut studio album Luv is Rage 2, which featured the caption:
Just a Vibe💨 ….👨🏾‍🎤vs🌎2® ?🙄
This caption would spark the assumption that the song would land on a forthcoming, as-of-yet unreleased, sequel to his Lil Uzi Vert Vs. The World mixtape from 2016.
In July 2019, FaZe Clan member Nordan, better known as FaZe Rain, took to Snapchat and played part of the song. He later joked about dropping it, however, he then went back on it, saying he “didn’t want to do Uzi like that.” Despite this, the song later leaked externally on August 10, 2019. It appears quite unfinished, featuring mostly the same lyrics looping throughout the track.

Song: Snooze (feat. Ryuichi Sakamoto, 김우성 of The Rose)
Title: Snooze!
About: “Snooze” features SZA trying to be with a person who she loves so much. In the bridge, SZA is longing to be loved by this person, and be their number one. The love song was released as the eighth track on SZA’s very highly anticipated sophomore album, SOS, on December 9, 2022, and re-released as a single on August 25th, 2023 alongside a music video and an EP full of different versions of the track.

Title: To D.R.E.A.M.!
About: Tory Lanez reminds us that he came from nothing, and all it took was a dream and hustle to get to where he is now – a life of luxury cars and expensive living.

Title: Face Off!
Singer: Tech N9ne, Joey Cool, King Iso & Dwayne Johnson
About:
Tech N9ne recruits his Strange Music signees, King Iso and Joey Cool, as well as the famous actor, Dwayne “The Rock” Johnson for “Face Off,” the fourth single for his twenty-third album, ASIN9NE.
The song serves as the debut for Dwayne Johnson on hip hop, which helped the song popularize on social media, especially on TikTok.

Title: golden hour!
About:
“golden hour” is the third song released off of JVKE’s debut album, this is what ____ feels like.
Prior to announcing the song, JVKE shared on his Instagram story on July 12, 2022:
This song has a special place in my heart and I can’t wait to share it with you all. I did my absolute best to embody beauty in every detail of the production.
In late August 2022, “golden hour” became the most viral song in the world on Spotify Viral 50 USA and Global chart following a video of himself playing the song on the piano for his childhood piano teacher went viral on numerous social platforms.

Title: Drowning!
About: On “Drowning,” A Boogie and Kodak rap about flexing jewelry and riches as they made it from the bottom and are now two of the hottest rappers in the game.

Title: Grandfather please stand on the shoulders of my father while he’s deep-sea fishing!
About:
“Grandfather please stand on the shoulders of my father while he’s deep-sea fishing” is the eleventh song on Lana Del Rey’s ninth studio album and eighth major-label studio album “Did you know that there’s a tunnel under Ocean Blvd”.
The song features French pianist RIOPY’s instrumental track “Flo”.
In “Grandfather please stand on the shoulders of my father while he’s deep-sea fishing,” Lana expresses her desire for spiritual guidance and connection with a higher power. The three white butterflies are symbolic of divine presence, and she requests signs to know that she is being heard and supported.

Title: Good Boy Gone Bad
About:
TOMORROW X TOGETHER’s “Good Boy Gone Bad”, a hip hop and rock-influenced track marks a dark and heavily emotional transformation of a titular “boy” with a good temperament after experiencing a typical break-up for the first time, as also shows the power of majesty in an awe thing.
The song serves as the title track off of the group’s fourth mini album, minisode 2: Thursday’s Child.

Title: 잠수이별 (Ghosting)!
About:
“Ghosting” is a digital single released by Woo and meenoi.
This catchy song with a laid-back melody addresses one of the most significant issues affecting Gen Z and millennials globally. The lyrics are about the concept of ghosting in a romantic relationship. Ghosting refers to the act of suddenly cutting off all communication and disappearing without any explanation.
The song was written and produced in the key C# Minor, with a tempo of 124 BPM.

Title: 연애소설 (Love Story)!
About:
According to an report on August 25, IU, is featured in Epik High’s comeback their first comeback after 3 years.
“LOVE STORY” is one of the double title tracks on We’ve Done Something Wonderful and is about the unforgettable memories that leave pain after a breakup.
Also the music video is composed of two songs, the first song is 빈차 (HOME IS FAR AWAY), and the second part featured 연애소설 (LOVE STORY)

Title: BTS - Life Goes On (English Translation)!
About:
This nostalgic song blurs alternative hip-hop sound with acoustic guitar and synth-pop. The lyrics offer comfort in a time when the world faces the pandemic.
The story behind the song begins from BTS declaring that “even in the face of this new normality, our life goes on and imparts a message of healing to fans and to the world.”
The theme “life goes on” was mentioned several times while BTS shared the process behind the album through vlogs over the months.
“Life goes on, let’s live on” was also the main message of their speech at the 75th UN General Assembly, given just a few months before the song release. The group appeared there as they’ve worked previously with UNICEF on the “LOVE MYSELF” campaign, this time giving the message of “let’s live on in a new world, breaking free from despair and supporting one another in warm solidarity.”
The song serves as the leading track and the second promotional single from BTS' fifth studio album, BE.
The single was announced by BigHit Entertainment via their official SNS and Weverse accounts on October 30, 2020.
The live debut stage took place during the American Music Awards on November 22, 2020.

Title: 0 to 100 / The Catch Up!
About:
This song features two parts. The first, “0 to 100,” is a typical braggadocio rap song resembling Drake’s smash “Started from The Bottom.” The second, “The Catch Up,” features an unreleased James Blake sample and is more reflective than the track’s first part.
This song was nominated for Best Rap Performance and Best Rap Song at the 57th Annual Grammy Awards in 2015 but didn’t win either category. It has gone Platinum in the United States.
In December 2014, Diddy claimed Drake “stole” the “0-100” beat. Diddy reportedly got physical with Drake outside a Miami nightclub. Diddy later clarified that he originally sent the beat for Drake to ghostwrite over, but he kept it for himself.

Title: Stray Kids - CASE 143 (English Translation)!
About:
Stray Kids' wholesome lead single “CASE 143” charms its way into their listeners' hearts as the song opens their seventh mini-album, MAXIDENT.
The song consists of their signature dynamic electronic dance pop-style. The song’s lyrics—written by the group’s production team, 3RACHA—express the uncontrollable feeling of attraction towards someone, but not being able to express their feelings.
This is why instead of just saying “l love you,” they use the numeric code instead (I (1), love (4), you (3)). 143 is a slang term that originated in the 1990s as a code back when pagers were popular.
The music video follows a mysterious investigation with Stray Kids acting as cops trying to catch their doppelgängers who transform into fluffy heart monsters, with the biggest one dubbed by fans as ‘Pipi.’

Title: Hold On, We’re Going Home!
About:
Drake’s single isn’t a rap song as we all know, but here’s the rapper-singer reiterating the fact for all us: Drizzy says him and Noah “40” Shebib “wanted to channelize the Michael Jackson-Quincy Jones sound with the song.”
SPIN describes the song as:
A dreamy, 100-BPM cut that nicely split the difference between synth-soaked R&B (think Tina Turner’s “What’s Love Got to Do with It”) and slow-motion deep house (Disclosure’s “Help Me Lose My Mind”).

Title: I’m the One!
About:
“I’m the One” features a star-studded line-up of artists in especially braggadocio spirits, with Justin Bieber, Chance The Rapper, Lil Wayne and Quavo boasting to the women in their lives that they are “the only one” for them. “I’m the One” is Khaled’s second single from his 2017 album Grateful, following the February release of “Shining.”
The collaboration was teased throughout videos and pictures via the Instagram profiles of the featured artists, showing the making of the music video.
The song debuted at No.1 on the Billboard Hot 100 chart and UK Official Singles Chart. It became the second song to debut at #1 on the Hot 100 in 2017 making it Bieber’s second feat and Khaled’s first.
